74:6.text: Do not confer a favor to gain more,

74:7.text: and be patient before your Lord.

74:8.text: When there is a blast on the trumpet,

74:9.text: that Day will be a hard Day

74:10.text: – far from easy on the disbelievers.

74:11.text: Leave Me (to deal with) him whom I created alone,

74:12.text: and for whom I supplied extensive wealth,

74:14.text: and made everything smooth for him.

74:15.text: Then he is eager that I should do more.

74:16.text: By no means! He is stubborn to Our signs.

74:17.text: I shall burden him with a hard climb.

74:19.text: so may he perish (for) how he decided!

74:20.text: Once again, may he perish (for) how he decided!

74:23.text: then he turned back and became arrogant,

74:24.text: and said, ‘This is nothing but ordinary magic.

74:25.text: This is nothing but the word of a human being.’

74:26.text: I shall burn him in Saqar!

74:27.text: And what will make you know what Saqar is?

74:28.text: It spares nothing, and leaves nothing,

74:31.text: We have made only angels as keepers of the Fire, and We have made their number only as a test for the disbelievers, so that those who have been given the Book may be certain, and that those who believe may increase in belief, and that those who have been given the Book and those who believe may not be in doubt, and that those in whose hearts is a sickness and the disbelievers may say, ‘What did God intend by this as a parable?’ In this way God leads astray whomever He pleases and guides whomever He pleases. No one knows the (angelic) forces of your Lord but Him. It is nothing but a reminder to humankind.

74:32.text: By no means! By the moon,

74:33.text: and the night when it retreats,

74:34.text: and the morning when it brightens!

74:35.text: Surely it is indeed one of the greatest things

74:37.text: to whoever of you pleases to go forward or lag behind.

74:38.text: Each person (is held) in pledge for what he has earned,

74:39.text: except for the companions on the right.

74:40.text: In Gardens they will ask each other questions

74:43.text: They will say, ‘We were not among the ones who prayed,

74:44.text: and we did not feed the poor,

74:45.text: and we bantered with the banterers,

74:46.text: and we called the Day of Judgment a lie,

74:47.text: until the certainty came to us.’

74:48.text: The intercession of the intercessors will not benefit them.

74:49.text: What is (the matter) with them, turning away from the Reminder,

74:50.text: as if they were frightened donkeys

74:52.text: No! Each one of them wants to be given scrolls unrolled.

74:53.text: By no means! No! They do not fear the Hereafter.

74:54.text: By no means! Surely it is a reminder,

74:55.text: and whoever pleases takes heed of it.

74:56.text: But they will not take heed unless God pleases. He is worthy of guarding (oneself) against, and worthy of (dispensing) forgiveness.

3:190.text: Surely in the creation of the heavens and earth, and (in) the alternation of the night and the day, (there are) signs indeed for those with understanding,

3:191.text: who remember God, whether standing or sitting or (lying) on their sides, and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth: ‘Our Lord, You have not created this in vain. Glory to You! Guard us against the punishment of the Fire.

3:192.text: Our Lord, surely You – whomever You cause to enter the Fire, You have disgraced him. The evildoers will have no helpers.

3:193.text: Our Lord, surely we have heard a caller calling (us) to belief (saying): “Believe in your Lord!” So we have believed. Our Lord, forgive us our sins, and absolve us of our evil deeds, and take us with the pious.

3:194.text: Our Lord, give us what You have promised us on (the assurance of) Your messengers, and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection. Surely You will not break the appointment.’

3:195.text: And their Lord responded to them: ‘Surely I do not let a deed of anyone of you go to waste – whether male or female – you are all alike. Those who have emigrated, and were expelled from their homes, and suffered harm in My way, and have fought and been killed – I shall indeed absolve them of their evil deeds, and I shall indeed cause them to enter Gardens through which rivers flow. A reward from God! God – with Him is the best reward.’

3:196.text: Do not let the disbelievers’ comings and goings in the lands deceive you.

3:197.text: A little enjoyment (of life), then their refuge is Gehenna – it is an evil resting place!

3:198.text: But the ones who guard (themselves) against their Lord – for them (there are) Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain. A reception from God! And what is with God is better for the pious.

3:199.text: Surely (there are) some of the People of the Book who indeed believe in God, and what has been sent down to you, and what has been sent down to them, humbling themselves before God. They do not sell the signs of God for a small price. Those – for them their reward is with their Lord. Surely God is quick at the reckoning.

3:200.text: You who believe! Be patient and strive in patience, and be steadfast, and guard (yourselves) against God, so that you may prosper.

61:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies God. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

61:2.text: You who believe! Why do you say what you do not do?

61:3.text: It is very hateful in the sight of God that you say what you do not do.

61:4.text: God loves those who fight in His way, (drawn up) in lines (for battle) as if they were a solid building.

61:5.text: (Remember) when Moses said to his people, ‘My people! Why do you hurt me, when you already know that I am the messenger of God to you?’ Then, when they turned aside, God caused their hearts to turn aside, (for) God does not guide the people who are wicked.

61:6.text: And (remember) when Jesus, son of Mary, said, ‘Sons of Israel! Surely I am the messenger of God to you, confirming what was before me of the Torah, and bringing good news of a messenger who will come after me, whose name will be Aḥmad.’ Then, when he brought them the clear signs, they said, ‘This is clear magic.’

61:7.text: Who is more evil than the one who forges lies against God, when he is called to Islam? God does not guide the people who are evildoers.

61:8.text: They want to extinguish the light of God with their mouths, but God will perfect His light, even though the disbelievers dislike (it).

61:9.text: He (it is) who has sent His messenger with the guidance and the religion of truth, so that He may cause it to prevail over religion – all of it – even though the idolaters dislike (it).

61:10.text: You who believe! Shall I direct you to a transaction that will rescue you from a painful punishment?

61:11.text: You (should) believe in God and His messenger, and struggle in the way of God with your wealth and your lives – that is better for you, if (only) you knew.

61:12.text: He will forgive you your sins, and cause you to enter Gardens through which rivers flow, and good dwelling places in Gardens of Eden – that is the great triumph! –

61:13.text: and another thing which you love: help from God and a victory near (at hand). Give good news to the believers!

61:14.text: You who believe! Be the helpers of God, as Jesus, son of Mary, said to the disciples, ‘Who will be my helpers to God?’ The disciples said, ‘We will be the helpers of God.’ One contingent of the Sons of Israel believed, and (another) contingent disbelieved. So We supported those who believed against their enemy, and they were the ones who prevailed.

3:161.text: It is not for a prophet to defraud. Whoever defrauds will bring what he has defrauded on the Day of Resurrection. Then everyone will be paid in full what they have earned – and they will not be done evil.

3:162.text: Is the one who follows after the approval of God like the one who incurs the anger of God? His refuge will be Gehenna – and it is an evil destination!

3:163.text: They (have different) ranks with God, and God sees what they do.

3:164.text: Certainly God bestowed favor on the believers when He raised up among them a messenger from among them, to recite His signs to them, and to purify them, and to teach them the Book and the wisdom, though before (this) they were indeed clearly astray.

3:165.text: Why, when a smiting smote you – you had already smitten twice (as many in comparison to) it – did you say, ‘How is this?’ Say: ‘You yourselves are to blame. Surely God is powerful over everything.’

3:166.text: What smote you on the day when the two forces met (happened) by the permission of God, so that He might know the (true) believers,

3:167.text: and that He might know those who played the hypocrite. It was said to them, ‘Come, fight in the way of God, or defend!’ But they said, ‘If we knew (how) to fight, we would indeed follow you.’ They were nearer to disbelief that day than to belief. They were saying with their mouths what was not in their hearts. But God knows what they were concealing –

3:168.text: those who said of their brothers, when they (themselves) sat (at home), ‘If they had obeyed us, they would not have been killed.’ Say: ‘Avert death from yourselves, if you are truthful.’

3:169.text: Do not think of those who have been killed in the way of God as dead. No! (They are) alive with their Lord (and) provided for,

3:170.text: gloating over what God has given them of his favor, and welcoming the good news about those who have not (yet) joined them of those who stayed behind – that (there will be) no fear on them, nor will they sorrow.

3:171.text: They welcome the good news of blessing from God, and favor, and that God does not let the reward of the believers go to waste.

3:172.text: Those who responded (to the call of) God and the messenger after the wound had smitten them – for those of them who have done good and guarded (themselves), (there is) a great reward.

3:173.text: (They are) those to whom the people said, ‘Surely the enemy has gathered against you, so fear them!’ But it increased them in belief, and they said, ‘God is enough for us. Excellent is the Guardian.’

3:174.text: So they turned back by the blessing and favor of God, without any evil touching them. They followed after the approval of God, and God is full of great favor.

3:175.text: That is only Satan (who) frightens his allies. Do not fear them, but fear Me, if you are believers.

3:176.text: Do not let those who are quick to disbelieve cause you sorrow. Surely they will not harm God at all. God does not wish to assign to them any share in the Hereafter. For them (there is) a great punishment.

3:177.text: Surely those who have purchased disbelief with the (price of) belief will not harm God at all. For them (there is) a painful punishment.

3:178.text: And let not those who disbelieve think that We spare them for their own good. We only spare them so that they will increase in sin! For them (there is) a humiliating punishment.

3:179.text: God is not one to leave the believers in (the situation) you are in until He separates the bad from the good. Nor is God one to inform you of the unseen, but God chooses from His messengers whomever He pleases. So believe in God and His messengers. If you believe and guard (yourselves), for you (there is) a great reward.

3:180.text: And let not those who are stingy with what God has given them of His favor think that it is good for them. No! It is bad for them. What they are stingy with will be hung about their necks on the Day of Resurrection. To God (belongs) the inheritance of the heavens and the earth. God is aware of what you do.

3:181.text: Certainly God has heard the words of those who said, ‘Surely God is poor and we are rich.’ We shall write down what they have said, along with their killing the prophets without any right, and We shall say, ‘Taste the punishment of the burning (Fire)!

3:182.text: That is for what your (own) hands have sent forward, and (know) that God is not an evildoer to (His) servants.’

3:183.text: Those (are the same people) who said, ‘Surely God has made us promise not to believe in any messenger until he brings a sacrifice which fire devours.’ Say: ‘Messengers have come to you before me with the clear signs, and with that which you spoke of. So why did you kill them, if you are truthful?’

3:184.text: If they call you a liar, (know that) messengers have been called liars before you, who brought the clear signs, and the scriptures, and the illuminating Book.

3:185.text: Every person will taste death, and you will only be paid your rewards in full on the Day of Resurrection. Whoever is removed from the Fire and admitted to the Garden has triumphed. This present life is nothing but a deceptive enjoyment.

3:186.text: You will indeed be tested concerning your wealth and your own lives, and you will indeed hear from those who were given the Book before you, and from those who are idolaters, much hurt. But if you are patient and guard (yourselves) – surely that is one of the determining factors in (all) affairs.

3:187.text: (Remember) when God took a covenant with those who had been given the Book: ‘You shall indeed make it clear to the people, and shall not conceal it.’ But they tossed it behind their backs, and sold it for a small price. Evil is what they purchased!

3:188.text: Do not think (that) those who gloat over what they have brought, and like to be praised for what they have not done – do not think that they are in (a place of) safety from the punishment. For them (there is) a painful punishment.

3:189.text: To God (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. God is powerful over everything.

77:1.text: By the ones sent forth in succession, and the ones blasting (with their) blast!

77:5.text: and the ones casting a reminder,

77:7.text: Surely what you are promised is indeed going to fall!

77:9.text: and when the sky is split open,

77:10.text: and when the mountains are scattered (as dust),

77:11.text: and when the messengers’ time is given –

77:12.text: for what Day are these things appointed?

77:14.text: And what will make you know what the Day of Decision is?

77:15.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:16.text: Did We not destroy those of old?

77:17.text: Then We caused later (generations) to follow them.

77:18.text: In this way We deal with the sinners.

77:19.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:20.text: Did We not create you from despicable water,

77:21.text: and put it in a secure dwelling place

77:23.text: We determined (it) – excellent were the Ones able (to do that)!

77:24.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:25.text: Did We not make the earth as a container

77:27.text: And did We not place on it lofty mountains, and give you fresh water to drink?

77:28.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:29.text: Depart to what you called a lie!

77:31.text: –(it affords) no sheltering (shade) and (it is of) no use against the flame.

77:32.text: Surely it shoots out sparks, (each one) the size of a castle,

77:33.text: as if it were (the color) of yellow camels.

77:34.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:35.text: This is a Day when they will not speak,

77:36.text: nor will it be permitted to them to make excuses.

77:37.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:38.text: ‘This is the Day of Decision. We have gathered you and those of old together.

77:39.text: If you have a plot, plot against Me!’

77:40.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:41.text: Surely the ones who guard (themselves) will be in (the midst of) shades and springs,

77:42.text: and fruits of whatever (kind) they desire:

77:43.text: ‘Eat and drink with satisfaction (in return) for what you have done.’

77:44.text: Surely in this way We repay the doers of good.

77:45.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:46.text: ‘Eat and enjoy (life) a little. Surely you are sinners!’

77:47.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:48.text: When it is said to them, ‘Bow down,’ they do not bow down.

77:49.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie!

77:50.text: In what proclamation will they believe after this?

42:3.text: In this way He inspires you, and those who were before you – God, the Mighty, the Wise.

42:4.text: To Him (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He is the Most High, the Almighty.

42:5.text: The heavens are nearly torn apart from above, when the angels glorify their Lord with praise, and ask forgiveness for those on the earth. Is it not a fact that God – He is the Forgiving, the Compassionate?

42:6.text: Those who have taken allies other than Him – God is watcher over them. You are not a guardian over them.

42:7.text: In this way We have inspired you (with) an Arabic Qur’ān, so that you may warn the Mother of Towns and those around it, and so that you may warn of the Day of Gathering – (there is) no doubt about it – (one) group in the Garden, and (another) group in the blazing (Fire).

42:8.text: If God had (so) pleased, He would indeed will have made them one community. But He causes whomever He pleases to enter into His mercy. The evildoers will have no ally and no helper.

42:9.text: Or have they taken allies other than Him? God – He is the (true) Ally. He gives the dead life. He is powerful over everything.

42:10.text: Whatever you differ about, judgment of it (belongs) to God. That is God, my Lord. In Him I have put my trust, and to Him I turn (in repentance).

42:11.text: (He is) the Creator of the heavens and the earth. He has made pairs for you from yourselves, and pairs (also) from the livestock. He scatters you by this means. There is nothing like Him. He is the Hearing, the Seeing.

42:12.text: To Him (belong) the keys of the heavens and the earth. He extends (His) provision to whomever He pleases, and restricts (it). Surely He has knowledge of everything.

42:13.text: He has instituted for you from the religion what He charged Noah with, and that which We have inspired you (with), and what We charged Abraham, and Moses, and Jesus with: ‘Observe the religion, and do not become divided in it.’ What you call them to is hard on the idolaters. God chooses for Himself whomever He pleases, and He guides to Himself whoever turns (to Him in repentance).

42:14.text: They did not become divided until after the knowledge had come to them, (because of) envy among themselves. Were it not for a preceding word from your Lord, until an appointed time, it would indeed have been decided between them. Surely those who inherited the Book after them are in grave doubt indeed about it.

42:15.text: So call (them) to that, and go straight as you have been commanded, and do not follow their (vain) desires, but say: ‘I believe in whatever Book God has sent down, and I have been commanded to act fairly among you. God is our Lord and your Lord. To us our deeds and to you your deeds. (There is) no argument between us and you. God will gather us together. To Him is the (final) destination.’

42:16.text: Those who (still) argue about God, after whatever response has been made to Him – their argument is refuted in the sight of their Lord. Anger (will fall) on them, and for them (there will be) a harsh punishment.

42:17.text: (It is) God who has sent down the Book with the truth, and (also) the scale. What will make you know? Perhaps the Hour is near.

42:18.text: Those who do not believe in it seek to hurry it, but those who believe in it are apprehensive about it, and know that it is the truth. Is it not a fact that those who are in doubt about the Hour are far astray?

109:2.text: I do not serve what you serve,

109:3.text: and you are not serving what I serve.

109:4.text: I am not serving what you have served,

109:5.text: and you are not serving what I serve.

109:6.text: To you your religion and to me my religion.’

16:1.text: The command of God has come! Do not seek to hurry it. Glory to Him! He is exalted above what they associate.

16:2.text: He sends down the angels with the spirit of His command on whomever He pleases of His servants: ‘Give warning that (there is) no god but Me, so guard (yourselves) against Me!’

16:3.text: He created the heavens and the earth in truth. He is exalted above what they associate.

16:4.text: He created the human from a drop, and suddenly he (becomes) a clear adversary.

16:5.text: And the cattle – He created them for you. (There is) warmth in them and (other) benefits, and from them you eat.

16:6.text: And (there is) beauty in them for you, when you bring them in and when you lead them out.

16:7.text: They carry your loads to a land you would (otherwise) not reach without exhausting yourselves. Surely your Lord is indeed kind, compassionate.

16:8.text: (He also created) horses and mules and donkeys for you to ride, and for display. And He creates what you do not know.

16:9.text: (It is incumbent) on God (to set the) direction of the way, yet (there is) deviation from it. If He had (so) pleased, He would indeed have guided you all.

16:10.text: He (it is) who sends down water from the sky. From it you have (something to) drink, and from it vegetation (grows) on which you pasture (livestock).

16:11.text: By means of it He causes the crops to grow for you, and (also) olives, and date palms, and grapes, and all (kinds of) fruit. Surely in that is a sign indeed for a people who reflect.

16:12.text: He subjected the night and the day for you, and the sun and the moon, and the stars (are) subjected by His command. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who understand.

16:13.text: And whatever He has scattered for you on the earth (with) its various colors – surely in that is a sign indeed for a people who take heed.

16:14.text: He (it is) who subjected the sea, so that you may eat fresh fish from it, and bring out of it an ornament which you wear, and you see the ship cutting through it, and (it is) so that you may seek some of His favor, and that you may be thankful.

16:15.text: And He cast on the earth firm mountains, so that it does not sway with you (on it), and rivers and (path)ways, that you may guide yourselves,

16:16.text: and landmarks (too). And by the stars they guide (themselves).

16:17.text: Is the One who creates like the one who does not create? Will you not take heed?

16:18.text: If you (try to) number God’s blessing, you will not (be able to) count it. Surely God is indeed forgiving, compassionate.

16:19.text: God knows what you keep secret and what you speak aloud.

16:20.text: Those they call on instead of God do not create anything, since they are (themselves) created.

16:21.text: (They are) dead, not alive, and they do not realize when they will be raised up.

16:22.text: Your God is one God. Those who do not believe in the Hereafter – their hearts are defiant, and they are arrogant.

16:23.text: (There is) no doubt that God knows what they keep secret and what they speak aloud. Surely He does not love the arrogant.

16:24.text: When it is said to them, ‘What has your Lord sent down?,’ they say, ‘Old tales!’

16:25.text: – that they may bear their own burdens fully on the Day of Resurrection, and (also) some of the burdens of those whom they led astray without (their) realizing (it). Evil is what they will bear!

16:26.text: Those who were before them schemed, but God came (against) their building from the foundations, and the roof fell down on them from above them, and the punishment came upon them from where they did not realize (it would).

16:27.text: Then on the Day of Resurrection He will disgrace them, and say, ‘Where are My associates for whose sake you broke away?’ Those who were given the knowledge will say, ‘Surely today disgrace and evil are on the disbelievers,

16:28.text: those who – the angels take them (while they are doing) themselves evil.’ They will offer peace: ‘We were not doing anything evil.’ ‘Yes indeed (you were)! Surely God is aware of what you have done.

16:29.text: So enter the gates of Gehenna, there to remain. Evil indeed is the dwelling place of the arrogant!’

16:30.text: And it is said to those who guard (themselves), ‘What has your Lord sent down?’ They say, ‘Good!’ For those who do good in this world (there is) good, but the Home of the Hereafter is indeed better. Excellent indeed is the Home of the ones who guard (themselves)

16:31.text: – Gardens of Eden, which they will enter, through which rivers flow, where they will have whatever they please. In this way God repays the ones who guard (themselves),

16:32.text: those who – the angels take them (while they are doing) good. They will say, ‘Peace (be) upon you! Enter the Garden for what you have done.’

16:33.text: Do they expect (anything) but the angels to come to them, or the command of your Lord to come? So did those who were before them. God did not do them evil, but they did themselves evil.

16:34.text: So the evils of what they had done smote them, and what they were mocking overwhelmed them.

16:35.text: The idolaters say, ‘If God had (so) pleased, we would not have served anything other than Him, neither we nor our fathers, and we would not have forbidden anything other than Him.’ So did those who were before them. (Does anything depend) on the messengers except the clear delivery (of the message)?

16:36.text: Certainly We have raised up in every community a messenger (saying): ‘Serve God and avoid al-Ṭāghūt!’ (There were) some of them whom God guided, and some whose going astray was deserved. Travel the earth and see how the end was for the ones who called (it) a lie.

16:37.text: If you are eager for their guidance – surely God does not guide those whom He leads astray. They will have no helpers.

16:38.text: They have sworn by God the most solemn of their oaths: ‘God will not raise up anyone who dies!’ Yes indeed! (It is) a promise (binding) on Him in truth, but most of the people do not know (it).

16:39.text: (They will be raised) so that He may make clear their differences to them, and so that those who disbelieved may know that they were liars.

16:40.text: Our only word to a thing, when We intend it, is that We say to it, ‘Be!’ and it is.

16:41.text: Those who emigrate in (the way of) God, after they have been done evil – We shall indeed give them a good settlement in this world, but the reward of the Hereafter is indeed greater, if (only) they knew.

16:42.text: (They are) those who are patient and put their trust in their Lord.

16:43.text: We have not sent (anyone) before you except men whom We inspired – just ask the People of the Reminder, if you do not know (it) –

16:44.text: with the clear signs and the scriptures, and We have sent down to you the Reminder, so that you may make clear to the people what has been sent down to them, and that they will reflect.

16:45.text: Do those who have schemed evils feel secure that God will not cause the earth to swallow them, or that the punishment will not come upon them from where they do not realize?

16:46.text: Or that He will not seize them in their comings and goings, and they will not be able to escape?

16:47.text: Or that He will not seize them with a (sudden) fright? Surely your Lord is indeed kind, compassionate.

16:48.text: Do they not see anything of what God has created? (How) its shadows revolve from the right and the left, prostrating themselves before God, and they are humble?

16:49.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth prostrates itself before God – every living creature and the angels (too) – and they are not arrogant.

16:50.text: They fear their Lord above them, and they do what they are commanded.

16:51.text: God has said: ‘Do not take two gods. He is only one God. So Me – fear Me (alone)!’

16:52.text: To Him (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and to Him (belongs) the religion forever. Will you guard (yourselves) against (anyone) other than God?

16:53.text: Whatever blessing you have is from God. Then when hardship touches you, (it is) to Him you cry out.

16:54.text: Then when He removes the hardship from you, suddenly a group of you associates with their Lord,

16:55.text: to show ingratitude for what We have given them. Enjoy (yourselves)! Soon you will know!

16:56.text: They assign to what they do not know a portion of what We have provided them. By God! Surely you will indeed be questioned about what you have forged.

16:57.text: And they assign daughters to God – glory to Him! – and to themselves (they assign) what they desire.

16:58.text: When one of them is given news of a female (child), his face turns dark and he chokes back his disappointment.

16:59.text: He hides himself from the people because of the evil of what he has been given news about. Should he keep it in humiliation or bury it in the dust? Is it not evil what they judge?

16:60.text: An evil parable (is fitting) for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, but (only) the highest parable (is fitting) for God. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

16:61.text: If God were to take the people to task for their evildoing, He would not leave on it any living creature. But He is sparing them until an appointed time. When their time comes, they will not delay (it) by an hour, nor will they advance (it by an hour).

16:62.text: They assign to God what they (themselves) dislike, and their tongues allege the lie that the best is for them. (There is) no doubt that the Fire (is fitting) for them, and that they (will) be rushed (into it).

16:63.text: By God! Certainly We sent messengers to communities before you, but Satan made their deeds appear enticing to them. So he is their ally today, and for them (there is) a painful punishment.

16:64.text: We have not sent down on you the Book, except for you to make clear their differences to them, and (We have sent it down) as a guidance and mercy for a people who believe.

16:65.text: (It is) God (who) sends down water from the sky, and by means of it gives the earth life after its death. Surely in that is a sign indeed for a people who hear.

16:66.text: And surely in the cattle is a lesson indeed for you: We give you to drink from what is in their bellies – between excretions and blood – pure milk, pleasant tasting to the drinkers.

16:67.text: And from the fruits of the date palms and the grapes, from which you take an intoxicating drink and a good provision – surely in that is a sign indeed for a people who understand.

16:68.text: And your Lord inspired the bee: ‘Make hives among the mountains, and among the trees, and among what they construct.

16:69.text: Then eat from all the fruits, and follow the ways of your Lord subserviently.’ (There) comes forth from their bellies a drink of various colors, in which (there is) healing for the people. Surely in that is a sign indeed for a people who reflect.

16:70.text: (It is) God (who) creates you. Then He will take you. But among you (there is) one who is reduced to the worst (stage) of life, so that he knows nothing after (having had) knowledge. Surely God is knowing, powerful.

16:71.text: God has favored some of you over others in the (matter of) provision, but those who have been favored do not give over their provision to what their right (hands) own, so (that) they are (all) equal in that respect. Is it the blessing of God they deny?

16:72.text: God has given you wives from yourselves, and from your wives He has given you sons and grandsons, and He has provided you with good things. Do they believe in falsehood, and do they disbelieve in the blessing of God?

16:73.text: Do they serve, instead of God, what has no power to provide anything for them from the heavens or the earth, nor are they able (to do anything)?

16:74.text: Do not strike any parables for God. Surely God knows and you do not know.

16:75.text: God strikes a parable: a slave (who is) owned – he has no power over anything – and (another) whom We have provided with a good provision from Us, and he contributes from it in secret and in public. Are they equal? Praise (be) to God! No! But most of them do not know (it).

16:76.text: God strikes a parable: two men, one of them cannot speak – he has no power over anything, and he is a burden on his master – wherever he directs him, he does not bring (back anything) good. Is he equal to the one who commands justice and is himself on a straight path?

16:77.text: To God (belongs) the unseen of the heavens and the earth, and the affair of the Hour is only like a blink of the eye, or it is nearer. Surely God is powerful over everything.

16:78.text: (It is) God (who) brought you forth from the bellies of your mothers – you did not know a thing – and made for you hearing and sight and hearts, so that you may be thankful.

16:79.text: Do they not see the birds, subjected in the midst of the sky? No one holds them (up) but God. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who believe.

16:80.text: God has made (a place of) rest for you from your houses, and made houses for you from the skins of the livestock, which you find light (to carry) on the day of your departure and on the day of your encampment. And from their wool and their fur and their hair (He has made for you) furnishings and enjoyment for a time.

16:81.text: God has made (places of) shade for you from what He has created, and made (places of) cover for you from the mountains, and made clothing for you to guard you from the heat, and clothing to guard you from your (own) violence. In this way He completes His blessing on you, so that you will submit.

16:82.text: If they turn away – only (dependent) on you is the clear delivery (of the message).

16:83.text: They recognize the blessing of God, (and) then they reject it. Most of them are ungrateful.

16:84.text: On the Day when We raise up a witness from every community, then no permission (to speak) will be given to those who have disbelieved, nor will they be allowed to make amends.

16:85.text: When those who have done evil see the punishment, it will not be lightened for them, nor will they be spared.

16:86.text: When those who were idolaters see their associates, they will say, ‘Our Lord, these are our associates, on whom we used to call instead of You.’ But they will cast (back) at them the word: ‘Surely you are liars indeed!’

16:87.text: And they will offer peace to God on that Day, and (then) what they have forged will abandon them.

16:88.text: Those who disbelieve and keep (people) from the way of God – We shall increase them in punishment upon punishment because they were fomenting corruption.

16:89.text: On the Day when We raise up in every community a witness against them from among them, and bring you as a witness against these (people) [. . .]. We have sent down on you the Book as an explanation for everything, and as a guidance and mercy, and as good news for those who submit.

16:90.text: Surely God commands justice and good, and giving to family, and He forbids immorality, and wrong, and envy. He admonishes you so that you may take heed.

16:91.text: Fulfill the covenant of God, when you have made a covenant, and do not break (your) oaths after their confirmation, when you have made God a guarantor over you. Surely God is aware of what you do.

16:92.text: Do not be like the one who unraveled her yarn, after (it was) firmly spun, (into) broken strands, (by) taking your oaths as a (means of) deception between you, because (one) community is more numerous than (another) community. God is only testing you by means of it. He will indeed make clear to you your differences on the Day of Resurrection.

16:93.text: If God had (so) pleased, He would indeed have made you one community, but He leads astray whomever He pleases and guides whomever He pleases. You will indeed be questioned about what you have done.

16:94.text: Do not take your oaths as a (means of) deception between you, so that a foot should slip after its standing firm, and you taste evil for having kept (people) from the way of God, and (there be) for you a great punishment.

16:95.text: Do not sell the covenant of God for a small price. Surely what is with God is better for you, if (only) you knew.

16:96.text: What is with you fails, but what is with God lasts, and We shall indeed pay those who are patient their reward for the best of what they have done.

16:97.text: Whoever does righteousness – whether male or female – and he is a believer – We shall indeed give him a good life, and We shall indeed pay them their reward for the best of what they have done.

16:98.text: When you recite the Qur’ān, take refuge with God from the accursed Satan.

16:99.text: Surely he has no authority over those who believe and put their trust in their Lord.

16:100.text: His authority is only over those who take him as an ally, and those who associate (other gods) with Him.

16:101.text: When We exchange a verse in place of (another) verse – and God knows what He sends down – they say, ‘You are only a forger!’ No! But most of them do not know (anything).

16:102.text: Say: ‘The holy spirit has brought it down from your Lord in truth, to make firm those who believe, and as a guidance and good news for those who submit.’

16:103.text: Certainly We know that they say, ‘Only a human being teaches him.’ The language of the one to whom they perversely allude is foreign, but this language is clear Arabic.

16:104.text: Surely those who do not believe in the signs of God – God will not guide them, and for them (there is) a painful punishment.

16:105.text: Only they forge lies who do not believe in the signs of God. Those – they are the liars!

16:106.text: Whoever disbelieves in God after having believed – except for someone who is compelled, yet his heart is (still) secure in belief – and whoever expands his heart in disbelief – on them is anger from God, and for them (there is) a great punishment.

16:107.text: That is because they loved this present life over the Hereafter, and because God does not guide the disbelievers.

16:108.text: Those – God has set a seal on their hearts and their hearing and their sight. And those – they are the oblivious.

16:109.text: (There is) no doubt that in the Hereafter they (will be) the losers.

16:110.text: Then surely your Lord – to those who emigrated after having been persecuted, (and) then struggled and were patient – surely your Lord after that is indeed forgiving, compassionate,

16:111.text: on the Day when each person will come disputing on his own behalf, and each person will be paid in full for what he has done – and they will not be done evil.

16:112.text: God strikes a parable: a town was secure (and) at rest, its provision coming to it in abundance from every place, but it was ungrateful for the blessings of God. So God caused it to wear the clothing of hunger and fear for what they had been doing.

16:113.text: Certainly a messenger had come to them from among them, but they called him a liar. So the punishment seized them while they were doing evil.

16:114.text: Eat from what God has provided you as permitted (and) good, and be thankful for the blessing of God, if it is Him you serve.

16:115.text: He has only forbidden you: the dead (animal), and the blood, and swine’s flesh, and what has been dedicated to (a god) other than God.’ But whoever is forced (by necessity), not desiring or (deliberately) transgressing – surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

16:116.text: (As) for what your tongues (may) allege, do not speak the (following) lie: ‘This is permitted but that is forbidden,’ so that you forge lies against God. Surely those who forge lies against God – they will not prosper.

16:117.text: A little enjoyment (of life), and (then) for them (there is) a painful punishment.

16:118.text: To those who are Jews, We have forbidden what We recounted to you before. We did not do them evil, but they did themselves evil.

16:119.text: Then surely your Lord – to those who have done evil in ignorance, (and) then repented and set (things) right – surely your Lord after that is indeed forgiving, compassionate.

16:120.text: Surely Abraham was a community obedient before God – a Ḥanīf – yet he was not one of the idolaters.

16:121.text: (He was) thankful for His blessings. He chose him and guided him to a straight path.

16:122.text: We gave him good in this world, and surely in the Hereafter he will indeed be among the righteous.

16:123.text: Then We inspired you: ‘Follow the creed of Abraham the Ḥanīf. He was not one of the idolaters.’

16:124.text: The sabbath was only made for those who differed concerning it. Surely on the Day of Resurrection your Lord will judge between them concerning their differences.

16:125.text: Call to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good admonition, and dispute with them by means of what is better. Surely your Lord – He knows who goes astray from His way, and He knows the ones who are (rightly) guided.

16:126.text: If you take retribution, take it in the same way as retribution was taken against you. But if indeed you are patient – it is indeed better for the ones who are patient.

16:127.text: And you be patient (too). Yet your patience (comes) only with (the help of) God. Do not sorrow over them, and do not be in distress because of what they are scheming.

16:128.text: Surely God is with those who guard (themselves), and those who do good.

3:98.text: Say: ‘People of the Book! Why do you disbelieve in the signs of God, when God is a witness of what you do?’

3:99.text: Say: ‘People of the Book! Why do you keep those who believe from the way of God, desiring (to make) it crooked, when you are witnesses? God is not oblivious of what you do.’

3:100.text: You who believe! If you obey a group of those who have been given the Book, they will turn you back (into) disbelievers after having believed.

3:101.text: Yet how can you disbelieve, when the signs of God are recited to you, and His messenger is among you? Whoever holds fast to God has been guided to a straight path.

3:102.text: You who believe! Guard (yourselves) against God – guarding (yourselves) against Him is an obligation – and (see to it that) you do not die unless you have submitted.

3:103.text: And hold fast to the rope of God – all (of you) – and do not become divided. Remember the blessing of God on you: when you were enemies and He united your hearts, so that by His blessing you became brothers. You were on the brink of a pit of the Fire, and He saved you from it. In this way God makes clear to you His signs, so that you may be (rightly) guided.

3:104.text: Let there be (one) community of you, calling (people) to good, and commanding right and forbidding wrong. Those – they are the ones who prosper.

3:105.text: Do not be like those who became divided and differed, after the clear signs had come to them. Those – for them (there is) a great punishment,

3:106.text: on the Day when (some) faces will become white and (other) faces will become black. As for those whose faces are blackened: ‘Did you disbelieve after having believed? Taste the punishment for what you were disbelieving!’

3:107.text: As for those whose faces are whitened, (they will be) in the mercy of God. There they will remain.

3:108.text: Those are the signs of God. We recite them to you in truth. God does not intend any evil to the worlds.

3:109.text: To God (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. To God all affairs are returned.

3:110.text: You are the best community (ever) brought forth for humankind, commanding right and forbidding wrong, and believing in God. If the People of the Book had believed, it would indeed have been better for them. Some of them are believers, but most of them are wicked.

3:111.text: They will not cause you any harm, except for a (little) hurt. And if they fight you, they will turn their backs to you, (and) then they will not be helped.

3:112.text: Humiliation will be stamped upon them wherever they are found, unless (they grasp) a rope from God and a rope from the people. They have incurred the anger of God, and poverty will be stamped upon them. That is because they have disbelieved in the signs of God and killed the prophets without any right. That is because they have disobeyed and transgressed.

3:113.text: (Yet) they are not (all) alike. Among the People of the Book (there is) a community (which is) upstanding. They recite the signs of God during the hours of the night and prostrate themselves.

3:114.text: They believe in God and the Last Day, and command right and forbid wrong, and are quick in the (doing of) good deeds. Those are among the righteous.

3:115.text: Whatever good they do, they will not be denied (the reward of) it. God knows the ones who guard (themselves).

3:116.text: Surely those who disbelieve – neither their wealth nor their children will be of any use against God – those are the companions of the Fire. There they will remain.

3:117.text: The parable of what they contribute in this present life is like the parable of a freezing wind, which smites the field of a people who have done themselves evil, and destroys it. God did not do them evil, but they did themselves evil.

3:118.text: You who believe! Do not take outsiders as intimate friends. They will not fail to cause you ruin. They desire what you are distressed at. (Their) hatred is already apparent from their mouths, but what their hearts hide is (even) greater. We have already made clear to you the signs, if you are understanding.

3:119.text: There you are! You are those who love them, but they do not love you. You believe in the Book – all of it. And when they meet you they say, ‘We believe,’ but when they are alone, they bite their fingers at you out of rage. Say: ‘Die in your rage! Surely God knows what is in the hearts.’

3:120.text: If some good touches you, it distresses them, but if some evil smites you, they gloat over it. Yet if you are patient and guard (yourselves), their plot will not harm you at all. Surely God encompasses what they do.

3:121.text: (Remember) when you went out early from your family to post the believers (in their) positions for the battle – God is hearing, knowing –

3:122.text: when two contingents of you were inclined to lose courage, though God was their ally – in God let the believers put their trust.

3:123.text: Certainly God helped you at Badr, when you were an utterly insignificant (force). So guard (yourselves) against God, that you may be thankful.

3:124.text: (Remember) when you said to the believers, ‘Is it not sufficient for you that your Lord increases you with three thousand angels (specially) sent down?

3:125.text: Yes indeed! If you are patient and guard (yourselves), and they come against you suddenly, your Lord will increase you with five thousand angels (specially) designated.’

3:126.text: God only intended that as good news for you, and to satisfy your hearts by means of it. Help (comes) only from God, the Mighty, the Wise,

3:127.text: so that He might cut off a part of those who disbelieve, or disgrace them, so that they would turn back disappointed.

3:128.text: You have nothing to do with the matter, whether He turns to them (in forgiveness) or punishes them. Surely they are evildoers.

3:129.text: To God (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He forgives whomever He pleases and He punishes whomever He pleases. God is forgiving, compassionate.

8:1.text: They ask you about the spoils. Say: ‘The spoils (belong) to God and the messenger. So guard (yourselves) against God, and set right what is between you. Obey God and His messenger, if you are believers.’

8:2.text: Only those are believers who, when God is mentioned, their hearts become afraid, and when His signs are recited to them, it increases them in belief. They put their trust in their Lord.

8:3.text: Those who observe the prayer, and contribute from what We have provided them,

8:4.text: those – they are the true believers. For them (there are) ranks (of honor) with their Lord, and forgiveness and generous provision.

8:5.text: – As your Lord brought you forth from your house with the truth, when a group of the believers were indeed unwilling,

8:6.text: disputing with you about the truth after it had become clear, as if they were being driven to death with their eyes wide open.

8:7.text: (Remember) when God was promising you that one of the two contingents would be yours, and you were wanting the unarmed one to be yours, but God wished to verify the truth by His words, and to cut off the last remnant of the disbelievers,

8:8.text: so that He might verify the truth and falsify the false, even though the sinners disliked (it).

8:9.text: (Remember) when you were calling on your Lord for help, and He responded to you: ‘I am going to increase you with a thousand angels following behind.’

8:10.text: God did it only as good news, and that your hearts might be satisfied by it. Help (comes) only from God. Surely God is mighty, wise.

8:11.text: (Remember) when He covered you with slumber as a security from Him, and sent down on you water from the sky, so that He might purify you by means of it, and take away from you the abomination of Satan, and that he might strengthen your hearts and make firm (your) feet by means of it.

8:12.text: When your Lord inspired the angels: ‘I am with you, so make firm those who believe. I shall cast dread into the hearts of those who disbelieve. So strike above (their) necks, and strike (off) all their fingers!’

8:13.text: That was because they broke with God and His messenger, and whoever breaks with God and His messenger – surely God is harsh in retribution.

8:14.text: ‘That is for you! So taste it! And (know) that the punishment of the Fire is for the disbelievers.’

8:15.text: You who believe! When you encounter those who disbelieve advancing (for battle), do not turn (your) backs to them.

8:16.text: Whoever turns his back to them on that day – unless turning aside to fight or to join (another) cohort – he has incurred the anger of God. His refuge will be Gehenna – and it is an evil destination!

8:17.text: You did not kill them, but God killed them, and you did not throw when you threw, but God threw, and (He did that) in order to test the believers (with) a good test from Himself. Surely God is hearing, knowing.

8:18.text: That is for you! (Know) that God weakens the plot of the disbelievers.

8:19.text: If you ask for a victory, the victory has already come to you. And if you stop, it will be better for you. But if you return, We shall return (too), and your cohort will be of no use to you, even if it should be numerous. (Know) that God is with the believers.

8:20.text: You who believe! Obey God and His messenger, and do not turn away from him when you hear (him).

8:21.text: Do not be like those who say, ‘We hear,’ when they do not hear.

8:22.text: Surely the worst of creatures in the sight of God are the deaf (and) the speechless – those which do not understand.

8:23.text: If God had known any good in them, He would indeed have made them hear. But (even) if He had made them hear, they would indeed have turned away in aversion.

8:24.text: You who believe! Respond to God and to the messenger, when he calls you to what gives you life. Know that God stands between a person and his (own) heart, and that to Him you will be gathered.

8:25.text: Guard (yourselves) against trouble, which will indeed smite not just the evildoers among you, and know that God is harsh in retribution.

8:26.text: Remember when you were few (and) weak on the earth, (and) you feared that the people might snatch you away, and He gave you refuge, and supported you with His help, and provided you with good things, so that you might be thankful.

8:27.text: You who believe! Do not betray God and the messenger, and do not betray your pledges when you know (better).

8:28.text: Know that your wealth and your children are a test, and that God – with Him (there is) a great reward.

8:29.text: You who believe! If you guard (yourselves) against God, He will grant deliverance for you, and absolve you of your evil deeds, and forgive you. God is full of great favor.

8:30.text: (Remember) when those who disbelieved were scheming against you, to confine you, or kill you, or expel you. They were scheming but God was scheming (too), and God is the best of schemers.

8:31.text: When Our signs are recited to them, they say, ‘We have already heard (this). If we wished, we could indeed say (something) like this. This is nothing but old tales.’

8:32.text: And (remember) when they said, ‘God! If this is the truth from You, rain down on us stones from the sky or bring us a painful punishment.’

8:33.text: But God was not one to punish them while you were among them, and God was not one to punish them while they were asking for forgiveness.

8:34.text: But what (excuse) have they (now) that God should not punish them, when they are keeping (people) from (going to) the Sacred Mosque, and they are not its (true) allies? Its only allies are the ones who guard (themselves), but most of them do not know (it).

8:35.text: Their prayer at the House is nothing but whistling and clapping of hands. So taste the punishment for what you disbelieve!

8:36.text: Surely those who disbelieve spend their wealth to keep (people) from the way of God – and they will (continue to) spend it. Then it will be a (cause of) regret for them, (and) then they will be overcome. Those who disbelieve will be gathered into Gehenna,

8:37.text: so that God may separate the bad from the good, and place the bad one on top of the other, and so pile them all up, and place them in Gehenna. Those – they are the losers.

8:38.text: Say to those who disbelieve (that) if they stop, whatever is already past will be forgiven them, but if they return, the customary way of those of old has already passed away.

8:39.text: Fight them until (there) is no persecution and the religion – all of it – (belongs) to God. If they stop – surely God sees what they do.

8:40.text: If they turn away, know that God is your Protector. Excellent is the Protector, and excellent is the Helper!

8:41.text: Know that whatever spoils you take, a fifth of it (belongs) to God and to the messenger, and to family, and the orphans, and the poor, and the traveler, if you believe in God and what We sent down on Our servant on the Day of Deliverance, the day the two forces met. God is powerful over everything.

8:42.text: (Remember) when you were on the nearer side, and they on the farther side, and the caravan was below you. (Even) if you had set a time (to fight), you would indeed have failed to keep the appointment. But (the battle took place) so that God might decide the affair – it was done! – (and it took place) so that those who perished might perish on (the basis of) a clear sign, and that those who lived might live on (the basis of) a clear sign. Surely God is indeed hearing, knowing.

8:43.text: (Remember) when God showed them to you in your dream as (only) a few, and if had He shown them as many, you would indeed have lost courage, and indeed argued about the matter. But God kept (you) safe. Surely He knows what is in the hearts.

8:44.text: (Remember) when He showed them to you – when you met – as few in your eyes, and He made you (appear as) few in their eyes. (This took place) so that God might decide the affair – it was done! To God all affairs are returned.

8:45.text: You who believe! When you encounter a (hostile) cohort, stand firm, and remember God often, so that you may prosper.

8:46.text: Obey God and His messenger, and do not argue, so that you lose courage and your strength fails. And be patient. Surely God is with the patient.

8:47.text: Do not be like those who went forth from their homes boastfully, and to show off to the people, and to keep (them) from the way of God. God encompasses what they do.

8:48.text: (Remember) when Satan made their deeds appear enticing to them, and said, ‘(There is) no one among the people to defeat you today. Surely I am your neighbor.’ But when the two cohorts saw each other, he turned on his heels, and said, ‘Surely I am free of you, (for) surely I see what you do not see. Surely I fear God, (for) God is harsh in retribution.’

8:49.text: (Remember) when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness were saying: ‘Their religion has deceived these (people).’ But whoever puts his trust in God – surely God is mighty, wise.

8:50.text: If (only) you could see when the angels take those who have disbelieved, striking their faces and their backs, and (saying): ‘Taste the punishment of the burning (Fire)!

8:51.text: That is for what your (own) hands have sent forward, and (know) that God is not an evildoer to (His) servants.’

8:52.text: (It will be) like the case of the house of Pharaoh, and those who were before them: they disbelieved in the signs of God, so God seized them for their sins. Surely God is strong, harsh in retribution.

8:53.text: That is because God is not one to change the blessing with which He has blessed a people, until they change what is within themselves. (Know) that God is hearing, knowing.

8:54.text: Like the case of the house of Pharaoh, and those who were before them: they called the signs of their Lord a lie, so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned the house of Pharaoh. All were evildoers.

8:55.text: Surely the worst of creatures in the sight of God are those who disbelieve – and they will not believe –

8:56.text: those of them with whom you have made a treaty, (and) then they break their treaty every time, and they do not guard (themselves).

8:57.text: If you come upon them in war, scatter with them those who are behind them, so that they may take heed.

8:58.text: If you fear treachery from a people, toss (the treaty) back at them likewise. Surely God does not love the treacherous.

8:59.text: Do not let those who disbelieve think they have gotten away. Surely they will not escape.

8:60.text: Prepare for them whatever force and cavalry you can, to terrify by this means the enemy of God and your enemy, and others besides them. You do not know them, but God knows them. Whatever you contribute in the way of God will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be done evil.

8:61.text: If they incline toward peace, you incline toward it (as well). Put (your) trust in God. Surely He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

8:62.text: But if they intend to deceive you – surely God is enough for you. He (it is) who supported you with His help and with the believers,

8:63.text: and He has brought their hearts together. If you had spent what is on the earth – all (of it) – you could not have brought their hearts together. But God has brought their hearts together. Surely He is mighty, wise.

8:64.text: Prophet! God is enough for you, and whoever follows you of the believers.

8:65.text: Prophet! Urge on the believers to the fighting. If (there) are twenty of you (who are) patient, they will overcome two hundred, and if (there) are a hundred of you, they will overcome a thousand of those who disbelieve, because they are a people without understanding.

8:66.text: Now God has lightened (the task) for you, and He knows that (there is) weakness in you. If (there) are a hundred of you (who are) patient, they will overcome two hundred, and if (there) are a thousand of you, they will overcome two thousand by the permission of God. God is with the patient.

8:67.text: It is not for a prophet to have captives, until he has subdued (the enemy) on the earth. You desire (the fleeting) goods of this world, but God desires the Hereafter. God is mighty, wise.

8:68.text: Were it not for a preceding Book from God, a great punishment would indeed have touched you for what you took.

8:69.text: So eat from what you have taken as spoils as permitted (and) good, and guard (yourselves) against God. Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

8:70.text: Prophet! Say to the captives in your hands: ‘If God knows of any good in your hearts, He will give you (something) better than what has been taken from you, and He will forgive you. Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.’

8:71.text: But if they intend to betray you, they have already betrayed God before (that). So He has given (you) power over them. God is knowing, wise.

8:72.text: Surely those who have believed and emigrated, and struggled with their wealth and their lives in the way of God, and those who have given refuge and help, those – they are allies of each other. But those who have believed and not emigrated – their protection is not (an obligation) on you at all, until they emigrate. Yet if they seek your help in the (matter of) religion, (their) help is (an obligation) on you, unless (it be) against a people with whom you have a treaty. God sees what you do.

8:73.text: Those who disbelieve are allies of each other. Unless you do this, (there) will be trouble on the earth and great corruption.

8:74.text: But those who have believed, and emigrated, and struggled in the way of God, and those who have given refuge and help, those – they are the true believers. For them (there is) forgiveness and generous provision.

8:75.text: But those who have believed after that, and emigrated, and struggled (along) with you, they (too) belong to you. Yet those related by blood are closer to one another in the Book of God. Surely God has knowledge of everything.

98:1.text: Those who disbelieve among the People of the Book, and the idolaters, were not (to be) set free until the clear sign had come to them

98:2.text: – a messenger from God, reciting purified pages,

98:4.text: Those who were given the Book did not become divided until after the clear sign had come to them.

98:5.text: They were commanded only to serve God, devoting (their) religion to Him, (being) Ḥanīfs, and to observe the prayer and give the alms. That is the right religion.

98:6.text: Surely those who disbelieve among the People of the Book, and the idolaters, will be in the Fire of Gehenna, there to remain. Those – they are the worst of creation.

98:7.text: Surely those who believe and do righteous deeds, those – they are the best of creation.

98:8.text: Their payment is with their Lord – Gardens of Eden through which rivers flow, there to remain forever. God is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. That is for whoever fears his Lord.

34:1.text: Praise (be) to God – to Him (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth – and to Him (be) praise in the Hereafter! He is the Wise, the Aware.

34:2.text: He knows what penetrates into the earth and what comes forth from it, and what comes down from the sky and what goes up into it. He is the Compassionate, the Forgiving.

34:3.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘The Hour will not come upon us.’ Say: ‘Yes indeed! By my Lord! It will indeed come to you! (He is the) Knower of the unseen. Not (even) the weight of a speck in the heavens and the earth escapes from Him, nor (is there anything) smaller than that or greater, except (that it is recorded) in a clear Book

34:7.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘Shall we direct you to a man who will inform you (that) when you have been completely torn to pieces, you will indeed (return) in a new creation?

34:8.text: Has he forged a lie against God, or is he possessed?’ No! Those who do not believe in the Hereafter are in punishment and far astray.

34:9.text: Do they not look to what is before them and what is behind them of the sky and the earth? If We (so) please, We could cause the earth to swallow them, or make fragments of the sky fall on them. Surely in that is a sign indeed for every servant who turns (in repentance).

34:10.text: Certainly We gave David favor from Us: ‘You mountains! Return (praises) with him, and you birds (too)!’ And We made iron malleable for him:

34:11.text: ‘Make full (coats of armor), and measure (well) in the sewing (of them).’ And: ‘Do righteousness, (for) surely I see what you do.’

34:12.text: And to Solomon (We subjected) the wind, its morning was a month’s (journey), and its evening was a month’s (journey), and We made a spring of molten brass to flow for him. And among the jinn, (there were) those who worked for him by the permission of his Lord. Whoever of them turns aside from Our command – We shall make him taste the punishment of the blazing (Fire).

34:13.text: They made for him whatever he pleased: places of prayer, and statues, and basins like cisterns, and fixed cooking pots. ‘House of David! Work in thankfulness, (for) few of My servants are thankful!’

34:15.text: Certainly for Sheba there was a sign in their dwelling place – two gardens, on the right and left: ‘Eat from the provision of your Lord, and be thankful to Him. A good land and a forgiving Lord.’

34:16.text: But they turned away, so We sent on them the flood of ‘Arim, and We replaced for them their two gardens (with) two gardens producing bitter fruit, and tamarisks, and a few lote trees.

34:17.text: We repaid them that because they disbelieved. We do not repay (anyone) but the ungrateful?

34:18.text: We set between them and the towns which We have blessed (other) towns (which are still) visible, and We measured out the traveling (distance) between them: ‘Travel among them by night and day in security!’

34:19.text: But they said, ‘Our Lord, lengthen (the distance) between our journeys.’ They did themselves evil, so We made them legendary, and We tore them completely to pieces. Surely in that are signs indeed for every patient (and) thankful one.

34:20.text: Certainly Iblīs confirmed his conjecture about them, and they followed him, except for a group of the believers.

34:21.text: But he had no authority over them, except that We might know the one who believed in the Hereafter from the one who was in doubt about it. Your Lord is a watcher over everything.

34:22.text: Say: ‘Call on those whom you claim (as gods) instead of God! They do not possess (even) the weight of a speck in the heavens or on the earth. They have no partnership in (the creation of) either of them, nor has He any support from them.’

34:23.text: Intercession will be of no benefit with Him, except for the one to whom He gives permission – until, when terror is removed from their hearts, they say, ‘What did your Lord say?,’ and they say, ‘The truth. He is the Most High, the Great.’

34:24.text: Say: ‘Who provides for you from the heavens and the earth?’ Say: ‘God. Surely (either) we or you (stand) indeed on guidance, or (are) clearly astray.’

34:25.text: Say: ‘You will not be questioned about what sins we have committed, nor shall we be questioned about what you do.’

34:26.text: Say: ‘Our Lord will gather us together, (and) then disclose the truth between us, (for) He is the Discloser, the Knowing.’

34:27.text: Say: ‘Show me those whom you have joined with Him as associates. By no means (can you do so)! No! He (alone) is God, the Mighty, the Wise.’

34:28.text: We have sent you only as a bringer of good news and a warner to the people all together. But most of the people do not know (it).

34:29.text: They say, ‘When (will) this promise (come to pass), if you are truthful?’

34:30.text: Say: ‘For you (there is) the appointment of a Day. You will not delay it by an hour, nor will you advance (it by an hour).’

34:31.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘We will not believe in this Qur’ān, nor in that which was before it.’ If (only) you could see when the evildoers are made to stand before their Lord, (how) some of them hurl the blame at others. Those who were weak will say to those who were arrogant, ‘If not for you, we would have been believers.’

34:32.text: Those who were arrogant will say to those who were weak, ‘Did we keep you from the guidance after it had come to you? No! You (yourselves) were sinners.’

34:33.text: Those who were weak will say to those who were arrogant, ‘No! (It was your) scheming by night and day, when you commanded us to disbelieve in God and to set up rivals to Him.’ They will be full of secret regret when they see the punishment. We shall put chains on the necks of those who disbelieved. Will they be repaid (for anything) except for what they have done?

34:34.text: We have not sent any warner to a town, except that its affluent ones said, ‘Surely we are disbelievers in what you are sent with.’

34:35.text: And they (also) said, ‘We (have) more wealth and children, and we shall not be punished.’

34:36.text: Say: ‘Surely my Lord extends (His) provision to whomever He pleases, and restricts (it), but most of the people do not know (it).’

34:37.text: Neither your wealth nor your children are the things which bring you near to Us in intimacy, except for whoever believes and does righteousness. And those – for them (there is) a double payment for what they have done, and they will be secure in exalted rooms.

34:38.text: But those who strive against Our signs to obstruct (them) – those will be brought forward to the punishment.

34:40.text: On the Day when He gathers them all together, He will say to the angels, ‘(Was it) you these were serving?’

34:41.text: They will say, ‘Glory to You! You are our ally, not they. No! They used to serve the jinn – most of them believed in them.’

34:42.text: ‘So today none of you has power to (cause) another benefit or harm.’ And We shall say to those who did evil, ‘Taste the punishment of the Fire which you called a lie.’

34:43.text: When Our clear signs are recited to them, they say, ‘This is only a man who wants to keep you from what your fathers have served.’ And they say, ‘This is nothing but a forged lie.’ Those who disbelieve say to the truth, when it comes to them, ‘This is nothing but clear magic’

34:44.text: – though We have not given them any Books to study, nor have We sent to them any warner before you.

34:45.text: Those who were before them (also) called (it) a lie, though they have not reached (even) a tenth of what We gave them. They (too) called My messengers liars, and how was My loathing (of them)!

34:46.text: Say: ‘I give you only one admonition, (namely) that you stand before God, in pairs or singly, (and) then reflect: (there are) not any jinn in your companion. He is only a warner for you in the face of a harsh punishment.’

34:47.text: Say: ‘I have not asked you for any reward, but it was (only) for your own sake. My reward (depends) only on God. He is a witness over everything.’

34:48.text: Say: ‘My Lord hurls the truth – Knower of the unseen.’

34:49.text: Say: ‘The truth has come! Falsehood (can) neither bring (anything) about, nor restore (it).’

34:50.text: Say: ‘If I go astray, I go astray only against myself, but if I am guided, it is by what my Lord inspires me (with). Surely He is hearing (and) near.’

34:51.text: If (only) you could see when they are terrified and (there is) no escape, and they are seized from a place nearby,

34:52.text: and say, ‘We believe in it (now).’ Yet how will they reach (it) from a place far away,

34:53.text: when they disbelieved in it before? They conjecture about the unseen from a place far away.

34:54.text: But (a barrier) has been set between them and what they desire, as was done with their parties before. Surely they (too) were in grave doubt indeed (about it).

29:2.text: Do the people think that they will be left (in such a position) that they (can) say, ‘We believe,’ but (that) they will not be tested?

29:3.text: Certainly We tested those who were before them, and God will indeed know those who are truthful, and He will indeed know the liars.

29:4.text: Or do those who do evil deeds think that they will escape Us? Evil is what they judge!

29:5.text: Whoever expects the meeting with God – surely the time of God is coming indeed! He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

29:6.text: Whoever struggles, struggles only for himself. Surely God is indeed wealthy beyond the worlds.

29:7.text: Those who believe and do righteous deeds – We shall indeed absolve them of their evil deeds, and indeed repay them (for the) best of what they have done.

29:8.text: We have charged each person (to do) good to his parents, but if they both struggle with you – to make you associate with Me what you have no knowledge of – do not obey them. To Me is your return, and I shall inform you about what you have done.

29:9.text: Those who believe and do righteous deeds – We shall indeed cause them to enter among the righteous.

29:10.text: Among the people (there is) one who says, ‘We believe in God,’ but when he is hurt in (the way of) God, he takes the persecution of the people as the punishment of God. But if indeed help comes to you from your Lord, they indeed say, ‘Surely we were with you.’ Does God not know what is in the hearts of all?

29:11.text: God indeed knows those who believe, and He indeed knows the hypocrites.

29:12.text: Those who disbelieve say to those who believe, ‘Follow our way, and let us bear your sins.’ Yet they cannot bear a single one of their own sins. Surely they are liars indeed!

29:13.text: But they will indeed bear their burdens, and (other) burdens with their burdens, and on the Day of Resurrection they will indeed be questioned about what they have forged.

29:14.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he stayed among them a thousand years, minus fifty years, and then the flood seized them while they were doing evil.

29:15.text: But We rescued him and (his) companions on the ship, and made it a miracle for all peoples.

29:16.text: And Abraham, when he said to his people: ‘Serve God, and guard (yourselves) against Him. That is better for you, if (only) you knew.

29:17.text: Instead of God, you only serve idols, and you create a lie. Surely those whom you serve, instead of God, do not possess any provision for you. Seek (your) provision from God, and serve Him, and be thankful to Him – to Him you will be returned.

29:18.text: But if you call (it) a lie, (know that) communities called (it) a lie before you. Nothing (depends) on the messenger except the clear delivery (of the message).’

29:19.text: Do they not see how God brings about the creation, (and) then restores it? Surely that is easy for God.

29:20.text: Say: ‘Travel the earth and see how He brought about the creation. Then God produces the latter growth. Surely God is powerful over everything.

29:21.text: He punishes whomever He pleases and has compassion on whomever He pleases – and to Him you will be turned.

29:22.text: You cannot escape (Him) either on the earth or in the sky, and you have no ally and no helper other than God.’

29:23.text: Those who disbelieve in the signs of God and the meeting with Him – those have no hope of My mercy, and those – for them (there is) a painful punishment.

29:24.text: But the only response of his people was that they said, ‘Kill him, or burn him!’ And then God rescued him from the fire. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who believe.

29:25.text: And he said, ‘Instead of God, you have only taken idols (in a bond of) friendship among you in this present life. Then on the Day of Resurrection some of you will deny others, and some will curse others, and your refuge will be the Fire, and you will have no helpers.’

29:26.text: Lot believed in him, and said, ‘I am going to flee to my Lord. Surely He – He is the Mighty, the Wise.’

29:27.text: And We granted him Isaac and Jacob, and We placed among his descendants the prophetic office and the Book. We gave him his reward in this world, and in the Hereafter he will indeed be among the righteous.

29:28.text: And Lot, when he said to his people: ‘Surely you commit (such) immorality (as) no one in all the worlds has committed before you.

29:29.text: Do you indeed approach men, and cut off the way, and commit wrong in your meeting?’ But the only response of his people was that they said, ‘Bring us the punishment of God, if you are one of the truthful.’

29:30.text: He said, ‘My Lord, help me against the people who foment corruption.’

29:31.text: When Our messengers brought Abraham the good news, they said, ‘Surely we are going to destroy the people of this town, (for) its people are evildoers.’

29:32.text: He said, ‘Surely Lot is in it.’ They said, ‘We know who is in it. We shall indeed rescue him and his family, except his wife. She will be one of those who stay behind.’

29:33.text: When Our messengers came to Lot, he became distressed about them, and felt powerless (to protect) them, but they said, ‘Do not fear and do not sorrow. Surely we are going to rescue you and your family, except your wife. She will be one of those who stay behind.

29:34.text: Surely We are going to send down wrath from the sky on the people of this town, because they have acted wickedly.’

29:35.text: Certainly We have left some of it as a clear sign for a people who understand.

29:36.text: And to Midian (We sent) their brother Shu‘ayb. He said, ‘My people! Serve God and expect the Last Day. Do not act wickedly on the earth, fomenting corruption.’

29:37.text: But they called him a liar, so the earthquake seized them, and morning found them leveled in their home(s).

29:38.text: And ‘Ād and Thamūd – it is clear to you from their dwellings. Satan made their deeds appear enticing to them, and kept them from the way, though they saw (it) clearly.

29:39.text: And Qārūn, and Pharaoh, and Haman – certainly Moses brought them the clear signs, but they became arrogant on the earth. Yet they did not outrun (Us).

29:40.text: We seized each one for his sin. We sent a sandstorm against one of them, and another of them was seized by the cry, and We caused the earth to swallow (yet) another of them, and We drowned (still) another of them. Yet God was not one to do them evil, but they did themselves evil.

29:41.text: The parable of those who take allies other than God is like the parable of the spider: it takes a house, but surely the house of the spider is indeed the most feeble of houses – if (only) they knew.

29:42.text: Surely God knows whatever they call on instead of Him. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

29:43.text: Those parables – We strike them for the people, but no one understands them except the ones who know.

29:44.text: God created the heavens and the earth in truth. Surely in that is a sign indeed for the believers.

29:45.text: Recite what you have been inspired (with) of the Book, and observe the prayer. Surely the prayer forbids immorality and wrong, yet the remembrance of God is indeed greater. God is aware of what you do.

29:46.text: Do not dispute with the People of the Book except with what is better – except for those of them who do evil. And say: ‘We believe in what has been sent down to us, and what has been sent down to you. Our God and your God is one, and to Him we submit.’

29:47.text: In this way We have sent down the Book to you. Those to whom We have given the Book believe in it, and among these (people) (there are) some who believe in it. No one denies Our signs but the disbelievers.

29:48.text: You were not accustomed to read from any book before it, or to write it with your right (hand), (for) then the perpetrators of falsehood would indeed have had (reason to) doubt (you).

29:49.text: No! It is clear signs in the hearts of those who have been given knowledge. No one denies Our signs but the evildoers.

29:50.text: They say, ‘If only signs were sent down on him from his Lord.’ Say: ‘The signs are only with God. I am only a clear warner.’
