29:51.text: Is it not sufficient for them that We have sent down on you the Book to be recited to them? Surely in that is a mercy indeed, and a reminder to a people who believe.

29:52.text: Say: ‘God is sufficient as a witness between me and you.’ He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Those who believe in falsehood and disbelieve in God, those – they are the losers.

29:53.text: They seek to hurry you with the punishment. If it were not for an appointed time, the punishment would indeed have come upon them (already). Yet it will indeed come upon them unexpectedly, when they do not realize (it).

29:54.text: They seek to hurry you with the punishment. Surely Gehenna will indeed encompass the disbelievers.

29:55.text: On the Day when the punishment will cover them – from above them and from beneath their feet – (then) He will say, ‘Taste what you have done!’

29:56.text: My servants who believe! Surely My earth is wide, so serve Me!

29:57.text: Every person will taste death, then to Us you will be returned.

29:58.text: Those who have believed and done righteous deeds – We shall indeed settle them in exalted rooms of the Garden, through which rivers flow, there to remain. Excellent is the reward of the doers,

29:59.text: who are patient and trust in their Lord.

29:60.text: How many a creature (there is which) does not carry its own provision, yet God provides for it and for you. He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

29:61.text: If indeed you ask them, ‘Who created the heavens and the earth, and subjected the sun and the moon?,’ they will indeed say, ‘God.’ How then are they (so) deluded?

29:62.text: God extends (His) provision to whomever He pleases of His servants, and restricts (it) from him (whom He pleases). God has knowledge of everything.

29:63.text: If indeed you ask them, ‘Who sends down water from the sky, and by means of it gives the earth life after its death?,’ they will indeed say, ‘God.’ Say: ‘Praise (be) to God!’ But most of them do not understand.

29:64.text: This present life is nothing but jest and diversion. Surely the Home of the Hereafter – it is life indeed, if (only) they knew.

29:65.text: When they sail in the ship, they call on God, devoting (their) religion to Him. But when He brings them safely to the shore, suddenly they associate (other gods with Him).

29:66.text: Let them be ungrateful for what We have given them, and enjoy (themselves). Soon they will know!

29:67.text: Do they not see that We established a secure sanctuary (for them), while all around them the people are plundered? Do they believe in falsehood, but disbelieve in the blessing of God?

29:68.text: Who is more evil than the one who forges a lie against God, or calls the truth a lie when it comes to him? Is there not in Gehenna a dwelling place for the disbelievers?

29:69.text: But those who struggle for Us, We shall indeed guide them in Our ways. Surely God is indeed with the doers of good.

58:1.text: God has heard the words of the woman who disputes with you about her husband, and (who) complains to God, and God hears the discussion of the two of you. Surely God is hearing, seeing.

58:2.text: Those of you who declare their wives to be as their mothers’ backs – they are not their mothers. Their mothers are only those who gave them birth. Surely they indeed say a wrong word and a falsehood. Yet surely God is indeed pardoning (and) forgiving.

58:3.text: Those who declare their wives to be as their mothers’ backs, (and) then return to what they have said, (the penalty is) the setting free of a slave before the two of them touch each other. That is what you are admonished. God is aware of what you do.

58:4.text: Whoever does not find (the means to do that), (the penalty is) a fast for two months consecutively, before the two of them touch each other. And whoever is not able (to do that), (the penalty is) the feeding of sixty poor persons. That is so that you may believe in God and His messenger. Those are the limits (set by) God – and for the disbelievers (there will be) a painful punishment.

58:5.text: Surely those who oppose God and His messenger have been disgraced, as those before them were disgraced. We have already sent down clear signs – and for the disbelievers (there will be) a humiliating punishment,

58:6.text: on the Day when God will raise them up – all (of them) – and inform them about what they have done. God has counted it up, though they have forgotten it. God is a witness over everything.

58:7.text: Do you not see that God knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? There is no secret talk of three men but He is the fourth of them, nor of five men but He is the sixth of them, nor less than that, nor more, but He is with them wherever they may be. Then on the Day of Resurrection He will inform them about what they have done. Surely God has knowledge of everything.

58:8.text: Do you not see those who were forbidden from secret talk, (and) then return to what they were forbidden, and converse secretly in sin and enmity and disobedience to the messenger? And when they come to you, they greet you with what God does not greet you with, and they say within themselves, ‘If only God would punish us for what we say.’ Gehenna will be enough for them, where they will burn – and it is an evil destination!

58:9.text: You who believe! When you converse secretly, do not converse in sin and enmity and disobedience to the messenger, but converse in piety and the guarding (of yourselves). Guard (yourselves) against God, to whom you will be gathered.

58:10.text: Secret talk is only from Satan, so that he may cause those who believe to grieve. But he will not harm them at all, except by the permission of God. In God let the believers put their trust.

58:11.text: You who believe! When it is said to you ‘Make room in the assemblies,’ make room! God will make room for you. And when it is said, ‘Rise up,’ rise up! God will raise in rank those of you who have believed and those who have been given knowledge. God is aware of what you do.

58:12.text: You who believe! When you converse privately with the messenger, send forward a freewill offering before your private talk. That is better for you and purer. If you do not find (the means to do so) – God is forgiving, compassionate.

58:13.text: Are you afraid to send forward freewill offerings before your private talk? When you do not (do so), and God has turned to you (in forgiveness), observe the prayer and give the alms, and obey God and His messenger. God is aware of what you do.

58:14.text: Do you not see those who have taken as allies a people with whom God is angry? They are neither of you nor of them. They swear upon lies – and they know (it).

58:15.text: God has prepared a harsh punishment for them. Surely they – evil indeed is what they have done!

58:16.text: They have taken their oaths as a cover, and kept (people) from the way of God. For them (there will be) a humiliating punishment.

58:17.text: Neither their wealth nor their children will be of any use against God. Those are the companions of the Fire. There they will remain.

58:18.text: On the Day when God will raise them up – all (of them) – they will swear to Him as they swear to you, and think they (are standing) on something. Is it not a fact that they – they are the liars?

58:19.text: Satan has prevailed over them, and made them forget the Reminder of God. Those are the faction of Satan. Is it not a fact that the faction of Satan – they are the losers?

58:20.text: Surely those who oppose God and His messenger – they will be among the most humiliated.

58:21.text: God has written, ‘I shall indeed conquer – I and My messengers!’ Surely God is strong, mighty.

58:22.text: You will not find a people who believe in God and the Last Day loving anyone who opposes God and His messenger, even if they were their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their clan. Those – He has written belief on their hearts, and supported them with a spirit from Him, and will cause them to enter Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain. God is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. Those are the faction of God. Is it not a fact that the faction of God – they are the ones who prosper?

107:1.text: Have you seen the one who calls the Judgment a lie?

107:2.text: That is the one who shoves away the orphan,

107:3.text: and does not urge (people) to the feeding of the poor.

107:4.text: Woe to the ones who pray,

107:5.text: who – they are heedless of their prayers,

63:1.text: When the hypocrites come to you, they say, ‘We bear witness that you are indeed the messenger of God.’ God knows that you are indeed His messenger, and God bears witness: ‘Surely the hypocrites are liars indeed!’

63:2.text: They have taken their oaths as a cover, and have kept (people) from the way of God. Surely they – evil is what they have done.

63:3.text: That is because they believed, (and) then they disbelieved. So a seal was set on their hearts, and they do not understand.

63:4.text: When you see them, their bodies please you, but when they speak, you hear their speech as if they were planks of wood propped up. They think every cry is against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. God fight them! How deluded they are!

63:5.text: When it is said to them, ‘Come, the messenger of God will ask forgiveness for you,’ they shake their heads, and you see them turning aside, and they become arrogant.

63:6.text: (It is) the same for to them whether you ask forgiveness for them or you do not ask forgiveness for them: God will not forgive them. Surely God does not guide the people who are wicked.

63:7.text: They are those who say, ‘Do not contribute to those who are with the messenger of God until they disperse,’ when the storehouses of the heavens and the earth (belong) to God. But the hypocrites do not understand (this).

63:8.text: They say, ‘If indeed we return to the city, the mightier in it will indeed expel the lowlier,’ when all honor (belongs) to God, and to His messenger, and to the believers. But the hypocrites do not know (this).

63:9.text: You who believe! Do not let your wealth or your children divert you from the remembrance of God. Whoever does that, those – they are the losers.

63:10.text: Contribute from what We have provided you, before death comes upon one of you, and he says, ‘My Lord, if only You would spare me for a time near (at hand), so that I might make a freewill offering, and become one of the righteous.’

63:11.text: But God will not spare anyone when his time comes. God is aware of what you do.

28:2.text: Those are the signs of the clear Book.

28:3.text: We recite to you some of the story of Moses and Pharaoh in truth, for a people who believe:

28:4.text: Surely Pharaoh had exalted himself on the earth, and divided its people (into) parties, weakening one contingent of them (by) slaughtering their sons and sparing their women. Surely he was one of the fomenters of corruption.

28:5.text: But We wanted to bestow favor on those who were weak on the earth, and make them leaders, and make them the inheritors,

28:6.text: and to establish them on the earth, and show Pharaoh and Haman, and their forces, what they had to beware of from them.

28:7.text: We inspired Moses’ mother: ‘Nurse him, and when you fear for him, cast him into the sea, but do not fear and do not sorrow. Surely We are going to return him to you, and make him one of the envoys.’

28:8.text: And the house of Pharaoh picked him up, so that he might be an enemy to them and a (cause of) sorrow. Surely Pharaoh and Haman, and their forces, were sinners.

28:9.text: The wife of Pharaoh said, ‘(He is) a comfort to me and to you. Do not kill him! It may be that he will benefit us, or we may adopt him as a son.’ But they did not realize (what they were doing).

28:10.text: The next day the heart of Moses’ mother was empty. She would almost have betrayed him, if We had not strengthened her heart, so that she might be one of the believers.

28:11.text: She said to his sister, ‘Follow him.’ So she watched him on the sly, though they did not realize (it).

28:12.text: Before (this) We had forbidden any wet nurses for him, so she said, ‘Shall I direct you to the people of a household who will take charge of him for you, and look after him?’

28:13.text: And We returned him to his mother, so that she might be comforted and not sorrow, and that she might know that the promise of God is true. But most of them do not know (it).

28:14.text: When he reached his maturity and established (himself), We gave him judgment and knowledge. In this way We reward the doers of good.

28:15.text: He entered the city at a time when its people were oblivious, and in it he found two men fighting: the one of his (own) party, and the other of his enemies. The one who was of his (own) party called him for help against the one who was of his enemies. So Moses struck him, and finished him off. He said, ‘This is the work of Satan. Surely he is a clear enemy (who) leads (people) astray.’

28:16.text: He said, ‘My Lord, surely I have done myself evil. Forgive me!’ So God forgave him. Surely He – He is the Forgiving, the Compassionate.

28:17.text: He said, ‘My Lord, because of the blessing with which You have blessed me, I shall not be a supporter of the sinners.’

28:18.text: The next day he was in the city, afraid (and) watchful, when suddenly the one who had sought his help the day before cried out to him for help (again). Moses said to him, ‘Surely you are in error indeed!’

28:19.text: But when he was about to attack the one who was an enemy to them both, he said, ‘Moses! Do you intend to kill me as you killed (that) person yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant on the earth, and you do not want to be one of those who set (things) right.’

28:20.text: And (just then) a man came running from the farthest part of the city. He said, ‘Moses! Surely the assembly is taking counsel about you, to kill you. So get out! Surely I am one of your trusty advisers.’

28:21.text: So he went forth from it, afraid (and) watchful. He said, ‘My Lord, rescue me from the people who are evildoers.’

28:22.text: When he turned his face toward Midian, he said, ‘It may be that my Lord will guide me to the right way.’

28:23.text: And when he came to the water of Midian, he found by it a community of the people watering (their flocks), and besides them he found two women driving off their flocks. He said, ‘What is the matter with you two?’ They said, ‘We may not water (our flocks) until the shepherds drive off (their flocks), and our father is very old.’

28:24.text: So he watered (their flocks) for them. Then he turned aside to the shade, and said, ‘My Lord, surely I am in need of whatever good You may send down to me.’

28:25.text: Then one of the two women came to him, walking shyly. She said, ‘My father calls you, so that he may pay you a reward for your watering (our flocks) for us.’ When he had come to him and recounted the story to him, he said, ‘Do not fear! You have escaped from the people who are evildoers.’

28:26.text: One of the two women said, ‘My father! Hire him, (for) surely the best man whom you can hire is the strong (and) the trustworthy one.’

28:27.text: He said, ‘Surely I wish to marry you to one of these two daughters of mine, on (the condition) that you hire yourself to me for eight years. But if you complete ten, that will be of your own accord, (for) I am not about to make it a hardship for you. You will find me, if God pleases, one of the righteous.’

28:28.text: He said, ‘That is between me and you. No matter which of the two terms I fulfill, (let there be) no enmity against me. God is guardian over what we say.’

28:29.text: When Moses had fulfilled the term and traveled with his family, he perceived a fire on the side of the mountain. He said to his family, ‘Stay (here). Surely I perceive a fire. Perhaps I shall bring you some news of it, or some wood from the fire, so that you may warm yourselves.’

28:30.text: But when he came to it, he was called out to from the right side of the wādī, in the blessed hollow, from the tree: ‘Moses! Surely I am God, Lord of the worlds.’

28:31.text: And: ‘Cast (down) your staff!’ And when he saw it wiggling as if it were a snake, he turned around, retreating, and did not look back. ‘Moses! Come forward and do not fear! Surely you are one of the secure.

28:32.text: Put your hand into your cloak. It will come out white, unharmed. Now draw your hand to your side from fear. Those are two proofs from your Lord for Pharaoh and his assembly. Surely they are a wicked people.’

28:33.text: He said, ‘My Lord, surely I have killed one of them, and I fear that they will kill me.

28:34.text: My brother Aaron – he is more eloquent than me in speech. Send him with me as a support (who) will confirm me. Surely I fear that they will call me a liar.’

28:35.text: He said, ‘We shall strengthen your arm by means of your brother, and give authority to both of you, so that they will be no match for you because of Our signs. You two, and whoever follows you, will be the victors.’

28:36.text: When Moses brought them Our clear signs, they said, ‘This is nothing but a magic trick. We have not heard of this among our fathers of old.’

28:37.text: But Moses said, ‘My Lord knows who brings the guidance from Him, and to whom the final Home (belongs). Surely the evildoers will not prosper.’

28:38.text: Pharaoh said, ‘Assembly! I know of no other god for you than me. So light a fire for me, Haman, on the clay, and make a tower for me, so that I may look at the god of Moses. Surely I think he is indeed one of the liars.’

28:39.text: He and his forces became arrogant on the earth without any right, and thought that they would not be returned to Us.

28:40.text: So We seized him and his forces, and tossed them into the sea. See how the end was for the evildoers!

28:41.text: We made them leaders (who) call (others) to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection they will not be helped.

28:42.text: We pursued them in this world with a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection they will be among the scorned.

28:43.text: Certainly We gave Moses the Book, after We had destroyed the former generations, as evidence for the people, and a guidance and mercy, so that they might take heed.

28:44.text: You were not on the western side when We decreed the command to Moses, nor were you among the witnesses.

28:45.text: But We produced (other) generations, and life was prolonged for them. You were not dwelling among the people of Midian, reciting to them Our signs, when We were sending (messengers).

28:46.text: You were not on the side of the mountain when We called out (to Moses), but (you were sent) as a mercy from your Lord, so that you might warn a people to whom no warner had come before you, so that they might take heed.

28:47.text: If (it were) not that a smiting might smite them for what their hands have sent forward, and (that) they might say, ‘Our Lord, why did You not send a messenger to us, so that we might have followed Your signs, and (so) been among the believers [. . .]?’

28:48.text: Yet when the truth did come to them from Us, they said, ‘If only he were given the same as what Moses was given.’ Did they not disbelieve in what was given to Moses before? They said, ‘Two magic (tricks) supporting each other,’ and they said, ‘Surely we are disbelievers in all (of it).’

28:49.text: Say: ‘Bring a Book from God that is a better guide than these two – I shall follow it, if you are truthful.’

28:50.text: If they do not respond to you, know that they are only following their (vain) desires. And who is farther astray than the one who follows his desire without guidance from God? Surely God does not guide the people who are evildoers.

28:51.text: Certainly We have caused the word to reach them, so that they may take heed.

28:52.text: Those to whom We gave the Book before it – they believe in it.

28:53.text: When it is recited to them, they say, ‘We believe in it. Surely it is the truth from our Lord. Surely we were Muslims before it.’

28:54.text: Those – they will be given their reward twice over for what they have endured. They avert evil by means of the good, and contribute from what We have provided them.

28:55.text: When they hear any frivolous talk, they turn away from it, and say, ‘To us our deeds and to you your deeds. Peace (be) upon you! We do not seek out the ignorant.’

28:56.text: Surely you will not guide whomever you like, but God guides whomever He pleases, and knows the ones who are (rightly) guided.

28:57.text: They say, ‘If we follow the guidance with you, we shall be snatched from our land.’ Have We not established a secure sanctuary for them, where fruits of every kind are brought as a provision from Us? But most of them do not know (it).

28:58.text: How many a town have We destroyed (which) boasted of its means of livelihood! Those are their dwelling places (which remain) uninhabited after them, except for a few. We became the inheritors!

28:59.text: Yet your Lord was not one to destroy the towns until He had raised up a messenger in their mother (city), reciting Our signs to them. We would not have destroyed the towns unless their people had been evildoers.

28:60.text: Whatever thing you have been given is (only) an enjoyment of this present life, and its (passing) splendor, but what is with God is better and more lasting. Will you not understand?

28:61.text: Is the one whom We have promised a good promise, and (who) receives it, like the one whom We have given the enjoyment of this present life, (and) then on the Day of Resurrection will be one of those brought forward (to the punishment)?

28:62.text: On the Day when He will call them, and say, ‘Where are My associates whom you used to claim (as gods)?,’

28:63.text: those against whom the word has proved true will say, ‘Our Lord, these are those whom we made err. We made them err as we had erred. We disown (them) before You. They were not serving us.’

28:64.text: And it will be said, ‘Call your associates!’ And they will call them, but they will not respond to them, and they will see the punishment. If only they had been guided!

28:65.text: On the Day when He will call them, and say, ‘What response did you give the envoys?’

28:66.text: The news will be dark for them on that Day, nor will they ask each other questions.

28:67.text: But as for the one who turns (in repentance), and believes, and does righteousness, it may be that he will be one of those who prosper.

28:68.text: Your Lord creates whatever He pleases, and chooses (whomever He pleases) – the choice is not theirs. Glory to God! He is exalted above what they associate.

28:69.text: Your Lord knows what their hearts conceal and what they speak aloud.

28:70.text: He is God – (there is) no god but Him. To Him (be) praise, at the first and at the last! To Him (belongs) the judgment, and to Him you will be returned.

28:71.text: Say: ‘Have you considered? If God makes the night continuous for you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than God will bring you light? Will you not hear?’

28:72.text: Say: ‘Have you considered? If God makes the day continuous for you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than God will bring you night to rest in? Will you not see?

28:73.text: But out of His mercy He has made the night and the day for you, so that you may rest in it, and that you may seek some of His favor, and that you may be thankful.’

28:74.text: On the Day when He will call them, and say, ‘Where are My associates whom you used to claim (as gods)?’

28:75.text: We shall draw out a witness from every community, and say, ‘Bring your proof!’ And then they will know that the truth (belongs) to God, and what they have forged will abandon them.

28:76.text: Surely Qārūn was one of the people of Moses, and acted oppressively toward them. We had given him treasures, the keys of which would indeed have been a burden for a group (of men) endowed with strength. (Remember) when his people said to him, ‘Do not gloat! Surely God does not love those who gloat.

28:77.text: But seek the Home of the Hereafter by means of what God has given you, and do not forget your portion of this world. Do good, as God has done good to you, and do not seek to foment corruption on the earth. Surely God does not love the fomenters of corruption.’

28:78.text: He said, ‘I have been given it only because of the knowledge (that is) in me.’ Did he not know that God had destroyed those of the generations before him who were stronger than him and had accumulated more? The sinners will not be questioned about their sins.

28:79.text: So he went forth to his people in his splendor. Those who desired this present life said, ‘Would that we had the same as what has been given to Qārūn! Surely he is indeed the possessor of great good luck.’

28:80.text: But those to whom knowledge had been given said, ‘Woe to you! The reward of God is better for the one who believes and does righteousness. But no one will obtain it except the patient.’

28:81.text: So We caused the earth to swallow him and his home, and he had no cohort to help him, other than God, and he was not one of those who could help themselves.

28:82.text: In the morning those who had longed (to be in) his place the day before were saying, ‘Woe (to Qārūn)! Surely God extends (His) provision to whomever He pleases of His servants, and restricts (it). If God had not bestowed favor on us, He would indeed have caused (the earth) to swallow us (too). Woe to him! The disbelievers will not prosper.’

28:83.text: That is the Home of the Hereafter: We assign it to those who do not desire haughtiness on the earth, nor corruption. The outcome (belongs) to the ones who guard (themselves).

28:84.text: Whoever brings a good (deed) will have a better one than it, and whoever brings an evil (deed) – those who have done evil deeds will only be repaid for what they have done.

28:85.text: Surely He who made the Qur’ān obligatory for you will indeed return you to (your) home. Say: ‘My Lord knows who brings the guidance, and who is clearly astray.’

28:86.text: You did not expect that the Book would be cast (down) to you, except as a mercy from your Lord. So do not be a supporter of the disbelievers.

28:87.text: Let them not keep you from the signs of God, after they have been sent down to you, but call (people) to your Lord, and do not be one of the idolaters.

28:88.text: Do not call on another god (along) with God. (There is) no god but Him. Everything perishes except His face. To Him (belongs) the judgment, and to Him you will be returned.

68:1.text: Nūn.By the pen and what they write!

68:2.text: You are not, by the blessing of your Lord, possessed.

68:3.text: Surely for you (there is) indeed a reward without end,

68:4.text: (for) surely you (are) indeed on a great undertaking.

68:5.text: So you will see, and they will see,

68:6.text: which of you is the troubled one.

68:7.text: Surely your Lord – He knows who goes astray from His way, and He knows the ones who are (rightly) guided.

68:8.text: So do not obey the ones who call (it) a lie.

68:9.text: They wish that you would compromise, and then they would compromise.

68:10.text: And do not obey any despicable swearer,

68:12.text: a hinderer of the good, a transgressor (and) sinner,

68:13.text: crude, and besides all that, a bastard,

68:14.text: (just) because he has wealth and sons.

68:15.text: When Our signs are recited to him, he says, ‘Old tales!’

68:16.text: We shall brand him on the snout!

68:17.text: Surely We have tested them as We tested the owners of the garden, when they swore they would indeed harvest it in the morning,

68:19.text: And so a circler from your Lord went around it while they were sleeping,

68:20.text: and in the morning it was as if it had been harvested.

68:21.text: They called to each other in the morning:

68:22.text: ‘Go out early to your field, if you are going to harvest (it).’

68:23.text: So they set out, murmuring among themselves:

68:24.text: ‘No poor person will enter it today in your presence.’

68:25.text: They went out early, able to (their) task.

68:26.text: But when they saw it, they said, ‘Surely we have gone astray indeed!

68:28.text: The most moderate one of them said, ‘Did I not say to you, “Why do you not glorify (God)?”’

68:29.text: They said, ‘Glory to our Lord! Surely we have been evildoers!’

68:30.text: So some of them approached others blaming each other.

68:31.text: They said, ‘Woe to us! Surely we have been insolent transgressors!

68:32.text: It may be that our Lord will give us a better one in exchange for it. Surely we turn in hope to our Lord.’

68:33.text: Such was the punishment. Yet the punishment of the Hereafter is indeed greater, if (only) they knew.

68:34.text: Surely for the ones who guard (themselves) (there will be) Gardens of Bliss with their Lord.

68:35.text: Shall We treat those who submit like the sinners?

68:36.text: What is (the matter) with you? How do you judge?

68:37.text: Or do you have a Book which you study?

68:38.text: Surely you (would) have in it whatever indeed you choose!

68:39.text: Or do you have guarantees from Us, reaching to the Day of Resurrection? Surely you (would) have whatever indeed you judge!

68:40.text: Ask them which of them will guarantee that.

68:41.text: Or do they have associates? Let them bring their associates, if they are truthful.

68:42.text: On the Day when the leg will be bared, and they will be called to (make) prostration, but are unable:

68:43.text: their sight will be downcast, and humiliation will cover them, because they had been called to (make) prostration when they were able.

68:44.text: So leave Me (to deal with) anyone who calls this proclamation a lie. We shall lead them on step by step without their realizing it.

68:45.text: And I shall spare them – surely My plan is strong.

68:46.text: Or do you ask them for a reward, so that they are burdened with debt?

68:47.text: Or is the unseen in their keeping, and so they are writing (it) down?

68:48.text: Be patient for the judgment of your Lord, and do not be like the companion of the fish, when he called out, choked with distress.

68:49.text: If a blessing from his Lord had not reached him, he would indeed have been tossed on the desert (shore), condemned.

68:50.text: But his Lord chose him, and made him one of the righteous.

68:51.text: Surely those who disbelieve almost indeed make you stumble with their look, when they hear the Reminder. They say, ‘Surely he is possessed indeed!’

68:52.text: Yet it is nothing but a reminder to the worlds.

97:1.text: Surely We sent it down on the Night of the Decree.

97:2.text: And what will make you know what the Night of the Decree is?

97:3.text: The Night of the Decree is better than a thousand months.

97:4.text: The angels and the spirit come down during it, by the permission of their Lord, on account of every command.

97:5.text: It is (a night of) peace, until the rising of the dawn.

80:2.text: because the blind man came to him.

80:3.text: What will make you know? Perhaps he will (yet) purify himself,

80:4.text: or take heed, and the Reminder will benefit him.

80:5.text: As for the one who considers himself independent,

80:6.text: you give your attention to him.

80:7.text: Yet it is not (dependent) on you if he does not purify himself.

80:8.text: But as for the one who comes running to you,

80:11.text: By no means! Surely it is a Reminder

80:12.text: – and whoever pleases (may) take heed of it –

80:17.text: May the human perish! How ungrateful he is!

80:18.text: From what did He create him?

80:19.text: From a drop! He created him, and determined him,

80:20.text: then He made the way easy for him,

80:21.text: then He caused him to die and buried him,

80:22.text: then, when He pleases, He will raise him (again).

80:23.text: By no means! He has not accomplished what He commanded him.

80:24.text: Let the human consider his food:

80:25.text: We pour out water in abundance,

80:26.text: then We split open the earth in cracks,

80:27.text: and We cause grain to grow in it,

80:32.text: – a provision for you and your livestock.

80:34.text: on the Day when a person will flee from his brother,

80:35.text: and his mother and his father,

80:36.text: and his consort and his sons,

80:37.text: each of them that Day will have some matter to keep him busy.

80:38.text: (Some) faces that Day will be shining,

80:39.text: laughing, rejoicing at the good news.

80:40.text: But (other) faces that Day – dust will be upon them,

80:42.text: Those – they are the disbelievers, the depraved.

103:2.text: Surely the human is indeed in (a state of) loss

103:3.text: – except for those who believe and do righteous deeds, and exhort (each other) in truth, and exhort (each other) in patience.

56:2.text: – at its falling there will be no calling (it) a lie –

56:4.text: when the earth is violently shaken,

56:8.text: the companions on the right – what are the companions on the right?

56:9.text: And the companions on the left – what are the companions on the left?

56:11.text: – those are the ones brought near,

56:13.text: a host from the ones of old,

56:16.text: reclining on them, facing each other.

56:17.text: Boys of eternal youth will circulate among them,

56:18.text: with cups and pitchers, and a cup from a flowing spring

56:19.text: – they do not suffer any headache from it, nor do they become drunk –

56:20.text: and with fruit of their own choosing,

56:21.text: and the meat of birds of their own desiring,

56:24.text: – a reward for what they have done.

56:25.text: There they will not hear any frivolous or sinful talk,

56:27.text: The companions on the right – what are the companions of the right?

56:28.text: (They will be) in (the midst of) thornless lote trees,

56:29.text: and acacia trees one after another,

56:38.text: for the companions on the right.

56:39.text: A host from the ones of old,

56:40.text: and a host from the later (generations).

56:41.text: The companions on the left – what are the companions on the left?

56:42.text: (They will be) in (the midst of) scorching (fire) and boiling (water),

56:43.text: and a shadow of black smoke,

56:46.text: and persisted in the great refusal,

56:47.text: and used to say, ‘When we are dead, and turned to dust and bones, shall we indeed be raised up?

56:49.text: Say: ‘Surely those of old and the later (generations)

56:50.text: will indeed be gathered to the meeting of a known Day.

56:51.text: Then surely you – you who have gone astray and called (it) a lie! –

56:52.text: will indeed eat from the tree of Zaqqūm,

56:53.text: and fill your bellies from it,

56:54.text: and drink on (top of) it from boiling water,

56:56.text: This will be their reception on the Day of Judgment.

56:57.text: We created you. Why will you not affirm (it)?

56:58.text: Do you see what you emit?

56:59.text: Do you create it, or are We the Creators?

56:60.text: We have decreed death (to be) among you – We are not (to be) outrun –

56:61.text: so that We may exchange the likes of you, and (re)produce you in what you do not know.

56:62.text: Certainly you have known the first growth. Why will you not take heed?

56:63.text: Do you see what you cultivate?

56:64.text: Do you (yourselves) sow it, or are We the Sowers?

56:65.text: If We (so) pleased, We could indeed make it broken debris, and you would be left rejoicing,

56:68.text: Do you see the water which you drink?

56:69.text: Do you send it down from the clouds, or are We the Ones who send (it) down?

56:70.text: If We (so) pleased, We could make it bitter. Why are you not thankful?

56:71.text: Do you see the fire which you ignite?

56:72.text: Do you produce the timber for it, or are We the Ones who produce (it)?

56:73.text: We have made it a reminder and a provision for the desert dwellers.

56:74.text: So glorify the name of your Lord, the Almighty.

56:75.text: I swear by the fallings of the stars

56:76.text: – surely it is a great oath indeed, if (only) you knew –

56:77.text: surely it is an honorable Qur’ān indeed,

56:79.text: No one touches it but the purified.

56:80.text: (It is) a sending down from the Lord of the worlds.

56:81.text: Do you hold this proclamation in disdain,

56:82.text: and do you make it your living to call (it) a lie?

56:83.text: Why not, when the life of the dying man leaps into his throat,

56:85.text: – though We are nearer to him than you, only you do not see (Us) –

56:86.text: why, if you are not (to be) judged,

56:87.text: do you not return it, if you are truthful?

56:88.text: If he is one of those brought near,

56:89.text: (there will be) comfort, and fragrance, and a Garden of Bliss.

56:90.text: And if he is one of the companions on the right:

56:91.text: ‘Peace (be) to you, from the companions on the right!’

56:92.text: But if he is one of those who called (it) a lie (and) went astray,

56:93.text: (there will be) a reception of boiling (water)

56:95.text: Surely this – it indeed is the certain truth.

56:96.text: So glorify the name of your Lord, the Almighty.

48:1.text: Surely We have given you a clear victory,

48:2.text: so that God may forgive you what is past of your sin and what is (still) to come, and complete His blessing on you, and guide you to a straight path,

48:3.text: and that God may help you with a mighty help.

48:4.text: He (it is) who sent down the Sakīna into the hearts of the believers, so that they might add belief to their belief – to God (belong) the forces of the heavens and the earth, and God is knowing, wise –

48:5.text: and that He may cause the believing men and the believing women to enter Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain, and absolve them of their evil deeds – that is the great triumph in the sight of God! –

48:6.text: and that He may punish the hypocrite men and the hypocrite women alike, and the idolatrous men and the idolatrous women alike, and the ones who think evil thoughts about God. The wheel of evil (will turn) against them. God is angry with them, and has cursed them, and has prepared Gehenna for them – and it is an evil destination!

48:7.text: To God (belong) the forces of the heavens and the earth. God is mighty, wise.

48:8.text: Surely We have sent you as a witness, and as a bringer of good news and a warner,

48:9.text: so that you may believe in God and His messenger, and support him, and respect him, and that you may glorify Him morning and evening.

48:10.text: Surely those who swear allegiance to you swear allegiance to God – the hand of God is over their hands. So whoever breaks (his oath), only breaks it against himself, but whoever fulfils what he has covenanted with God – He will give him a great reward.

48:11.text: Those of the Arabs who stayed behind will say to you, ‘Our wealth and our families kept us busy, so ask forgiveness for us.’ They say with their tongues what is not in their hearts. Say: ‘Who has any power for you against God, whether He intends harm for you or intends benefit for you? No! God is aware of what you do.

48:12.text: No! You thought that the messenger and the believers would never return to their families, and that was made to appear enticing in your hearts, and you thought evil thoughts, and became a ruined people.’

48:13.text: Whoever does not believe in God and His messenger – surely We have prepared for the disbelievers a blazing (Fire).

48:14.text: To God (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He forgives whomever he pleases and punishes whomever He pleases. God is forgiving, compassionate.

48:15.text: The ones who stayed behind will say, when you set out to take spoils, ‘Let us follow you.’ They want to change the word of God. Say: ‘You will not follow us. So God has said before.’ They will say, ‘No! You are jealous of us.’ No! They have not understood, except for a little.

48:16.text: Say to those of the Arabs who stayed behind: ‘You will be called to (fight) a people of harsh violence. You will fight them or they will surrender. If you obey, God will give you a good reward, but if you turn away, as you turned away before, He will punish you with a painful punishment.’

48:17.text: There is no blame on the blind, and no blame on the disabled, and no blame on the sick. Whoever obeys God and His messenger – He will cause him to enter Gardens through which rivers flow; but whoever turns away – He will punish him with a painful punishment.

48:18.text: Certainly God was pleased with the believers when they were swearing allegiance to you under the tree, and He knew what was in their hearts. So He sent down the Sakīna on them, and rewarded them with a near victory,

48:19.text: and many spoils to take. God is mighty, wise.

48:20.text: And God has promised you many (more) spoils to take, and He has hurried this for you, and has restrained the hands of the people from you. (This happened) so that it might be a sign to the believers, and guide you to a straight path.

48:21.text: The other (spoils) which you were not able (to take), God has already encompassed them. God is powerful over everything.

48:22.text: If those who disbelieve fight you, they will indeed turn their backs, (and) then they will not find any ally or any helper.

48:23.text: (That was) the customary way of God (concerning) those who have passed away before, and you will find no change in the customary way of God.

48:24.text: He (it is) who restrained their hands from you, and your hands from them, in the heart of Mecca, after He gave you victory over them – God sees what you do.

48:25.text: They are those who disbelieved, and kept you from the Sacred Mosque, and (also) the offering, (which was) prevented from reaching its lawful place. If not for (certain) believing men and believing women, whom you did not know, or you would have trampled them, and guilt smitten you without (your) realizing (it) because of them – so that God may cause to enter into His mercy whomever He pleases – if they had been separated out (clearly), We would indeed have punished those among them who disbelieved with a painful punishment.

48:26.text: When those who disbelieved fostered a fury in their hearts – the fury of the (time of) ignorance – God sent down His Sakīna on His messenger and on the believers, and fastened to them the word of guarding (themselves). They have more right to it and are worthy of it. God has knowledge of everything.

48:27.text: Certainly God has spoken the truth in the vision to His messenger: ‘You will indeed enter the Sacred Mosque, if God pleases, in security, your heads shaved, your hair cut short, not fearing.’ He knew what you did not know, and besides that produced a near victory.

48:28.text: He (it is) who has sent His messenger with the guidance and the religion of truth, so that He may cause it to prevail over religion – all of it. God is sufficient as a witness.

48:29.text: Muḥammad is the messenger of God. Those who are with him are harsh against the disbelievers, (but) compassionate among themselves. You see them bowing and prostrating themselves, seeking favor from God and approval. Their marks on their faces are the trace of prostration. That is their image in the Torah, and their image in the Gospel is like a seed (that) puts forth its shoot, and strengthens it, and it becomes stout and stands straight on its stalk, pleasing the sowers – so that He may enrage the disbelievers by means of them. God has promised those of them who believe and do righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward.

3:69.text: A contingent of the People of the Book would like to lead you astray, but they only lead themselves astray, though they do not realize (it).

3:70.text: People of the Book! Why do you disbelieve in the signs of God, when you are witnesses (to them)?

3:71.text: People of the Book! Why do you mix the truth with falsehood, and conceal the truth, when you know (better)?

3:72.text: A contingent of the People of the Book has said, ‘Believe in what has been sent down on those who believe at the beginning of the day, and disbelieve at the end of it, perhaps (then) they may return.’

3:73.text: And: ‘Do not believe (anyone) except the one who follows your religion.’ Say: ‘Surely the (true) guidance is the guidance of God – that anyone should be given what you have been given, or (that) they should dispute with you before your Lord!’ Say: ‘Surely favor is in the hand of God. He gives it to whomever He pleases. God is embracing, knowing.

3:74.text: He chooses whomever He pleases for His mercy, and God is full of great favor.’

3:75.text: Among the People of the Book (there is) one who, if you entrust him with a qinṭār, will pay it back to you, but among them there is one who, if you entrust him with a dīnār, will not pay it back to you unless you stand over him. That is because they say, ‘There is no way (of obligation) on us concerning the common people.’ They speak lies against God, and they know (it).

3:76.text: Yes indeed! Whoever fulfills his covenant and guards (himself) – surely God loves the ones who guard (themselves).

3:77.text: Surely those who sell the covenant of God and their oaths for a small price will have no share in the Hereafter. God will not speak to them or look at them on the Day of Resurrection, nor will He purify them. For them (there is) a painful punishment.

3:78.text: Surely (there is) indeed a group of them who twist their tongues with the Book, so that you will think it is from the Book, when it is not from the Book. And they say, ‘It is from God,’ when it is not from God. They speak lies against God, and they know (it).

3:79.text: It is not (possible) for a human being that God should give him the Book, and the judgment, and the prophetic office, (and) then he should say to the people, ‘Be my servants instead of God’s.’ Rather (he would say), ‘Be rabbis by what you have been teaching of the Book and by what you have been studying (of it).’

3:80.text: He would not command you to take the angels and the prophets as Lords. Would he command you to disbelief after you have submitted?

3:81.text: (Remember) when God took a covenant with the prophets: ‘Whatever indeed I have given you of the Book and wisdom, when a messenger comes to you confirming what is with you, you are to believe in him and you are to help him.’ He said, ‘Do you agree and accept My burden on that (condition)?’ They said, ‘We agree.’ He said, ‘Bear witness, and I shall be with you among the witnesses.’

3:82.text: Whoever turns away after that, those – they are the wicked.

3:83.text: Do they desire a religion other than God’s, when whoever is in the heavens and the earth has submitted to Him, willingly or unwillingly, and to Him they will be returned?

3:84.text: Say: ‘We believe in God, and what has been sent down on us, and what has been sent down on Abraham, and Ishmael, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the tribes, and what was given to Moses, and Jesus, and the prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and to Him we submit.’

3:85.text: Whoever desires a religion other than Islam, it will not be accepted from him, and in the Hereafter he will be one of the losers.

3:86.text: How will God guide a people who have disbelieved after having believed, and (after) they have borne witness that the messenger is true, and the clear signs have come to them? God does not guide the people who are evildoers.

3:87.text: Those – their payment is that on them (rests) the curse of God, and the angels, and the people all together.

3:88.text: There (they will) remain – the punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be spared

3:89.text: – except for those who turn (in repentance) after that and set (things) right. Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

3:90.text: Surely those who disbelieve after their believing, (and) then increase in disbelief – their repentance will not be accepted. And those – they are the ones who go astray.

3:91.text: Surely those who disbelieve, and die while they are disbelievers – not all the world’s gold would be accepted from (any) one of them, even if he (tried to) ransom (himself) with it. Those – for them (there is) a painful punishment. They will have no helpers.

3:92.text: You will not attain piety until you contribute from what you love, and whatever you contribute, surely God knows it.

3:93.text: All food was permitted to the Sons of Israel, except for what Israel forbade himself before the Torah was sent down. Say: ‘Bring the Torah and read it, if you are truthful.’

3:94.text: Whoever forges lies against God after that, those – they are the evildoers.

3:95.text: Say: ‘God has spoken the truth, so follow the creed of Abraham the Ḥanīf. He was not one of the idolaters.’

3:96.text: Surely the first House laid down for the people was indeed that at Becca, a blessed (House) and a guidance for the worlds.

3:97.text: In it are clear signs: the standing place of Abraham. Whoever enters it is secure. Pilgrimage to the House is (an obligation) on the people to God – (for) anyone who is able (to make) a way to it. Whoever disbelieves – surely God is wealthy beyond the worlds.

33:1.text: Prophet! Guard (yourself) against God, and do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites. Surely God is knowing, wise.

33:2.text: Follow what you are inspired (with) from your Lord. Surely God is aware of what you do.

33:3.text: Put your trust in God, (for) God is sufficient as a guardian.

33:4.text: God has not placed two hearts inside anyone. He has not made your wives whom you declare to be as your mothers’ backs your (real) mothers, nor has He made your adopted sons your (real) sons. That is what you say with your mouths, but God speaks the truth and guides (you) to the (right) way.

33:5.text: Call them by (the names of) their (real) fathers: that is more just in the sight of God. If you do not know their (real) fathers, (regard them) as your brothers in religion, and your clients. There is no blame on you in any mistakes you have made, but only in what your hearts have intended. God is forgiving, compassionate.

33:6.text: The prophet is closer to the believers than they are to themselves, and his wives are their mothers, but those related by blood are closer to one another in the Book of God than the believers and the emigrants – but you should do right by your allies. That is written in the Book.

33:7.text: (Remember) when We took a covenant with the prophets – and from you, and from Noah, and Abraham, and Moses, and Jesus, son of Mary – We took a firm covenant with them,

33:8.text: so that He might question the truthful about their truthfulness. He has prepared a painful punishment for the disbelievers.

33:9.text: You who believe! Remember the blessing of God on you, when the forces came upon you, and We sent against them a wind, and (also) forces which you did not see. God sees what you do.

33:10.text: When they came upon you from above you and from below you, and when (your) sight turned aside and (your) hearts reached (your) throats, and you were thinking about God (all kinds of) thoughts,

33:11.text: there and then the believers were tested and severely shaken.

33:12.text: And when the hypocrites, and those in whose hearts is a sickness, said, ‘God and His messenger have promised us nothing but deception.’

33:13.text: And when a group of them said, ‘People of Yathrib! (There is) no dwelling place for you (here), so return!’ And (another) contingent of them was asking permission of the prophet, saying, ‘Surely our houses are vulnerable’ – yet they were not vulnerable, they only wished to flee.

33:14.text: If an entrance had been made against them from that side, (and) then they had been asked (to join in) the troublemaking, they would indeed have done it, and scarcely have hesitated with it.

33:15.text: Certainly they had made a covenant with God before (this), that they would not turn their backs, and a covenant with God is (something) to be responsible for.

33:16.text: Say: ‘Flight will not benefit you. If you flee from death or killing, you will only enjoy (life) a little (while).’

33:17.text: Say: ‘Who is the one who will protect you against God, if He intends evil for you, or intends mercy for you?’ They will not find for themselves any ally or helper other than God.

33:18.text: God knows those of you who are a hindrance, and those who say to their brothers, ‘Come to us,’ but who seldom come out to the battle,

33:19.text: (in their) greed toward you. When fear comes (upon them), you see them looking at you, their eyes rolling around like one who faints at the point of death. But when fear departs, they sting you with (their) sharp tongues (in their) greed for the good (that has come to you). Those – they have not believed. God has made their deeds worthless. That is easy for God.

33:20.text: They think (that) the factions have not gone away. If the factions come (again), they will wish that they were living in the desert among the Arabs, asking for news of you. Yet (even) if they were among you, they would seldom fight.

33:21.text: Certainly the messenger of God has been a good example for you – for the one who hopes in God and the Last Day, and remembers God often.

33:22.text: When the believers saw the factions, they said, ‘This is what God and His messenger promised us, and God and His messenger were truthful.’ It only increased them in belief and submission.

33:23.text: Among the believers are men who have been truthful to the covenant which they made with God: some of them have fulfilled their vow, and some of them are (still) waiting (to do so). They have not changed in the least,

33:24.text: so that God may repay the truthful for their truthfulness, and punish the hypocrites, if He (so) pleases, or turn to them (in forgiveness). Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

33:25.text: God turned back those who disbelieved in their rage, and they did not attain any advantage. God was sufficient for the believers in the fighting. Surely God is strong, mighty.

33:26.text: He brought down from their fortifications those of the People of the Book who supported them, and cast dread into their hearts. You killed a group (of them), and took captive (another) group.

33:27.text: And He caused you to inherit their land, their homes, and their wealth, and a land you had not set foot on. God is powerful over everything.

33:28.text: Prophet! Say to your wives: ‘If you desire this present life and its (passing) splendor, come! I shall make provision for you, and release you gracefully.

33:29.text: But if you desire God and His messenger, and the Home of the Hereafter – surely God has prepared a great reward for the doers of good among you.’

33:30.text: Wives of the prophet! Whoever among you commits clear immorality, for her the punishment will be doubled. That is easy for God.

33:31.text: But whoever among you is obedient to God and His messenger, and does righteousness – We shall give her her reward twice over. We have prepared a generous provision for her.

33:32.text: Wives of the prophet! You are not like any of the (other) women. If you guard (yourselves), do not be beguiling in (your) speech, or he in whose heart is a sickness will become lustful, but speak in a rightful fashion.

33:33.text: Stay in your houses, and do not flaunt (yourselves) with the flaunting of the former ignorance, but observe the prayer and give the alms, and obey God and His messenger. God only wishes to take away the abomination from you, People of the House, and to purify you completely.

33:34.text: Remember what is recited in your houses of the signs of God and the wisdom. Surely God is astute, aware.

33:35.text: Surely the submitting men and the submitting women, the believing men and the believing women, the obedient men and the obedient women, the truthful men and the truthful women, the patient men and the patient women, the humble men and the humble women, the charitable men and the charitable women, the fasting men and the fasting women, the men who guard their private parts and the women who guard (them), the men who remember God often and the women who remember (Him) – for them God has prepared forgiveness and a great reward.

33:36.text: It is not for a believing man or a believing woman, when God and His messenger have decided a matter, to have the choice in their matter. Whoever disobeys God and His messenger has very clearly gone astray.

33:37.text: (Remember) when you said to the one whom God had blessed, and whom you had blessed: ‘Keep your wife to yourself, and guard (yourself) against God,’ and you hid within yourself what God was going to reveal, and feared the people, when God had a better right that you feared Him. So when Zayd had gotten what he needed from her, We married her to you, so that there should not be any blame on the believers concerning the wives of their adopted sons, when they have gotten what they needed from them. The command of God was (to be) fulfilled.

33:38.text: There is no blame on the prophet concerning what God has made obligatory for him. (That was) the customary way of God concerning those who passed away before – and the command of God is a determined decree –

33:39.text: who were delivering the messages of God, and fearing Him, and not fearing anyone but Him. God is sufficient as a reckoner.

33:40.text: Muḥammad is not the father of any of your men, but the messenger of God and the seal of the prophets. God has knowledge of everything.

33:41.text: You who believe! Remember God often,

33:42.text: and glorify Him morning and evening.

33:43.text: He (it is) who prays over you, and His angels (do too), to bring you out of the darkness to the light. He is compassionate with the believers.

33:44.text: On the Day when they meet Him, their greeting will be: ‘Peace!’ He has prepared a generous reward for them.

33:45.text: Prophet! Surely We have sent you as a witness, and as a bringer of good news and a warner,

33:46.text: and as one calling to God, by His permission, and as an illuminating lamp.

33:47.text: Give good news to the believers that they have great favor from God.

33:48.text: Do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites. Ignore their hurt but put your trust in God. God is sufficient as a guardian.

33:49.text: You who believe! When you marry believing women (and) then divorce them before you touch them, you have no waiting period to count for them. So make provision for them, and release them gracefully.

33:50.text: Prophet! Surely We have made lawful for you your wives to whom you have granted their marriage gifts, and what your right (hand) owns from what God has given you, and the daughters of your paternal uncles and paternal aunts, your maternal uncles and maternal aunts, who have emigrated with you, and any believing woman, if she gives herself to the prophet, and if the prophet wishes to take her in marriage. (That is) exclusively for you, apart from the believers – We know what We have made obligatory for them concerning their wives and what their right (hands) own – so that there may be no blame on you. God is forgiving, compassionate.

33:51.text: You may put off whomever you please of them, and you may take to yourself whomever you please. And whomever you desire of those you have set aside, (there is) no blame on you (if you take her again). That is more appropriate, so that they may be comforted and not sorrow, and (that) they may be pleased with what you give them – all of them. God knows what is in your hearts. God is knowing, forbearing.

33:52.text: Beyond (that) women are not permitted to you, nor (is it permitted to you) to take (other) wives in exchange for them, even though their beauty pleases you, except for what your right (hand) owns. God is watching over everything.

33:53.text: You who believe! Do not enter the houses of the prophet to (attend) a meal without waiting (until it) is ready, unless permission is given to you. But when you are invited, enter, and when you have eaten, disperse, and do not linger for conversation. Surely that is hurtful to the prophet, and he is ashamed of you, but God is not ashamed of the truth. When you ask them for anything, ask them from behind a veil. That is purer for your hearts and their hearts. It is not for you to hurt the messenger of God, nor to marry his wives after him – ever. Surely that is a great (offense) in the sight of God.

33:54.text: Whether you reveal a thing or hide it, surely God has knowledge of everything.

33:55.text: (There is) no blame on them concerning their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their brothers’ sons, or their sisters’ sons, or their women, or what their right (hands) own. But guard (yourselves) against God. Surely God is a witness over everything.

33:56.text: Surely God and His angels pray for the prophet. You who believe! You pray for him (too), and greet him (with a worthy) greeting.

33:57.text: Surely those who hurt God and His messenger – God has cursed them in this world and the Hereafter, and has prepared a humiliating punishment for them.

33:58.text: Those who hurt believing men and believing women – other than what they have earned – they will indeed bear (the burden of) slander and clear sin.

33:59.text: Prophet! Say to your wives, and your daughters, and the believing women, to draw some of their outer clothes over themselves. That is more appropriate for their being recognized and not hurt. God is forgiving, compassionate.

33:60.text: If indeed the hypocrites do not stop – and those in whose hearts is a sickness, and those who cause commotion in the city – We shall indeed incite you against them, (and) then they will not be your neighbors there, except for a little (while).

33:61.text: (They will be) accursed! Wherever they are found, they will be seized and completely killed.

33:62.text: (That was) the customary way of God concerning those who have passed away before, and you will find no change in the customary way of God.

33:63.text: The people ask you about the Hour. Say: ‘Knowledge of it is only with God. What will make you know? Perhaps the Hour is near.’

33:64.text: Surely God has cursed the disbelievers, and prepared for them a blazing (Fire),

33:65.text: there to remain forever. They will not find any ally or helper.

33:66.text: On the Day when their faces will be turned about in the Fire, they will say, ‘Oh, would that we had obeyed God, and obeyed the messenger!’

33:67.text: And they will say, ‘Our Lord, surely we obeyed our men of honor and our great men, and so they led us astray from the way.

33:68.text: Our Lord, give them a double (share) of the punishment, and curse them with a great curse!’

33:69.text: You who believe! Do not be like those who hurt Moses, but God cleared him of what they said, and he was eminent in the sight of God.

33:70.text: You who believe! Guard (yourselves) against God, and speak a direct word.

33:71.text: He will set right your deeds for you, and forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys God and His messenger has attained a great triumph.

33:72.text: Surely We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth, and the mountains, but they refused to bear it, and were afraid of it, and (instead) the human bore it. Surely he has become an evildoer (and) ignorant.

33:73.text: (It is) so that God might punish the hypocrite men and the hypocrite women (alike), and the idolatrous men and the idolatrous women alike, and so that God might turn (in forgiveness) to the believing men and the believing women (alike). God is forgiving, compassionate.

30:3.text: in the nearest (part) of the land, but after their conquering, they will conquer

30:4.text: in a few years. The affair (belongs) to God before and after, and on that day the believers will gloat

30:5.text: over the help of God. He helps whomever He pleases. He is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

30:6.text: The promise of God! God will not break His promise, but most of the people do not know (it).

30:7.text: They perceive (only) what is obvious in this present life, but they are oblivious of the Hereafter.

30:8.text: Do they not reflect within themselves? God did not create the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, except in truth and (for) an appointed time. Yet surely many of the people are indeed disbelievers in the meeting with their Lord.

30:9.text: Have they not traveled on the earth and seen how the end was for those who were before them? They were stronger than them in power, and they ploughed the earth and populated it more than they have populated it. Their messengers brought them the clear signs. God was not one to do them evil, but they did themselves evil.

30:10.text: Then the end of those who had done evil was evil, because they had called the signs of God a lie and mocked them.

30:11.text: God brings about the creation, then restores it, (and) then to Him you will be returned.

30:12.text: On the Day when the Hour strikes, the sinners will despair.

30:13.text: They will not have any intercessors among their associates, but they (will come to) disbelieve in their associates.

30:14.text: On the Day when the Hour strikes, on that Day they will be separated.

30:15.text: As for those who have believed and done righteous deeds, they will be made happy in a meadow.

30:16.text: But as for those who have disbelieved, and called Our signs a lie, and the meeting of the Hereafter, those will be brought forward to the punishment.

30:17.text: So glory to God, when you come to evening and when you come to morning.

30:18.text: Praise (be) to Him in the heavens and the earth – and at night and when you appear (in the day)!

30:19.text: He brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living. He gives the earth life after its death, and in this way you (too) will be brought forth.

30:20.text: (One) of His signs is that He created you from dust, and now you are human beings spreading (far and wide).

30:21.text: (Another) of His signs is that He created spouses for you from yourselves, so that you may live with them, and He has established love and mercy between you. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who reflect.

30:22.text: (Another) of His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the variety of your languages and colors. Surely in that are signs indeed for those who know.

30:23.text: (Another) of His signs is your sleeping by night and day, and your seeking some of His favor. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who hear.

30:24.text: (Another) of His signs (is that) He shows you lightning – in fear and desire – and He sends down water from the sky, and by means of it gives the earth life after its death. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who understand.

30:25.text: (Another) of His signs is that the sky and the earth stand (fast) by His command. Then, when He calls you out of the earth once and for all, suddenly you will come forth.

30:26.text: To Him (belongs) whoever is in the heavens and the earth: all are obedient before Him.

30:27.text: He (it is) who brings about the creation, then restores it – it is easy for Him. (Only) the highest parable in the heavens and the earth (is fitting) for Him. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

30:28.text: He has struck a parable for you from yourselves: Among what your right (hands) own, do you have associates in what We have provided you with, so that you are (all) equal in that respect – (you) fearing them as you fear each other? In this way We make the signs distinct for a people who understand.

30:29.text: No! Those who do evil follow their own (vain) desires without any knowledge. So who will guide those whom God has led astray? They have no helpers.

30:30.text: Set your face to the religion (as) a Ḥanīf – the creation of God for which He created humankind. (There is) no change in the creation of God. That is the right religion, but most of the people do not know (it) –

30:31.text: turning to Him (in repentance). Guard (yourself) against Him, and observe the prayer, and do not be one of the idolaters,

30:32.text: one of those who have divided up their religion and become parties, each faction gloating over what was with them.

30:33.text: When hardship touches the people, they call on their Lord, turning to Him (in repentance). Then, when He gives them a taste of mercy from Himself, suddenly a group of them associates (other gods) with their Lord.

30:34.text: Let them be ungrateful for what We have given them: ‘Enjoy (yourselves)! Soon you will know!’

30:35.text: Or have We sent down any authority on them (for this), and does it speak about what they associate with Him?

30:36.text: When We give the people a taste of mercy, they gloat over it, but if some evil smites them because of what their (own) hands have sent forward, suddenly they despair.

30:37.text: Do they not see that God extends (His) provision to whomever He pleases, and restricts (it)? Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who believe.

30:38.text: Give the family its due, and the poor, and the traveler – that is better for those who desire the face of God, and those – they are the ones who prosper.

30:39.text: Whatever you give in usury, in order that it may increase on the wealth of the people, does not increase with God, but what you give in alms, desiring the face of God – those are the ones who gain double.

30:40.text: (It is) God who created you, then provided for you, then causes you to die, (and) then gives you life. (Are there) any of your associates who (can) do any of that? Glory to Him! He is exalted above what they associate.

30:41.text: Corruption has appeared on the shore and the sea because of what the hands of the people have earned, so that He may give them a taste of what they have done, that they may return.

30:42.text: Say: ‘Travel the earth and see how the end was for those who were before (you). Most of them were idolaters.’

30:43.text: Set your face to the right religion, before a Day comes from God which cannot be turned back. On that Day they will be divided:

30:44.text: whoever disbelieves – his disbelief (will be) on him, but whoever does righteousness – they are smoothing (the way) for themselves,

30:45.text: so that He may repay from His favor those who believe and do righteous deeds. Surely He does not love the disbelievers.

30:46.text: (One) of His signs is that He sends the winds as bringers of good news, so that He may give you a taste of His mercy, and that the ship may run by His command, and that you may seek some of His favor, and that you may be thankful.

30:47.text: Certainly We sent messengers to their people before you, and they brought them the clear signs. Then We took vengeance on those who sinned, but it was an obligation on Us (to) help the believers.

30:48.text: (It is) God who sends the winds, and it stirs up a cloud, and He spreads it in the sky as He pleases, and breaks it into fragments, and you see the rain coming forth from the midst of it. When He smites with it whomever He pleases of His servants, suddenly they welcome the good news,

30:49.text: though before (this), before it was sent down on them, they were in despair.

30:50.text: Observe the traces of the mercy of God, how He gives the earth life after its death. Surely that One will indeed give the dead life. He is powerful over everything.

30:51.text: If indeed We send a wind, and they see it growing yellow, they indeed remain disbelievers after that.

30:52.text: You cannot make the dead to hear, nor can you make the deaf to hear the call when they turn away, withdrawing.

30:53.text: You cannot guide the blind out of their straying, nor can you make (anyone) hear, except for those who believe in Our signs, and so they submit.

30:54.text: (It is) God who created you from weakness, then after weakness He made strength, (and) then after strength He made weakness and grey hair. He creates whatever He pleases. He is the Knowing, the Powerful.

30:55.text: On the Day when the Hour strikes, the sinners will swear they remained (in the grave) only for an hour – that is how deluded they were –

30:56.text: but those who have been given knowledge and belief will say (to them), ‘You have remained in the Book of God until the Day of Raising Up, and this is the Day of Raising Up, but you did not know (it).’

30:57.text: On that Day their excuses will not benefit those who have done evil, nor will they be allowed to make amends.

30:58.text: Certainly We have struck for the people every (kind of) parable in this Qur’ān. But if indeed you bring them a sign, those who disbelieve will indeed say, ‘You are nothing but perpetrators of falsehood.’

30:59.text: In this way God sets a seal on the hearts of those who do not know.

30:60.text: So be patient! Surely the promise of God is true. And (let) not those who are uncertain unsettle you.

44:3.text: Surely We sent it down on a blessed night – surely We were warning –

44:4.text: during which every wise command was divided out,

44:5.text: as a command from Us – surely We were sending –

44:6.text: as a mercy from your Lord. Surely He – He is the Hearing, the Knowing,

44:7.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, if you (would) be certain.

44:8.text: (There is) no god but Him. He gives life and causes death – your Lord and the Lord of your fathers of old.

44:9.text: No! They are in doubt (while) they jest.

44:10.text: So watch for the Day when the sky will bring a visible smoke

44:11.text: covering the people: ‘This is a painful punishment!

44:12.text: Our Lord, remove the punishment from us! Surely We are believers.’

44:13.text: How will the reminder be for them, when a clear messenger has already come to them?

44:14.text: Then they turned away from him, and said, ‘(He is) tutored, (he is) possessed!’

44:15.text: ‘Surely We are going to remove the punishment a little, (but) surely you are going to revert!’

44:16.text: On the Day when We attack with the great attack, surely We are going to take vengeance.

44:17.text: Certainly before them We tested the people of Pharaoh, when an honorable messenger came to them:

44:18.text: ‘Deliver to me the servants of God! Surely I am a trustworthy messenger for you.’

44:19.text: And: ‘Do not exalt yourselves against God! Surely I bring you clear authority.

44:20.text: Surely I take refuge with my Lord and your Lord, for fear that you stone me.

44:21.text: If you do not believe me, withdraw from me!’

44:22.text: So he called on his Lord: ‘These are a sinful people.’

44:23.text: And: ‘Journey with My servants by night. Surely you will be followed.

44:24.text: And leave the sea parted, (for) surely they are a force (to be) drowned.’

44:25.text: How many gardens and springs they left (behind),

44:26.text: and (fields of) crops, and an honorable place,

44:27.text: and prosperity in which they used to rejoice.

44:28.text: So (it was), and We caused another people to inherit them.

44:29.text: Neither the sky nor the earth wept for them, nor were they spared.

44:30.text: Certainly We rescued the Sons of Israel from the humiliating punishment,

44:31.text: (and) from Pharaoh. Surely he was haughty, one of the wanton.

44:32.text: Certainly We chose them, on (the basis of) knowledge, over the worlds,

44:33.text: and gave them signs in which (there was) a clear test.

44:35.text: ‘There is nothing but our first death. We are not going to be raised.

44:36.text: Bring (back) our fathers, if you are truthful!’

44:37.text: Are they better, or the people of Tubba‘, and those who were before them? We destroyed them. Surely they were sinners.

44:38.text: We did not create the heavens and earth, and whatever is between them, in jest.

44:39.text: We created them only in truth, but most of them do not know (it).

44:40.text: Surely the Day of Decision is their meeting – all (of them) –

44:41.text: a Day when a protector will be of no use at all as a protector, and they will not be helped

44:42.text: – except for the one on whom God has compassion. Surely He – He is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

44:44.text: is the food of the sinner,

44:45.text: like molten metal boiling in the belly,

44:47.text: ‘Seize him and drag him into the midst of the Furnace.

44:48.text: Then pour over his head from the punishment of hot (water)!’

44:49.text: ‘Taste (it)! Surely you are the mighty, the honorable!

44:50.text: Surely this is what you doubted about.’

44:51.text: Surely the ones who guard (themselves) are in a secure place,

44:52.text: in (the midst of) gardens and springs,

44:53.text: wearing clothes of silk and brocade, facing each other.

44:54.text: So (it is), and We shall marry them to (maidens) with dark, wide eyes.

44:55.text: There they will call for every (kind of) fruit, secure.

44:56.text: There they will not taste death, except the first death, and He will guard them against the punishment of the Furnace.

44:57.text: Favor from your Lord! That is the great triumph!

44:58.text: Surely We have made it easy in your language, so that they may take heed.

44:59.text: So watch! Surely they (too) are watching.

57:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies God. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

57:2.text: To Him (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. He is powerful over everything.

57:3.text: He is the First and the Last, the Outer and the Inner. He has knowledge of everything.

57:4.text: He (it is) who created the heavens and the earth in six days. Then He mounted the throne. He knows what penetrates into the earth, and what comes forth from it, and what comes down from the sky, and what goes up into it. He is with you wherever you are. God sees what you do.

57:5.text: To Him (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and to God (all) matters are returned.

57:6.text: He causes the night to pass into the day, and causes the day to pass into the night. He knows what is in the hearts.

57:7.text: Believe in God and His messenger, and contribute from what He has made you inheritors in. Those of you who believe and contribute – for them (there will be) a great reward.

57:8.text: What is (the matter) with you that you do not believe in God, when the messenger calls you to believe in your Lord, and He has already taken a covenant with you, if you are believers?

57:9.text: He (it is) who sends down on His servant clear signs, so that He may bring you forth from the darkness to the light. Surely God is indeed kind (and) compassionate with you.

57:10.text: What is (the matter) with you that you do not contribute in the way of God, when the inheritance of the heavens and the earth (belongs) to God? The one among you who contributed and fought before the victory is not equal. They are higher in rank than those who contributed and fought after that. Yet to each God has promised the good (reward). God is aware of what you do.

57:11.text: Who is the one who will lend to God a good loan, and He will double it for him? For him (there will be) a generous reward.

57:12.text: On the Day when you see the believing men and the believing women: their light will run before them, and at their right (hands): ‘Good news for you today! Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain. That is the great triumph!’

57:13.text: On the Day when the hypocrite men and the hypocrite women will say to those who believed: ‘Wait for us!
