Let us borrow your light!’ It will be said, ‘Turn back and search for a light!’ And a wall with a door will be set up between them: on the inside of it (there is) mercy, and on the outside of it – facing (it) – (there is) the punishment.

57:14.text: They will call out to them: ‘Were we not with you?’ They will say, ‘Yes, indeed! But you tempted yourselves, and you waited and doubted, and wishful thinking deceived you, until the command of God came, and the Deceiver deceived you about God.

57:15.text: So today no ransom will be accepted from you, nor from those who have disbelieved. Your refuge is the Fire – it is your protector – and it is an evil destination!’

57:16.text: Is it not time for those who believe that their hearts become humble before the Reminder of God, and (before) what has come down of the truth, and (that) they not be like those to whom the Book was given before, and for whom the time lasted too long, so that their hearts became hard, and many of them were wicked?

57:17.text: Know that God gives the earth life after its death. We have made clear to you the signs, so that you may understand.

57:18.text: Surely the charitable men and the charitable women, and (those who) have lent to God a good loan – it will be doubled for them, and for them (there will be) a generous reward.

57:19.text: Those who believe in God and His messengers, those – they are the truthful and the martyrs in the sight of their Lord – they have their reward and their light. But those who disbelieve and call Our signs a lie – those are the companions of the Furnace.

57:20.text: Know that this present life is nothing but jest and diversion, and a (passing) splendor, and a (cause for) boasting among you, and a rivalry in wealth and children. (It is) like rain: the vegetation it produces pleases the disbelievers, (but) then it withers and you see it turning yellow, (and) then it becomes broken debris. In the Hereafter (there is) a harsh punishment,

57:21.text:
and forgiveness from God and approval. But this present life is nothing but the enjoyment of deception.

Race toward forgiveness from your Lord, and a Garden – its width is like the width of the sky and the earth – prepared for those who believe in God and His messengers. That is the favor of God. He gives it to whomever He pleases. God is full of great favor.

57:22.text: No smiting smites in the earth or among yourselves, except that it was in a Book before We brought it about – surely that is easy for God –

57:23.text: so that you may not grieve over what eludes you, nor gloat about what has come to you. God does not love anyone who is arrogant (and) boastful,

57:24.text: (nor) those who are stingy and command the people to be stingy. Whoever turns away – surely God – He is the wealthy One, the Praiseworthy.

92:1.text: By the night when it covers!

92:2.text: By the day when it reveals its splendor!

92:3.text: By what created the male and the female!

92:4.text: Surely your striving is indeed (to) divided (ends).

92:5.text: As for the one who gives and guards (himself),

92:7.text: We shall ease him to ease.

92:8.text: But as for the one who is stingy, and considers himself independent,

92:9.text: and calls the best (reward) a lie,

92:10.text: We shall ease him to hardship.

92:11.text: His wealth will be of no use to him when he perishes.

92:12.text: Surely on Us (depends) the guidance indeed.

92:13.text: Surely to Us indeed (belong) the last and the first.

92:14.text: I have warned you of a flaming Fire.

92:15.text: Only the most miserable will burn in it:

92:16.text: the one who called (it) a lie and turned away.

92:17.text: But the one who guards (himself) will avoid it:

92:18.text: the one who gives his wealth to purify himself,

92:19.text: and (confers) no blessing on anyone (expecting) to be repaid,

92:20.text: but only seeks the face of his Lord, the Most High.

92:21.text: Soon indeed he will be pleased.

85:1.text: By the sky full of constellations,

85:3.text: by a witness and what is witnessed!

85:4.text: May the companions of the Pit perish

85:6.text: when they are sitting over it,

85:7.text: and they (themselves) are witnesses of what they have done to the believers.

85:8.text: They took vengeance on them only because they believed in God, the Mighty, the Praiseworthy,

85:9.text: the One who – to Him (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. God is a witness over everything.

85:10.text: Surely those who persecute the believing men and the believing women, (and) then have not turned (in repentance) – for them (there is) the punishment of Gehenna, and for them (there is) the punishment of the burning (Fire).

85:11.text: Surely those who believe and do righteous deeds – for them (there are) Gardens through which rivers flow. That is the great triumph!

85:12.text: Surely your Lord’s attack is harsh indeed.

85:13.text: Surely He – He brings about (the creation) and restores (it).

85:14.text: He is the Forgiving, the Loving,

85:15.text: Holder of the throne, the Glorious,

85:17.text: Has the story of the forces come to you,

85:19.text: No! But those who disbelieve persist in calling (it) a lie.

85:20.text: Yet God surrounds them from behind.

85:21.text: Yes! It is a glorious Qur’ān,

79:1.text: By the ones who snatch violently!

79:2.text: By the ones who draw out completely!

79:3.text: By the ones who glide smoothly,

79:6.text: On the Day when the (earth)quake quakes,

79:7.text: and that which ensues follows it,

79:8.text: hearts on that Day will pound,

79:10.text: They will say, ‘Are we indeed being turned back into (our) former state?

79:12.text: They will say, ‘That would then be a losing turn!’

79:13.text: Yet it will only be a single shout,

79:14.text: and suddenly they will be awakened.

79:15.text: Has the story of Moses come to you?

79:16.text: When his Lord called to him in the holy wādī of Ṭuwā:

79:17.text: ‘Go to Pharaoh! Surely he has transgressed insolently.

79:18.text: And say: “Do you have (any desire) to purify yourself?”

79:19.text: And: “I would guide you to your Lord, and then perhaps you will fear (Him).”’

79:20.text: So he showed him the great sign,

79:21.text: but he called (it) a lie and disobeyed.

79:22.text: Then he turned away in haste,

79:23.text: and he gathered (his people) and called out,

79:24.text: and said, ‘I am your Lord, the Most High!’

79:25.text: So God seized him with the punishment of the last and the first.

79:26.text: Surely in that is a lesson indeed for whoever fears.

79:27.text: Are you a stronger creation or the sky? He built it.

79:28.text: He raised its roof and fashioned it.

79:29.text: He darkened its night and brought forth its morning light.

79:30.text: And the earth, after that, He spread it out.

79:31.text: He brought forth from it its water and its pasture

79:32.text: – and the mountains, He anchored it (to them) –

79:33.text: a provision for you and for your livestock.

79:35.text: on the Day when a person will remember what he strove for,

79:36.text: and the Furnace will come forth for all to see:

79:37.text: as for the one who transgressed insolently,

79:39.text: surely the Furnace – it will be the refuge.

79:40.text: But as for the one who feared the position of his Lord, and restrained himself from (vain) desire,

79:41.text: surely the Garden – it will be the refuge.

79:42.text: They ask you about the Hour: ‘When is its arrival?’

79:43.text: What do you have to do with the mention of it?

79:44.text: To your Lord is its (ultimate) goal.

79:45.text: You are only a warner for whoever fears it.

79:46.text: On the Day when they see it, (it will seem) as if they had remained (in the grave) for only an evening or its morning light.

94:1.text: Did We not expand your heart for you,

94:2.text: and deliver you of your burden,

94:4.text: Did We not raise your reputation for you?

94:7.text: So when you are free, work on.

94:8.text: And to your Lord set (your) desire.

91:1.text: By the sun and her morning light!

91:2.text: By the moon when he follows her!

91:3.text: By the day when it reveals her!

91:4.text: By the night when it covers her!

91:5.text: By the sky and what built it!

91:6.text: By the earth and what spread it!

91:7.text: By the self and what fashioned it,

91:8.text: and instilled it with its (tendency to) depravity and its (sense of) guarding (itself)!

91:9.text: He has prospered who purifies it,

91:10.text: and he has failed who corrupts it.

91:11.text: Thamūd called (it) a lie by their insolent transgression,

91:12.text: when the most miserable (one) of them was raised up,

91:13.text: and the messenger of God said to them, ‘The she-camel of God and her drink!’

91:14.text: But they called (him) a liar and wounded her. So their Lord covered them over for their sin and leveled it.

91:15.text: He was not afraid of its outcome.

100:4.text: when they kick up a (cloud of) dust,

100:5.text: and pierce through the midst of it all together!

100:6.text: Surely the human is indeed an ingrate to his Lord,

100:7.text: and surely he is indeed a witness to that,

100:8.text: and surely he is indeed harsh in (his) love for (worldly) goods.

100:9.text: Does he not know? When what is in the graves is ransacked,

100:10.text: and what is in the hearts is extracted

100:11.text: – surely on that Day their Lord will indeed be aware of them.

106:2.text: for their uniting for the caravan of the winter and the summer:

106:3.text: Let them serve the Lord of this House,

106:4.text: who has fed them on account of (their) hunger, and secured them on account of (their) fear.

5:1.text: You who believe! Fulfill (your) pledges. Permitted to you (to eat) is (any) animal of the livestock, except for what is recited to you. The hunting (of wild game) is not permitted when you are (in a state of) sanctity. Surely God decrees whatever He wills.

5:2.text: You who believe! Do not profane the symbols of God, nor the sacred month, nor the offering, nor the ornaments, nor (those) going to the Sacred House seeking favor from their Lord and approval. But when you are free (from your state of sanctity), hunt (wild game). Do not let hatred of the people who kept you from (going to) the Sacred Mosque provoke you to commit aggression. Help one another to piety and the guarding (of yourselves), and do not help each other to sin and enmity. Guard (yourselves) against God. Surely God is harsh in retribution.

5:3.text: Forbidden to you (to eat) are: the dead (animal), and the blood, and swine’s flesh, and what has been dedicated to (a god) other than God, and the strangled (to death), and the beaten (to death), and the fallen (to death), and the gored (to death), and what a wild animal has devoured – except what you have slaughtered – and what has been sacrificed on stones. And (it is forbidden) that you should divide by divination arrows – that is wickedness for you. Today those who disbelieve have no hope of (ever destroying) your religion. So do not fear them, but fear Me. Today I have perfected your religion for you, and I have completed My blessing on you, and I have approved Islam for you as a religion. But if anyone is forced by hunger, without intending to sin – surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

5:4.text: They ask you what is permitted to them (to eat). Say: ‘The good things are permitted to you, and what you have taught some of (your) hunting animals (to catch), training (them), (and) teaching them some of what God has taught you. So eat from what they catch for you, and mention the name of God over it, and guard (yourselves) against God. Surely God is quick at the reckoning.’

5:5.text: Today the good things are permitted to you, and the food of those who have been given the Book is permitted to you, and your food is permitted to them. (Permitted to you are) the chaste women among the believers, and the chaste women among those who have been given the Book before you, once you have given them their marriage gifts, taking (them) in marriage, not in immorality, nor taking (them) as secret lovers. Whoever disbelieves in the faith, his deed has come to nothing, and in the Hereafter he will be one of the losers.

5:6.text: You who believe! When you stand up for the prayer, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, and wipe your heads and your feet up to the ankles. If you are defiled, purify yourselves. If you are sick or on a journey, or if one of you has come from the toilet, or if you have touched women, and you do not find any water, take clean earth and wipe your faces and your hands with it. God does not wish to place any difficulty on you, but He wishes to purify you and to complete His blessing on you, so that you may be thankful.

5:7.text: Remember the blessing of God on you, and His covenant with which He bound you, when you said, ‘We hear and obey.’ Guard (yourselves) against God. Surely God knows what is in the hearts.

5:8.text: You who believe! Be supervisors for God, witnesses in justice, and do not let hatred of a people provoke you to act unfairly. Act fairly! It is nearer to guarding (yourselves). Guard (yourselves) against God. Surely God is aware of what you do.

5:9.text: God has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds (that there is) forgiveness for them and a great reward.

5:10.text: But those who disbelieve and call Our signs a lie – those are the companions of the Furnace.

5:11.text: You who believe! Remember the blessing of God on you. When a people were determined to stretch out their hands against you, He restrained their hands from you. Guard (yourselves) against God, and in God let the believers put their trust.

5:12.text: Certainly God took a covenant with the Sons of Israel, and We raised up among them twelve chieftains, and God said, ‘Surely I am with you. If indeed you observe the prayer and give the alms, and believe in My messengers and support them, and lend to God a good loan, I shall indeed absolve you of your evil deeds, and cause you to enter Gardens through which rivers flow. But whoever of you disbelieves after that has gone astray from the right way.’

5:13.text: For their breaking their covenant, We cursed them and made their hearts hard. They alter words from their positions, and have forgotten part of what they were reminded of. You will continue to see treachery from them, except for a few of them. Yet pardon them and excuse (them). Surely God loves the doers of good.

5:14.text: And with those who say, ‘Surely we are Christians,’ We took a covenant, but they have forgotten part of what they were reminded of. So We stirred up enmity and hatred among them, until the Day of Resurrection, and (then) God will inform them about what they have done.

5:15.text: People of the Book! Our messenger has come to you, making clear to you much of what you have been hiding of the Book, and overlooking much. Now a light and a clear Book from God has come to you.

5:16.text: By means of it God guides those who follow after His approval (in the) ways of peace, and He brings them out of the darkness to the light, by His permission, and guides them to a straight path.

5:17.text: Certainly they disbelieve who say, ‘Surely God – He is the Messiah, son of Mary.’ Say: ‘Who could do anything against God if He wished to destroy the Messiah, son of Mary, and his mother, and whoever is on the earth – all (of them) together? To God (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them. He creates whatever He pleases. God is powerful over everything.’

5:18.text: The Jews and the Christians say, ‘We are the sons of God, and His beloved.’ Say: ‘Then why does He punish you for your sins? No! You are human beings, (part) of what He created. He forgives whomever He pleases and He punishes whomever He pleases. To God (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them. To Him is the (final) destination.’

5:19.text: People of the Book! Our messenger has come to you, making (things) clear to you after an interval between the messengers, in case you should say, ‘No bringer of good news has come to us, nor any warner.’ Now a bringer of good news and a warner has come to you. God is powerful over everything.

5:20.text: (Remember) when Moses said to his people, ‘My people! Remember the blessing of God on you, when He made prophets among you, and made you kings, and gave you what He had not given to anyone of the worlds.

5:21.text: My people! Enter the Holy Land which God has prescribed for you, and do not turn your backs, or you will turn out (to be) losers.’

5:22.text: They said, ‘Moses! Surely (there is) an oppressive people in it, and we shall not (be able to) enter it until they depart from it. If they depart from it, we shall enter (it).’

5:23.text: Two men among those who feared (God), whom God had blessed, said, ‘Enter (through) the gate against them. When you have entered it, you will be victorious. Put your trust in God, if you are believers.’

5:24.text: They said, ‘Moses! Surely we shall never enter it as long as they remain in it. So you and your Lord go, and both of you fight. Surely we shall be sitting here.’

5:25.text: He said, ‘My Lord, surely I have no control over (anyone) but myself and my brother. Make a separation between us and this wicked people.’

5:26.text: He said, ‘Surely it is forbidden to them for forty years, while they wander on the earth. So do not grieve over this wicked people.’

5:27.text: Recite to them the story of Adam’s two sons in truth: when they both offered a sacrifice, and it was accepted from one of them, but was not accepted from the other. (One) said, ‘I shall indeed kill you.’ (The other) said, ‘God only accepts (offerings) from the ones who guard (themselves).

5:28.text: If indeed you stretch out your hand against me, to kill me, (still) I shall not stretch out my hand against you, to kill you. Surely I fear God, Lord of the worlds.

5:29.text: Surely I wish that you would incur my sin and your sin, so that you may be one of the companions of the Fire. That is the reward of the evildoers.’

5:30.text: Then his (own) self compelled him to the killing of his brother. So he killed him and became one of the losers.

5:31.text: Then God raised up a raven, scratching in the earth, to show him how to hide the shame of his brother. He said, ‘Woe is me! Am I unable to be like this raven, and hide the shame of my brother?’ And then he became one of the regretful.

5:32.text: From that time We prescribed for the Sons of Israel that whoever kills a person, except (in retaliation) for another, or (for) fomenting corruption on the earth, (it is) as if he had killed all the people. And whoever gives (a person) life, (it is) as if he had given all the people life. Certainly Our messengers have brought them the clear signs, yet even after that many of them act wantonly on the earth.

5:33.text: The penalty (for) those who wage war (against) God and His messenger, and who strive in fomenting corruption on the earth, is that they be killed or crucified, or their hands and feet on opposite sides be cut off, or they be banished from the earth. That is a disgrace for them in this world, and in the Hereafter (there will be) a great punishment for them,

5:34.text: except those who turn (in repentance) before you have them in your power. Know that God is forgiving, compassionate.

5:35.text: You who believe! Guard (yourselves) against God, and seek access to Him, and struggle in His way, so that you may prosper.

5:36.text: Surely those who disbelieve, even if they had whatever is on the earth – all (of it) and as much again – to ransom (themselves) with it from punishment on the Day of Resurrection, it would not be accepted from them. For them (there is) a painful punishment.

5:37.text: They will wish to get out of the Fire, but they will not get out of it. For them (there is) a lasting punishment.

5:38.text: (As for) the male thief and the female thief: cut off their hands as a penalty for what they have done – a punishment from God. God is mighty, wise.

5:39.text: Whoever turns (in repentance) after his evildoing and sets (things) right – surely God will turn to him (in forgiveness). Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

5:40.text: Do you not know that God – to Him (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth – He punishes whomever He pleases and He forgives whomever He pleases. God is powerful over everything.

5:41.text: Messenger! Do not let those who are quick to disbelief cause you sorrow. (They are) among those who say with their mouths, ‘We believe,’ but their hearts do not believe. Among those who are Jews (there are) those who listen to lies, (and who) listen to (other) people who have not come to you. They alter words from their positions, (and) say, ‘If you are given this, take it, but if you are not given it, beware.’ If God wishes to test anyone, you will not have any power for him against God. Those are the ones whose hearts God does not wish to purify. For them (there is) disgrace in this world, and in the Hereafter (there will be) a great punishment for them.

5:42.text: (They are) listeners to lies (and) consumers of what is forbidden. If they come to you, judge between them or turn away from them. If you turn away from them, they will not harm you at all. But if you judge, judge between them in justice. Surely God loves the ones who act fairly.

5:43.text: Yet how will they make you (their) judge, when they have the Torah, containing the judgment of God, (and) then turn away after that? Those (people) are not with the believers. The Quran contains numerous verses that show suspicion about the treatment of the pre-Islamic scriptures by the communities who possessed these scriptures. The meanings of the verses are not clear because important details are missing, and the actions referred to are often obscure. However, this has not prevented Muslim polemicists from using these verses to accuse the Bible of being corrupt and falsified. The Quran refers to the pre-Islamic scriptures by the names tawrāt (Torah, eighteen times), injīl (Gospel, twelve times), and zabūr (Psalms, three times). Wherever the Quran actually names these scriptures, it speaks of them only in the most positive and respectful terms. In several verses the Quran describes the Torah as “complete,” “detailed,” “guidance,” and “a mercy” (e.g., 6.154) and the Gospel as “guidance,” “light,” and “admonition” (5.46). The Quran also frequently characterizes its relationship to the earlier scriptures as “confirming” what came before (muṣaddiqan; e.g., 3.3). Suggestions of doubtful treatment of these earlier scriptures come in verses that use a series of important Arabic verbs and expressions of action. The most common verbs in this group mean “to conceal” (katama, asarra, and akhfā’). For example, already in the first part of the second sūra, the Quran commands the Children of Israel not to “conceal (katama) the truth knowingly” (2.42). By the end of the sixth sūra, the Quran has used these verbs an additional ten times, apparently with reference to treatment of the earlier scriptures. More difficult to interpret are the verses using the Arabic verbs ḥarrafa (2.75; 4.46; 5.13, 41) and baddala (2.59; 7.162), or expressions like “twist tongues” (3.78) and “write the book with hands” (2.79). These verses generally lack information as to the precise nature of the action, who is doing the action, and what text, if any, is being acted upon. Muslim commentaries on the Quran have interpreted most of these verses to mean a range of actions of resistance to the messenger of Islam. The earliest commentaries explain the verb ḥarrafa in line with the elastic English concept of “tampering.” The commentaries also contain accusations of falsification of the Torah, especially in comments on 2.79 and 3.78. Interpreting these verses, the commentaries often claimed that the People of the Book were changing or removing references to Muhammad from their scriptures. Between the early commentaries and the later harsh Muslim accusations against the Bible often heard today, the development of the doctrine of tampering with pre-Islamic scriptures is something of a curiosity. None of the early Muslim stories of the life of Islam’s messenger include an episode of falsification, nor does the messenger make any such accusation. In the six authoritative collections of hadith (sayings attributed to the messenger), only a single tradition in a single collection accuses Jews and Christians of falsification, and this is attributed not to Muhammad but to a Muslim of a later generation. By contrast, many stories and traditions mentioning the earlier scriptures seem to assume intact texts in the hands of Jews and Christians. It has been therefore works of Muslim polemic that have given this accusation its great currency and popularity. Many scholars locate the first major development of the accusation in the writings of Ibn Ḥazm (d. 1064) of Cordoba. Ibn Ḥazm’s Kitāb al-fiṣal fī ’l-milal set up the main lines of the accusation. He presented examples from the Hebrew Bible of what he considered to be chronological and geographical inaccuracies, theological impossibilities, and behavior of prophets that did not match the Islamic doctrine of the infallibility (‘iṣma) of prophets. Ibn Ḥazm’s critique provided the main inspiration for the accusation until Rahmat Allah Kayranwi added material from European works of “higher criticism” in his 1864 polemic, Iẓhār al-ḥaqq. As far as the Quran is concerned, if the Quran does indeed make an accusation of falsification against the Bible, it is a bare claim, not supported in the Quran with anything like material evidence. The accusation therefore needs to be evaluated academically in light of the history of biblical manuscripts – from the earliest Qumran evidence (third century BC), through the origins of the Quran (seventh century AD), and up to the famous manuscripts dated to the first centuries of Islam (e.g., Masoretic Text, tenth century AD). Academic studies have revealed a few inconsistencies in this manuscript history, but none has indicated that any possible material about Muhammad was altered or erased.

5:44.text: Surely We sent down the Torah, containing guidance and light. By means of it the prophets who had submitted rendered judgment for those who were Jews, and (so did) the rabbis and the teachers, with what they were entrusted of the Book of God, and they were witnesses to it. So do not fear the people, but fear Me, and do not sell My signs for a small price. Whoever does not judge by what God has sent down, those – they are the disbelievers.

5:45.text: We prescribed for them in it: ‘The life for the life, and the eye for the eye, and the nose for the nose, and the ear for the ear, and the tooth for the tooth, and (for) the wounds retaliation.’ But whoever remits it as a freewill offering, it will be an atonement for him. Whoever does not judge by what God has sent down, those – they are the evildoers.

5:46.text: And in their footsteps We followed up with Jesus, son of Mary, confirming what was with him of the Torah, and We gave him the Gospel, containing guidance and light, and confirming what was with him of the Torah, and as guidance and admonition to the ones who guard (themselves).

5:47.text: So let the People of the Gospel judge by what God has sent down in it. Whoever does not judge by what God has sent down, those – they are the wicked.

5:48.text: And We have sent down to you the Book with the truth, confirming what was with him of the Book, and as a preserver of it. So judge between them by what God has sent down, and do not follow their (vain) desires (away) from what has come to you of the truth. For each of you We have made a pathway and an open road. If God had (so) pleased, He would indeed have made you one community, but (He did not do so) in order to test you by what He has given you. So race (toward doing) good deeds. To God is your return – all (of you) – and then He will inform you about your differences.

5:49.text: (So) judge between them by what God has sent down, and do not follow their (vain) desires, and beware of them in case they tempt you (to turn away) from any part of what God has sent down to you. If they turn away, know that God intends to smite them for some of their sins. Surely many of the people are wicked indeed.

5:50.text: Is it the judgment of the (time of) ignorance they seek? Yet who is better in judgment than God, for a people who are certain (in their belief)?

5:51.text: You who believe! Do not take the Jews and the Christians as allies. They are allies of each other. Whoever of you takes them as allies is already one of them. Surely God does not guide the people who are evildoers.

5:52.text: Yet you see those in whose hearts is a sickness – they are quick (to turn) to them, (and) they say, ‘We fear that disaster may smite us.’ But it may be that God will bring the victory, or some command from Himself, and they will be full of regret for what they kept secret within themselves.

5:53.text: But those who believe will say, ‘Are these those who swore by God the most solemn of their oaths: (that) surely they were indeed with you? Their deeds have come to nothing, and they are the losers.’

5:54.text: You who believe! Whoever of you turns back from his religion, God will bring (another) people whom He loves, and who love Him, (who are) humble toward the believers, mighty toward the disbelievers, (who) struggle in the way of God, and do not fear the blame of anyone. That is the favor of God. He gives it to whomever He pleases. God is embracing, knowing.

5:55.text: Your only ally is God, and His messenger, and the believers who observe the prayer and give the alms, and who bow.

5:56.text: Whoever takes God as an ally, and His messenger, and those who believe – surely the faction of God, they are the victors.

5:57.text: You who believe! Do not take those who take your religion in mockery and jest as allies, (either) from those who were given the Book before you, or (from) the disbelievers. Guard (yourselves) against God, if you are believers.

5:58.text: When you make the call to prayer, they take it in mockery and jest. That is because they are a people who do not understand.

5:59.text: Say: ‘People of the Book! Do you take vengeance on us (for any other reason) than that we believe in God and what has been sent down to us, and what was sent down before (this), and because most of you are wicked?’

5:60.text: Say: ‘Shall I inform you of (something) worse than that? Retribution with God! Whomever God has cursed, and whomever He is angry with – some of whom He made apes, and pigs, and slaves of al-Ṭāghūt – those are in a worse situation and farther astray from the right way.’

5:61.text: When they come to you, they say, ‘We believe,’ but they have already entered in disbelief and will depart in it. God knows what they are concealing.

5:62.text: You see many of them being quick to sin and enmity, and consuming what is forbidden. Evil indeed is what they have done!

5:63.text: Why do the rabbis and the teachers not forbid them from their saying what is a sin and (from) their consuming what is forbidden? Evil indeed is what they have done!

5:64.text: The Jews say, ‘The hand of God is chained.’ (May) their hands (be) chained, and (may) they (be) cursed for what they say! No! Both His hands are outstretched: He gives as He pleases. What has been sent down to you from your Lord will indeed increase many of them in insolent transgression and disbelief. We have cast enmity and hatred among them until the Day of Resurrection. Whenever they light the fire of war, God extinguishes it. But they strive (at) fomenting corruption on the earth, and God does not love the fomenters of corruption.

5:65.text: Had the People of the Book believed and guarded (themselves), We would indeed have absolved them of their evil deeds, and caused them to enter Gardens of Bliss.

5:66.text: Had they observed the Torah and the Gospel, and what was sent down to them from their Lord, they would indeed have eaten from (what was) above them and from (what was) beneath their feet. Some of them are a moderate community, but most of them – evil is what they do.

5:67.text: Messenger! Deliver what has been sent down to you from your Lord. If you do not, you have not delivered His message. God will protect you from the people. Surely God does not guide the people who are disbelievers.

5:68.text: Say: ‘People of the Book! You are (standing) on nothing until you observe the Torah and the Gospel, and what has been sent down to you from your Lord.’ But what has been sent down to you from your Lord will indeed increase many of them in insolent transgression and disbelief. So do not grieve over the people who are disbelievers.

5:69.text: Surely those who believe, and those who are Jews, and the Sabians, and the Christians – whoever believes in God and the Last Day, and does righteousness – (there will be) no fear on them, nor will they sorrow.

5:70.text: Certainly We took a covenant with the Sons of Israel, and We sent messengers to them. Whenever a messenger brought them what they themselves did not desire, some they called liars and some they killed.

5:71.text: They thought that there would be no trouble (for them), so they became blind and deaf. Then God turned to them (in forgiveness), (and) then many of them became blind and deaf (again). Yet God sees what they do.

5:72.text: Certainly they have disbelieved who say, ‘Surely God – He is the Messiah, son of Mary,’ when the Messiah said, ‘Sons of Israel! Serve God, my Lord and your Lord. Surely he who associates (anything) with God, God has forbidden him (from) the Garden, and his refuge is the Fire. The evildoers have no helpers.’

5:73.text: Certainly they have disbelieved who say, ‘Surely God is the third of three,’ when (there is) no god but one God. If they do not stop what they are saying, a painful punishment will indeed strike those of them who disbelieve.

5:74.text: Will they not turn to God (in repentance) and ask forgiveness from Him? God is forgiving, compassionate.

5:75.text: The Messiah, son of Mary, was only a messenger. Messengers have passed away before him. His mother was a truthful woman. They both ate food. See how We make clear the signs to them, then see how deluded they are.

5:76.text: Say: ‘Do you serve what has no power to (cause) you harm or benefit, instead of God (alone)? God – He is the Hearing, the Knowing.’

5:77.text: Say: ‘People of the Book! Do not go beyond the limits in your religion, (saying anything) other than the truth, and do not follow (the vain) desires of a people who went astray before (you). They have led many astray, and they have gone astray from the right way.’

5:78.text: Those of the Sons of Israel who disbelieved were cursed by the tongue of David and Jesus, son of Mary – that was because they disobeyed and were transgressing.

5:79.text: They did not forbid each other any evildoing. Evil indeed is what they have done!

5:80.text: You see many of them taking those who disbelieve as allies. Evil indeed is what they have sent forward for themselves! That (is why) God became angry with them, and in the punishment they will remain.

5:81.text: If they had believed in God and the prophet, and what has been sent down to him, they would not have taken them as friends. But many of them are wicked.

5:82.text: Certainly you will find that the most violent of people in enmity to the believers are the Jews and the idolaters. Certainly you will find that the closest of them in affection to the believers are those who say, ‘We are Christians.’ That is because (there are) priests and monks among them, and because they are not arrogant.

5:83.text: When they hear what has been sent down to the messenger, you see their eyes overflowing with tears because of what they recognize of the truth. They say, ‘Our Lord, we believe, so write us down among those who bear witness.

5:84.text: Why should we not believe in God and (in) what has come to us of the truth, when we are eager for our Lord to cause us to enter with the people who are righteous?’

5:85.text: So God has rewarded them for what they said (with) Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain. That is the reward for the doers of good.

5:86.text: But those who disbelieve and call Our signs a lie, those are the companions of the Furnace.

5:87.text: You who believe! Do not forbid the good things which God has permitted to you, and do not transgress. Surely God does not love the transgressors.

5:88.text: Eat from what God has provided you as permitted (and) good, and guard (yourselves) against God – the One in whom you are believers.

5:89.text: God will not take you to task for a slip in your oaths, but He will take you to task for what you have pledged by oath. Atonement for it is the feeding of ten poor persons with the average (amount of food) which you feed your households, or clothing them, or the setting free of a slave. Whoever does not find (the means to do that), (the penalty is) a fast for three days. That is the atonement for your oaths when you have sworn (them, and broken them). But guard your oaths! In this way God makes clear to you His signs, so that you may be thankful.

5:90.text: You who believe! Wine, games of chance, stones, and divination arrows are an abomination, part of the work of Satan. So avoid it in order that you may prosper.

5:91.text: Satan only wishes to cause enmity and hatred among you with wine and games of chance, and to keep you from the remembrance of God and from the prayer. Will you refrain?

5:92.text: Obey God, and obey the messenger, and beware! If you do turn away, know that only (dependent) on Our messenger is the clear delivery (of the message).

5:93.text: There is no blame on those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they may have eaten, so long as they guard (themselves) and believe and do righteous deeds, (and) then (again) guard (themselves) and believe, (and) then (again) guard (themselves) and do good. God loves the doers of good.

5:94.text: You who believe! God will indeed test you with some of the wild game which your hands and spears obtain, so that God may know who fears Him in the unseen. Whoever transgresses after that, for him (there is) a painful punishment.

5:95.text: You who believe! Do not kill wild game when you are (in a state of) sanctity. Whoever of you kills it intentionally, (there is) a penalty equivalent (to) what he has killed from the livestock – as two just men among you will determine it – as an offering to reach the Ka‘ba. Or (there is) a penalty of the feeding of poor persons, or the equivalent of that in fasting, so that he may taste the consequence of his action. God pardons whatever is past, but whoever returns (to repeat his offense) – God will take vengeance on him. God is mighty, a taker of vengeance.

5:96.text: Permitted to you is the wild game of the sea and its food, as a provision for you and for the travelers. But forbidden to you is the wild game on the shore, as long as you are (in a state of) sanctity. Guard (yourselves) against God, the One to whom you will be gathered.

5:97.text: God has made the Ka‘ba – the Sacred House – an establishment for the people, and (also) the sacred month, the offering, and the ornaments. That is so that you may know that God knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and that God has knowledge of everything.

5:98.text: Know that God is harsh in retribution, and that God is forgiving, compassionate.

5:99.text: Nothing (depends) on the messenger except the delivery (of the message). God knows what you reveal and what you conceal.

5:100.text: Say: ‘The bad and the good are not equal, even though the abundance of bad may cause you to wonder.’ Guard (yourselves) against God – those (of you) with understanding! – so that you may prosper.

5:101.text: You who believe! Do not ask about anything which, if it were disclosed to you, would distress you. But if you do ask about it, when the Qur’ān is being sent down, it will be disclosed to you. God pardons it, (for) God is forgiving, forbearing.

5:102.text: A people before you asked about it, (and) then became disbelievers in it.

5:103.text: God has not appointed any baḥ īra or sā’iba or waṣīla or ḥ āmi, but those who disbelieve forge lies against God. Most of them do not understand.

5:104.text: When it is said to them, ‘Come to what God has sent down, and to the messenger,’ they say, ‘What we found our fathers doing is (good) enough for us.’ Even if their fathers had no knowledge and were not (rightly) guided?

5:105.text: You who believe! Look to yourselves. No one who goes astray can harm you, if you are (rightly) guided. To God is your return – all (of you) – and then He will inform you about what you have done.

5:106.text: You who believe! When death approaches one of you, the testimony among you at the time (of making) bequests will be (that of) two just men of you, or two others of (a people) other than you, if you strike forth on the earth and the smiting of death smites you. Detain them both after the prayer, and let them both swear by God, if you have your doubts (about them): ‘We will not sell it for a price, even if he happens to be a family member, and we will not conceal the testimony of God. Surely then we would indeed be among the sinners.’

5:107.text: If it is discovered that they both (were guilty of) sin, let two others take their place, from those who have a rightful claim against the two former (false witnesses), and let them both swear by God: ‘Certainly our testimony is truer than the testimony of the other two, and we have not transgressed. Surely then we would indeed be among the evildoers.’

5:108.text: That will make it more likely that they will give testimony directly, or (else) they will be afraid that (their) oaths will be turned back after they have sworn them. Guard (yourselves) against God and hear! God does not guide the people who are wicked.

5:109.text: On the Day when God gathers the messengers, He will say, ‘What response were you given?’ They will say, ‘We have no knowledge. Surely You – You are the Knower of the unseen.’

5:110.text: (Remember) when God said, ‘Jesus, son of Mary! Remember My blessing on you and on your mother, when I supported you with the holy spirit, (and) you spoke to the people (while you were still) in the cradle, and in adulthood. And when I taught you the Book and the wisdom, and the Torah and the Gospel. And when you created the form of a bird from clay by My permission, and you breathed into it, and it became a bird by My permission, and you healed the blind and the leper by My permission. And when you brought forth the dead by My permission, and when I restrained the Sons of Israel from (violence against) you. When you brought them the clear signs, those among them who had disbelieved said, “This is nothing but clear magic.”’

5:111.text: (Remember) when I inspired the disciples: ‘Believe in Me and in My messenger.’ They said, ‘We believe. Bear witness that we submit.’

5:112.text: And when the disciples said, ‘Jesus, son of Mary! Is your Lord able to send down on us a table from the sky?,’ he said, ‘Guard (yourselves) against God, if you are believers.’

5:113.text: They said, ‘We wish to eat from it and satisfy our hearts, so that we may know with certainty that you have spoken truthfully to us, and that we may be among the witnesses to it.’

5:114.text: Jesus, son of Mary, said, ‘God! Our Lord, send down on us a table from the sky, to be a festival for us – for the first of us and last of us – and a sign from You. Provide for us, (for) You are the best of providers.’

5:115.text: God said, ‘Surely I am going to send it down on you. Whoever of you disbelieves after that – surely I shall punish him (with) a punishment (as) I have not punished anyone among the worlds.’

5:116.text: (Remember) when God said, ‘Jesus, son of Mary! Did you say to the people, “Take me and my mother as two gods instead of God (alone)”?’ He said, ‘Glory to You! It is not for me to say what I have no right (to say). If I had said it, You would have known it. You know what is within me, but I do not know what is within You. Surely You – You are the Knower of the unseen.

5:117.text: I only said to them what You commanded me: “Serve God, my Lord and your Lord!” And I was a witness over them as long as I was among them. But when You took me, You became the Watcher over them. You are a Witness over everything.

5:118.text: If You punish them – surely they are Your servants. If You forgive them – surely You are the Mighty, the Wise.’

5:119.text: God said, ‘This is the Day when their truthfulness will benefit the truthful. For them (there are) Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain forever. God is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. That is the great triumph!’

5:120.text: To God (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is in them. He is powerful over everything.

51:5.text: Surely what you are promised is true indeed!

51:6.text: Surely the Judgment is indeed going to fall!

51:7.text: By the sky with all its tracks!

51:8.text: Surely you differ indeed in what you say!

51:9.text: Whoever is deluded about it is (really) deluded.

51:11.text: those who are in a flood (of confusion), heedless.

51:12.text: They ask, ‘When is the Day of Judgment?’

51:13.text: The Day when they will be tried over the Fire:

51:14.text: ‘Taste your trial! This is what you were seeking to hurry.’

51:15.text: Surely the ones who guard (themselves) will be in (the midst of) gardens and springs,

51:16.text: taking whatever their Lord has given them. Surely before (this) they were doers of good.

51:17.text: Little of the night would they sleep,

51:18.text: and in the mornings they would ask for forgiveness,

51:19.text: and in their wealth (there was) a due (portion) for the beggar and the outcast.

51:20.text: In the earth (there are) signs for the ones who are certain,

51:21.text: and (also) in yourselves. Do you not see?

51:22.text: And in the sky is your provision and what you are promised.

51:23.text: By the Lord of the sky and the earth! Surely it is true indeed – (even) as what you (are able to) speak.

51:24.text: Has the story come to you of the honored guests of Abraham?

51:25.text: When they entered upon him, and said, ‘Peace!,’ he said, ‘Peace! (You are) a people unknown (to me).’

51:26.text: So he turned to his family and brought a fattened calf,

51:27.text: and he placed it near them. He said, ‘Will you not eat?’

51:28.text: And he began to feel a fear of them. They said, ‘Do not fear!’ And they gave him good news of a knowing boy.

51:29.text: And then his wife came forward in a loud voice, and struck her face, and said, ‘An old woman, barren!’

51:30.text: They said, ‘So (it will be)! Your Lord has said. Surely He – He is the Wise, the Knowing.’

51:31.text: He said, ‘What is your business, you envoys?’

51:32.text: They said, ‘Surely we have been sent to a sinful people,

51:33.text: to send (down) on them stones of clay,

51:34.text: marked by your Lord for the wanton.’

51:35.text: We (would have) brought out any of the believers who were in it,

51:36.text: but We found in it only one house of those who had submitted.

51:37.text: And We left in it a sign for those who fear the painful punishment.

51:38.text: And (there is also a sign) in Moses: when We sent him to Pharaoh with clear authority.

51:39.text: But he turned away with his supporter(s), and said, ‘A magician or a man possessed!’

51:40.text: So We seized him and his forces, and tossed them into the sea, (for) he was to blame.

51:41.text: And (there is also a sign) in ‘Ād: when We sent upon them the desolating wind.

51:42.text: It left nothing it came upon, but made it like decayed (ruins).

51:43.text: And (there is also a sign) in Thamūd: when it was said to them, ‘Enjoy (yourselves) for a time!’

51:44.text: But they disdained the command of their Lord, and the thunderbolt took them while they were looking on,

51:45.text: and they were not able to stand, nor were they helped.

51:46.text: And the people of Noah before (them) – surely they were a wicked people.

51:47.text: The sky – We built it with (Our own) hands, and surely We were (its) extenders indeed.

51:48.text: And the earth – We spread it out. Excellent were the smoothers!

51:49.text: And We created pairs of everything, so that you might take heed.

51:50.text: So flee to God! Surely I am a clear warner for you from Him.

51:51.text: And do not set up another god with God. Surely I am a clear warner for you from Him.

51:52.text: (Even) so, not a messenger came to those who were before them, except they said, ‘A magician or a man possessed!’

51:53.text: Have they bequeathed it to each other? No! They are a people who transgress insolently.

51:54.text: So turn away from them, (for) you are not to be blamed,

51:55.text: but remind (them). Surely the Reminder will benefit the believers.

51:56.text: I did not create the jinn and humankind except to serve Me.

51:57.text: I do not desire any provision from them, nor do I desire that they should feed Me.

51:58.text: Surely God – He is the Provider, One full of power, the Firm.

51:59.text: Surely for the ones who do evil (there will be) a portion like the portion of their companions. Let them not seek to hurry Me!

51:60.text: Woe to those who disbelieve on account of their Day which they are promised!

13:1.text: Alif Lām Mīm Rā’. Those are the signs of the Book. What has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth, but most of the people do not believe.

13:2.text: (It is) God who raised up the heavens without pillars that you (can) see. Then He mounted the throne, and subjected the sun and the moon, each one running (its course) for an appointed time. He directs the (whole) affair. He makes the signs distinct, so that you may be certain of the meeting with your Lord.

13:3.text: He (it is) who stretched out the earth, and placed on it firm mountains and rivers. And of all the fruits He has placed on it two in pairs. He covers the day with the night. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who reflect.

13:4.text: On the earth (there are) parts neighboring (each other), and gardens of grapes, and (fields of) crops, and palm trees, (growing in) bunches and singly, (all) watered with one water. Yet We favor some of it over others in fruit. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who understand.

13:5.text: If you are amazed, their saying is amazing: ‘When we have turned to dust, shall we indeed (return) in a new creation?’ Those are the ones who have disbelieved in their Lord, and those – the chains will be on their necks – those are the companions of the Fire. There they will remain.

13:6.text: They seek to hurry you with the evil before the good, though the examples (of punishment) have already happened before them. Surely your Lord is indeed full of forgiveness for the people, despite their evildoing, yet surely your Lord is (also) indeed harsh in retribution.

13:7.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘If only a sign were sent down on him from his Lord.’ You are only a warner, and for every people (there is) a guide.

13:8.text: God knows what every female bears, and (in) what (way) the womb shrinks and (in) what (way) it swells. Everything with Him has (its) measure.

13:9.text: (He is) the Knower of the unseen and the seen, the Great, the Exalted.

13:10.text: (It is) the same (for) any of you who keeps (his) saying secret or who makes it public, and (for) anyone who hides in the night or goes about in the day.

13:11.text: For him (there is) a following, before him and behind him, who watch over him by the command of God. Surely God does not change what is in a people, until they change what is in themselves. And when God wishes evil for a people, (there is) no turning (it) back for them. They have no ally other than Him.

13:12.text: He (it is) who shows you the lightning – in fear and desire – and He produces the clouds heavy (with rain).

13:13.text: The thunder glorifies (Him) with His praise, and the angels (too) out of awe of Him. He sends the thunderbolts, and smites with it whomever He pleases. Yet they dispute about God, when He is mighty in power.

13:14.text: The true call (is) to Him, and those whom they call on instead of Him do not respond to them at all. (They are) only like someone stretching out his hands toward water, so that it may reach his mouth, but it does not reach it. The call of the disbelievers only goes astray.

13:15.text: Whatever is in the heavens and the earth prostrates itself before God, willingly or unwillingly, and (so do) their shadows in the morning and the evenings.

13:16.text: Say: ‘Who is Lord of the heavens and the earth?’ Say: ‘God.’ Say: ‘Have you taken allies other than Him? They do not have power to (cause) themselves benefit or harm.’ Say: ‘Are the blind and the sighted equal, or are the darkness and the light equal? Or have they set up associates for God who have created a creation like His, so that the creation is (all) alike to them?’ Say: ‘God is the Creator of everything. He is the One, the Supreme.’

13:17.text: He sends down water from the sky, and the wādīs flow, (each) in its measure, and the torrent carries a rising (layer of) froth (on top), like the froth that arises from what they heat in the fire, seeking some ornament or utensil. In this way God strikes (a parable of) the true and the false. As for the froth, it becomes worthless, but as for what benefits the people, it remains on the earth. In this way God strikes parables.

13:18.text: For those who respond to their Lord (there is) the good (reward), but those who do not respond to Him – (even) if they had what is on the earth – all (of it) – and as much again, they would indeed (try to) ransom (themselves) with it. Those – for them (there is) the evil reckoning. Their refuge is Gehenna – it is an evil bed!

13:19.text: Is the one who knows that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth, like the one who is blind? Only those with understanding take heed:

13:20.text: those who fulfill the covenant of God and do not break the compact,

13:21.text: and who join together what God has commanded to be joined with it, and fear their Lord, and are afraid of the evil reckoning,

13:22.text: and who are patient in seeking the face of their Lord, and observe the prayer, and contribute from what We have provided them, in secret and in open, and avert evil by means of the good. Those – for them (there is) the outcome of the Home:

13:23.text: Gardens of Eden which they (will) enter, and (also) those who were righteous among their fathers, and their wives, and their descendants. The angels (will) come in to them from every gate:

13:24.text: ‘Peace (be) upon you because you were patient! Excellent is the outcome of the Home!’

13:25.text: But those who break the covenant of God, after its ratification, and sever what God has commanded to be joined, and foment corruption on the earth, those – for them (there is) the curse, and for them (there is) the evil Home.

13:26.text: God extends (His) provision to whomever He pleases, and restricts (it). They gloat over this present life, but this present life is nothing but a (fleeting) enjoyment in (comparison to) the Hereafter.

13:27.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘If only a sign were sent down on him from his Lord?’ Say: ‘Surely God leads astray whomever He pleases and guides to Himself whoever turns (to Him).’

13:28.text: Those who believe and whose hearts are secure in the remembrance of God – surely hearts are secure in the remembrance of God –

13:29.text: those who believe and do righteous deeds – for them (there is) happiness and a good (place of) return.

13:30.text: In this way We have sent you among a community – before it (other) communities have already passed away – in order that you might recite to them what We have inspired you (with). Yet they disbelieve in the Merciful. Say: ‘He is my Lord – (there is) no god but Him. In Him I have put my trust, and to Him is my turning (in repentance).’

13:31.text: If (only there were) a Qur’ān by which the mountains were moved, or by which the earth were split open, or by which the dead were spoken to. No! The affair (belongs) to God altogether. Have those who believe no hope that, if God (so) pleased, He would indeed guide all the people? (As for) those who disbelieve, a striking will continue to smite them for what they have done, or it will descend near their home(s), until the promise of God comes. Surely God will not break the appointment.

13:32.text: Certainly messengers have been mocked before you, but I spared those who disbelieved. Then I seized them – and how was my retribution?

13:33.text: Is He who stands over every person for what he has earned [. . .]? They have set up associates for God. Say: ‘Name them! Or will you inform Him about what He does not know on the earth, or about what is said openly?’ No! Their scheming is made to appear enticing to those who disbelieve, and they are kept from the way. Whoever God leads astray has no guide.

13:34.text: For them (there is) punishment in this present life, yet the punishment of the Hereafter is indeed harder. They have no defender against God.

13:35.text: A parable of the Garden which is promised to the ones who guard (themselves): through it rivers flow, its fruit is unending, and (also) its shade. That is the outcome for the ones who guard (themselves), but the outcome for the disbelievers is the Fire.

13:36.text: Those to whom We have given the Book rejoice in what has been sent down to you, though some among the factions reject part of it. Say: ‘I am only commanded to serve God, and not to associate (anything) with Him. To Him do I call (you), and to Him is my return.’

13:37.text: In this way We have sent it down as an Arabic judgment. If indeed you follow their (vain) desires, after what has come to you of the knowledge, you will have no ally and no defender against God.

13:38.text: Certainly We sent messengers before you, and gave them wives and descendants, but it was not for any messenger to bring a sign, except by the permission of God. For every (period of) time (there is) a written decree.

13:39.text: God blots out whatever He pleases, and He confirms (whatever He pleases). With Him is the mother of the Book.

13:40.text: Whether We let you see part of what We promise them, or We take you, only (dependent) on you is the delivery (of the message). (Dependent) on Us is the reckoning.

13:41.text: Do they not see that We come to the land, pushing back its borders? God judges, (and there is) no revision of His judgment. He is quick at the reckoning.

13:42.text: Those who were before them schemed, but the scheme (belongs) to God altogether. He knows what every person earns, and soon the disbelievers will know to whom the outcome of the Home (belongs).

13:43.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘You are not an envoy.’ Say: ‘God is sufficient as a witness between me and you, and (so is) whoever has knowledge of the Book.’

88:1.text: Has the story of the Covering come to you?

88:2.text: (Some) faces that Day will be downcast,

88:4.text: They will burn in a scorching Fire.

88:5.text: They will be made to drink from a boiling spring.

88:6.text: They will have no food except dry thorns,

88:8.text: (Other) faces that Day will be blessed,

88:11.text: – where they will hear no frivolous talk,

88:17.text: Will they not look at the camels, how they were created,

88:18.text: and at the sky, how it was raised up,

88:19.text: and at the mountains, how they were constructed,

88:20.text: and at the earth, how it was spread flat?

88:21.text: So remind (them)! You are only a reminder.

88:22.text: You are not a record-keeper over them

88:23.text: – except for the one who turns away and disbelieves.

88:24.text: God will punish him with the greatest punishment.

88:25.text: Surely to Us is their return.

88:26.text: Then surely on Us (depends) their reckoning.

53:1.text: By the star when it falls!

53:2.text: Your companion has not gone astray, nor has he erred,

53:3.text: nor does he speak on a whim.

53:4.text: It is nothing but an inspiration inspired.

53:5.text: One harsh in power has taught him

53:6.text: – One full of strength! He stood poised,

53:7.text: while He was at the highest horizon,

53:8.text: then He drew near and came down.

53:9.text: He was two bow-lengths tall, or nearly.

53:10.text: And so He inspired His servant (with) what He inspired.

53:11.text: His heart did not lie about what it saw.

53:12.text: Will you dispute with him about what he sees?

53:13.text: Certainly he saw Him at a second descent,

53:14.text: by the Lote Tree of the Boundary,

53:15.text: near which is the Garden of the Refuge,

53:16.text: when (there) covered the Lote Tree what covered (it).

53:17.text: His sight did not turn aside, nor did it transgress.

53:18.text: Certainly he saw one of the greatest signs of his Lord.

53:19.text: Have you seen al-Lāt, and al-’Uzzā,

53:20.text: and Manāt, the third, the other?

53:21.text: Do you have male (offspring) while He has female?

53:22.text: Then that (would be) an unfair division!

53:23.text: They are only names which you have named, you and your fathers. God has not sent down any authority for it. They only follow conjecture and whatever they themselves desire – when certainly the guidance has come to them from their Lord.

53:24.text: Or will a person have whatever he longs for?

53:25.text: To God (belongs) the last and the first.

53:26.text: How many an angel there is in the heavens whose intercession is of no use at all, until God gives permission to whomever He pleases and approves.

53:27.text: Surely those who do not believe in the Hereafter indeed name the angels with the names of females.

53:28.text: But they have no knowledge about it. They only follow conjecture, and surely conjecture is of no use at all against the truth.

53:29.text: So turn away from the one who turns away from Our reminder and desires nothing but this present life.

53:30.text: That is the extent of their knowledge. Surely your Lord – He knows who goes astray from His way, and He knows who is (rightly) guided.

53:31.text: To God (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, so that He may repay those who do evil for what they have done, and repay those who do good with the best (reward).

53:32.text: Those who avoid great sins and immoral deeds, except for inadvertent ones – surely your Lord is embracing in forgiveness. He knows about you, when He produced you from the earth, and when you were (still) embryos in the bellies of your mothers. So do not claim purity for yourselves. He knows the one who guards (himself).

53:33.text: Do you see the one who turns away,

53:35.text: Is knowledge of the unseen in his keeping, and so he sees (it)?

53:36.text: Or has he not been informed about what is in the pages of Moses

53:37.text: and Abraham, who paid (his debt) in full?

53:38.text: – That no one bearing a burden bears the burden of another;

53:39.text: and that a person will receive only what he (himself) strives for;

53:40.text: and that his striving will be seen,

53:41.text: (and) then he will be paid for it with the fullest payment;

53:42.text: and that to your Lord is the (ultimate) goal;

53:43.text: and that He causes laughter and causes weeping;

53:44.text: and that He causes death and gives life;

53:45.text: and that He created pairs, the male and the female,

53:46.text: from a drop, when it is emitted;

53:47.text: and that the second growth (depends) on Him;

53:48.text: and that He enriches (people) and gives wealth;

53:49.text: and that He is the Lord of Sirius;

53:50.text: and that He destroyed ‘Ād of old,

53:51.text: and Thamūd – He did not spare (them)

53:52.text: – or the people of Noah before (them) – surely they were – they (were) evil and insolent transgressor(s) –

53:55.text: So which of the blessings of your Lord will you dispute?

53:56.text: This (man) is a warner, of the warners of old.

53:58.text: There is no one to remove it, other than God.

53:59.text: Are you amazed at this proclamation?

53:60.text: And do you laugh and not weep,

53:62.text: Prostrate yourselves before God and serve (Him)!

19:2.text: A remembrance of the mercy of your Lord (to) His servant Zachariah:

19:3.text: When he called on his Lord in secret,

19:4.text: he said, ‘My Lord, surely I – (my) bones have become weak within me, and (my) head is aflame (with) white (hair), but I have not been disappointed in calling on You (before), my Lord.

19:5.text: Surely I fear (who) the heirs (will be) after me, (for) my wife cannot conceive. So grant me from Yourself an heir,

19:6.text: (who) will inherit from me and inherit from the house of Jacob, and make him, my Lord, pleasing.’

19:7.text: ‘Zachariah! Surely We give you good news of a boy. His name (will be) John. We have not given (this) name to anyone before.’

19:8.text: He said, ‘My Lord, how shall I have a boy, when my wife cannot conceive and I have already reached extreme old age?’

19:9.text: He said, ‘So (it will be)! Your Lord has said, “It is easy for Me, seeing that I created you before, when you were nothing.”’

19:10.text: He said, ‘My Lord, give me a sign.’ He said, ‘Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three days exactly.’

19:11.text: So he came out to his people from the place of prayer and inspired them: ‘Glorify (Him) morning and evening.’

19:12.text: ‘John! Hold fast the Book!’ And We gave him the judgment as a child,

19:13.text: and grace from Us, and purity. He was one who guarded (himself)

19:14.text: and was dutiful to his parents. He was not a tyrant (or) disobedient.

19:15.text: Peace (be) upon him the day he was born, and the day he dies, and the day he is raised up alive.

19:16.text: And remember in the Book Mary: When she withdrew from her family to an eastern place,

19:17.text: and took a veil apart from them, We sent to her Our spirit, and it took for her the form of a human being exactly.

19:18.text: She said, ‘Surely I take refuge with the Merciful from you, if you are one who guards (yourself).’

19:19.text: He said, ‘I am only a messenger of your Lord (sent) to grant you a boy (who is) pure.’

19:20.text: She said, ‘How can I have a boy, when no human being has touched me, nor am I a prostitute?’

19:21.text: He said, ‘So (it will be)! Your Lord has said: “It is easy for Me. And (it is) to make him a sign to the people and a mercy from Us. It is a thing decreed.”’

19:22.text: So she conceived him, and withdrew with him to a place far away.

19:23.text: The pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of the date palm. She said, ‘I wish I had died before (this) and was completely forgotten!’

19:24.text: And then he called out to her from beneath her, ‘Do not sorrow! Your Lord has made a stream beneath you.

19:25.text: Shake the trunk of the date palm toward you, and it will drop on you fresh ripe (dates).

19:26.text: Eat and drink and be comforted. If you see any human being, say: “Surely I have vowed a fast to the Merciful, and so I shall not speak to any human today.”’

19:27.text: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said, ‘Mary! Certainly you have brought something strange.

19:28.text: Sister of Aaron! Your father was not a bad man, nor was your mother a prostitute.’

19:29.text: But she referred (them) to him. They said, ‘How shall we speak to one who is in the cradle, a (mere) child?’

19:30.text: He said, ‘Surely I am a servant of God. He has given me the Book and made me a prophet.

19:31.text: He has made me blessed wherever I am, and He has charged me with the prayer and the alms as long as I live,

19:32.text: and (to be) respectful to my mother. He has not made me a tyrant (or) miserable.

19:33.text: Peace (be) upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I am raised up alive.’

19:34.text: That was Jesus, son of Mary – a statement of the truth about which they are in doubt.

19:35.text: It is not for God to take any son. Glory to Him! When He decrees something, He simply says to it, ‘Be!’ and it is.

19:36.text: ‘Surely God is my Lord and your Lord, so serve Him! This is a straight path.’

19:37.text: But the factions differed among themselves. So woe to those who disbelieve on account of (their) witnessing a great Day!

19:38.text: How well they will hear on it! How well they will see on the Day when they come to Us! But the evildoers today are clearly astray.

19:39.text: Warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be decided while they are (still) oblivious and disbelieving.

19:40.text: Surely We shall inherit the earth, and whoever is on it, and to Us they will be returned.

19:41.text: And remember in the Book Abraham: Surely he was a man of truth, a prophet.

19:42.text: When he said to his father, ‘My father! Why do you serve what does not hear and does not see, and is of no use to you at all?

19:43.text: My father! Surely some knowledge has come to me that has not come to you. So follow me, and I shall guide you to an even path.

19:44.text: My father! Do not serve Satan! Surely Satan is disobedient to the Merciful.

19:45.text: My father! I fear that punishment from the Merciful will touch you, and you become an ally of Satan.’

19:46.text: He said, ‘Do you forsake my gods, Abraham? If indeed you do not stop, I shall indeed stone you. So leave me for a long time!’

19:47.text: He said, ‘Peace (be) upon you! I shall ask forgiveness for you from my Lord. Surely He has been gracious to me.

19:48.text: I shall withdraw from you and what you call on instead of God, and I shall call on my Lord. It may be that I shall not be disappointed in calling on my Lord.’

19:49.text: So when he had withdrawn from them and what they were serving instead of God, We granted him Isaac and Jacob, and each one We made a prophet.

19:50.text: We granted them some of Our mercy, and We assigned to them a true (and) high reputation.

19:51.text: And remember in the Book Moses: Surely he was devoted, and he was a messenger, a prophet.

19:52.text: We called him from the right side of the mountain, and We brought him near in conversation.

19:53.text: And We granted him some of Our mercy: his brother Aaron, a prophet.

19:54.text: And remember in the Book Ishmael: Surely he was true to the promise, and he was a messenger, a prophet.

19:55.text: He commanded his people with the prayer and the alms, and he was pleasing before his Lord.

19:56.text: And remember in the Book Idrīs: Surely he was a man of truth, a prophet.

19:57.text: We raised him up to a high place.

19:58.text: Those were the ones whom God has blessed among the prophets from the descendants of Adam, and from those We carried with Noah, and from the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and from those whom We have guided and chosen. When the signs of the Merciful were recited to them, they fell down in prostration and weeping.

19:59.text: But after them came successors (who) neglected the prayer and followed (their own) desires. Soon they will meet error

19:60.text: – except for the one who turns (in repentance), and believes, and does righteousness. Those will enter the Garden, and they will not be done evil at all

19:61.text: – Gardens of Eden, which the Merciful has promised to His servants in the unseen. Surely He – His promise will come to pass.

19:62.text: There they will not hear any frivolous talk, only ‘Peace!’ And there they will have their provision morning and evening.

19:63.text: That is the Garden which We give as an inheritance to those of Our servants who guard (themselves).

19:64.text: ‘We only come down by the command of your Lord. To Him (belongs) whatever is before us and whatever is behind us, and whatever is between that. Your Lord is not forgetful

19:65.text: – Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them. So serve Him and be patient in His service! Do you know (another) name for Him?’

19:66.text: The human says, ‘When I am dead, shall I indeed be brought forth alive?’

19:67.text: Does the human not remember that We created him before, when he was nothing?

19:68.text: By your Lord! We shall indeed gather them together, and (also) the satans. Then We shall indeed bring them around Gehenna (on) bended knees.

19:69.text: Then We shall indeed draw out from each party those of them (who are) most (in) rebellion against the Merciful.

19:70.text: Then indeed We shall know those who most deserve burning with it.

19:71.text: (There is) not one of you (who is) not coming to it – (that) is for your Lord an inevitability decreed.

19:72.text: Then We shall rescue the ones who guarded (themselves), and leave the evildoers in it (on) bended knees.

19:73.text: When Our signs are recited to them as clear signs, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, ‘Which of the two groups is better in status and better as a cohort?’

19:74.text: But how many a generation We have destroyed before them! They were better in wealth and outward appearance.

19:75.text: Say: ‘Whoever is astray, let the Merciful prolong his life until, when they see what they are promised – either the punishment or the Hour – they will know who is worse in position and weaker in forces.’

19:76.text: But God will increase in guidance those who are guided. And the things that endure – righteous deeds – are better with your Lord, and better in return.

19:77.text: Have you seen the one who disbelieves in Our signs, and says, ‘I shall indeed be given wealth and children’?

19:78.text: Has he looked into the unseen, or has he taken a covenant with the Merciful?

19:79.text: By no means! We shall write down what he says, and We shall increase the punishment for him.

19:80.text: We shall inherit from him what he says, and he will come to Us alone.

19:81.text: They have taken gods other than God, so that they might be a (source of) honor for them.

19:82.text: By no means! They will deny their service, and they will be opposed to them.

19:83.text: Do you not see that We have sent the satans against the disbelievers to incite them.

19:84.text: So do not be in a hurry with them. We are only counting for them a (certain) number (of years).

19:85.text: On the Day when We shall gather to the Merciful the ones who guarded (themselves) like a delegation,

19:86.text: and drive the sinners into Gehenna like a herd,

19:87.text: they will have no power of intercession, except for the one who has made a covenant with the Merciful.

19:88.text: They say, ‘The Merciful has taken a son.’

19:89.text: Certainly you have put forth something abhorrent!

19:90.text: The heavens are nearly torn apart because of it, and the earth split open, and the mountains collapse in pieces

19:91.text: – that they should attribute to the Merciful a son,

19:92.text: when it is not fitting for the Merciful to take a son.

19:93.text: (There is) no one in the heavens and the earth who comes to the Merciful except as a servant.

19:94.text: Certainly He has counted them and numbered them exactly.

19:95.text: Each one of them will come to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.

19:96.text: Surely those who believe and do righteous deeds – to them the Merciful will show (His) love.

19:97.text: Surely We have made it easy in your language, so that you may give good news by means of it to the ones who guard (themselves), and warn by means of it a contentious people.

19:98.text: But how many a generation We have destroyed before them! Do you see a single one of them, or hear (even) a whisper of them?

14:1.text: Alif Lām Rā’. A Book – We have sent it down to you, so that you may bring the people out of the darkness to the light, by the permission of their Lord, to the path of the Mighty, the Praiseworthy.

14:2.text: God who – to Him (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Woe to the disbelievers because of a harsh punishment!

14:3.text: Those who love this present life more than the Hereafter, and keep (people) from the way of God, and desire (to make) it crooked – those are far astray!

14:4.text: We have not sent any messenger except in the language of his people, so that he might make (things) clear to them. Then God leads astray whomever He pleases and guides whomever He pleases. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

14:5.text: Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs: ‘Bring your people out of the darkness to the light, and remind them of the days of God.’ Surely in that are signs indeed for every patient (and) thankful one.

14:6.text: (Remember) when Moses said to his people, ‘Remember the blessing of God on you, when He rescued you from the house of Pharaoh. They were inflicting on you the evil punishment, and slaughtering your sons and sparing your women. In that was a great test from your Lord.’

14:7.text: And (remember) when your Lord proclaimed, ‘If indeed you are thankful, I shall indeed give you more, but if indeed you are ungrateful, surely My punishment is harsh indeed.’

14:8.text: And Moses said, ‘(Even) if you disbelieve, you and whoever is on the earth all together – surely God is indeed wealthy, praiseworthy.’

14:9.text: Has no story come to you of those who were before you: the people of Noah, ‘Ād, Thamūd, and those who (came) after them? No one knows them but God. Their messengers brought them the clear signs, but they put their hands in their mouths, and said, ‘Surely We disbelieve in what you are sent with, and surely we are in grave doubt indeed about what you call us to.’

14:10.text: Their messengers said, ‘(Is there any) doubt about God, Creator of the heavens and the earth? He calls you so that He may forgive you of your sins and spare you for an appointed time.’ They said, ‘You are nothing but human beings like us. You want to keep us from what our fathers have served. Bring us some clear authority (for this).’

14:11.text: Their messengers said to them, ‘We are nothing but human beings like you, but God bestows favor on whomever He pleases of His servants. It is not for us to bring you any authority, except by the permission of God. In God let the believers put their trust.

14:12.text: Why should we not put our trust in God, when He has guided us to our ways. Indeed we shall patiently endure whatever harm you do us. In God let the trusting put their trust.’

14:13.text: Those who had disbelieved said to their messengers, ‘We shall indeed expel you from our land, or (else) you will return to our creed.’ Then their Lord inspired them: ‘We shall indeed destroy the evildoers

14:14.text: and cause you to inhabit the land after them. That is for whoever fears My position and fears My promise.’

14:15.text: They asked for victory, and every stubborn tyrant despaired.

14:16.text: Behind him is Gehenna, and he is given a drink of filthy water.

14:17.text: He gulps it but can hardly swallow it. Death comes upon him from every side, yet he does not die, and behind him is a stern punishment.

14:18.text: A parable of those who disbelieve in their Lord: their deeds are like ashes, on which the wind blows strongly on a stormy day. They have no power over anything of what they have earned. That is straying far.

14:19.text: Do you not see that God created the heavens and the earth in truth? If He (so) pleases, He will do away with you and bring a new creation.

14:20.text: That is no great matter for God.
