14:21.text: They will come forth to God all together, and the weak will say to those who were arrogant, ‘Surely we were your followers, so are you going relieve us (now) of any of the punishment of God?’ They will say, ‘If God had guided us, we would indeed have guided you. (It is) the same for us whether we become distressed or are patient. (There is) no place of escape for us.’

14:22.text: And Satan will say, when the matter is decided, ‘Surely God promised you a true promise, and I (too) promised you, (but) then I broke (my promise) to you. I had no authority over you, except that I called you and you responded to me. So do not blame me, but blame yourselves. I am not going to help you, nor are you going to help me. Surely I disbelieved in your associating me (with God) before.’ Surely the evildoers – for them (there is) a painful punishment.

14:23.text: But those who believe and do righteous deeds – they are made to enter Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain by the permission of their Lord. Their greeting there is: ‘Peace!’

14:24.text: Do you not see how God has struck a parable? A good word is like a good tree. Its root is firm and its branch (reaches) to the sky,

14:25.text: giving its fruit every season by the permission of its Lord. God strikes parables for the people so that they may take heed.

14:26.text: But the parable of a bad word is like a bad tree, uprooted from the earth, without any support for it.

14:27.text: God makes firm those who believe by the firm word in this present life and in the Hereafter. But God leads astray the evildoers. God does whatever He pleases.

14:28.text: Do you not see those who have exchanged the blessing of God for disbelief, and caused their people to descend to the home of ruin –

14:29.text: Gehenna – where they will burn? It is an evil dwelling place!

14:30.text: They have set up rivals to God in order to lead (people) astray from His way. Say: ‘Enjoy (yourselves)! Surely your destination is to the Fire!’

14:31.text: Say to My servants who believe (that) they should observe the prayer, and contribute from what We have provided them, in secret and in open, before a Day comes when (there will be) no bargaining and no friendship.

14:32.text: (It is) God who created the heavens and the earth, and sent down water from the sky, and brought forth fruits by means of it as a provision for you. And He subjected the ship to you, to run on the sea by His command, and subjected the rivers to you.

14:33.text: And He subjected the sun and the moon to you, both being constant (in their courses), and subjected the night and the day to you.

14:34.text: He has given you some of all that you have asked Him for. If you (try to) number God’s blessing, you will not (be able to) count it. Surely the human is indeed an evildoer (and) ungrateful!

14:35.text: (Remember) when Abraham said, ‘My Lord, make this land secure, and keep me and my sons away from serving the idols.

14:36.text: My Lord, surely they have led many of the people astray. Whoever follows me, surely he belongs to me, and whoever disobeys me – surely You are forgiving, compassionate.

14:37.text: Our Lord, I have settled some of my descendants in a wādī without any cultivation, near your Sacred House, Our Lord, in order that they may observe the prayer. So cause the hearts of some of the people to yearn toward them, and provide them with fruits, so that they may be thankful.

14:38.text: Our Lord, You know what we hide and what we speak aloud. Nothing is hidden from God (either) on the earth or in the sky.

14:39.text: Praise (be) to God, who has granted me Ishmael and Isaac in (my) old age. Surely my Lord is indeed the Hearer of the call.

14:40.text: My Lord, make me observant of the prayer, and (also) some of my descendants, our Lord, and accept my call.

14:41.text: Our Lord, forgive me, and my parents, and the believers, on the Day when the reckoning takes place.’

14:42.text: Do not think (that) God is oblivious of what the evildoers do. He is only sparing them for a Day when (their) eyes will stare,

14:43.text: (as they go) rushing with their heads raised up, unable to turn back their gaze, and their hearts empty.

14:44.text: Warn the people (of) a Day when the punishment will come to them, and those who have done evil will say, ‘Our Lord, spare us for a time near (at hand)! We shall respond to Your call and follow the messengers.’ ‘Did you not swear before that (there would be) no end for you?

14:45.text: You dwell in the (same) dwelling places as those who did themselves evil, and it became clear to you how We dealt with them, and (how) We struck parables for you.

14:46.text: They schemed their scheme, but their scheme was known to God, even though their scheme was (such as) to remove the mountains by it.’

14:47.text: Do not think (that) God is going to break His promise to His messengers. Surely God is mighty, a taker of vengeance.

14:48.text: On the Day when the earth will be changed (into something) other (than) the earth, and the heavens (as well), and they will go forth to God, the One, the Supreme,

14:49.text: and you will see the sinners on that Day bound together in chains,

14:50.text: their clothing (made) of pitch, and the Fire will cover their faces,

14:51.text: so that God may repay everyone for what he has earned. Surely God is quick at the reckoning.

14:52.text: This is a delivery for the people, and (it is delivered) so that they may be warned by means of it, and that they may know that He is one God, and that those with understanding may take heed.

75:1.text: I swear by the Day of Resurrection!

75:2.text: And I swear by the accusing self!

75:3.text: Does the human think that We shall not gather his bones?

75:4.text: Yes indeed! We are (even) able to fashion his fingers (again).

75:5.text: Yet the human (still) wants to know what is in store for him.

75:6.text: He asks, ‘When is the Day of Resurrection?’

75:9.text: and the sun and moon are brought together,

75:10.text: on that Day the human will say, ‘Where is the escape?’

75:11.text: By no means! (There is) no refuge!

75:12.text: The (only) dwelling place on that Day will be to your Lord.

75:13.text: On that Day the human will be informed about what has he sent forward and kept back.

75:14.text: No! The human will be a clear proof against himself,

75:15.text: even though he offers his excuses.

75:16.text: Do not move your tongue with it to hurry it.

75:17.text: Surely on Us (depends) its collection and its recitation.

75:18.text: When We recite it, follow its recitation.

75:19.text: Then surely on Us (depends) its explanation.

75:20.text: By no means! No! You love this fleeting (world),

75:22.text: (Some) faces that Day will be radiant,

75:24.text: and (other) faces that Day will be scowling,

75:25.text: thinking that a calamity will be visited on them.

75:26.text: By no means! When it reaches the collarbones,

75:27.text: and it is said, ‘Who will carry (him) off?,’

75:28.text: and he thinks that the parting has come,

75:29.text: when leg is tangled with leg,

75:30.text: the (only) drive on that Day will be to your Lord.

75:31.text: (For) he did not affirm (it), nor did he pray,

75:32.text: but he called (it) a lie and turned away.

75:33.text: Then he went to his household with an arrogant swagger.

75:35.text: Once again, nearer to you and nearer!

75:36.text: Does the human think that he will be left to go about at will?

75:37.text: Was he not a drop of semen emitted?

75:38.text: Then he was a clot, and He created and fashioned (him),

75:39.text: and made from it the two sexes, the male and the female.

75:40.text: Is that One not able to give the dead life?

9:1.text: A renunciation from God and His messenger to those of the idolaters with whom you have made a treaty:

9:2.text: ‘Move about (freely) on the earth for four months, and know that you cannot escape God, and that God will disgrace the disbelievers.’

9:3.text: And a proclamation from God and His messenger to the people on the day of the great pilgrimage: ‘God renounces the idolaters, and (so does) His messenger. If you turn (in repentance), it will be better for you, but if you turn away, know that you cannot escape God.’ Give those who disbelieve news of a painful punishment,

9:4.text: except those of the idolaters with whom you have made a treaty, (and who) since then have not failed you in anything and have not supported anyone against you. Fulfill their treaty with them until their term. Surely God loves the ones who guard (themselves).

9:5.text: Then, when the sacred months have passed, kill the idolaters wherever you find them, and seize them, and besiege them, and sit (in wait) for them at every place of ambush. If they turn (in repentance), and observe the prayer and give the alms, let them go their way. Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

9:6.text: If one of the idolaters seeks your protection, grant him protection until he hears the word of God. Then convey him to his place of safety – that is because they are a people who have no knowledge.

9:7.text: How can the idolaters have a treaty with God and with His messenger, except those with whom you have made a treaty at the Sacred Mosque? So long as they go straight with you, go straight with them. Surely God loves the ones who guard (themselves).

9:8.text: How (can there be a treaty with them)? If they were to prevail over you, they would not respect any bond or agreement with you. They please you with their mouths, but their hearts refuse (you). Most of them are wicked.

9:9.text: They have sold the signs of God for a small price, and kept (people) from His way. Surely evil is what they have done.

9:10.text: They do not respect any bond or agreement with a believer. Those – they are the transgressors.

9:11.text: If they turn (in repentance), and observe the prayer and give the alms, (they are) your brothers in the religion. We make the signs distinct for a people who know.

9:12.text: But if they break their oaths, after their treaty, and vilify your religion, fight the leaders of disbelief – surely they have no (binding) oaths – so that they stop (fighting).

9:13.text: Will you not fight (against) a people who have broken their oaths, and are determined to expel the messenger, and started (to attack) you the first time? Are you afraid of them? God – (it is more) right that you should fear Him, if you are believers.

9:14.text: Fight them! God will punish them by your hands, and disgrace them, and help you against them, and heal the hearts of a people who believe,

9:15.text: and take away (all) rage from their hearts. God turns (in forgiveness) to whomever He pleases. God is knowing, wise.

9:16.text: Or did you think that you would be left (in peace), when God did not (yet) know those of you who have struggled, and have not taken any ally other than God and His messenger and the believers? God is aware of what you do.

9:17.text: It is not for the idolaters to inhabit the mosques of God, (while) bearing witness against themselves of disbelief. Those – their deeds have come to nothing, and in the Fire they will remain.

9:18.text: Only he will inhabit the mosques of God who believes in God and the Last Day, and observes the prayer and gives the alms, and does not fear (anyone) but God. It may be that those – they will be among the (rightly) guided ones.

9:19.text: Do you make the giving of water to the pilgrims and the inhabiting of the Sacred Mosque like the one who believes in God and the Last Day and struggles in the way of God? They are not equal with God, and God does not guide the people who are evildoers.

9:20.text: Those who have believed, and emigrated, and struggled in the way of God with their wealth and their lives are higher in rank with God. Those – they are the triumphant.

9:21.text: Their Lord gives them good news of mercy from Himself, and approval, and (there are) Gardens for them in which (there is) lasting bliss,

9:22.text: there to remain forever. Surely God – with Him is a great reward.

9:23.text: You who believe! Do not take your fathers and your brothers as allies, if they prefer disbelief over belief. Whoever among you takes them as allies, those – they are the evildoers.

9:24.text: Say: ‘If your fathers, and your sons, and your brothers, and your wives, and your clan, and wealth you have acquired, and (business) transaction(s) you fear (may) fall off, and dwellings you take pleasure in are dearer to you than God and His messenger, and struggling in His way, then wait until God brings His command. God does not guide the people who are wicked.’

9:25.text: Certainly God has helped you on many (battle)fields, and on the day of Ḥunayn, when your multitude impressed you but was of no use to you at all, and the earth was too narrow for you, despite its breadth, and you turned back, retreating.

9:26.text: Then God sent down His Sakīna on His messenger and on the believers, and He sent down forces you did not see, and punished those who disbelieved – that was the payment of the disbelievers.

9:27.text: Then, after that, God turns (in forgiveness) to whomever He pleases. God is forgiving, compassionate.

9:28.text: You who believe! Only the idolaters are impure, so let them go near the Sacred Mosque after this, their (final) year. If you fear poverty, God will enrich you from His favor, if He pleases. Surely God is knowing, wise.

9:29.text: Fight those who do not believe in God or the Last Day, and do not forbid what God and His messenger have forbidden, and do not practice the religion of truth – from among those who have been given the Book – until they pay tribute out of hand, and they are disgraced.

9:30.text: The Jews say, ‘Ezra is the son of God,’ and the Christians say, ‘The Messiah is the son of God.’ That is their saying with their mouths. They imitate the saying of those who disbelieved before (them). (May) God fight them. How are they (so) deluded?

9:31.text: They have taken their teachers and their monks as Lords instead of God, and (also) the Messiah, son of Mary, when they were only commanded to serve one God. (There is) no god but Him. Glory to Him above what they associate!

9:32.text: They want to extinguish the light of God with their mouths, but God refuses (to do anything) except perfect His light, even though the disbelievers dislike (it).

9:33.text: He (it is) who has sent His messenger with the guidance and the religion of truth, so that He may cause it to prevail over religion – all of it – even though the idolaters dislike (it).

9:34.text: You who believe! Surely many of the teachers and the monks consume the wealth of the people by means of falsehood, and keep (people) from the way of God. Those who hoard the gold and the silver, and do not spend it in the way of God – give them news of a painful punishment.

9:35.text: On the Day when it will be heated in the Fire of Gehenna, and their foreheads and their sides and their backs will be branded with it: ‘This is what you hoarded for yourselves, so taste what you have hoarded!’

9:36.text: Surely the number of months with God is twelve, (written) in the Book of God on the day when He created the heavens and the earth. Of them, four are sacred. That is the right religion. Do not do yourselves evil during them, but fight (against) the idolaters all together, as they fight you all together, and know that God is with the ones who guard (themselves).

9:37.text: The postponement is an increase of disbelief by which those who disbelieve go astray. They make it profane (one) year, and make it sacred (another) year, to adjust the number (of months) God has made sacred, and to profane what God has made sacred. The evil of their deeds is made to appear enticing to them, but God does not guide the people who are disbelievers.

9:38.text: You who believe! What is (the matter) with you? When it is said to you, ‘Go forth in the way of God,’ you slump to the earth. Are you pleased with this present life, rather than the Hereafter? Yet what enjoyment (there is) of this present life is only a little (thing) in (comparison to) the Hereafter.

9:39.text: If you do not go forth, He will punish you (with) a painful punishment, and exchange a people other than you. You will not harm Him at all, (for) God is powerful over everything.

9:40.text: If you do not help him, God has already helped him, when those who disbelieved expelled him, the second of two: when the two were in the cave, (and) when he said to his companion, ‘Do not sorrow, (for) surely God is with us.’ Then God sent down His Sakīna on him, and supported him with forces which you did not see, and made the word of those who disbelieved the lowest, while the word of God is the highest. God is mighty, wise.

9:41.text: Go forth, light and heavy, and struggle in the way of God with your wealth and your lives. That is better for you, if (only) you knew.

9:42.text: If it were (some fleeting) gain near (at hand), and an easy journey, they would indeed have followed you, but the distance is (too) far for them. (Still) they will swear by God, ‘If we had been able, we would indeed have gone out with you.’ (In this way) they destroy themselves. But God knows: ‘Surely they are liars indeed!

9:43.text: God pardon you! Why did you give them permission, before (it was) clear to you (who were) those who spoke the truth, and (before) you knew (who) the liars (were)?

9:44.text: Those who believe in God and the Last Day do not ask your permission, so that they may struggle with their wealth and their lives. God knows the ones who guard (themselves).

9:45.text: Only those who do not believe in God and the Last Day ask your permission, and their hearts are filled with doubt, and they waver in their doubt.

9:46.text: If they had intended to go forth, they would indeed have made some preparation for it. But God disliked their rising up, so He held them back, and it was said (to them), ‘Sit (at home) with the ones who sit.’

9:47.text: If they had gone forth with you, they would have added to you nothing but ruin, and would indeed have run around in your midst, seeking to stir up trouble among you – and some of you would have listened to them. But God knows the evildoers.

9:48.text: Certainly they sought to stir up trouble before (this), and upset matters for you, until the truth came and the command of God prevailed, even though they were unwilling.

9:49.text: (There is) one of them who says, ‘Give me permission, and do not tempt me.’ Is it not (a fact) that they have (already) fallen into temptation? Surely Gehenna will indeed encompass the disbelievers.

9:50.text: If some good smites you, it distresses them, but if some smiting smites you, they say, ‘We took hold of our affair before (this),’ and they turn away, gloating.

9:51.text: Say: ‘Nothing smites us except what God has prescribed for us. He is our Protector, and in God let the believers put (their) trust.’

9:52.text: Say: ‘Do you wait for anything in our case except for one of the two good (rewards)? But we are waiting in your case for God to smite you with punishment from Him or at our hands. (Just) wait! Surely we shall be waiting with you.’

9:53.text: Say: ‘Contribute willingly or unwillingly, it will not be accepted from you. Surely you are a wicked people.’

9:54.text: Nothing prevents their contributions being accepted from them, except that they have not believed in God and in His messenger, and they do not come to the prayer, except in a lazy fashion, and they do not contribute, except unwillingly.

9:55.text: Do not let their wealth and their children impress you. God only intends to punish them by means of it in this present life, and (that) they themselves should pass away while they are disbelievers.

9:56.text: They swear by God that they indeed belong to you, but they do not belong to you. They are a people who are afraid.

9:57.text: If they could find a shelter, or caves, or a place to hide, they would indeed resort to it and rush off.

9:58.text: (There is) one of them who finds fault with you concerning freewill offerings. Yet if they are given (a share) of it, they are pleased, but if they are not given (a share) of it, they are angry.

9:59.text: If (only) they had been pleased with what God gave them, and His messenger, and had said, ‘God is enough for us. God will give us (more) of His favor, and (so will) His messenger. Surely we turn in hope to God.’

9:60.text: Freewill offerings are only for the poor and the needy, and the ones who collect it, and the ones whose hearts are united, and for the (freeing of) slaves, and the (relief of) debtors, and for the way of God, and the traveler. (That is) an obligation from God. God is knowing, wise.

9:61.text: (There are) some of them who hurt the prophet, and say, ‘He is all ears!’ Say: ‘Good ears for you! He believes in God and believes in the believers, and (he is) a mercy for those of you who believe. But those who hurt the messenger of God – for them (there is) a painful punishment.’

9:62.text: They swear to you by God in order to please you, but God and His messenger – (it is more) right that they should please Him, if they are believers.

9:63.text: Do they not know that the one who opposes God and His messenger – surely for him (there is) the Fire of Gehenna, there to remain? That is the great humiliation!

9:64.text: The hypocrites are afraid that a sūra will be sent down against them, informing them of what is in their hearts. Say: ‘Go on mocking! Surely God will bring forth what you are afraid of.’

9:65.text: If indeed you ask them, they will indeed say, ‘We were only bantering and jesting.’ Say: ‘Were you mocking God, and His signs, and His messenger?

9:66.text: Do not make excuses! You have disbelieved after your believing. If We pardon (one) contingent of you, We will punish (another) contingent because they have been sinners.’

9:67.text: The hypocrite men and the hypocrite women are all alike. They command wrong and forbid right, and they withdraw their hands. They have forgotten God, so He has forgotten them. Surely the hypocrites – they are the wicked.

9:68.text: God has promised the hypocrite men and the hypocrite women, and the disbelievers, the Fire of Gehenna, there to remain. It will be enough for them. God has cursed them, and for them (there is) a lasting punishment.

9:69.text: Like those before you: they were stronger than you in power and (had) more wealth and children, and they took enjoyment in their share. You have taken enjoyment in your share, as those before you took enjoyment in their share. You have bantered as they bantered. Those – their deeds have come to nothing in this world and the Hereafter. And those – they are the losers.

9:70.text: Has no story come to them of those who were before them: the people of Noah, and ‘Ād, and Thamūd, and the people of Abraham, and the companions of Midian, and the overturned (cities)? Their messengers brought them the clear signs. God was not one to do them evil, but they did themselves evil.

9:71.text: The believing men and the believing women are allies of each other. They command right and forbid wrong, they observe the prayer and give the alms, and they obey God and His messenger. Those – God will have compassion on them. God is mighty, wise.

9:72.text: God has promised the believing men and the believing women Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain, and good dwellings in Gardens of Eden – but the approval of God is greater. That is the great triumph!

9:73.text: Prophet! Struggle against the disbelievers and the hypocrites, and be stern with them. Their refuge is Gehenna – and it is an evil destination!

9:74.text: They swear by God that they did not say (it), but certainly they have said the word of disbelief, and have disbelieved after their submission. They determined (to do) what they did not attain, and they took vengeance for no other reason than that God and His messenger had enriched them from His favor. If they turn (in repentance) it will be better for them, but if they turn away, God will punish them (with) a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter. They have no ally and no helper on the earth.

9:75.text: (There is) one of them who has made a covenant with God: ‘If He gives us some of His favor, we shall indeed make contributions and indeed be among the righteous.’

9:76.text: But when He gave them some of His favor, they were stingy with it and turned away in aversion.

9:77.text: So He placed hypocrisy in their hearts until the Day when they meet Him, because they broke (with) God (concerning) what they promised Him, and because they have lied.

9:78.text: Do they not know that God knows their secret and their secret talk, and that God is the Knower of the unseen?

9:79.text: Those who find fault with those of the believers who contribute voluntarily, and those who ridicule those (believers) who do not find (anything to offer) but their effort – God has ridiculed them, and for them (there is) a painful punishment.

9:80.text: Ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them. (Even) if you ask forgiveness for them seventy times, God will not forgive them. That is because they have disbelieved in God and His messenger. God does not guide the wicked.

9:81.text: The ones who stayed behind gloated over their sitting (at home) behind the messenger of God, and disliked (it) that they should (have to) struggle with their wealth and their lives in the way of God. They said, ‘Do not go forth in the heat.’ Say: ‘The Fire of Gehenna is hotter!’ If (only) they understood!

9:82.text: So let them laugh a little (now) and weep a lot in payment for what they have earned.

9:83.text: If God brings you back to some contingent of them, and they ask your permission to go forth, say: ‘You will never go forth with me, nor will you ever fight any enemy with me. Surely you were pleased with sitting (at home) the first time, so sit with the ones who stay behind.’

9:84.text: Never pray over anyone of them who has died, nor stand over his grave. Surely they disbelieved in God and His messenger, and died while they were wicked.

9:85.text: Do not let their wealth and their children impress you. God only intends to punish them by means of it in this world, and (that) they themselves should pass away while they are disbelievers.

9:86.text: When a sūra is sent down (stating): ‘Believe in God, and struggle alongside His messenger,’ the wealthy among them ask your permission, and say, ‘Let us be with the ones who sit (at home).’

9:87.text: They are pleased to be with the ones who stay behind, and a seal is set on their hearts, and so they do not understand.

9:88.text: But the messenger and those who believe with him have struggled with their wealth and their lives. And those – for them (there are) the good things, and those – they are the ones who prosper.

9:89.text: God has prepared for them Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain. That is the great triumph!

9:90.text: The excuse-makers among the Arabs came to get permission for themselves, and those who lied to God and His messenger sat (at home). A painful punishment will smite those of them who disbelieve.

9:91.text: There is no blame on the weak or on the sick or on those who find nothing to contribute, if they are true to God and His messenger. (There is) no way against the doers of good – God is forgiving, compassionate –

9:92.text: nor against those (to) whom, when they came to you for you to give them mounts, you said, ‘I cannot find a mount for you.’ They turned away, and their eyes were full of the tears of sorrow, because they did not find anything to contribute.

9:93.text: The way is only open against those who ask your permission when they are rich. They are pleased to be with the ones who stay behind. God has set a seal on their hearts, but they do not know (it).

9:94.text: They will make excuses to you when you return to them. Say: ‘Do not make excuses, (for) we do not believe you. God has already informed us of the reports about you. God will see your deed, and (so will) His messenger. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the seen, and He will inform you about what you have done.’

9:95.text: They will swear to you by God, when you turn back to them, that you may turn away from them. Turn away from them, (for) surely they are an abomination. Their refuge is Gehenna – a payment for what they have earned.

9:96.text: They will swear to you in order that you may be pleased with them. Yet (even) if you are pleased with them, surely God will not be pleased with the people who are wicked.

9:97.text: The Arabs are (even) stronger in disbelief and hypocrisy, and more likely not to know the limits of what God has sent down on His messenger. God is knowing, wise.

9:98.text: Among the Arabs (there is) one who regards what he contributes as a fine, and waits for the wheels (of fortune to turn) against you. The wheel of evil (will turn) against them! God is hearing, knowing.

9:99.text: Among the Arabs (there is) one who believes in God and the Last Day, and takes what he contributes as a (means of) drawing near to God, and (likewise) the prayers of the messenger. Is it not a fact that it is a (means of) drawing near for them? God will cause them to enter into His mercy. God is forgiving, compassionate.

9:100.text: The foremost – the first of the emigrants and the helpers, and those who have followed them in doing good – God is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. He has prepared for them Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain forever. That is the great triumph!

9:101.text: Some of the Arabs who (dwell) around you are hypocrites, and some of the people of the city (also). They have become obstinate in (their) hypocrisy. You do not know them, (but) We know them, (and) We shall punish them twice. Then they will be returned to a great punishment.

9:102.text: Others have acknowledged their sins. They have mixed a righteous deed and another (that is) evil. It may be that God will turn (in forgiveness) to them. Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

9:103.text: Take from their wealth a contribution, to cleanse them and purify them by means of it, and pray over them. Surely your prayers are a rest for them. God is hearing, knowing.

9:104.text: Do they not know that God – He accepts repentance from His servants and takes (their) contributions, and that God – He is the One who turns (in forgiveness), the Compassionate?

9:105.text: Say: ‘Work! God will see your deed, and (so will) His messenger and the believers, and you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the seen, and He will inform you about what you have done.’

9:106.text: (There are) others (who will be) deferred to the command of God, (to see) whether He will punish them or turn (in forgiveness) to them. God is knowing, wise.

9:107.text: Those who have taken a mosque (to cause) harm and disbelief and division among the believers, and (to provide) a place of ambush for those who fought against God and His messenger before – they will indeed swear, ‘We wanted nothing but good!’ But God bears witness: ‘Surely they are liars indeed!’

9:108.text: Never stand in it! A mosque founded from the first day on the (obligation of) guarding (oneself) is indeed (more) worthy for you to stand in. In it (there are) are men who love to purify themselves, and God loves the ones who purify themselves.

9:109.text: So is someone who founded his building on (the obligation of) guarding (oneself) against God, and (on His) approval, better, or someone who founded his building on the brink of a crumbling precipice, (which) then collapsed with him into the Fire of Gehenna? God does not guide the people who are evildoers.

9:110.text: Their building which they have built will continue (to be a cause of) doubt in their hearts, unless their hearts are cut (to pieces). God is knowing, wise.

9:111.text: Surely God has purchased from the believers their lives and their wealth with (the price of) the Garden (in store) for them. They fight in the way of God, and they kill and are killed. (That is) a promise binding on Him in the Torah, and the Gospel, and the Qur’ān. Who fulfills his covenant better than God? So welcome the good news of the bargain you have made with Him. That is the great triumph!

9:112.text: The ones who turn (in repentance), the ones who serve, the ones who praise, the ones who wander, the ones who bow, the ones who prostrate themselves, the ones who command right, and the ones who forbid wrong, (and) the ones who keep the limits of God – give good news to the believers.

9:113.text: It is not for the prophet and those who believe to ask forgiveness for the idolaters, even though they may be family, after it has become clear to them that they are the companions of the Furnace.

9:114.text: Abraham’s asking forgiveness for his father was only because of a solemn promise he had made to him. But when it became clear to him that he was an enemy to God, he disowned him. Surely Abraham was indeed kind (and) forbearing.

9:115.text: God is not one to lead a people astray after He has guided them, until He makes clear to them what they should guard (themselves) against. Surely God has knowledge of everything.

9:116.text: Surely God – to Him (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. You have no ally and no helper other than God.

9:117.text: Certainly God has turned (in forgiveness) to the prophet, and (to) the emigrants and the helpers who followed him in the hour of hardship, after the hearts of a group of them had nearly turned aside. Then He turned to them (in forgiveness). Surely He was kind (and) compassionate with them.

9:118.text: And to the three who stayed behind, when the earth became narrow for them despite its breadth, and they themselves were constrained, and they thought that (there was) no shelter from God except (going) to Him, then He turned to them (in forgiveness), so that they might (also) turn (in repentance). Surely God – He is the One who turns (in forgiveness), the Compassionate.

9:119.text: You who believe! Guard (yourselves) against God, and be with the truthful.

9:120.text: It is not for the people of the city, and those of the Arabs who (dwell) around them, to lag behind the messenger of God, nor should they prefer their lives to his. That is because no thirst and no weariness and no emptiness smites them in the way of God, nor do they make any attack (that) enrages the disbelievers, nor do they take any gain from an enemy, except that a righteous deed is thereby written down for them. Surely God does not let the reward of the doers of good go to waste.

9:121.text: Nor do they make any contribution, small or great, nor cross any wādī, except that it is written down for them, so that God may repay them (for the) best of what they have done.

9:122.text: It is not for the believers to go forth all together. Why not have a contingent of every group of them go forth, so that they may gain understanding in religion, and that they may warn their people when they return to them, so that they (in turn) may beware?

9:123.text: You who believe! Fight those of the disbelievers who are close to you, and let them find sternness in you, and know that God is with the ones who guard (themselves).

9:124.text: Whenever a sūra is sent down, some of them say, ‘Which of you has this increased in belief?’ As for those who believe, it increases them in belief, and they welcome the good news.

9:125.text: But as for those in whose hearts is a sickness, it increases them in abomination (added) to their abomination, and they die while they are disbelievers.

9:126.text: Do they not see that they are tested every year once or twice? Yet still they do not turn (in repentance), nor do they take heed.

9:127.text: Whenever a sūra is sent down, some of them look at others: ‘Does anyone see you?’ Then they turn away. God has turned away their hearts, because they are a people who do not understand.

9:128.text: Certainly a messenger has come to you from among you. What you suffer is a mighty (weight) on him, (for he has) concern over you, (and he is) kind (and) compassionate with the believers.

9:129.text: If they turn away, say: ‘God is enough for me. (There is) no god but Him. In Him have I put my trust. He is the Lord of the great throne.’

69:3.text: And what will make you know what the payment due is?

69:4.text: Thamūd and ‘Ād called the striking a lie.

69:5.text: As for Thamūd, they were destroyed by the outbreak.

69:6.text: And as for ‘Ād, they were destroyed by a furious, violent wind,

69:7.text: which He forced on them for seven nights and eight days consecutively, and during which you (could) see the people lying flat, as if they were the trunks of collapsed date palms.

69:8.text: Do you see any remnant of them (now)?

69:9.text: And Pharaoh (too) – and those who were before him, and the overturned (cities) – committed sin,

69:10.text: and they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a surpassing seizing.

69:11.text: Surely We – when the waters overflowed – We carried you in the running (ship),

69:12.text: so that We might make it a reminder to you, and (that) an attentive ear might attend to it.

69:13.text: When a single blast is blown on the trumpet,

69:14.text: and the earth and the mountains are lifted up and shattered with a single shattering,

69:15.text: on that Day the falling will fall,

69:16.text: and the sky will be split open, (for) on that Day it will be frail,

69:17.text: and the angels (will stand) on its borders, and they will bear the throne of your Lord above them on that Day – eight (of them).

69:18.text: On that Day you will (all) be presented – not a secret of yours will be hidden.

69:19.text: As for the one who is given his book in his right (hand), he will say, ‘Take (and) read my book.

69:20.text: Surely I thought that I would meet my reckoning.’

69:21.text: And he will be in a pleasing life,

69:24.text: ‘Eat and drink with satisfaction, (in return) for what you did in days past.’

69:25.text: But as for the one who is given his book in his left (hand), he will say, ‘Would that I had not been given my book,

69:26.text: and not known what my reckoning is!

69:27.text: Would that it had been the end!

69:28.text: My wealth is of no use to me.

69:29.text: My authority has perished from me.’

69:31.text: Then burn him in the Furnace,

69:32.text: (and) then put him in a chain of seventy cubits.

69:33.text: Surely he never believed in God, the Almighty,

69:34.text: nor did he ever urge the feeding of the poor.

69:35.text: So today he has no friend here,

69:38.text: I swear by what you see

69:39.text: and what you do not see!

69:40.text: Surely it is indeed the word of an honorable messenger.

69:41.text: It is not the word of a poet – little do you believe!

69:42.text: Nor (is it) the word of an oracle-giver – little do you take heed!

69:43.text: (It is) a sending down from the Lord of the worlds.

69:44.text: If he had forged any (false) words against Us,

69:45.text: We would indeed have seized him by the right (hand).

69:46.text: Then We would indeed have cut his (main) artery,

69:47.text: and not one of you could have defended him from it.

69:48.text: Surely it is a reminder indeed to the ones who guard (themselves).

69:49.text: Yet surely We indeed know that some of you are calling (it) a lie.

69:50.text: Surely it will be a (cause of) regret indeed to the disbelievers.

69:51.text: Yet surely it is the certain truth indeed.

69:52.text: So glorify the name of your Lord, the Almighty.

36:3.text: Surely you are indeed one of the envoys,

36:5.text: a sending down of the Mighty, the Compassionate,

36:6.text: so that you may warn a people. Their fathers have not been warned, and so they are oblivious.

36:7.text: Certainly the word has proved true against most of them: ‘They will not believe.’

36:8.text: Surely We have placed chains on their necks, and it (reaches up) to the chin, and so they (are forced to) hold their heads up.

36:9.text: We have made a barrier before them and a barrier behind them, and We have covered them, and so they do not see.

36:10.text: (It is) the same for them whether you warn them or you do not warn them. They will not believe.

36:11.text: You warn only the one who follows the Reminder and fears the Merciful in the unseen. So give him the good news of forgiveness and a generous reward.

36:12.text: Surely We – We give the dead life and We write down what they have sent forward and their traces. And everything – We have counted it up in a clear record.

36:13.text: Strike a parable for them: the companions of the town, when the envoys came to it.

36:14.text: When We sent two men to them, and they called them liars, We reinforced (them) with a third. They said, ‘Surely we are envoys to you.’

36:15.text: They said, ‘You are nothing but human beings like us. The Merciful has not sent down anything. You are only lying.’

36:16.text: They said, ‘Our Lord knows that we are indeed envoys to you.

36:17.text: Nothing (depends) on us except the clear delivery (of the message).’

36:18.text: They said, ‘Surely we have an evil omen about you. If indeed you do not stop, we shall indeed stone you, and a painful punishment from us will indeed touch you.’

36:19.text: They said, ‘Your evil omen refers to yourselves. If you had taken heed – No! You are a wanton people!’

36:20.text: (Just then) a man came running from the farthest part of the city. He said, ‘My people! Follow the envoys!

36:21.text: Follow those who do not ask you for any reward, and (who) are (rightly) guided.

36:22.text: Why should I not serve Him who created me? You will (all) be returned to Him.

36:23.text: Shall I take (other) gods instead of Him? If the Merciful intends any harm for me, their intercession will be of no use to me at all, nor will they save me.

36:24.text: Surely then I would indeed be far astray.

36:25.text: Surely I believe in your Lord, so listen to me!’

36:26.text: It was said, ‘Enter the Garden!’ He said, ‘Would that my people knew

36:27.text: that my Lord has forgiven me, and made me one of the honored.’

36:28.text: We did not send down on his people after him any force from the sky, nor are We (in the habit of) sending down (such a force).

36:29.text: It was only a single cry, and suddenly they were snuffed out.

36:30.text: Alas for the servants! Not one messenger comes to them whom they do not mock.

36:31.text: Do they not see how many generations We destroyed before them, (and) that they do not return to them?

36:32.text: But every one of them will be brought forward before Us.

36:33.text: A sign for them is the dead earth: We give it life, and bring forth grain from it, and from it they eat.

36:34.text: And We have placed in it gardens of date palms and grapes, and We have caused springs to gush forth in it,

36:35.text: so that they may eat from its fruit and what their hands have made. Will they not be thankful?

36:36.text: Glory to the One who created pairs of all that the earth grows, and of themselves, and of what they do not know.

36:37.text: A sign for them is the night: We strip the day from it, and suddenly they are in darkness.

36:38.text: And the sun: it runs to a dwelling place (appointed) for it. That is the decree of the Mighty, the Knowing.

36:39.text: And the moon: We have determined it by stations, until it returns like an old palm branch.

36:40.text: It is not fitting for the sun to overtake the moon, nor does the night outrun the day, but each floats in (its own) orbit.

36:41.text: A sign for them is that We carried their descendants in the loaded ship,

36:42.text: and We have created for them (ships) like it (in) which they sail.

36:43.text: If We (so) please, We drown them, and then (there is) no cry (for help) for them, nor are they saved,

36:44.text: except as a mercy from Us, and enjoyment (of life) for a time.

36:45.text: When it is said to them, ‘Guard (yourselves) against what is before you and what is behind you, so that you may receive compassion’ –

36:46.text: yet not a sign comes to them from the signs of their Lord without their turning away from it.

36:47.text: When it is said to them, ‘Contribute from what God has provided you,’ those who disbelieve say to those who believe, ‘Shall we feed one whom, if God (so) pleased, He would have fed? You are only far astray!’

36:48.text: They say, ‘When (will) this promise (come to pass), if you are truthful?’

36:49.text: They are only waiting for a single cry – it will seize them while they are (still) disputing,

36:50.text: and then they will not be able to make a bequest, nor will they return to their (own) families.

36:51.text: There will be a blast on the trumpet, and suddenly they will come swooping down from the graves to their Lord.

36:52.text: They will say, ‘Alas for us! Who has raised us up from our sleeping place? This is what the Merciful promised, and the envoys were truthful.’

36:53.text: It was only a single cry, and suddenly they are all brought forward before Us.

36:54.text: ‘Today no one will be done evil at all, nor will you be repaid (for anything) except what you have done.’

36:55.text: Surely the companions of the Garden today are busy rejoicing

36:56.text: – they and their spouses – reclining on couches in (places of) shade.

36:57.text: There they have fruit, and they have whatever they call for.

36:58.text: ‘Peace!’ – a word (of greeting) from a compassionate Lord.

36:60.text: Did I not make a covenant with you, sons of Adam, that you should not serve Satan – surely he is a clear enemy to you –

36:61.text: and that you should serve Me? This is a straight path.

36:62.text: Certainly he has led astray many multitudes of you. Did you not understand?

36:63.text: This is Gehenna, which you were promised.

36:64.text: Burn in it today for what you disbelieved!’

36:65.text: Today We will set a seal on their mouths, but their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will bear witness about what they have earned.

36:66.text: If We (so) pleased, We would indeed have obliterated their eyes, and they would race to the path, but how could they see?

36:67.text: And if We (so) pleased, We would indeed have transformed them where they were, and they could not go on, nor could they return.

36:68.text: (To) whomever We grant a long life, We reverse him in (his) constitution. Do they not understand?

36:69.text: We have not taught him the (art of) poetry, nor is it fitting for him. It is nothing but a Reminder and a clear Qur’ān,

36:70.text: so that he may warn whoever is living, and that the word may be proved true against the disbelievers.

36:71.text: Do they not see that We created for them – from what Our hands have made – livestock, and (that) they are their masters?

36:72.text: We have made them subservient to them, and some of them they ride, and some they eat.

36:73.text: And they have (other) benefits in them, and drinks. Will they not be thankful?

36:74.text: But they have taken (other) gods, instead of God, so that they might be helped.

36:75.text: (But) they cannot help them, (for) they will be brought forward before them as a group.

36:76.text: So do not let their saying cause you sorrow. Surely We know what they keep secret and what they speak aloud.

36:77.text: Does the human not see that We created him from a drop? Yet suddenly he is a clear adversary.

36:78.text: He has struck a parable for Us and forgotten (the fact of) his creation. He says, ‘Who will give the bones life when they are decayed?’

36:79.text: Say: ‘He will give them life who produced them the first time. He has knowledge of all creation,

36:80.text: who has made fire for you from the green tree – and so you (too) light a fire from it.’

36:81.text: Is not the One who created the heavens and the earth able to create their equivalent? Yes indeed! He is the Creator, the Knowing.

36:82.text: His only command, when He intends something, is to say to it, ‘Be!’ and it is.

36:83.text: Glory to the One in whose His hand is the kingdom of everything! To Him you will be returned.

111:1.text: The hands of Abū Lahab have perished, and he has perished.

111:2.text: His wealth and what he has earned were of no use to him.

111:3.text: He will burn in a flaming Fire,

111:4.text: and his wife (will be) the carrier of the firewood,

111:5.text: with a rope of fiber around her neck.

17:1.text: Glory to the One who sent His servant on a journey by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Distant Mosque, whose surroundings We have blessed, so that We might show him some of Our signs. Surely He – He is the Hearing, the Seeing.

17:2.text: We gave Moses the Book, and made it a guidance for the Sons of Israel: ‘Do not take any guardian other than Me!’

17:3.text: (They were) descendants of those whom We carried with Noah. Surely he was a thankful servant.

17:4.text: And We decreed for the Sons of Israel in the Book: ‘You will indeed foment corruption on the earth twice, and you will indeed rise to a great height.’

17:5.text: When the first promise came (to pass), We raised up against you servants of Ours, men of harsh violence, and they invaded (your) homes, and it was a promise fulfilled.

17:6.text: Then We returned to you (another) chance against them, and increased you with wealth and sons, and made you more numerous.

17:7.text: ‘If you do good, you do good for yourselves, but if you do evil, (it is likewise) for yourselves.’ When the second promise came (to pass), (We raised up against you servants of Ours) to cause you distress, and to enter the Temple as they entered it the first time, and to destroy completely what they had conquered.

17:8.text: It may be that your Lord will have compassion on you. But if you return, We shall return, and We have made Gehenna a prison for the disbelievers.

17:9.text: Surely this Qur’ān guides to that which is more upright, and gives good news to the believers who do righteous deeds, that for them (there is) a great reward,

17:10.text: and that those who do not believe in the Hereafter – We have prepared for them a painful punishment.

17:11.text: But the human calls for evil (as if) calling for good, (for) the human is (always) hasty.

17:12.text: We have made the night and the day as two signs: We have blotted out the sign of the night and made the sign of the day to (let you) see, so that you may seek some favor from your Lord, and that you may know the number of the years and the reckoning (of time). Everything – We have made it distinct.

17:13.text: And every human – We have fastened his fate to him on his neck, and We shall bring forth a book for him on the Day of Resurrection, which he will find unrolled.

17:14.text: ‘Read your book! You are sufficient today as a reckoner against yourself.’

17:15.text: Whoever is (rightly) guided, is guided only for himself, and whoever goes astray, goes astray only against himself. No one bearing a burden bears the burden of another. We never punish until We have raised up a messenger.

17:16.text: When We wish to destroy a town, We (first) command its affluent ones, and they act wickedly in it, so that the word against it is proved true, and We destroy it completely.

17:17.text: How many generations have We destroyed after Noah! Your Lord is sufficient (as One who) is aware of (and) sees the sins of His servants.

17:18.text: Whoever desires this hasty (world) – We hasten to (give) him in it whatever We please to whomever We wish. Then We have made Gehenna for him, (where) he will burn, condemned (and) rejected.

17:19.text: But whoever desires the Hereafter and strives with effort for it, and he is a believer, those – their striving will be thanked.

17:20.text: Each one We increase – these and those – with some gift of your Lord. The gift of your Lord is not limited.

17:21.text: See how We have favored some of them over others. Yet the Hereafter is indeed greater in ranks (of honor) and greater in favor.

17:22.text: Do not set up another god with God, or you will sit down condemned (and) forsaken.

17:23.text: Your Lord has decreed that you do not serve any but Him, and (that you do) good to your parents, whether one or both of them reaches old age with you. Do not say to them, ‘Uff,’ and do not repulse them,

17:24.text: but speak to them an honorable word. And conduct yourself humbly toward them out of mercy, and say: ‘My Lord, have compassion on both of them, as they brought me up (when I was) small.’

17:25.text: Your Lord knows what is in you. If you are righteous, surely He is forgiving to those who regularly turn (to Him in repentance).

17:26.text: Give the family member his due, and the poor and the traveler, but do not squander (your wealth) wastefully.

17:27.text: Surely the squanderers are brothers of the satans, and Satan is ungrateful to his Lord.

17:28.text: But if you turn away from them, seeking a mercy from your Lord that you expect, speak to them a gentle word.

17:29.text: Do not keep your hand chained to your neck, nor extend it all the way, or you will sit down blamed (and) impoverished.

17:30.text: Surely your Lord extends (His) provision to whomever He pleases, and restricts (it). Surely He is aware of His servants (and) sees (them).

17:31.text: Do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We will provide for them and for you. Surely their killing is a great sin.

17:32.text: Do not go near adultery. Surely it is an immoral act and evil as a way.

17:33.text: Do not kill the person whom God has forbidden (to be killed), except by right. Whoever is killed in an evil manner – We have given authority to his ally, but he should not be excessive in the killing, (for) surely he has been helped.

17:34.text: Do not go near the property of the orphan, except to improve it, until he reaches his maturity. Fulfill the covenant. Surely you are responsible for a covenant.

17:35.text: Fill up the measure when you measure, and weigh with the straight balance. That is better and fairer in interpretation.

17:36.text: Do not pursue what you have no knowledge of. Surely the hearing and the sight and the heart – all those you are responsible for.

17:37.text: Do not walk on the earth in jubilation. Surely you will not plumb the depths of the earth, nor reach the mountains in height.

17:38.text: All that – the evil of it – is hateful in the sight of your Lord.

17:39.text: That is some of the wisdom your Lord has inspired you (with). Do not set up another god with God, or you will be cast into Gehenna, blamed (and) rejected.

17:40.text: Has your Lord distinguished you with sons and taken (for Himself) females from the angels? Surely you speak a dreadful word indeed!

17:41.text: Certainly We have varied (the signs) in this Qur’ān, so that they may take heed, but it only increases them in aversion (to it).

17:42.text: Say: ‘If there were (other) gods with Him, as they say, they would indeed have sought a way to the Holder of the throne.’

17:43.text: Glory to Him! He is exalted a great height above what they say.

17:44.text: The seven heavens and the earth, and whatever is in them, glorify Him, and (there is) nothing that does not glorify (Him) with His praise, but you do not understand their glorifying. Surely He is forbearing, forgiving.

17:45.text: When you recite the Qur’ān, We place between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter an obscuring veil.

17:46.text: And We make coverings over their hearts, so that they do not understand it, and a heaviness in their ears. When you mention your Lord alone in the Qur’ān, they turn their backs in aversion (to it).

17:47.text: We know what they listen to when they listen to you, and when they (are in) secret talk, when the evildoers say, ‘You are only following a man (who is) bewitched.’

17:48.text: See how they strike parables for you! But they have gone astray and cannot (find) a way.

17:49.text: They say, ‘When we have become bones and fragments, shall we indeed be raised up as a new creation?’

17:51.text: or something greater still in your estimation!’ And then they will say, ‘Who will restore us?’ Say: ‘(He) who created you the first time.’ And then they will shake their heads at you, and say, ‘When will it be?’ Say: ‘It may be that it is near

17:52.text: – the Day when He will call you, and you will respond with His praise, and you will think that you remained (in the grave) only for a little (while).’

17:53.text: Say to My servants (that) they should say that which is best. Surely Satan provokes discord among them. Surely Satan is a clear enemy to humankind.

17:54.text: Your Lord knows about you. If He pleases, He will have compassion on you, or if He pleases, He will punish you. We have not sent you as a guardian over them.

17:55.text: Your Lord knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Certainly We have favored some of the prophets over others, and We gave David (the) Psalms.

17:56.text: Say: ‘Call on those whom you have claimed (as gods) instead of Him. They have no power (to) remove hardship from you, nor (to) change (it).’

17:57.text: Those whom they call on seek access to their Lord, whichever of them (may be) nearest, and they hope for His mercy and fear His punishment. Surely the punishment of your Lord is something to beware of.

17:58.text: (There is) no town that We are not going to destroy before the Day of Resurrection, or are not going to punish (with a) harsh punishment. That is written in the Book.

17:59.text: Nothing prevented Us from sending the signs, except that those of old called them a lie. We gave Thamūd the she-camel as a visible (sign), but they did her evil. We send the signs only to frighten.

17:60.text: (Remember) when We said to you, ‘Surely your Lord encompasses the people,’ and We made the vision which We showed you only a test for the people, and (also) the cursed tree in the Qur’ān. We frighten them, but it only increases them in great(er) insolent transgression.

17:61.text: (Remember) when We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate yourselves before Adam,’ and they prostrated themselves, except Iblīs. He said, ‘Shall I prostrate myself before one whom You have created (from) clay?’

17:62.text: He said, ‘Do You see this (creature) whom You have honored above me? If indeed You spare me until the Day of Resurrection, I shall indeed root out his descendants, except for a few.’

17:63.text: He said, ‘Go, and any of them who follows you! Surely Gehenna will be your payment – an ample payment!

17:64.text: Scare any of them you can with your voice, and assemble against them with your cavalry and your infantry, and associate with them in (their) wealth and children, and make promises to them.’ Yet Satan does not promise them (anything) but deception.

17:65.text: ‘Surely My servants – you will have no authority over them.’ Your Lord is sufficient as a guardian.

17:66.text: Your Lord (it is) who drives the ship on the sea for you, so that you may seek some of His favor. Surely He is compassionate with you.

17:67.text: When hardship touches you on the sea, (all those) whom you call on abandon (you), except Him, but when He has delivered you (safely) to the shore, you turn away. The human is ungrateful.

17:68.text: Do you feel secure that He will not cause the shore to swallow you, or send a sandstorm against you? Then you will find no guardian for yourselves.

17:69.text: Or do you feel secure that He will not send you back into it a second time, and send a hurricane against you, and drown you because you were ungrateful? Then you will find no attendant (to help) you with it against Us.

17:70.text: Certainly We have honored the sons of Adam, and carried them on the shore and the sea, and provided them with good things, and favored them greatly over many of those whom We have created.

17:71.text: On the Day when We shall call all people with their record, whoever is given his book in his right (hand) – those will read their book, and they will not be done evil in the slightest.

17:72.text: Whoever is blind in this (world will be) blind in the Hereafter, and farther astray (from the) way.

17:73.text: Surely they almost tempted you away from what We inspired you (with), so that you might forge against Us (something) other than it, and then they would indeed have taken you as a friend.

17:74.text: And had We not made you (stand) firm, you would almost have been disposed toward them a little.

17:75.text: Then We would have made you taste the double of life and the double of death, (and) then you would have found no helper for yourself against Us.

17:76.text: They almost scared you from the land, so that they might expel you from it, but then they would not have remained (there) after you, except for a little (while).

17:77.text: (That was Our) customary way (concerning) those of Our messengers whom We sent before you, and you will find no change in Our customary way.

17:78.text: Observe the prayer at the setting of the sun until the darkness of the night, and (deliver) a recitation at the dawn – surely a recitation at the dawn is witnessed.

17:79.text: And a part of the night – keep watch in it as a gift for you. It may be that your Lord will raise you up to a praised position.

17:80.text: And say: ‘My Lord, cause me to enter a truthful entrance, and cause me to exit a truthful exit, and grant me authority from Yourself (to) help (me).’

17:81.text: And say: ‘The truth has come and falsehood has passed away. Surely falsehood is (bound) to pass away.’

17:82.text: What We send down of the Qur’ān is a healing and mercy for the believers, but it only increases the evildoers in loss.

17:83.text: When We bless a person, he turns away and distances himself, but when evil touches him, he is in despair.

17:84.text: Say: ‘Each does according to his own disposition, but your Lord knows who is best guided (as to the) way.’

17:85.text: They ask you about the spirit. Say: ‘The spirit (comes) from the command of my Lord. You have only been given a little knowledge (of it).’

17:86.text: If We (so) pleased, We could indeed take away what We have inspired you (with). Then you would find no guardian for yourself against Us concerning it,

17:87.text: except as a mercy from your Lord. Surely His favor toward you is great.

17:88.text: Say: ‘If indeed humankind and the jinn joined together to produce something like this Qur’ān, they would not produce anything like it, even if they were supporters of each other.’

17:89.text: Certainly We have varied (the signs) for the people in this Qur’ān by means of every (kind of) parable, yet most of the people refuse (everything) but disbelief.

17:90.text: They say, ‘We shall not believe you until you cause a spring to gush forth for us from the earth,

17:91.text: or (until) you have a garden of date palms and grapes, and cause rivers to gush forth abundantly in the midst of it,

17:92.text: or (until) you make the sky fall on us in fragments, as you have claimed, or (until) you bring God and the angels before (us),

17:93.text: or (until) you have a decorative house, or (until) you ascend into the sky. And we shall not believe in your ascent until you bring down on us a book, so that we may read it.’ Say: ‘Glory to my Lord! Am I anything but a human being, a messenger?’

17:94.text: What prevented the people from believing when the guidance came to them, except that they said, ‘Has God raised up a human being as a messenger?’

17:95.text: Say: ‘If there were angels walking contentedly on the earth, We would indeed have sent down on them an angel from the sky as a messenger.’

17:96.text: Say: ‘God is sufficient as a witness between me and you. Surely He is aware of His servants (and) sees (them).’

17:97.text: Whoever God guides is the (rightly) guided one, and whoever He leads astray – you will not find for them any allies other than Him. We shall gather them on the Day of Resurrection on their faces – without sight, or speech, or hearing. Their refuge is Gehenna – whenever it dies down We increase (for) them a blazing (Fire).

17:98.text: That is their payment because they disbelieved in Our signs, and said, ‘When we have become bones and fragments, shall we indeed be raised up as a new creation?’

17:99.text: Do they not see that God, who created the heavens and the earth, is able to create their equivalent? He has appointed a time for them – (there is) no doubt about it – yet the evildoers refuse (everything) but disbelief.

17:100.text: Say: ‘(Even) if you possessed the storehouses of my Lord’s mercy, you would (still) hold back (out of) a fear (of) spending (it). Humankind is stingy.’

17:101.text: Certainly We gave Moses nine clear signs – (just) ask the Sons of Israel. (Remember) when he came to them, and Pharaoh said to him, ‘Moses! Surely I think you are bewitched indeed.’

17:102.text: He said, ‘Certainly you know that no one has sent down these (signs) as clear proofs except the Lord of the heavens and the earth. Pharaoh! Surely I think you are doomed indeed.’

17:103.text: He wanted to scare them from the land, but We drowned him and those who were with him – all (of them).

17:104.text: After that We said to the Sons of Israel, ‘Inhabit the land, and when the promise of the Hereafter comes, We shall bring you (all together) as a mob.’

17:105.text: With the truth We have sent it down, and with the truth it has come down, and We have sent you only as a bringer of good news and a warner.

17:106.text: (It is) a Qur’ān – We have divided it, so that you may recite it to the people at intervals, and We have sent it down once and for all.

17:107.text: Say: ‘Believe in it, or do not believe. Surely those who were given the knowledge before it – when it is recited to them, they fall down on their chins in prostration,

17:108.text: and say, “Glory to our Lord! Surely our Lord’s promise has been fulfilled indeed.”

17:109.text: They fall down on their chins weeping, and it increases them in humility.’

17:110.text: Say: ‘Call on God or call on the Merciful – whichever you call on, to Him (belong) the best names.’ And do not be loud in your prayer, nor silent in it, but seek a way between that.

17:111.text: Say: ‘Praise (be) to God, who has not taken a son, nor has He any associate in the kingdom, nor has He any (need of) an ally (to protect Him) from disgrace.’ Magnify Him (with all) magnificence.

32:2.text: The sending down of the Book – (there is) no doubt about it – (is) from the Lord of the worlds.

32:3.text: Or do they say, ‘He has forged it’? No! It is the truth from your Lord, so that you may warn a people to whom no warner has come before you, so that they may be (rightly) guided.

32:4.text: (It is) God who created the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, in six days. Then He mounted the throne. You have no ally and no intercessor other than Him. Will you not take heed?

32:5.text: He directs the (whole) affair from the sky to the earth; then it will go up to Him in a day, the measure of which is a thousand years of what you count.

32:6.text: That One is the Knower of the unseen and the seen, the Mighty, the Compassionate,

32:7.text: who made well everything He created. He brought about the creation of the human from clay,

32:8.text: then He made his progeny from an extract of despicable water,

32:9.text: then He fashioned him and breathed into him some of His spirit, and made for you hearing and sight and hearts. Little thanks you show!

32:10.text: They say, ‘When we have gotten lost in the earth, shall we indeed (return) in a new creation?’ Yes! But they are disbelievers in the meeting with their Lord.

32:11.text: Say: ‘The angel of death, who is put in charge of you, will take you, (and) then you will be returned to your Lord.’

32:12.text: If (only) you (could) see when the sinners are hanging their heads before their Lord: ‘Our Lord, (now) we have seen and heard, so let us return (and) we shall do righteousness. Surely (now) we are certain.’

32:13.text: ‘If We had (so) pleased, We would indeed have given every person his guidance. But My word has proved true: “I shall indeed fill Gehenna with jinn and people – all (of them)!”

32:14.text: So taste (the punishment) because you have forgotten the meeting of this Day of yours. Surely We have forgotten you! Taste the punishment of eternity for what you have done!’

32:15.text: Only those believe in Our signs who, when they are reminded of them, fall down in prostration and glorify their Lord with praise. They are not arrogant.

32:16.text: They forsake their beds (during the night) to call on their Lord in fear and eagerness, and they contribute from what We have provided them.

32:17.text: No one knows what comfort is hidden (away) for them in payment for what they have done.

32:18.text: So is the one who believes like the one who is wicked? They are not equal!

32:19.text: As for those who believe and do righteous deeds, for them (there are) Gardens of the Refuge, as a reception for what they have done.

32:20.text: But as for those who act wickedly, their refuge is the Fire. Whenever they want to come out of it, they will be sent back into it, and it will be said to them: ‘Taste the punishment of the Fire which you called a lie!’

32:21.text: And We shall indeed make them taste the nearer punishment, before the greater punishment, so that they may return.

32:22.text: Who is more evil than the one who is reminded of the signs of his Lord, (and) then turns away from them? Surely We are going to take vengeance on the sinners.

32:23.text: Certainly We gave Moses the Book – so do not be in doubt of meeting Him – and We made it a guidance for the Sons of Israel.

32:24.text: And We appointed from among them leaders (who) guide (others) by Our command, when they were patient and were certain of Our signs.

32:25.text: Surely your Lord – He will distinguish between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning their differences.

32:26.text: Is it not a guide for them how many generations We have destroyed before them, (seeing that) they walk in (the midst of) their dwelling places? Surely in that are signs indeed. Will they not hear?

32:27.text: Do they not see that We drive water to the barren earth, and bring forth crops by means of it, from which their livestock and they themselves eat? Will they not see?

32:28.text: They say, ‘When will the victory take place, if you are truthful?’

32:29.text: Say: ‘On the Day of Victory, their belief will not benefit those who disbelieve, nor will they be spared.’

32:30.text: So turn away from them, and wait. Surely they (too) are waiting.

87:1.text: Glorify the name of your Lord, the Most High,

87:5.text: and then turns it into darkened ruins.

87:6.text: We shall make you recite, and you will not forget – except whatever God pleases.

87:7.text: Surely He knows what is spoken publicly and what is hidden.

87:8.text: We shall make it very easy for you.

87:9.text: So remind (them), if the reminder benefits.

87:10.text: He who fears will take heed,

87:11.text: but the most miserable will turn away from it

87:12.text: – who will burn in the great Fire.

87:13.text: Then he will neither die there nor live.

87:14.text: Prosperous is he who purifies himself,

87:15.text: and remembers the name of his Lord, and prays.

87:16.text: No! But you prefer this present life,

87:17.text: when the Hereafter is better and more lasting.

87:18.text: Surely this is indeed in the former pages,

87:19.text: the pages of Abraham and Moses.

42:19.text: God is astute with His servants, providing for whomever He pleases. He is the Strong, the Mighty.

42:20.text: Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter – We shall give him increase in his harvest, and whoever desires the harvest of this world – We shall give him some of it, but he will not have any portion in the Hereafter.

42:21.text: Or do they have associates who have instituted for them from the religion what God has not given permission for? Were it not for a decisive word, it would indeed have been decided between them. Surely the evildoers – for them (there is) a painful punishment.

42:22.text: You will see the evildoers apprehensive about what they have earned, when it falls on them, while those who believe and do righteous deeds are in meadows of the Gardens. They will have whatever they please in the presence of their Lord. That is the great favor!

42:23.text: That is the good news which God gives to His servants who believe and do righteous deeds. Say: ‘I do not ask you for any reward for it, except love for family.’ Whoever acquires a good (deed) – We shall increase the good for him in it. Surely God is forgiving, thankful.

42:24.text: Or do they say, ‘He has forged a lie against God?’ If God pleases, He will set a seal on your heart, and God will blot out falsehood and verify the truth by His words. Surely He knows what is in the hearts.

42:25.text: He (it is) who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons evil deeds. He knows what you do.

42:26.text: He responds to those who believe and do righteous deeds, and gives them increase from His favor. But the disbelievers – for them (there is) a harsh punishment.

42:27.text: If God were to extend (His) provision to His servants, they would indeed act oppressively on the earth, but He sends down in measure whatever He pleases. Surely He is aware of His servants (and) sees (them).

42:28.text: He (it is) who sends down the rain after they have despaired, and displays His mercy. He is the Ally, the Praiseworthy.

42:29.text: Among His signs are the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the creatures He has scattered in both of them. He has power over gathering them whenever He pleases.

42:30.text: Whatever smiting may smite you is because of what your (own) hands have earned – yet He pardons much.

42:31.text: You cannot escape (Him) on the earth, and you have no ally and no helper other than God.

42:32.text: Among His signs are the (ships) running on the sea, like landmarks.

42:33.text: If He pleases, He stills the wind and they remain motionless on its surface. Surely in that are signs indeed for every patient (and) thankful one.

42:34.text: Or He wrecks them for what they have earned – yet He pardons much –

42:35.text: and (it is so that) He may know those who dispute about Our signs. For them (there is) no place of escape.

42:36.text: Whatever things you have been given are (only) the enjoyment of this present life, but what is with God is better and more lasting for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord

42:44.text: Whoever God leads astray has no ally after Him, and you will see the evildoers, when they see the punishment, saying, ‘(Is there) any way to return?’

42:45.text: You will see them presented to it, humbled by the disgrace, looking with furtive glance(s). And those who believe will say, ‘Surely the losers are those who have lost their (own) selves and their families on the Day of Resurrection.’ Is it not a fact that the evildoers (will remain) in lasting punishment?

42:46.text: They will have no allies to help them, other than God, and whoever God leads astray has no way.

42:47.text: Respond to your Lord, before a Day comes from God which cannot be turned back. You will not have any shelter on that Day, nor any denial (of what you have done).

42:48.text: If they turn away – We have not sent you as a watcher over them. Nothing (depends) on you except the delivery (of the message). Surely We – when We give a person a taste of mercy from Us, he gloats about it, but if some evil smites them because of what their (own) hands have sent forward – surely the human is ungrateful.

42:49.text: To God (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He creates whatever He pleases. He grants females to whomever He pleases, and He grants males to whomever He pleases,

42:50.text: or He pairs them males and females. He makes barren whomever He pleases. Surely He is knowing, powerful.

42:51.text: It is not (fitting) for any human being that God should speak to him, except (by) inspiration, or from behind a veil, or (that) He should send a messenger and he inspire by His permission whatever He pleases. Surely He is most high, wise.

42:52.text: In this way We have inspired you (with) a spirit of Our command. You did not know what the Book was, nor (what) belief (was), but We have made it a light by means of which We guide whomever We please of Our servants. Surely you will guide (people) to a straight path,

42:53.text: the path of God, the One to whom (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Is it not a fact that all affairs are returned to God?

50:2.text: – No! They are amazed that a warner has come to them from among them, and the disbelievers say, ‘This is an amazing thing!

50:3.text: When we are dead, and turned to dust [. . .]? That is a far return!’

50:4.text: We know what the earth takes away from them, and with Us is a Book (that is) keeping watch.

50:5.text: No! They called the truth a lie when it came to them, and they are in a confused state.

50:6.text: Do they not look at the sky above them, how We have built it, and adorned it, and it has no cracks?

50:7.text: And the earth – We stretched it out, and cast on it firm mountains, and caused every beautiful kind (of plant) to grow on it,

50:8.text: as evidence and a reminder to every servant who turns (in repentance).

50:9.text: And We sent down blessed water from the sky, and caused gardens to grow by means of it, and grain for harvest,

50:10.text: and tall date palms with bunches (of fruit),

50:11.text: as a provision for the servants, and We give a barren land life by means of it. In this way the coming forth (will take place).

50:12.text: Before them the people of Noah called (it) a lie, and the people of al-Rass, and Thamūd,

50:13.text: and ‘Ād, and Pharaoh, and the brothers of Lot,

50:14.text: and the people of the Grove, and the people of Tubba‘ – each (of them) called the messengers liars, and My promise was proved true.

50:15.text: Were We tired out by the first creation? No! They are in doubt about a new creation.

50:16.text: Certainly We created the human, and We know what his own self whispers within him, (for) We are closer to him than (his) jugular vein.

50:17.text: When the two meeters meet together, (one) seated on the right, and (one) on the left,

50:18.text: he does not utter a word without (there being) a watcher ready beside him.

50:19.text: The daze of death comes in truth: ‘That is what you were trying to avoid!’

50:20.text: There will be a blast on the trumpet: ‘That is the Day of Promise.’

50:21.text: Each person will come, (and) with him a driver and a witness.

50:22.text: ‘Certainly you were oblivious of this, so We have removed your covering, and today your sight is sharp.’

50:23.text: His comrade will say, ‘This is what I have ready.’

50:24.text: ‘(You two), cast into Gehenna every stubborn disbeliever,

50:25.text: preventer of the good, transgressor, doubter,

50:26.text: who set up another god with God. Cast him into the harsh punishment.’

50:27.text: His (other) comrade will say, ‘Our Lord, I did not make him transgress insolently, but he was far astray.’

50:28.text: He will say, ‘Do not dispute in My presence, when I have already sent forth the promise to you.

50:29.text: The word is not going to change with Me. I am not an evildoer to (My) servants.’
