50:30.text: On the Day when We say to Gehenna, ‘Are you filled?,’ and it says, ‘Are there any more (to come)?,’

50:31.text: and the Garden is brought near for the ones who guard (themselves) – (it is) not far:

50:32.text: ‘This is what you were promised. (It is) for everyone who turns (in repentance and) keeps watch

50:33.text: – whoever fears the Merciful in the unseen, and brings a heart turning (in repentance).

50:34.text: Enter it in peace!’ That is the Day of Eternity.

50:35.text: There they will have whatever they please, and with Us (there is still) more.

50:36.text: How many a generation We have destroyed before them! They were stronger than them in power, and they searched about in the lands – was there any place of escape?

50:37.text: Surely in that is a reminder indeed to whoever has a heart or listens attentively, and he is a witness.

50:38.text: Certainly We created the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, in six days. No weariness touched Us in (doing) that.

50:39.text: Be patient with what they say, and glorify your Lord with praise before the rising of the sun, and before its setting.

50:40.text: And glorify Him during part of the night, and at the ends of the prostration.

50:41.text: And listen for the Day when the caller will call from a place nearby.

50:42.text: The Day when they hear the cry in truth – that is the Day of Coming Forth.

50:43.text: Surely We – We give life and cause death, and to Us is the (final) destination.

50:44.text: On the Day when the earth is split open from them, (and they come forth from the graves) rushing – that is an easy gathering for Us.

50:45.text: We know what they say. You are not a tyrant over them. So remind, by means of the Qur’ān, anyone who fears My promise.

45:2.text: The sending down of the Book is from God, the Mighty, the Wise.

45:3.text: Surely in the heavens and the earth (there are) signs indeed for the believers.

45:4.text: And in your creation, and what He scatters of the creatures, (there are) signs for a people who are certain.

45:5.text: And (in the) alternation of the night and the day, and what God sends down from the sky of (His) provision, and by means of it gives the earth life after its death, and (in the) changing of the winds, (there are) signs for a people who understand.

45:6.text: Those are the signs of God. We recite them to you in truth. In what (kind of) proclamation – after God and His signs – will they believe?

45:8.text: He hears the signs of God recited to him, (but) then persists in being arrogant, as if he had not heard them. Give him the news of a painful punishment.

45:9.text: When he comes to know any of Our signs, he takes them in mockery. Those – for them (there is) a humiliating punishment.

45:10.text: Behind them is Gehenna, and what they have earned will be of no use to them at all, nor those whom they have taken as allies instead of God. For them (there is) a great punishment.

45:11.text: This is guidance, but those who disbelieve in the signs of their Lord – for them (there is) a punishment of painful wrath.

45:12.text: (It is) God who has subjected the sea to you, so that the ship may run on it by His command, and so that you may seek some of His favor, and that you may be thankful.

45:13.text: And He has subjected to you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth – all (of it is) from Him. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who reflect.

45:14.text: Say to those who believe to forgive those who do not expect the days of God, so that He may repay a people for what they have earned.

45:15.text: Whoever does righteousness, it is for himself, and whoever does evil, it is (likewise) against himself – then to your Lord you will be returned.

45:16.text: Certainly We gave the Sons of Israel the Book, and the judgment, and the prophetic office. We provided them with good things and favored them over the worlds.

45:17.text: And We gave them clear signs of the matter. They did not differ until after the knowledge had come to them, (because of) envy among themselves. Surely your Lord will decide between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning their differences.

45:18.text: Then We placed you on a pathway of the matter. So follow it, and do not follow the (vain) desires of those who do not know.

45:19.text: Surely they will be of no use to you at all against God. Surely the evildoers are allies of each other, but God is the Ally of the ones who guard (themselves).

45:20.text: This is evidence for the people, and a guidance and mercy for a people who are certain.

45:21.text: Or do those who commit evil deeds think that We shall treat them as those who believe and do righteous deeds – alike in their life and their death? Evil is what they judge!

45:22.text: God created the heavens and the earth in truth, and so that each person may be paid for what he has earned – and they will not be done evil.

45:23.text: Have you seen the one who has taken his (vain) desire as his god? God has led him astray on (the basis of) knowledge, and set a seal on his hearing and his heart, and made a covering on his sight. Who will guide him after God? Will you not take heed?

45:24.text: But they say, ‘There is nothing but our present life. We die, and we live, and nothing destroys us but time.’ They have no knowledge about that. They only conjecture.

45:25.text: When Our signs are recited to them as clear signs, their only argument is that they say, ‘Bring (back) our fathers, if you are truthful!’

45:26.text: Say: ‘God gives you life, then causes you to die, (and) then He gathers you to the Day of Resurrection – (there is) no doubt about it. But most of the people do not know (it).’

45:27.text: To God (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. On the Day when the Hour strikes, on that Day the perpetrators of falsehood will lose.

45:28.text: You will see each community kneeling, each community called to its Book: ‘Today you will be repaid for what you have done.

45:29.text: This is Our Book – it speaks about you in truth. Surely We have been copying down what you were doing.’

45:30.text: As for those who have believed and done righteous deeds, their Lord will cause them to enter into His mercy. That is the clear triumph!

45:31.text: But as for those who have disbelieved: ‘Were My signs not recited to you? Yet you became arrogant and were a sinful people.

45:32.text: And when it was said, “Surely the promise of God is true, and the Hour – (there is) no doubt about it,” you said, “We do not know what the Hour is. We think (it is) only conjecture, and we are not certain.”’

45:33.text: The evils of what they have done will become apparent to them, and what they were mocking will overwhelm them.

45:34.text: And it will be said, ‘Today We forget you, as you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours. Your refuge is the Fire, and you have no helpers.

45:35.text: That is because you took the signs of God in mockery, and this present life deluded you.’ So today they will not be brought forth from it, nor will they be allowed to make amends.

45:36.text: Praise (be) to God, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of the worlds!

45:37.text: To Him (belongs) the greatness in the heavens and the earth. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

95:1.text: By the fig and the olive!

95:4.text: Certainly We created the human in the finest state.

95:5.text: Then We return him to the lowest of the low

95:6.text: – except for those who believe and do righteous deeds. For them (there is) a reward without end.

95:7.text: What will call you a liar after (that) in (regard to) the Judgment?

95:8.text: Is God not the most just of judges?

26:2.text: Those are the signs of the clear Book.

26:3.text: Perhaps you are going to destroy yourself because they do not believe.

26:4.text: If We (so) please, We shall send down on them a sign from the sky, and their necks will stay bowed before it.

26:5.text: But no new reminder comes to them from the Merciful without their turning away from it.

26:6.text: They have called (it) a lie, so the story of what they were mocking will come to them.

26:7.text: Do they not look at the earth – how many (things) of every excellent kind We have caused to grow in it?

26:8.text: Surely in that is a sign indeed, but most of them are not believers.

26:9.text: Surely your Lord – He indeed is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

26:10.text: (Remember) when your Lord called to Moses: ‘Go to a people who are evildoers,

26:11.text: the people of Pharaoh. Will they not guard (themselves)?’

26:12.text: He said, ‘My Lord, surely I fear that they will call me a liar,

26:13.text: and my heart will be distressed, and my tongue will not work. So send for Aaron.

26:14.text: And they (also) have a crime against me, and I fear they will kill me.’

26:15.text: He said, ‘By no means! Go, both of you, with Our signs. Surely We shall be with you, hearing (everything).

26:16.text: So come, both of you, to Pharaoh, and say: “Surely we are the messenger of the Lord of the worlds.

26:17.text: Send forth the Sons of Israel with us.”’

26:18.text: He said, ‘Did we not bring you up among us as a child, and did you not remain among us for some years of your life?

26:19.text: Yet you did the deed you did, and were one of the ungrateful.’

26:20.text: He said, ‘I did it when I was one of those who had gone astray,

26:21.text: and I fled from you when I became afraid of you. But my Lord granted me judgment, and made me one of the envoys.

26:22.text: And that is a blessing you bestow on me, that you have enslaved the Sons of Israel.’

26:23.text: Pharaoh said, ‘What is the Lord of the worlds?’

26:24.text: He said, ‘The Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, if you (would) be certain.’ He said to those who were around him,

26:26.text: He said, ‘Your Lord and the Lord of your fathers of old.’

26:27.text: He said, ‘Surely your messenger who has been sent to you is possessed indeed.’

26:28.text: He said, ‘The Lord of the East and the West, and whatever is between them, if you (would) understand.’

26:29.text: He said, ‘If indeed you take a god other than me, I shall indeed make you one of the imprisoned.’

26:30.text: He said, ‘Even if I brought you something clear?’

26:31.text: He said, ‘Bring it, if you are one of the truthful.’

26:32.text: So he cast (down) his staff, and suddenly it became a real snake,

26:33.text: and he drew forth his hand, and suddenly it became white to the onlookers.

26:34.text: He said to the assembly around him, ‘Surely this is a skilled magician indeed.

26:35.text: He wants to expel you from your land by his magic. So what do you command?’

26:36.text: They said, ‘Put him and his brother off (for a while), and raise up searchers in the cities

26:37.text: to bring you every skilled magician.’

26:38.text: So the magicians were gathered together for the meeting on a day made known.

26:39.text: And it was said to the people, ‘Will you (too) gather together?’

26:40.text: ‘Perhaps we will follow the magicians, if they are the victors.’

26:41.text: When the magicians came, they said to Pharaoh, ‘(Will there) surely (be) for us a reward indeed, if we are the victors?’

26:42.text: He said, ‘Yes, and surely then you will indeed be among the ones brought near.’

26:43.text: Moses said to them, ‘Cast (down) what you are going to cast.’

26:44.text: So they cast their ropes and their staffs, and said, ‘By the honor of Pharaoh! Surely we shall be the victors indeed.’

26:45.text: And then Moses cast his staff, and suddenly it swallowed up what they were falsely contriving.

26:46.text: And the magicians were cast (down) in prostration.

26:47.text: They said, ‘We believe in the Lord of the worlds,

26:48.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.’

26:49.text: He said, ‘You have believed in him before I gave you permission. Surely he is indeed your master, the (very) one who taught you magic. But soon indeed you will know! I shall indeed cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I shall indeed crucify you – all (of you)!’

26:50.text: They said, ‘No harm! Surely we are going to return to our Lord.

26:51.text: Surely we are eager that our Lord should forgive us our sins, because we are the first of the believers.’

26:52.text: And We inspired Moses: ‘Journey with My servants. Surely you will be followed.’

26:53.text: So Pharaoh sent searchers into the cities:

26:54.text: ‘Surely these (people) are indeed a small band,

26:55.text: and surely they are indeed enraging us,

26:56.text: but surely we are indeed all vigilant.’

26:57.text: So We brought them forth from gardens and springs,

26:58.text: and treasures and an honorable place.

26:59.text: So (it was), and We caused the Sons of Israel to inherit them.

26:60.text: So they followed them at sunrise,

26:61.text: and when the two forces saw each other, the companions of Moses said, ‘Surely we are indeed overtaken!’

26:62.text: He said, ‘By no means! Surely my Lord is with me. He will guide me.’

26:63.text: And so We inspired Moses: ‘Strike the sea with your staff!’ And it parted, and each part was like a great mountain.

26:64.text: We brought the others near to that place,

26:65.text: and We rescued Moses and those who were with him – all (of them).

26:67.text: Surely in that is a sign indeed, but most of them do not believe.

26:68.text: Surely your Lord – He indeed is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

26:69.text: Recite to them the story of Abraham:

26:70.text: When he said to his father and his people, ‘What do you serve?’

26:71.text: They said, ‘We serve idols, and continue (to be) devoted to them.’

26:72.text: He said, ‘Do they hear you when you call,

26:73.text: or do they benefit you or harm (you)?’

26:74.text: They said, ‘No! But we found our fathers doing so.’

26:75.text: He said, ‘Do you see what you have been serving,

26:76.text: you and your fathers who preceded (you)?

26:77.text: Surely they are an enemy to me – except the Lord of the worlds,

26:78.text: who created me, and He guides me,

26:79.text: and who – He gives me food and gives me drink,

26:80.text: and when I am sick, He heals me.

26:81.text: He (it is) who causes me to die, (and) then gives me life,

26:82.text: and who – I am eager that He should forgive me my sin on the Day of Judgment.

26:83.text: My Lord, grant me judgment, and join me with the righteous,

26:84.text: and assign to me a good reputation among later (generations).

26:85.text: Make me one of the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss.

26:86.text: And forgive my father, (for) surely he is one of those who have gone astray.

26:87.text: Do not disgrace me on the Day when they are raised up,

26:88.text: the Day when neither wealth nor children will benefit (them),

26:89.text: except for the one who comes to God with a sound heart.

26:90.text: The Garden will be brought near for the ones who guard themselves,

26:91.text: and the Furnace will come forth for the ones who are in error.

26:92.text: And it will be said to them, “Where is what you were serving

26:93.text: instead of God? (Can) they defend you or defend themselves?”

26:94.text: And then they will be tossed into it – they and the ones who are in error –

26:95.text: and the forces of Iblīs – all (of them).

26:96.text: They will say, as they are disputing there,

26:97.text: “By God! We were indeed far astray,

26:98.text: when we made you equal with the Lord of the worlds.

26:99.text: (It was) only the sinners who led us astray.

26:102.text: If only we had (another) turn, and (could) be among the believers!”’

26:103.text: Surely in that is a sign indeed, but most of them do not believe.

26:104.text: Surely your Lord – He indeed is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

26:105.text: The people of Noah called the envoys liars:

26:106.text: When their brother Noah said to them, ‘Will you not guard (yourselves)?

26:107.text: Surely I am a trustworthy messenger for you,

26:108.text: so guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.

26:109.text: I do not ask you for any reward for it. My reward (depends) only on the Lord of the worlds.

26:110.text: Guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.’

26:111.text: They said, ‘Shall we believe you, when (only) the worst (people) follow you?’

26:112.text: He said, ‘What do I know about what they have done?

26:113.text: Their reckoning is only with my Lord, if (only) you realized (it).

26:114.text: I am not going to drive away the believers.

26:115.text: I am only a clear warner.’

26:116.text: They said, ‘If indeed you do not stop, Noah, you will indeed be one of the stoned.’

26:117.text: He said, ‘My Lord, surely my people have called me a liar,

26:118.text: so disclose (the truth) decisively between me and them, and rescue me and those of the believers who are with me.’

26:119.text: So We rescued him, and those who were with him, in the loaded ship.

26:120.text: Then, after that, We drowned the rest.

26:121.text: Surely in that is a sign indeed, but most of them do not believe.

26:122.text: Surely your Lord – He indeed is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

26:124.text: When their brother Hūd said to them, ‘Will you not guard (yourselves)?

26:125.text: Surely I am a trustworthy messenger for you,

26:126.text: so guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.

26:127.text: I do not ask you for any reward for it. My reward (depends) only on the Lord of the worlds.

26:128.text: Do you build a sign on every high place in vain,

26:129.text: and take strongholds in the hope that you may remain there?

26:130.text: And when you attack (someone), do you attack like tyrants?

26:131.text: Guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.

26:132.text: Guard (yourselves) against the One who has increased you with what you know.

26:133.text: He has increased you with livestock and sons,

26:135.text: Surely I fear for you the punishment of a great day.’

26:136.text: They said, ‘(It is) the same for us whether you admonish (us) or are not one of the admonishers.

26:137.text: This is nothing but the creation of those of old.

26:138.text: We are not going to be punished.’

26:139.text: So they called him a liar, and We destroyed them. Surely in that is a sign indeed, but most of them do not believe.

26:140.text: Surely your Lord – He indeed is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

26:142.text: When their brother Ṣāliḥ said to them, ‘Will you not guard (yourselves)?

26:143.text: Surely I am a trustworthy messenger for you,

26:144.text: so guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.

26:145.text: I do not ask you for any reward for it. My reward (depends) only on the Lord of the worlds.

26:146.text: Will you be left secure in what is here,

26:148.text: and (fields of) crops and date palms (with) its slender sheath?

26:149.text: Will you (continue to) carve houses out of the mountains with skill?

26:150.text: Guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.

26:151.text: Do not obey the command of the wanton,

26:152.text: those who foment corruption on the earth and do not set (things) right.’

26:153.text: They said, ‘You are only one of the bewitched.

26:154.text: You are nothing but a human being like us. Bring (us) a sign, if you are one of the truthful.’

26:155.text: He said, ‘This is a she-camel: to her a drink and to you a drink, on a day made known.

26:156.text: Do not touch her with evil, or the punishment of a great day will seize you.’

26:157.text: But they wounded her, and morning found them full of regret,

26:158.text: and the punishment seized them. Surely in that is a sign indeed, but most of them do not believe.

26:159.text: Surely your Lord – He indeed is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

26:160.text: The people of Lot called the envoys liars:

26:161.text: When their brother Lot said to them, ‘Will you not guard (yourselves)?

26:162.text: Surely I am a trustworthy messenger for you,

26:163.text: so guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.

26:164.text: I do not ask you for any reward for it. My reward (depends) only on the Lord of the worlds.

26:165.text: Do you approach the males of all peoples,

26:166.text: and leave your wives whom your Lord created for you? Yes! You are a people who transgress!’

26:167.text: They said, ‘If indeed you do not stop, Lot, you will indeed be one of the expelled.’

26:168.text: He said, ‘Surely I am one of those who despise what you do.

26:169.text: My Lord, rescue me and my family from what they do.’

26:170.text: So We rescued him and his family – all (of them) –

26:171.text: except for an old woman among those who stayed behind.

26:173.text: We rained down on them a rain, and evil was the rain on those who had been warned!

26:174.text: Surely in that is a sign indeed, but most of them do not believe.

26:175.text: Surely your Lord – He indeed is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

26:176.text: The people of the Grove called the envoys liars:

26:177.text: When Shu‘ayb said to them, ‘Will you not guard (yourselves)?

26:178.text: I am a trustworthy messenger for you,

26:179.text: so guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.

26:180.text: I do not ask you for any reward for it. My reward (depends) only on the Lord of the worlds.

26:181.text: Fill up the measure, and do not be cheaters,

26:182.text: and weigh with the even scale,

26:183.text: and do not shortchange the people of their wealth, and do not act wickedly on the earth, fomenting corruption.

26:184.text: Guard (yourselves) against the One who created you and the multitudes of old.’

26:185.text: They said, ‘You are only one of the bewitched.

26:186.text: You are nothing but a human being like us. Surely we think (that) you are indeed one of the liars.

26:187.text: Make fragments of the sky fall on us, if you are one of the truthful.’

26:188.text: He said, ‘My Lord knows what you are doing.’

26:189.text: But they called him a liar, and the punishment of the Day of Shadow seized them. Surely it was the punishment of a great day.

26:190.text: Surely in that is a sign indeed, but most of them do not believe.

26:191.text: Surely your Lord – He indeed is the Mighty, the Compassionate.

26:192.text: Surely it is indeed a sending down from the Lord of the worlds.

26:193.text: The trustworthy spirit has brought it down

26:194.text: on your heart, so that you may be one of the warners,

26:196.text: Surely it is indeed in the scriptures of those of old.

26:197.text: Was it not a sign for them that it was known to the learned of the Sons of Israel?

26:198.text: If We had sent it down on one of the foreigners,

26:199.text: and he had recited it to them, they would not have believed in it.

26:200.text: In this way We put it into the hearts of the sinners.

26:201.text: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment,

26:202.text: and it will come upon them unexpectedly, when they do not realize (it),

26:203.text: and they will say, ‘Are we going to be spared?’

26:204.text: Do they seek to hurry Our punishment?

26:205.text: Have you considered? If We give them enjoyment (of life) for (some) years,

26:206.text: (and) then what they were promised comes upon them,

26:207.text: what use will the enjoyment they were given be to them?

26:208.text: We have not destroyed any town without its having warners

26:209.text: as a reminder. We have not been evildoers.

26:210.text: The satans have not brought it down.

26:211.text: It is not fitting for them (to do), nor are they able (to).

26:212.text: Surely they are removed indeed from the hearing (of it).

26:213.text: Do not call on another god (along) with God, or you will be one of the punished.

26:215.text: and lower your wing to whoever follows you of the believers.

26:216.text: If they disobey you, say: ‘I am free of what you do.’

26:217.text: Put your trust in the Mighty, the Compassionate,

26:218.text: who sees you when you stand,

26:219.text: and when you turn about among the ones who prostrate themselves.

26:220.text: Surely He – He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

26:221.text: Shall I inform you on whom the satans come down?

26:222.text: They come down on every liar (and) sinner.

26:223.text: They listen attentively, but most of them are liars.

26:224.text: And the poets – the ones who are in error follow them.

26:225.text: Do you not see that they wander in every wādī,

26:226.text: and that they say what they do not do?

26:227.text: – except for those who believe, and do righteous deeds, and remember God often, and defend themselves after they have suffered evil. Those who have done evil will come to know what a complete overturning they will suffer.

72:1.text: Say: ‘I am inspired that a band of the jinn listened, and they said, “Surely we have heard an amazing Qur’ān!

72:2.text: It guides to the right (course). We believe in it, and we shall not associate anyone with our Lord.

72:3.text: And (we believe) that He – exalted (be) the majesty of our Lord! – He has not taken a consort or son.

72:4.text: And that the foolish among us used to say an outrageous thing against God.

72:5.text: And that we had thought that humans and jinn would never say any lie against God.

72:6.text: And that individuals of humankind used to take refuge with individuals of the jinn, and they increased them in depravity.

72:7.text: And that they thought as you (also) thought, that God will not raise up anyone.

72:8.text: And that we touched the sky and found it filled with harsh guards and piercing flames.

72:9.text: And that we used to sit there on seats to listen (in), but whoever listens now finds a piercing flame lying in wait for him.

72:10.text: And that we do not know whether evil is intended for those who are on the earth, or whether their Lord intends right (guidance) for them.

72:11.text: And that some of us are righteous, and some of us are other than that – we are on different roads.

72:12.text: And that we (now) think that we shall not be able to escape God on the earth, and shall not escape Him by flight.

72:13.text: And that when we heard the guidance, we believed in it, and whoever believes in his Lord will not fear any deprivation or depravity.

72:14.text: And that some of us have submitted, and some of us are the ones who have deviated. Whoever submits, those have sought out right (guidance),

72:15.text: but as for the ones who have deviated, they have become firewood for Gehenna!”’

72:16.text: And (We say) that if they had gone straight on the road, We would indeed have given them water to drink in abundance,

72:17.text: so that We might test them concerning it. Whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord – He will place him in hard punishment.

72:18.text: And that the mosques (belong) to God, so do not call on anyone (along) with God.

72:19.text: And that when the servant of God stood calling on Him, they were almost upon him in hordes.

72:20.text: Say: ‘I call only on my Lord, and I do not associate anyone with Him.’

72:21.text: Say: ‘Surely I possess no power over you, either for harm or for right (guidance).’

72:22.text: Say: ‘No one will protect me from God, and I shall not find any refuge other than Him.

72:23.text: (I bring) only a delivery from God and His messages.’ Whoever disobeys God and His messenger, surely for him (there is) the Fire of Gehenna, there to remain forever.

72:24.text: – Until, when they see what they are promised, they will know who is weaker in helper(s) and fewer in number.

72:25.text: Say: ‘I do not know whether what you are promised is near, or whether my Lord will appoint a (distant) time for it.

72:26.text: (He is) the Knower of the unseen, and He does not disclose His unseen to anyone,

72:27.text: except to a messenger whom He has approved, and then He dispatches before him and behind him (watchers) lying in wait,

72:28.text: so that He may know that they have delivered the messages of their Lord. He encompasses all that is with them, and He counts everything by number.’

47:1.text: Those who disbelieve and keep (people) from the way of God – He will lead their deeds astray.

47:2.text: But those who believe and do righteous deeds, and believe in what has been sent down on Muḥammad – and it is the truth from their Lord – He will absolve them of their evil deeds, and set their case right.

47:3.text: That is because those who disbelieve follow falsehood, and because those who believe follow the truth from their Lord. In this way God strikes parables for the people.

47:4.text: When you meet those who disbelieve, (let there be) a striking of the necks, until, when you have subdued them, bind (them) securely, and then either (set them free) as a favor or by ransom, until the war lays down its burdens. That (is the rule). If God had (so) pleased, He would indeed have defended Himself against them, but (He allows fighting) so that He may test some of you by means of others. Those who are killed in the way of God – He will not lead their deeds astray.

47:5.text: He will guide them and set their case right,

47:6.text: and He will cause them to enter the Garden – He has made it known to them.

47:7.text: You who believe! If you help God, He will help you, and make firm your feet.

47:8.text: But those who disbelieve – (there will be) downfall for them, and He will lead their deeds astray.

47:9.text: That is because they disliked what God sent down, and so He has made their deeds worthless.

47:10.text: Have they not traveled on the earth and seen how the end was for those who were before them? God destroyed them. The disbelievers have examples of it.

47:11.text: That is because God is the Protector of those who believe, and because the disbelievers have no protector.

47:12.text: Surely God will cause those who believe and do righteous deeds to enter Gardens through which rivers flow. But those who disbelieve – they take their enjoyment and eat as the cattle eat. The Fire will be their dwelling place.

47:13.text: How many a town We have destroyed that was stronger in power than your town which expelled you! And there was no helper for them.

47:14.text: Is the one who (stands) on a clear sign from his Lord like the one who – the evil of his deeds is made to appear enticing to him, and they follow their (vain) desires?

47:15.text: A parable of the Garden which is promised to the ones who guard (themselves): In it (there are) rivers of water without pollution, and rivers of milk – its taste does not change – and rivers of wine – delicious to the drinkers – and rivers of purified honey. In it (there is) every (kind of) fruit for them, and forgiveness from their Lord. (Are they) like those who remain in the Fire? They are given boiling water to drink, and it cuts their insides (to pieces).

47:16.text: (There are) some of them who listen to you, until, when they go forth from your presence, they say to those who have been given knowledge, ‘What did he say just now?’ Those are the ones on whose hearts God has set a seal, and they follow their (vain) desires.

47:17.text: But those who are (rightly) guided – He increases them in guidance, and gives them their (sense of) guarding (themselves).

47:18.text: So are they looking for anything but the Hour – that it will come upon them unexpectedly? The conditions for it have already come, and when it comes upon them, how will they have their reminder?

47:19.text: Know that He – (there is) no god but God. Ask forgiveness for your sin, and for the believing men and the believing women. God knows your comings and goings, and your dwelling place.

47:20.text: Those who believe say, ‘If only a sūra were sent down.’ But when a clearly composed sūra is sent down, and fighting is mentioned in it, you see those in whose hearts is a sickness looking at you with the look of one who faints at the point of death. Woe to them!

47:21.text: Obedience and rightful words (are called for)! When the matter is determined, and if they are true to God, it will indeed be better for them.

47:22.text: Is it possible, if you turned away, that you would foment corruption on the earth, and sever your family ties?

47:23.text: Those are the ones whom God has cursed, and made them deaf, and blinded their sight.

47:24.text: Do they not contemplate the Qur’ān, or (are there) locks on their hearts?

47:25.text: Surely those who have turned their backs, after the guidance has become clear to them – (it was) Satan (who) contrived (it) for them, but He has spared them.

47:26.text: That is because they said to those who disliked what God had sent down, ‘We will obey you in part of the matter’ – but God knows their secrets.

47:27.text: How (will it be) when the angels take them, striking their faces and their backs?

47:28.text: That is because they have followed what angers God, and have disliked His approval, so He has made their deeds worthless.

47:29.text: Or do those in whose hearts is a sickness think that God will not bring to light their malice?

47:30.text: If We had (so) pleased, We would indeed have shown them to you, and you would indeed know them by their marks – indeed you do know them by their devious speech. God knows your deeds,

47:31.text: and We shall indeed test you, until We know those of you who struggle and those who are patient, and We shall test the reports about you.

47:32.text: Surely those who disbelieve, and keep (people) from the way of God, and break with the messenger, after the guidance has become clear to them – they will not harm God at all, and He will make their deeds worthless.

47:33.text: You who believe! Obey God, and obey the messenger, and do not invalidate your (own) deeds.

47:34.text: Surely those who disbelieve and keep (people) from the way of God, (and) then die while they are disbelievers – God will not forgive them.

47:35.text: Do not grow weak and call for peace, when you are the prevailing (force), and God is with you, and will not deprive you of your deeds.

47:36.text: This present life is nothing but jest and diversion, but if you believe and guard (yourselves), He will give you your rewards and not ask you for your wealth.

47:37.text: If He asks you for it, and presses you, you are stingy, and He brings to light your malice.

47:38.text: There you are! These (people)! You are called on to contribute in the way of God, and (there are) some of you who are stingy. Whoever is stingy is stingy only to himself. God is the wealthy One, and you are the poor (ones). If you turn away, He will exchange a people other than you. Then they will not be like you.

110:1.text: When the help of God comes, and the victory,

110:2.text: and you see the people entering into the religion of God in crowds,

110:3.text: glorify your Lord with praise, and ask forgiveness from Him. Surely He turns (in forgiveness).

3:23.text: Have you not seen those who were given a portion of the Book? They were called to the Book of God in order that it might judge between them. Then a group of them turned away in aversion.

3:24.text: That is because they said, ‘The Fire will only touch us for a number of days.’ What they forged has deceived them in their religion.

3:25.text: How (will it be) when We gather them for a Day – (there is) no doubt about it – and everyone will be paid in full what he has earned, and they will not be done evil?

3:26.text: Say: ‘God! Master of the kingdom, You give the kingdom to whomever You please and You take away the kingdom from whomever You please. You exalt whomever You please and You humble whomever You please. In Your hand is the good. Surely You are powerful over everything.

3:27.text: You cause the night to pass into the day, and cause the day to pass into the night. You bring forth the living from the dead, and bring forth the dead from the living. You provide for whomever You please without reckoning.’

3:28.text: Let not the believers take the disbelievers as allies, rather than the believers – whoever does that, he has nothing from God – unless you guard (yourselves) against them as a precaution. God warns you to beware of Him. To God is the (final) destination.

3:29.text: Say: ‘Whether you hide what is in your hearts or reveal it, God knows it. He knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. God is powerful over everything.’

3:30.text: On the Day when everyone will find the good he has done brought forward, and (also) the evil he has done, he will wish that there were a great distance between himself and it. God warns you to beware of Him. God is kind with (His) servants.

3:31.text: Say: ‘If you love God, follow me. God will love you and will forgive you your sins. God is forgiving, compassionate.’

3:32.text: Say: ‘Obey God and the messenger.’ If they turn away – surely God does not love the disbelievers.

3:33.text: Surely God has chosen Adam and Noah, and the house of Abraham and the house of ‘Imrān over the worlds,

3:34.text: some of them descendents of others. God is hearing, knowing.

3:35.text: (Remember) when the wife of ‘Imrān said, ‘My Lord, surely I vow to You what is in my belly, (to be) dedicated (to Your service). Accept (it) from me. Surely You – You are the Hearing, the Knowing.’

3:36.text: And when she had delivered her, she said, ‘My Lord, surely I have delivered her, a female’ – God knew very well what she had delivered, (since) the male is not like the female – ’and I have named her Mary, and I seek refuge for her with You, and for her descendants, from the accursed Satan.’

3:37.text: So her Lord accepted her fully and caused her to grow up well, and Zachariah took charge of her. Whenever Zachariah entered upon her (in) the place of prayer, he found a provision (of food) with her. He said, ‘Mary! Where does this (food) come to you from?’ She said, ‘It is from God. Surely God provides for whomever He pleases without reckoning.’

3:38.text: There Zachariah called on his Lord. He said, ‘My Lord, grant me a good descendant from Yourself. Surely You are the Hearer of the call.’

3:39.text: And the angels called him while he was standing, praying in the place of prayer: ‘God gives you good news of John, confirming a word from God. (He will be) a man of honor, and an ascetic, and a prophet from among the righteous.’

3:40.text: He said, ‘My Lord, how shall I have a boy, when old age has already come upon me and my wife cannot conceive?’ He said, ‘So (it will be)! God does whatever He pleases.’

3:41.text: He said, ‘My Lord, make a sign for me.’ He said, ‘Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three days, except by gestures. Remember your Lord often, and glorify (Him) in the evening and the morning.’

3:42.text: And (remember) when the angels said, ‘Mary! Surely God has chosen you and purified you, and He has chosen you over all other women.

3:43.text: Mary! Be obedient to your Lord, and prostrate yourself and bow with the ones who bow.’

3:44.text: – That is one of the stories of the unseen. We inspired you (with) it. You were not with them when they cast their pens (as lots to see) which of them would take charge of Mary. Nor were you with them when they were disputing. –

3:45.text: When the angels said, ‘Mary! Surely God gives you good news of a word from Him: his name is the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, eminent in this world and the Hereafter, and one of those brought near.

3:46.text: He will speak to the people (while he is still) in the cradle and in adulthood, and (he will be) one of the righteous.’

3:47.text: She said, ‘My Lord, how shall I have a child, when no man has touched me?’ He said, ‘So (it will be)! God creates whatever He pleases. When He decrees something, He simply says to it, “Be!” and it is.’

3:48.text: And He will teach him the Book and the wisdom, and the Torah and the Gospel.

3:49.text: And (He will make him) a messenger to the Sons of Israel. ‘Surely I have brought you a sign from your Lord: I shall create for you the form of a bird from clay. Then I will breathe into it and it will become a bird by the permission of God. And I shall heal the blind and the leper, and give the dead life by the permission of God. And I shall inform you of what you may eat, and what you may store up in your houses. Surely in that is a sign indeed for you, if you are believers.

3:50.text: And (I come) confirming what was before me of the Torah, and to make permitted to you some things which were forbidden to you (before). I have brought you a sign from your Lord, so guard (yourselves) against God, and obey me.

3:51.text: Surely God is my Lord and your Lord, so serve Him! This is a straight path.’

70:1.text: A questioner questioned about the punishment going to fall

70:2.text: – the disbelievers have no one to repel it! –

70:3.text: from God, controller of the stairways.

70:4.text: The angels and the spirit ascend to Him in a day, the measure of which is fifty thousand years.

70:5.text: So be patient with a patience that becomes (you).

70:6.text: Surely they see it as far off,

70:7.text: but We see it is near

70:8.text: – the Day when the sky will be like molten metal,

70:9.text: and the mountains will be like (tufts of) wool,

70:10.text: and friend will not question friend.

70:11.text: (As) they come into sight of each other, the sinner will wish that he (could) ransom (himself) from the punishment of that Day with his sons,

70:12.text: and his consort, and his brother,

70:13.text: and his family who gave him refuge,

70:14.text: and whoever is on the earth – all (of them) – (wishing that) then it might rescue him.

70:15.text: By no means! Surely (there is) a flame,

70:17.text: It will call the one who turned and went away,

70:19.text: Surely the human was created anxious (for gain).

70:20.text: When misfortune touches him, (he is) complaining,

70:21.text: but when good touches him, refusing (to give),

70:22.text: except for the ones who pray

70:24.text: and in whose wealth (there is) an acknowledged (portion) due

70:25.text: for the beggar and the outcast,

70:26.text: and who affirm the Day of Judgment,

70:27.text: and who are apprehensive of the punishment of their Lord

70:28.text: – surely no one feels secure (against) the punishment of their Lord –

70:29.text: and who guard their private parts,

70:30.text: except concerning their wives or what their right (hands) own – surely then they are not (to be) blamed,

70:31.text: but whoever seeks beyond that, those – they are the transgressors –

70:32.text: and those who keep their pledges and their promise(s),

70:33.text: and who stand by their testimonies,

70:35.text: Those will be honored in Gardens.

70:36.text: What is (the matter) with those who disbelieve, rushing toward you,

70:37.text: from the right (hand) and from the left in groups?

70:38.text: Is every person among them eager to enter a Garden of Bliss?

70:39.text: By no means! Surely We have created them from what they know.

70:40.text: I swear by the Lord of the Easts and the Wests! Surely We are able indeed

70:41.text: to exchange (others who are) better than them – We are not (to be) outrun!

70:42.text: So leave them! Let them banter and jest, until they meet their Day which they are promised,

70:43.text: the Day when they will come forth from the graves rushing – as if they were running to some goal –

70:44.text: their sight downcast, humiliation covering them. That is the Day which they were promised.

27:1.text: Ṭā’ Sīn. Those are the signs of the Qur’ān and a clear Book,

27:2.text: a guidance and good news for the believers,

27:3.text: who observe the prayer and give the alms, and they are certain of the Hereafter.

27:4.text: Those who do not believe in the Hereafter – We have made their deeds appear enticing to them, and they wander blindly.

27:5.text: Those are the ones for whom (there is) an evil punishment, and in the Hereafter they will be the worst losers.

27:6.text: Surely you have indeed received the Qur’ān from One (who is) wise, knowing.

27:7.text: (Remember) when Moses said to his family: ‘Surely I perceive a fire. I shall bring you some news of it, or I shall bring you a flame – a torch – so that you may warm yourselves.’

27:8.text: But when he came to it, he was called: ‘Blessed is He who is in the fire, and whoever is around it. Glory to God, Lord of the worlds!

27:9.text: Moses! Surely I am God, the Mighty, the Wise.’

27:10.text: And: ‘Cast (down) your staff!’ When he saw it wiggling as if it were a snake, he turned around, retreating, and did not look back. ‘Moses! Do not fear! Surely in My presence the envoys do not fear

27:11.text: – except for the one who has done evil, (and) then has exchanged good after evil – surely I am forgiving, compassionate.

27:12.text: Put your hand inside your cloak. It will come out white, unharmed – (these are two) among nine signs for Pharaoh and his people. Surely they are a wicked people.’

27:13.text: But when Our signs came to them visibly, they said, ‘This is clear magic.’

27:14.text: They denied them, even though they were convinced of them in themselves, out of evil and haughtiness. See how the end was for the fomenters of corruption!

27:15.text: Certainly We gave David and Solomon knowledge, and they said, ‘Praise (be) to God, who has favored us over many of His believing servants!’

27:16.text: Solomon inherited (it) from David, and said, ‘People! We have been taught the speech of birds, and we have been given (some) of everything. Surely this – it indeed is clear favor.’

27:17.text: Gathered before Solomon were his forces – jinn, and men, and birds – and they were arranged (in rows)

27:18.text: – until, when they came upon the Wādī of the Ants, an ant said, ‘Ants! Enter your dwellings, or Solomon and his forces will crush you without realizing (it).’

27:19.text: But he smiled, laughing at its words, and said, ‘My Lord, (so) dispose me that I may be thankful for your blessing with which You have blessed me and my parents, and that I may do righteousness (that) pleases You, and cause me to enter, by Your mercy, among your righteous servants.’

27:20.text: He reviewed the birds, and said, ‘Why do I not see the hudhud? Or is it one of the absent?

27:21.text: I shall indeed punish it severely, or slaughter it, or it will bring me a clear authority.’

27:22.text: But it did not stay (away) for long, and said, ‘I have encompassed what you have not encompassed, and I have brought you reliable news from (the people of) Sheba.

27:23.text: Surely I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given (some) of everything, and she has a great throne.

27:24.text: I found her and her people prostrating themselves before the sun instead of God. Satan has made their deeds appear enticing to them, and he has kept them from the way, and they are not (rightly) guided.

27:25.text: (He did this) so that they would not prostrate themselves before God, who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and the earth. He knows what you hide and what you speak aloud.

27:26.text: God – (there is) no god but Him, Lord of the great throne.’

27:27.text: He said, ‘We shall see whether you have spoken the truth or are one of the liars.

27:28.text: Go with this letter of mine, and cast it (down) to them. Then turn away from them and see what they return.’

27:29.text: She said, ‘Assembly! Surely an honorable letter has been cast (down) to me.

27:30.text: Surely it is from Solomon, and surely it (reads): “In the Name of God, the Merciful, the Compassionate.

27:31.text: Do not exalt yourselves over me, but come to me in surrender.”’

27:32.text: She said, ‘Assembly! Make a pronouncement to me about my affair. I do not decide any affair until you bear me witness.’

27:33.text: They said, ‘We are full of strength and full of harsh violence, but the affair (belongs) to you. See what you will command.’

27:34.text: She said, ‘Surely kings, when they enter a town, corrupt it, and make the upper class of its people the lowest, and that is what they will do.

27:35.text: Surely I am going to send a gift to them, and see what the envoys bring back.’

27:36.text: When he came to Solomon, he said, ‘Would you increase me with wealth, when what God has given me is better than what He has given you? No! (It is) you (who) gloat over your own gift.

27:37.text: Return to them! We shall indeed come upon them with forces which they have no power to face, and we shall indeed expel them from there in humiliation, and they will be disgraced.’

27:38.text: He said, ‘Assembly! Which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in surrender?’

27:39.text: A crafty one of the jinn said, ‘I shall bring it to you before you (can) rise from your place. Surely I have strength for it (and am) trustworthy.’

27:40.text: One who had knowledge of the Book said, ‘I will bring it to you in the wink of an eye.’ So when he saw it set before him, he said, ‘This is from the favor of my Lord to test me (to see) whether I am thankful or ungrateful. Whoever is thankful is thankful only for his own good, and whoever is ungrateful – surely my Lord is wealthy, generous.’

27:41.text: He said, ‘Disguise her throne for her. We shall see whether she is (rightly) guided or is one of those who are not (rightly) guided.’

27:42.text: So when she came, it was said, ‘Is your throne like this?’ She said, ‘It seems like it.’ ‘And we had been given the knowledge before her, and were in surrender,

27:43.text: but what she served, instead of God, kept her back. Surely she was from a disbelieving people.’

27:44.text: It was said to her, ‘Enter the palace.’ When she saw it, she thought it was a pool (of water), and she uncovered her legs. He said, ‘Surely it is a polished palace of crystal.’ She said, ‘My Lord, surely I have done myself evil. I surrender with Solomon to God, Lord of the worlds.’

27:45.text: Certainly We sent to Thamūd their brother Ṣāliḥ: ‘Serve God!’ And suddenly they were two groups disputing each other.

27:46.text: He said, ‘My people! Why do you seek to hurry the evil before the good? Why do you not ask forgiveness from God, so that you may receive compassion?’

27:47.text: They said, ‘We have an evil omen about you and those who are with you.’ He said, ‘Your evil omen is with God. But you are a people being tested!’

27:48.text: In the city (there) was a group of nine persons who were fomenting corruption on the earth, and not setting (things) right.

27:49.text: They said, ‘Swear to each other by God, “We shall indeed attack him and his family by night.” Then we shall indeed say to his ally, “We were not witnesses of the destruction of his family” and “Surely we are truthful indeed.”’

27:50.text: They schemed a scheme, but We (too) schemed a scheme, though they did not realize (it).

27:51.text: See how the end of their scheme was: We destroyed them and their people – all (of them)!

27:52.text: Those are their houses, collapsed because of the evil they did. Surely in that is a sign indeed for a people who know.

27:53.text: And We rescued those who believed and guarded (themselves).

27:54.text: And Lot, when he said to his people: ‘Do you commit immorality with your eyes open?

27:55.text: Do you indeed approach men with lust instead of women? But you are an ignorant people!’

27:56.text: The only response of his people was that they said, ‘Expel the house(hold) of Lot from your town, (for) surely they are men who keep themselves clean.’

27:57.text: So We rescued him and his family, except for his wife. We decreed that she (would be) one of those who stayed behind.

27:58.text: And We rained down on them a rain. Evil was the rain on those who had been warned!

27:59.text: Say: ‘Praise (be) to God, and peace (be) upon His servants whom He has chosen!’ Is God better, or what they associate?

27:60.text: Or (is He not better) who created the heavens and earth, and sent down water from the sky for you, and then by means of it We cause orchards to grow, full of beauty, whose trees you could never grow? (Is there any other) god with God? No! But they are a people who equate (others to Him).

27:61.text: Or (is He not better) who made the earth a dwelling place, and placed rivers in the midst of it, and made firm mountains for it, and placed a partition between the two seas? (Is there any other) god with God? No! But most of them do not know (it).

27:62.text: Or (is He not better) who responds to the distressed (person) when he calls on Him and removes the evil, and establishes you as successors on the earth? (Is there any other) god with God? Little do you take heed!

27:63.text: Or (is He not better) who guides you in the darkness of the shore and the sea, and who sends the winds as good news before His mercy? (Is there any other) god with God? God is exalted above what they associate!

27:64.text: Or (is He not better) who brings about the creation, (and) then restores it, and who provides for you from the sky and the earth? (Is there any other) god with God? Say: ‘Bring your proof, if you are truthful.’

27:65.text: Say: ‘No one in the heavens or the earth knows the unseen except God. They do not realize when they will be raised up.

27:66.text: No! Their knowledge is confused concerning the Hereafter. No! They are in doubt about it. No! They are blind to it.’

27:67.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘When we have become dust, and our fathers (too), shall we indeed be brought forth?

27:68.text: Certainly we have been promised this before, we and our fathers. This is nothing but old tales.’

27:69.text: Say: ‘Travel the earth and see how the end was for the sinners.’

27:70.text: Do not sorrow over them, nor be in distress because of what they are scheming.

27:71.text: They say, ‘When (will) this promise (come to pass), if you are truthful?’

27:72.text: Say: ‘It may be that part of what you seek to hurry is bearing down on you (now).’

27:73.text: Surely your Lord is indeed full of favor to the people, but most of them are not thankful (for it).

27:74.text: Surely your Lord indeed knows what their hearts conceal and what they speak aloud.

27:75.text: (There is) nothing hidden in the sky or the earth, except (that it is recorded) in a clear Book.

27:76.text: Surely this Qur’ān recounts to the Sons of Israel most of their differences,

27:77.text: and surely it is indeed a guidance and mercy to the believers.

27:78.text: Surely your Lord will decide between them by His judgment. He is the Mighty, the Knowing.

27:79.text: Put your trust in God, (for) surely you (stand) on the clear truth.

27:80.text: Surely you cannot make the dead to hear, nor can you make the deaf to hear the call, when they turn away, withdrawing.

27:81.text: Nor can you guide the blind out of their straying. You cannot make (anyone) hear, except the one who believes in Our signs, and so they submit.

27:82.text: When the word falls upon them, We shall bring forth for them a creature from the earth, (which) will speak to them: ‘The people were not certain of Our signs.’

27:83.text: On the Day when We shall gather from every community a crowd of those who have called Our signs a lie, and they are arranged (in rows)

27:84.text: –until, when they come, He will say, ‘Did you call My signs a lie, when you did not encompass them in knowledge, or what (it was) you were doing?’

27:85.text: And the word will fall upon them because of the evil they have done, and they will not speak.

27:86.text: Do they not see that We made the night for them to rest in, and the day to see? Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who believe.

27:87.text: On the Day when there is a blast on the trumpet, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified, except for whomever God pleases, and all will come to Him humbled,

27:88.text: and you see the mountains, supposedly solid, yet passing by (as) the clouds pass by – (such is) the work of God who has perfected everything. Surely He is aware of what you do.

27:89.text: (On that Day) whoever brings a good (deed) will have a better one than it, and they will be secure from the terror of that Day.

27:90.text: But whoever brings an evil (deed), they will be cast down face first into the Fire: ‘Are you repaid (for anything) except for what you have done?’

27:91.text: ‘I have only been commanded to serve the Lord of this land, who has made it sacred. To Him everything (belongs). And I have been commanded to be one of those who submit,

27:92.text: and to recite the Qur’ān. Whoever is (rightly) guided is guided only for himself, and whoever goes astray [. . .].’ Say: ‘I am only one of the warners.’

27:93.text: And say: ‘Praise (be) to God! He will show you His signs and you will recognize them. Your Lord is not oblivious of what you do.’

11:1.text: Alif Lām Rā’. A Book – its verses have been clearly composed (and) then made distinct – (sent down) from One (who is) wise, aware.

11:2.text: ‘Do not serve (anyone) but God! Surely I am a warner and bringer of good news to you from Him.’

11:3.text: And: ‘Ask forgiveness from your Lord, then turn to Him (in repentance). He will give you good enjoyment (of life) for an appointed time, and give His favor to everyone (deserving) of favor. If you turn away – surely I fear for you the punishment of a great Day.

11:4.text: To God is your return. He is powerful over everything.’

11:5.text: Is it not a fact that they cover their hearts to hide from Him? Is it not (a fact) that (even) when they cover themselves with their clothing, He knows what they keep secret and what they speak aloud? Surely He knows what is in the hearts.

11:6.text: (There is) not a creature on the earth but its provision (depends) on God. He knows its dwelling place and its storage place. Everything is (recorded) in a clear Book.

11:7.text: He (it is) who created the heavens and the earth in six days – and His throne was upon the water – that He might test you (to see) which of you is best in deed. If indeed you say, ‘Surely you will be raised up after death,’ those who disbelieve will indeed say, ‘This is nothing but clear magic.’

11:8.text: If indeed We postpone the punishment from them until a set period (of time), they will indeed say, ‘What is holding it back?’ Is it not (a fact) that on the Day when it comes to them, it will not be diverted from them, and what they were mocking will overwhelm them?

11:9.text: If indeed We give a person a taste of mercy from Us, (and) then We withdraw it from him, surely he is indeed despairing (and) ungrateful.

11:10.text: But if indeed We give him a taste of blessing, after hardship has touched him, he will indeed say, ‘The evils have gone from me.’ Surely he is indeed gloating (and) boastful

11:11.text: – except those who are patient and do righteous deeds. Those – for them (there is) forgiveness and a great reward.

11:12.text: Perhaps you are leaving out part of what you are inspired (with), and your heart is weighed down by it, because they say, ‘If only a treasure were sent down on him or an angel came with him.’ You are only a warner. God is guardian over everything.

11:13.text: Or do they say, ‘He has forged it’? Say: ‘Then bring ten sūras forged like it, and call on whomever you can, other than God, if you are truthful.’

11:14.text: If they do not respond to you, know that it has been sent down with the knowledge of God, and that (there is) no god but Him. So (will) you submit?

11:15.text: Whoever desires this present life and its (passing) splendor – We shall pay them in full for their deeds in it, and they will not be shortchanged in it.

11:16.text: Those are the ones who – for them there is nothing in the Hereafter but the Fire. What they have done will come to nothing there. What they have done will be in vain.

11:17.text: Is the one who (stands) on a clear sign from his Lord, and recites it as a witness from Him, and before it was the Book of Moses as a model and mercy [. . .]? Those believe in it, but whoever disbelieves in it from the factions – the Fire is his appointed place. So do not be in doubt about it. Surely it is the truth from your Lord, but most of the people do not believe.

11:18.text: Who is more evil than the one who forges a lie against God? Those will be presented before their Lord, and the witnesses will say, ‘These are those who lied against their Lord.’ Is it not (a fact) that the curse of God is on the evildoers,

11:19.text: who keep (people) from the way of God and desire (to make) it crooked, and they are disbelievers in the Hereafter?

11:20.text: Those – they cannot escape (Him) on the earth, and they have no allies other than God. The punishment will be doubled for them. They could not hear and did not see.

11:21.text: Those are the ones who have lost their (own) selves, and what they forged has abandoned them.

11:22.text: (There is) no doubt that they will be the worst losers in the Hereafter.

11:23.text: Surely those who believe, and do righteous deeds, and humble themselves to their Lord – those are the companions of the Garden. There they will remain.

11:24.text: The parable of the two groups is like the blind and the deaf, and the sighted and the hearing. Are they equal in comparison? Will you not take heed?

11:25.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people: ‘I am a clear warner for you.

11:26.text: Do not serve (anyone) but God! Surely I fear for you the punishment of a painful Day.’

11:27.text: The assembly of those who disbelieved of his people said, ‘We do not see you as (anything) but a human being like us, and we do not see following you (any) but the worst (and) most gullible of us. We do not see in you any superiority over us. No! We think you are liars.’

11:28.text: He said, ‘My people! Do you see? If I (stand) on a clear sign from my Lord, and He has given me mercy from Himself, but it has been obscured for you, shall we compel you (to accept) it when you are unwilling?

11:29.text: My people! I do not ask you for any money for it. My reward (depends) only on God. I am not going to drive away those who believe. Surely they are going to meet their Lord, but I see that you are an ignorant people.

11:30.text: My people! Who would help me against God if I drove them away? Will you not take heed?

11:31.text: I do not say to you, “I possess the storehouses of God,” nor do I know the unseen. And I do not say, “I am an angel,” nor do I say to those your eyes look down on, “God will not give them any good.” God knows what is in them. Surely then I would indeed be one of the evildoers.’

11:32.text: They said, ‘Noah! You have disputed with us, and disputed (too) much with us. Bring us what you promise us, if you are one of the truthful.’

11:33.text: He said, ‘Only God will bring it to you, if He (so) pleases, and you will not escape.

11:34.text: My advice will not benefit you – (even) if I wish to advise you – if God wishes to make you err. He is your Lord, and to Him you will be returned.’

11:35.text: Or do they say, ‘He has forged it’? Say: ‘If I have forged it, my sin is on me, but I am free of the sins you commit.’

11:36.text: And Noah was inspired: ‘None of your people will believe, except for the one who has (already) believed, so do not be distressed by what they have done.

11:37.text: Build the ship under Our eyes and Our inspiration, and do not address Me concerning those who have done evil. Surely they are going to be drowned.’

11:38.text: And he was building the ship, and whenever the assembly of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him. He said, ‘If you ridicule us, surely we shall ridicule you as you ridicule.

11:39.text: Soon you will know (on) whom punishment will come, disgracing him, and on whom a lasting punishment will descend.’

11:40.text: – Until, when Our command came and the oven boiled, We said, ‘Load into it two of every kind, a pair, and your family – except for the one against whom the word has (already) gone forth – and whoever has believed.’ But only a few had believed with him.

11:41.text: And he said, ‘Sail in it! In the name of God (is) its running and its anchoring. Surely my Lord is indeed forgiving, compassionate.’

11:42.text: It ran with them in (the midst of) wave(s) like mountains, and Noah called out to his son, since he was in a place apart, ‘My son! Sail with us and do not be with the disbelievers!’

11:43.text: He said, ‘I shall take refuge on a mountain (that) will protect me from the water.’ He said, ‘(There is) no protector today from the command of God, except for the one on whom He has compassion.’ And the waves came between them, and he was among the drowned.

11:44.text: And it was said: ‘Earth! Swallow your water! And sky! Stop!’ And the waters subsided, and the command was accomplished, and it came to rest on al-Jūdī. And it was said: ‘Away with the people who were evildoers!’

11:45.text: And Noah called out to his Lord, and said, ‘My Lord, surely my son is one of my family, and surely Your promise is the truth, and You are the most just of judges.’

11:46.text: He said, ‘Noah! Surely he is not one of your family. Surely it is an unrighteous deed. So do not ask Me about what you have no knowledge of. Surely I admonish you not to be one of the ignorant.’

11:47.text: He said, ‘My Lord, surely I take refuge with You for asking You about what I have no knowledge of, and unless You forgive me and have compassion on me, I shall be one of the losers.’

11:48.text: It was said, ‘Noah! Go down with peace from Us, and blessings on you and on the communities of those who are with you. But (to other) communities We shall give enjoyment (of life), (and) then a painful punishment from Us will touch them.’

11:49.text: That is one of the stories of the unseen. We inspired you (with) it. You did not know it, (neither) you nor your people, before (this). So be patient. Surely the outcome (belongs) to the ones who guard (themselves).

11:50.text: And to ‘Ād (We sent) their brother Hūd. He said, ‘My people! Serve God! You have no god other than Him. You are nothing but forgers (of lies).

11:51.text: My people! I do not ask you for any reward for it. My reward (depends) only on the One who created me. Will you not understand?’

11:52.text: And: ‘My people! Ask forgiveness from your Lord, (and) then turn to Him (in repentance). He will send the sky (down) on you in abundance (of rain), and increase you in strength upon your strength. Do not turn away as sinners.’

11:53.text: They said, ‘Hūd! You have not brought us any clear sign, and we are not going to abandon our gods on your saying, (for) we do not believe in you.

11:54.text: We (can) only say (that) one of our gods has seized you with evil.’ He said, ‘Surely I call God to witness, and you bear witness (too), that I am free of what you associate,

11:55.text: other than Him. So plot against me, all of you, (and) then do not spare me.

11:56.text: Surely I have put my trust in God, my Lord and your Lord. (There is) no creature He does not seize by its hair. Surely my Lord is on a straight path.

11:57.text: If you turn away, I have delivered to you what I was sent to you with, and my Lord will make another people succeed you, and you will not harm Him at all. Surely my Lord is a watcher over everything.’

11:58.text: And when Our command came, We rescued Hūd, and those who believed with him, by a mercy from Us, and We rescued them from a stern punishment.

11:59.text: That was ‘Ād: they denied the signs of their Lord, and disobeyed His messengers, and followed the command of every stubborn tyrant.

11:60.text: And they were followed in this world (by) a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection: ‘Is it not a fact that ‘Ād disbelieved their Lord? Is it not, “Away with ‘Ād, the people of Hūd”?’

11:61.text: And to Thamūd (We sent) their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, ‘My people! Serve God! You have no god other than Him. He produced you from the earth and settled you in it. So ask forgiveness from Him, (and) then turn to Him (in repentance). Surely my Lord is near (and) responsive.’

11:62.text: They said, ‘Ṣāliḥ! You were among us as someone in whom hope was placed before. Do you forbid us to serve what our fathers have served? Surely we are in grave doubt indeed about what you call us to.’

11:63.text: He said, ‘My people! Do you see? If I (stand) on a clear sign from my Lord, and He has given me mercy from Himself, who would help me against God if I disobeyed Him? You would only increase my loss.

11:64.text: My people! This is the she-camel of God, a sign for you. Let her graze on God’s earth, and do not touch her with evil, or a punishment near (at hand) will seize you.’

11:65.text: But they wounded her, and he said, ‘Enjoy (yourselves) in your home(s) for three days – that is a promise not to be denied.’

11:66.text: And when Our command came, We rescued Ṣāliḥ, and those who believed with him, by a mercy from Us, and from the disgrace of that day. Surely your Lord – He is the Strong, the Mighty.

11:67.text: And the cry seized those who did evil, and morning found them leveled in their homes.

11:68.text: (It was) as if they had not lived in prosperity there. ‘Is it not a fact that Thamūd disbelieved their Lord? Is it not, “Away with Thamūd”?’

11:69.text: Certainly Our messengers brought Abraham the good news. They said, ‘Peace!’ He said, ‘Peace!,’ and did not delay in bringing a roasted calf.

11:70.text: When he saw their hands not reaching for it, he became suspicious of them and began to feel fear of them. They said, ‘Do not fear! Surely we have been sent to the people of Lot.’

11:71.text: His wife was standing (there), and she laughed. And so We gave her the good news of Isaac, and after Isaac, Jacob.

11:72.text: She said, ‘Woe is me! Shall I give birth when I am an old woman and my husband here is an old man? Surely this is an amazing thing indeed!’

11:73.text: They said, ‘Are you surprised by the command of God? The mercy of God and His blessings (be) upon you, People of the House! Surely He is indeed praiseworthy, glorious.’

11:74.text: When the fright had left Abraham and the good news had come to him, he was disputing with Us concerning the people of Lot.

11:75.text: Surely Abraham was indeed tolerant, kind, (and) turning (in repentance).

11:76.text: ‘Abraham! Turn away from this! Surely it has come – the command of your Lord. Surely they – a punishment is coming upon them which cannot be turned back.’

11:77.text: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he became distressed about them, and felt powerless (to protect) them, and he said, ‘This is a hard day.’

11:78.text: His people came to him, rushing to him, (for) they had been in the habit of doing evil deeds before (this). He said, ‘My people! These are my daughters, they are purer for you. So guard (yourselves) against God, and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is (there) no one among you of right mind?’

11:79.text: They said, ‘Certainly you know that we have no right to your daughters, and surely you know indeed what we want.’

11:80.text: He said, ‘If only I had the strength for you, or could take refuge in a strong supporter!’

11:81.text: They said, ‘Lot! Surely we are messengers of your Lord. They will not reach you. So journey with your family in a part of the night, and let none of you turn around, except your wife, (for) surely what is about to smite them is going to smite her. Surely their appointment is the morning. Is the morning not near?’

11:82.text: So when Our command came, We turned it upside down, and rained on it stones of baked clay, one after another,

11:83.text: marked in the presence of your Lord. It is not far from the evildoers.

11:84.text: And to Midian (We sent) their brother Shu‘ayb. He said, ‘My people! Serve God! You have no god other than Him. Do not diminish the measure or the scale. Surely I see you in prosperity, but surely I fear for you the punishment of an overwhelming day.

11:85.text: My people! Fill up the measure and the scale in justice, and do not shortchange the people of their wealth, and do not act wickedly on the earth, fomenting corruption.

11:86.text: A remnant of God is better for you, if you are believers. I am not a watcher over you.’

11:87.text: They said, ‘Shu‘ayb! Does your prayer command you that we should abandon what our fathers have served, or that (we should abandon) doing what we please with our wealth? Surely you – you indeed are the tolerant (and) right-minded one.’

11:88.text: He said, ‘My people! Have you considered? If I (stand) on a clear sign from my Lord, and He has provided me with good provision from Himself [. . .]. I do not wish to go behind your backs to (do) what I forbid you from. I only wish to set (things) right, as much as I am able, but my success is only with God. In Him I have put my trust, and to Him I turn (in repentance).

11:89.text: My people! Do not let my defiance (of you) provoke you to sin, or something will smite you similar to what smote the people of Noah, or the people of Hūd, or the people of Ṣāliḥ. And the people of Lot are not far from you.

11:90.text: Ask forgiveness from your Lord, (and) then turn to Him (in repentance). Surely my Lord is compassionate, loving.’

11:91.text: They said, ‘Shu‘ayb! We do not understand much of what you say. Surely we indeed see you as weak among us. But (for) your gang (of followers) we would indeed have stoned you, (for) you are not mighty against us.’

11:92.text: He said, ‘My people! Is my gang (of followers) mightier against you than God? Have you taken Him (as something to cast) behind you? Surely my Lord encompasses what you do.

11:93.text: My people! Do as you are able. Surely I am going to do (what I can). Soon you will know the one (on) whom punishment will come, disgracing him, and the one who is a liar. (Just) watch! Surely I am watching with you.’

11:94.text: And when Our command came, We rescued Shu‘ayb, and those who believed with him, by a mercy from Us. And the cry seized those who did evil, and morning found them leveled in their homes.

11:95.text: (It was) as if they had not lived there. ‘Is it not away with Midian, (just) as Thamūd was done away with?’

11:96.text: Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs and clear authority

11:97.text: to Pharaoh and his assembly, but they followed the command of Pharaoh, when the command of Pharaoh was not right-minded.

11:98.text: He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection, and lead them to the Fire. Evil is the place (to which they are) led!

11:99.text: They were followed in this (world by) a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection – evil is the gift (they will be) given!

11:100.text: That is from the stories of the towns (which) We recount to you. Some of them are (still) standing and some (are already) cut down.

11:101.text: Yet We did not do them evil, but they did themselves evil. Their gods, on whom they called instead of God, were of no use to them at all, when the command of your Lord came, and they only added to their ruin.

11:102.text: Such was the seizing of your Lord, when He seized the towns while they were doing evil. Surely His seizing was painful (and) harsh.

11:103.text: Surely in that is a sign indeed for whoever fears the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day to which the people will be gathered, and that is a Day (that will be) witnessed.

11:104.text: We postpone it only for a set time.

11:105.text: (When that) Day comes, no one will speak, except by His permission. Some of them will be miserable, and some happy.

11:106.text: As for those who are miserable, (they will be) in the Fire, where (there will be) a moaning and panting for them,

11:107.text: remaining there as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except as your Lord pleases. Surely your Lord accomplishes whatever He pleases.

11:108.text: But as for those who are happy, (they will be) in the Garden, there to remain as long as the heavens and earth endure, except as your Lord pleases – an unceasing gift.

11:109.text: Do not be in doubt about what these (people) serve: they only serve as their fathers served before (them). Surely We shall indeed pay them their portion in full, undiminished.

11:110.text: Certainly We gave Moses the Book, and then differences arose about it. Were it not for a preceding word from your Lord, it would indeed have been decided between them. Surely they are in grave doubt indeed about it.

11:111.text: Surely each (of them) – when your Lord will indeed pay them in full for their deeds. Surely He is aware of what they do.

11:112.text: So go straight, as you have been commanded, (you) and those who have turned (in repentance) with you. Do not transgress insolently, (for) surely He sees what you do.

11:113.text: Do not incline toward those who do evil, or the Fire will touch you – you have no allies other than God – (and) then you will not be helped.

11:114.text: And observe the prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Surely good (deeds) take away evil (ones). That is a reminder to the mindful.

11:115.text: And be patient. Surely God does not let the reward of the doers of good go to waste.

11:116.text: If only there had been a remnant of men, among the generations before you, to forbid the (fomenting of) corruption on the earth – aside from a few of those whom We rescued among them. But those who did evil pursued what luxury they were given to delight in, and became sinners.

11:117.text: Yet your Lord was not one to destroy the towns in an evil manner, while its people were setting (things) right.

11:118.text: If your Lord had (so) pleased, He would indeed have made the people one community, but they will continue to differ,

11:119.text: except for the one on whom your Lord has compassion, and for that (purpose) He created them. But the word of your Lord is fulfilled: ‘I shall indeed fill Gehenna with jinn and people – all (of them)!’

11:120.text: Everything We recount to you from the stories of the messengers (is) what We make firm your heart with, and by this means the truth has come to you, and an admonition, and a reminder to the believers.

11:121.text: Say to those who do not believe: ‘Do as you are able. Surely we are going to do (what we can).’

11:122.text: And: ‘(Just) wait! Surely we (too) are waiting.’

11:123.text: To God (belongs) the unseen in the heavens and the earth, and to Him the affair – all of it – will be returned. So serve Him and put your trust in Him! Your Lord is not oblivious of what you do.

71:1.text: Surely We sent Noah to his people: ‘Warn your people before a painful punishment comes upon them.’

71:2.text: He said, ‘My people! I am a clear warner for you.

71:3.text: Serve God, and guard (yourselves) against Him, and obey me!

71:4.text: He will forgive you your sins, and spare you until an appointed time. Surely the time of God, when it comes, cannot be postponed. If (only) you knew!’

71:5.text: He said, ‘My Lord, surely I have called my people night and day,

71:6.text: but my calling has only increased them in flight.

71:7.text: Surely I – whenever I called them, so that You might forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, and covered themselves with their clothes, and persisted (in disbelief), and became very arrogant.

71:8.text: Then surely I called them publicly,

71:9.text: then surely I spoke openly to them, and I confided to them in secret,

71:10.text: and I said, “Ask forgiveness from your Lord, surely He is forgiving,

71:11.text: and He will send the sky (down) on you in abundance,

71:12.text: and increase you with wealth and sons, and make gardens for you, and make rivers for you.

71:13.text: What is (the matter) with you that do not expect seriousness (of purpose) on the part of God,

71:14.text: when He created you in stages?

71:15.text: Do you not see how God created seven heavens in stories,
