71:16.text: and placed the moon in them as a light, and placed the sun (in them) as a lamp?

71:17.text: And God caused you to grow from of the earth,

71:18.text: (and) then He will return you into it, and bring you forth again.

71:19.text: God has made the earth an expanse for you,

71:20.text: so that you may traverse its open (path)ways.”’

71:21.text: Noah said, ‘My Lord, surely they have disobeyed me, and followed one whose wealth and children increase him only in loss,

71:22.text: and they have schemed a great scheme,

71:23.text: and said, “Do not forsake your gods, and do not forsake Wadd, nor Suwā’, nor Yaghūth, and Ya‘ūq, and Nasr.”

71:24.text: And they have led many astray. Increase the evildoers only in going astray!’

71:25.text: They were drowned on account of their sins, and forced to enter a fire, and they found they had no helpers other than God.

71:26.text: Noah said, ‘My Lord, do not leave any of the disbelievers as an inhabitant on the earth.

71:27.text: Surely You – if You leave them, they will lead Your servants astray, and will give birth only to depraved disbeliever(s).

71:28.text: My Lord, forgive me and my parents, and whoever enters my house believing, and the believing men and the believing women, and increase the evildoers only in destruction!’

46:2.text: The sending down of the Book is from God, the Mighty, the Wise.

46:3.text: We did not create the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, except in truth and (for) an appointed time, but those who disbelieve are turning away from what they are warned of.

46:4.text: Say: ‘Do you see what you call on instead of God? Show me what (part) of the earth they have created. Or do they have any partnership in (the creation of) the heavens? Bring me any Book before this (one) or any trace of knowledge, if you are truthful.’

46:5.text: Who is farther astray than the one who, instead of God, calls on those who will not respond to him until the Day of Resurrection, while they are (otherwise) oblivious of their calling?

46:6.text: When the people are gathered, they will be enemies to them, and will deny their service.

46:7.text: When Our signs are recited to them as clear signs, those who disbelieve say to the truth, when it has come to them, ‘This is clear magic.’

46:8.text: Or do they say, ‘He has forged it’? Say: ‘If I have forged it, you (would) have no power at all to (help) me against God. He knows what you are busy with. He is sufficient as a witness between me and you. He is the Forgiving, the Compassionate.’

46:9.text: Say: ‘I am not the first of the messengers, and I do not know what will be done with me or with you. I only follow what I am inspired (with). I am only a clear warner.’

46:10.text: Say: ‘Do you see? If it is from God, and you disbelieve in it, and a witness from the Sons of Israel has borne witness to (a Book) like it, and believed, and you become arrogant – surely God does not guide the people who are evildoers.’

46:11.text: Those who disbelieve say to those who believe, ‘If it had been something good, they would not have gotten to it before us’ – even when they are not guided by it. And they say, ‘This is an old lie!’

46:12.text: Yet before it was the Book of Moses as a model and mercy; and this is a Book confirming (it) in the Arabic language, to warn those who do evil, and as good news for the doers of good.

46:13.text: Surely those who say, ‘Our Lord is God,’ (and) then go straight – (there will be) no fear on them, nor will they sorrow.

46:14.text: Those are the companions of the Garden, there to remain – a payment for what they have done.

46:15.text: We have charged each person (to do) good to his parents – his mother bore him with difficulty, and she delivered him with difficulty – his bearing and his weaning are thirty months – until, when he reaches his maturity, and reaches forty years, he says, ‘My Lord, (so) dispose me that I may be thankful for your blessing with which You have blessed me and my parents, and that I may do righteousness pleasing to You, and do right by me concerning my descendants. Surely I turn to You (in repentance), and surely I am one of those who submit.’

46:16.text: Those are the ones from whom We shall accept the best of what they have done, and We shall pass over their evil deeds. (They will be) among the companions of the Garden – the promise of truth which they were promised.

46:17.text: But the one who says to his parents, ‘Uff to both of you! Do you promise me that I shall be brought forth, when generations have already passed away before me?,’ while both of them call on God for help: ‘Woe to you! Believe! Surely the promise of God is true!,’ and he says, ‘This is nothing but old tales’ –

46:18.text: those are the ones against whom the word has proved true about the communities of jinn and humankind (which) have already passed away before them. Surely they were losers.

46:19.text: For each (there are) ranks, according to what they have done, and so that He may pay them in full for their deeds – and they will not be done evil.

46:20.text: On the Day when those who disbelieve are presented to the Fire: ‘You squandered your good things in your present life, and enjoyed them. So today you will be paid the punishment of humiliation because you became arrogant on the earth without any right, and because you have acted wickedly.’

46:21.text: Remember the brother of ‘Ād: When he warned his people at the sand dunes – and warners had already passed away before him and after him – (saying): ‘Do not serve (anyone) but God! Surely I fear for you the punishment of a great Day.’

46:22.text: They said, ‘Have you come to defraud us of our gods? Bring us what you promise us, if you are one of the truthful.’

46:23.text: He said, ‘The knowledge (of it) is only with God. I deliver to you what I was sent with, but I see you are an ignorant people.’

46:24.text: When they saw it as a cloud approaching their wādīs, they said, ‘This is a cloud (which) is going to give us rain.’ ‘No! It is what you were seeking to hurry – a wind in which (there is) a painful punishment,

46:25.text: destroying everything by the command of its Lord.’ And morning found them not to be seen, except for their dwelling places. In this way We repay the people who are sinners.

46:26.text: Certainly We had established them with what We have not established you, and We gave them hearing and sight and hearts. Yet their hearing and their sight and their hearts were of no use to them at all, since they denied the signs of God, and what they were mocking overwhelmed them.

46:27.text: Certainly We destroyed the towns around you, and varied the signs so that they might return.

46:28.text: Why did they not help them – those gods whom they had taken, instead of God, as a (means of) drawing near (to Him)? No! They abandoned them. That was their lie and what they had forged.

46:29.text: (Remember) when We turned a band of jinn to you to listen to the Qur’ān: When they were in its presence, they said, ‘Be silent!’ And when it was finished, they turned back to their people as warners.

46:30.text: They said, ‘Our people! Surely We have heard a Book (which) has been sent down after Moses, confirming what was before it, guiding to the truth and to a straight road.

46:31.text: Our people! Respond to the caller of God, and believe in Him. He will forgive you some of your sins, and protect you from a painful punishment.

46:32.text: Whoever does not respond to the caller of God – there is no escaping (Him) on the earth, and he has no allies other than Him. Those are clearly astray.’

46:33.text: Do they not see that God, who created the heavens and earth, and was not tired out by their creation, is able to give the dead life? Yes indeed! Surely He is powerful over everything.

46:34.text: On the Day when those who disbelieve are presented to the Fire: ‘Is this not the truth?’ They will say, ‘Yes indeed! By our Lord!’ He will say, ‘Taste the punishment for what you have disbelieved.’

46:35.text: Be patient, as the messengers of firm resolve were (also) patient. Do not seek to hurry it for them. On the Day when they see what they are promised, (it will seem) as if they had remained (in the grave) for only an hour of the day. A delivery! Will any be destroyed but the people who are wicked?

37:1.text: By the ones who line up in lines,

37:2.text: and the shouters of a shout,

37:3.text: and the reciters of a reminder!

37:5.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and of whatever is between them, and Lord of the Easts.

37:6.text: Surely We have made the sky of this world appear enticing by means of the splendor of the stars,

37:7.text: and (We have made them) a (means of) protection from every rebelling satan.

37:8.text: They do not listen to the exalted Assembly,

37:9.text: but they are pelted from every side, driven off – for them (there is) punishment forever –

37:10.text: except for the one who snatches a word, and then a piercing flame pursues him.

37:11.text: So ask them for a pronouncement: ‘Are they a stronger creation, or those (others) whom We have created?’ Surely We created them from sticky clay.

37:12.text: But you are amazed when they ridicule,

37:13.text: and, when they are reminded, do not take heed,

37:14.text: and, when they see a sign, ridicule,

37:15.text: and say, ‘This is nothing but clear magic.

37:16.text: When we are dead, and turned to dust and bones, shall we indeed be raised up?

37:18.text: Say: ‘Yes, and you will be humbled.’

37:19.text: (For) it will only be a single shout, and suddenly they will see,

37:20.text: and say, ‘Woe to us! This is the Day of Judgment.’

37:21.text: ‘This is the Day of Decision, which you called a lie.

37:22.text: Gather those who have done evil, and their wives, and what they used to serve,

37:23.text: instead of God, and guide them to the path of the Furnace.

37:24.text: And stop them (there), (for) they are to be questioned:

37:25.text: “What is (the matter) with you (that) you do you not help each other?”

37:27.text: Some of them will approach others asking each other questions.

37:28.text: They will say, ‘Surely you used to come to us from the right (side).’

37:29.text: They will say, ‘No! You were not believers.

37:30.text: We had no authority over you. No! You were a people who transgressed insolently.

37:31.text: So the word of our Lord has proved true against us. Surely we are indeed tasting (it).

37:32.text: We made you err (because) we were in error.’

37:33.text: Surely on that Day they will be partners in the punishment.

37:34.text: Surely in this way We deal with the sinners.

37:35.text: Surely they – when it was said to them, ‘(There is) no god but God,’ they became arrogant,

37:36.text: and said, ‘Are we to abandon our gods for a possessed poet?’

37:37.text: ‘No! He has brought the truth and confirmed the envoys.

37:38.text: Justice you will indeed taste the painful punishment,

37:39.text: and you will not be repaid (for anything) except what you have done.’

37:40.text: – Except for the devoted servants of God.

37:41.text: Those – for them (there will be) a known provision

37:42.text: (of) fruits, and they will be honored

37:45.text: (and) a cup from a flowing spring will be passed around among them –

37:47.text: (there is) no ill effect in it, nor do they become drunk from it.

37:48.text: With them (there will be maidens) restraining (their) glances, wide-eyed,

37:49.text: as if they were hidden eggs.

37:50.text: Some of them will approach others asking each other questions.

37:51.text: One of them will say, ‘Surely I had a comrade,

37:52.text: who used to say, “Are you indeed one of the confirmers?

37:53.text: When we are dead, and turned to dust and bones, shall we indeed be judged?”’

37:55.text: So he looks (down) and sees him in the midst of the Furnace.

37:56.text: He will say, ‘By God! You nearly brought me to ruin.

37:57.text: Were it not for the blessing of my Lord, I (too) would have been one of those brought forward (to the punishment).

37:59.text: except for our first death, and are we not punished?

37:60.text: Surely this – it indeed is the great triumph!

37:61.text: Let the workers work for something like this!’

37:62.text: Is that better as a reception, or the tree of al-Zaqqūm?

37:63.text: Surely We have made it a test for the evildoers.

37:64.text: It is a tree which comes forth from the root of the Furnace.

37:65.text: Its fruits are like the heads of the satans,

37:66.text: and they eat from it, and fill their bellies from it.

37:67.text: Then on (top of) it they have a drink of boiling (water).

37:68.text: Then their return is to the Furnace.

37:69.text: Surely they found their fathers astray,

37:70.text: and they run in their footsteps.

37:71.text: Certainly most of those of old went astray before them,

37:72.text: even though We sent warners among them.

37:73.text: See how the end was for those who were warned

37:74.text: – except for the devoted servants of God.

37:75.text: Certainly Noah called on Us, and excellent indeed were the responders!

37:76.text: We rescued him and his family from great distress,

37:77.text: and We made his descendants – they were the survivors.

37:78.text: We left (this blessing) upon him among the later (generations):

37:80.text: In this way We repay the doers of good.

37:81.text: Surely he was one of Our believing servants.

37:83.text: Surely Abraham was indeed of his party:

37:84.text: When he came to his Lord with a sound heart,

37:85.text: when he said to his father and his people, ‘What do you serve?

37:86.text: (Is it) a lie – gods other than God – you desire?

37:87.text: What do you think about the Lord of the worlds?’

37:88.text: And he took a look at the stars,

37:90.text: So they turned away from him, withdrawing.

37:91.text: But he turned to their gods, and said, ‘Do you not eat?

37:92.text: What is (the matter) with you (that) you do not speak?’

37:93.text: So he turned on them, striking (them) with the right (hand).

37:94.text: Then they came running to him.

37:95.text: He said, ‘Do you serve what you carve,

37:96.text: when God created you and what you make?’

37:97.text: They said, ‘Build a building for him, and cast him into the blazing (Fire)!’

37:98.text: They intended a plot against him, but We brought them down.

37:99.text: He said, ‘Surely I am going to my Lord. He will guide me.

37:100.text: My Lord, grant me one of the righteous.’

37:101.text: So We gave him the good news of a forbearing boy.

37:102.text: When he had reached the (age of) running with him, he said, ‘My son! Surely I saw in a dream that I am going to sacrifice you. So look, what do you think?’ He said, ‘My father! Do what you are commanded. You will find me, if God pleases, one of the patient.’

37:103.text: When they both had submitted, and he had laid him face down,

37:105.text: Now you have confirmed the vision. Surely in this way We repay the doers of good.

37:106.text: Surely this – it indeed was the clear test.’

37:107.text: And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice,

37:108.text: and left (this blessing) on him among the later (generations):

37:110.text: In this way We repay the doers of good.

37:111.text: Surely he was one of Our believing servants.

37:112.text: And We gave him the good news of Isaac, a prophet, one of the righteous.

37:113.text: We blessed him and Isaac, and some of their descendants are doers of good, and some clearly do themselves evil.

37:114.text: Certainly We bestowed favor on Moses and Aaron,

37:115.text: and We rescued them and their people from the great distress.

37:116.text: We helped them, and they were the victors.

37:117.text: We gave them both the clarifying Book,

37:118.text: and guided them to the straight path,

37:119.text: and left (this blessing) on both of them among the later (generations):

37:121.text: In this way We repay the doers of good.

37:122.text: Surely they were two of Our believing servants.

37:123.text: Surely Elijah was indeed one of the envoys:

37:124.text: When he said to his people, ‘Will you not guard (yourselves)?

37:125.text: Do you call on Baal, and abandon the best of creators

37:126.text: – God – your Lord and the Lord of your fathers of old?’

37:127.text: Yet they called him a liar. Surely they will indeed be brought forward (to the punishment)

37:128.text: – except for the devoted servants of God.

37:129.text: And We left (this blessing) on him among the later (generations):

37:131.text: In this way We repay the doers of good.

37:132.text: Surely he was one of Our believing servants.

37:133.text: Surely Lot was indeed one of the envoys:

37:134.text: When We rescued him and his family – all (of them) –

37:135.text: except for an old woman among those who stayed behind.

37:137.text: Surely you indeed pass near them in the morning

37:138.text: and in the night. Will you not understand?

37:139.text: Surely Jonah was indeed one of the envoys:

37:140.text: When he ran away to the loaded ship,

37:141.text: and cast lots, but was one of the losers.

37:142.text: So the fish swallowed him, seeing that he was to blame.

37:143.text: Were it not that he was one of those who glorified (God),

37:144.text: he would indeed have remained in its belly until the Day when they are raised up.

37:145.text: But We tossed him on the desert (shore) while he was (still) sick,

37:146.text: and We caused a gourd tree to grow over him.

37:147.text: We sent him to a hundred thousand, or more,

37:148.text: and they believed. So We gave them enjoyment (of life) for a time.

37:149.text: Ask them for a pronouncement: ‘Does your Lord have daughters while they have sons?

37:150.text: Or did We create the angels female while they were witnesses?’

37:151.text: Is it not a fact that out of their own lie they indeed say,

37:152.text: ‘God has begotten’? Surely they are liars indeed!

37:153.text: Has He chosen daughters over sons?

37:154.text: What is (the matter) with you? How do you judge?

37:156.text: Or do you have any clear authority?

37:157.text: Bring your Book, if you are truthful.

37:158.text: They have fabricated an affiliation between Him and the jinn. Yet certainly the jinn know that they will indeed be brought forward (to the punishment)

37:159.text: – glory to God above what they allege! –

37:160.text: except for the devoted servants of God.

37:161.text: Surely you and what you serve –

37:162.text: you will not tempt (anyone to rebellion) against Him,

37:163.text: except for the one who is (destined) to burn in the Furnace.

37:164.text: (There is) not one of us who does not have an assigned position.

37:165.text: Surely we – we indeed are the ones who line up,

37:166.text: and surely we – we indeed are the ones who glorify (God).

37:168.text: ‘If (only) we had a reminder from those of old,

37:169.text: we would indeed have been the devoted servants of God.’

37:170.text: Yet they have disbelieved in it. Soon they will know!

37:171.text: Certainly Our word has (already) gone forth to Our servants, the envoys.

37:172.text: Surely they – they indeed are the ones who will be helped.

37:173.text: Surely Our army – they indeed are the victors.

37:174.text: So turn away from them for a time,

37:175.text: and observe them. Soon they will observe!

37:176.text: Do they seek to hurry Our punishment?

37:177.text: When it comes down in their (own) courtyard, (how) evil the morning will be for those who were warned!

37:178.text: So turn from away them for a time,

37:179.text: and observe (them). Soon they will observe!

37:180.text: Glory to your Lord, Lord of honor, above what they allege!

37:182.text: and praise (be) to God, Lord of the worlds!

65:1.text: Prophet! When you divorce women, divorce them when they have reached (the end of) their waiting period. Count the waiting period, and guard (yourselves) against God your Lord. Do not expel them from their houses, nor let them leave, unless they commit clear immorality. Those are the limits (set by) God. Whoever transgresses the limits (set by) God has done himself evil. You do not know, perhaps after that God may bring about a new situation.

65:2.text: When they reach their term, either retain them rightfully, or part from them rightfully. Call in two of your just men as witnesses, and conduct the witnessing (as if) before God. That is what anyone who believes in God and the Last Day is admonished. Whoever guards (himself) against God – He will make a way out for him,

65:3.text: and will provide for him from where he was not expecting. Whoever puts his trust in God – He will be enough for him. Surely God attains his purpose. God has appointed a measure for everything.

65:4.text: (As for) those of your women who have no hope of (further) menstruation: if you are in doubt, their waiting period is three months, and (also for) those who have not (yet) menstruated. (As for) those who are pregnant, their term (is) when they deliver what they bear. Whoever guards (himself) against God – He will bring about some relief for him from His command.

65:5.text: That is the command of God, which He has sent down to you. Whoever guards (himself) against God – He will absolve him of his evil deeds, and make his reward great.

65:6.text: Let them reside where you are residing, according to your means, and do not treat them harshly, so that you cause distress for them. If they are pregnant, support them until they deliver what they bear. If they nurse (the child) for you, give them their payment, and consult together rightfully. But if you encounter difficulties, another woman will nurse (the child) for him.

65:7.text: Let a man of means spend out of his means, and whoever is limited in provision, let him spend out of what God has given him. God does not burden anyone except (according to) what He has given him. God will bring about some ease after hardship.

65:8.text: How many a town disdained the command of its Lord and His messengers, and We made a harsh reckoning with it, and punished it with a terrible punishment.

65:9.text: So it tasted the consequence of its action, and the result of its action was loss.

65:10.text: God prepared a harsh punishment for them. Guard (yourselves) against God, those (of you) with understanding! (You) who believe! God has sent down to you a reminder

65:11.text: – a messenger reciting over you the clear signs of God, so that He may bring those who believe and do righteous deeds out of the darkness to the light. Whoever believes in God and does righteousness – He will cause him to enter Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain forever. God has made good provision for him.

65:12.text: (It is) God who created seven heavens, and of the earth a similar (number) to them. The command descends between them, so that you may know that God is powerful over everything, and that God encompasses everything in knowledge.

10:1.text: Alif Lām Rā’. Those are the signs of the wise Book.

10:2.text: Is it amazing to the people that We have inspired a man from among them: ‘Warn the people, and give good news to those who believe, that for them (there is) a sure footing with their Lord’? (But) the disbelievers say, ‘Surely this (man) is a clear magician indeed.’

10:3.text: Surely your Lord is God, who created the heavens and the earth in six days. Then He sat down on the throne. He directs the (whole) affair. (There is) no intercessor without His permission. That is God, your Lord, so serve Him! Will you not take heed?

10:4.text: To Him is your return – all (of you) – the promise of God in truth! Surely He brought about the creation, (and) then He restores it, so that He may repay those who believe and do righteous deeds in justice. But those who disbelieve – for them (there is) a drink of boiling (water) and a painful punishment, because they were disbelieving.

10:5.text: He (it is) who made the sun an illumination, and the moon a light, and determined it by stations, so that you might know the number of the years and the reckoning (of time). God created that only in truth. He makes the signs distinct for a people who know.

10:6.text: Surely in the alternation of the night and the day, and (in) what God has created in the heavens and the earth, (there are) signs indeed for a people who guard (themselves).

10:7.text: Surely those who do not expect to meet Us, and are satisfied with this present life and feel secure in it, and those who are oblivious of Our signs,

10:8.text: those – their refuge is the Fire for what they have earned.

10:9.text: Surely those who believe and do righteous deeds – their Lord guides them for their belief. Beneath them rivers flow in Gardens of Bliss.

10:10.text: Their call there is: ‘Glory to You, God!,’ and their greeting there is: ‘Peace!,’ and the last (part) of their call is: ‘Praise (be) to God, Lord of the worlds!’

10:11.text: If God were to hurry the evil for the people, (as) their seeking to hurry the good, their time would indeed have been completed for them. But We leave those who do not expect the meeting with Us in their insolent transgression, wandering blindly.

10:12.text: When hardship touches a person, he calls on Us, (whether lying) on his side or sitting or standing. But when We have removed his hardship from him, he continues on, as if he had not called on Us about the hardship (that) had touched him. In this way what the wanton do is made to appear enticing to them.

10:13.text: Certainly We destroyed the generations before you when they did evil, when their messengers brought them the clear signs and they would not believe. In this way We repay the people who are sinners.

10:14.text: Then, after them, We made you successors on the earth, so that We might see how you would do.

10:15.text: When Our signs are recited to them as clear signs, those who do not expect the meeting with Us say, ‘Bring a different Qur’ān than this one, or change it.’ Say: ‘It is not for me to change it of my own accord. I only follow what I am inspired (with). Surely I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the punishment of a great Day.’

10:16.text: Say: ‘If God had (so) pleased, I would not have recited it to you, nor would He have made it known to you. I had already spent a lifetime among you before it (came to me). Will you not understand?’

10:17.text: Who is more evil than the one who forges a lie against God, or calls His signs a lie? Surely the sinners will not prosper.

10:18.text: They serve what neither harms them nor benefits them, instead of God (alone), and they say, ‘These are our intercessors with God.’ Say: ‘Will you inform God about what He does not know either in the heavens or on the earth? Glory to Him! He is exalted above what they associate.’

10:19.text: The people were (once) one community, then they differed. Were it not for a preceding word from your Lord, it would indeed have been decided between them concerning their differences.

10:20.text: They say, ‘If only a sign were sent down on him from his Lord.’ Say: ‘The unseen (belongs) only to God. (Just) wait! Surely I shall be one of those waiting with you.’

10:21.text: When We give the people a taste of mercy, after hardship has touched them, suddenly they (devise) some scheme against Our signs. Say: ‘God is quicker (at devising) a scheme. Surely Our messengers are writing down what you are scheming.’

10:22.text: He (it is) who enables you to travel on the shore and the sea, until, when you are on the ship, and they sail with them by means of a fair wind, and they gloat over it, a violent wind comes upon it and the waves come at them from every side, and they think they are encompassed by them. (Then) they call on God, devoting (their) religion to Him: ‘If indeed you rescue us from this, we shall indeed be among the thankful.’

10:23.text: Yet when He has rescued them, suddenly they become greedy on the earth without any right. People! Your envy is only against yourselves – (the fleeting) enjoyment of this present life. Then to Us is your return, and We shall inform you about what you have done.

10:24.text: A parable of this present life: (It is) is like water which We send down from the sky, and (there) mingles with it the vegetation of the earth from which the people and livestock eat, until, when the earth takes on its decoration and is adorned, and its people think that they have power over it, Our command comes on it by night or by day, and We cut it down, as if it had not flourished the day before. In this way We make the signs distinct for a people who reflect.

10:25.text: God calls to the Home of peace, and guides whomever He pleases to a straight path.

10:26.text: For those who have done good, (there is) the good (reward) and more (besides). Neither dust nor humiliation will cover their faces. Those are the companions of the Garden. There they will remain.

10:27.text: But those who have done evil deeds – (the) payment for an evil deed is (an evil) like it – humiliation will cover them. They will have no protector from God. (It will be) as if their faces were covered (with) pieces of the darkness of night. Those are the companions of the Fire. There they will remain.

10:28.text: On the Day when We shall gather them all together, then We shall say to those who associated: ‘(Take) your place, you and your associates!’ Then We shall separate them, and their associates will say, ‘You were not serving us.

10:29.text: God is sufficient as a witness between us and you that we were indeed oblivious of your service.’

10:30.text: There every person will stand trial (for) what he has done, and they will be returned to God, their true Protector, and (then) what they forged will abandon them.

10:31.text: Say: ‘Who provides for you from the sky and the earth? Or who has power over hearing and sight? Who brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living, and who directs the (whole) affair?’ Then they will say, ‘God.’ So say: ‘Will you not guard (yourselves)?’

10:32.text: That is God, your true Lord. And what (is there) after the truth except straying (from it)? How (is it that) you are turned away?

10:33.text: In this way the word of your Lord has proved true against those who acted wickedly: ‘They will not believe.’

10:34.text: Say: ‘(Is there) any of your associates who (can) bring about the creation, (and) then restore it?’ Say: ‘God – He brings about the creation, (and) then He restores it. How are you (so) deluded?’

10:35.text: Say: ‘(Is there) any of your associates who (can) guide to the truth?’ Say: ‘God – He guides to the truth. Is He who guides to the truth more worthy to be followed, or he who does not guide unless he is guided? What is (the matter) with you? How do you judge?’

10:36.text: Most of them only follow conjecture, (and) surely conjecture is of no use at all against the truth. Surely God is aware of what they do.

10:37.text: This Qur’ān is not the kind (of Book) that it could have been forged apart from God. (It is) a confirmation of what was before it, and a distinct setting forth of the Book – (there is) no doubt about it – from the Lord of the worlds.

10:38.text: Or do they say, ‘He has forged it’? Say: ‘Then bring a sūra like it, and call on anyone you can, other than God, if you are truthful.’

10:39.text: No! They have called a lie what they cannot encompass in (their) knowledge of it, and when the interpretation of it has not (yet) come to them. Those who were before them called (it) a lie (too), and see how the end was for the evildoers!

10:40.text: (There is) one of them who believes in it, and (there is) one of them who does not believe in it, but your Lord knows the ones who foment corruption.

10:41.text: If they call you a liar, say: ‘To me my deed, and to you your deed. You are free of what I do, and I am free of what you do.’

10:42.text: (There is) one of them who listens to you, but can you make the deaf hear, when they do not understand?

10:43.text: (There is) one of them who looks to you, but can you guide the blind, when they do not see?

10:44.text: Surely God does not do the people any evil at all, but the people do themselves evil.

10:45.text: On the Day when He gathers them, (it will seem) as if they had remained (in the grave) only for an hour of the day, (and) they will recognize each other. Lost (are) those who called the meeting with God a lie, and were not (rightly) guided.

10:46.text: Whether We show you some of that which We promise them, or take you, to Us is their return. Then God is a witness over what they do.

10:47.text: For every community (there is) a messenger. When their messenger comes, it will be decided between them in justice – and they will not be done evil.

10:48.text: They say, ‘When (will) this promise (come to pass), if you are truthful?’

10:49.text: Say: ‘I do not have power to (cause) myself harm or benefit – except whatever God pleases. For every community (there is) a time. When their time comes, they will not delay (it) by an hour, nor will they advance (it by an hour).’

10:50.text: Say: ‘Do you see? If His punishment comes to you by night or by day, what (part) of it would the sinners seek to hurry?

10:51.text: When it falls, will you believe in it? Now? When you had been seeking to hurry it?’

10:52.text: Then it will be said to those who have done evil: ‘Taste the punishment of eternity! Are you being repaid (for anything) except for what you have earned?’

10:53.text: They ask you to inform them: ‘Is it true?’ Say: ‘Yes, by my Lord! Surely it is true indeed! You will not escape (it).’

10:54.text: If each person who has done evil had all that is on the earth, he would indeed (try to) ransom (himself) with it. They will be full of secret regret when they see the punishment. It will be decided between them in justice – and they will not be done evil.

10:55.text: Is it not a fact that to God (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and the earth? Is it not a fact that the promise of God is true? But most of them do not know (it).

10:56.text: He gives life and causes death, and to Him you will be returned.

10:57.text: People! An admonition has come to you from your Lord, and a healing for what is in the hearts, and a guidance and mercy for the believers.

10:58.text: Say: ‘In the favor of God and in His mercy – let them gloat over that, (for) it is better than what they accumulate.’

10:59.text: Say: ‘Have you seen what God has sent down for you from (His) provision, yet you have made some of it forbidden and (some) permitted?’ Say: ‘Has God given permission to you, or do you forge (lies) against God?’

10:60.text: What will they think who forge lies against God on the Day of Resurrection? Surely God is indeed full of favor to the people, but most of them are not thankful (for it).

10:61.text: You are not (engaged) in any matter, nor do you recite any recitation of it, nor do you do any deed, except (that) We are witnesses over you when you are busy with it. Not (even) the weight of a speck on the earth or in the sky escapes from your Lord, nor (is there anything) smaller than that or greater, except (that it is recorded) in a clear Book.

10:62.text: Is it not a fact that the allies of God – (there will be) no fear on them, nor will they sorrow?

10:64.text: for them (there is) good news in this present life and in the Hereafter. No one can change the words of God. That is the great triumph!

10:65.text: Do not let their speech cause you sorrow. Surely honor (belongs) to God altogether. He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

10:66.text: Is it not a fact that to God (belongs) whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth? They follow – those who call on associates other than God – they only follow conjecture and they only guess.

10:67.text: He (it is) who made the night for you to rest in and the day to see. Surely in that are signs indeed for a people who hear.

10:68.text: They say, ‘God has taken a son.’ Glory to Him! He is the wealthy One. To Him (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. You have no authority for this (claim). Do you say about God what you do not know?

10:69.text: Say: ‘Surely those who forge lies against God will not prosper.’

10:70.text: A (little) enjoyment in this world, then to Us is their return. Then We (shall) make them taste the harsh punishment for what they have disbelieved.

10:71.text: Recite to them the story of Noah: when he said to his people, ‘My people! If my stay (here) and my reminding (you) by the signs of God are hard on you, yet in God have I put my trust. So put together your plan, (you) and your associates. Then do not let your plan (be a cause of) distress for you. Then decide about me and do not spare me.

10:72.text: If you turn away, (know that) I have not asked you for any reward. My reward (depends) only on God, and I have been commanded to be one of those who submit.’

10:73.text: But they called him a liar, so We rescued him and those who were with him in the ship, and We made them successors, and We drowned those who called Our signs a lie. See how the end was for the ones who were warned!

10:74.text: Then, after him, We raised up messengers for their people, and they brought them the clear signs. But they would not believe in what they had called a lie before. In this way We set a seal on the hearts of the transgressors.

10:75.text: Then, after them, We raised up Moses and Aaron for Pharaoh and his assembly with Our signs, but they became arrogant and were a sinful people.

10:76.text: When the truth came to them from Us, they said, ‘Surely this is clear magic indeed.’

10:77.text: Moses said, ‘Do you say (this) about the truth, when it has come to you? Is this magic? Yet magicians do not prosper.’

10:78.text: They said, ‘Have you come to us in order to turn us away from what we found our fathers doing, and (in order that) you two (might) have greatness on the earth? We do not believe in you two.’

10:79.text: And Pharaoh said, ‘Bring me every skilled magician.’

10:80.text: When the magicians came, Moses said to them, ‘Cast (down) what you are going to cast.’

10:81.text: Then, when they had cast, Moses said, ‘What you have brought is magic. Surely God will invalidate it. Surely God does not set right any deed of the fomenters of corruption.

10:82.text: God verifies the truth by His words, even though the sinners dislike (it).’

10:83.text: So no one believed in Moses, except for the descendants of his people, out of fear that Pharaoh and their assembly would persecute them. Surely Pharaoh was indeed haughty on the earth. Surely he was indeed one of the wanton.

10:84.text: Moses said, ‘My people! If you believe in God, put your trust in Him, if you have submitted.’

10:85.text: They said, ‘In God we have put our trust. Our Lord, do not make us an (object of) persecution for the people who are evildoers,

10:86.text: but rescue us by Your mercy from the people who are disbelievers.’

10:87.text: And We inspired Moses and his brother: ‘Establish houses for your external people in Egypt, and make your houses a direction (of prayer), and observe the prayer, and give good news to the believers.’

10:88.text: Moses said, ‘Our Lord, surely You have given Pharaoh and his assembly splendor and wealth in this present life, Our Lord, so that they might lead (people) astray from Your way. Our Lord, obliterate their wealth and harden their hearts, so that they do not believe until they see the painful punishment.’

10:89.text: He said, ‘The request of both of you has been answered. So both of you go straight, and do not follow the way of those who do not know.’

10:90.text: And We crossed the sea with the Sons of Israel, and Pharaoh and his forces followed them (out of) envy and enmity, until, when the drowning overtook him, he said, ‘I believe that (there is) no god but the One in whom the Sons of Israel believe. I am one of those who submit.’

10:91.text: ‘Now? When you had disobeyed before and were one of the fomenters of corruption?

10:92.text: Today We rescue you with your body, so that you may be a sign for those who succeed you. Yet surely many of the people are indeed oblivious of Our signs.’

10:93.text: Certainly We settled the Sons of Israel in a sure settlement and provided them with good things. They did not (begin to) differ until (after) the knowledge had come to them. Surely your Lord will decide between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning their differences.

10:94.text: If you are in doubt about what We have sent down to you, ask those who have been reciting the Book before you. The truth has come to you from your Lord, so do not be one of the doubters.

10:95.text: And do not be one of those who call the signs of God a lie, or you will be one of the losers.

10:96.text: Surely those against whom the word of your Lord has proved true will not believe,

10:97.text: even though every sign comes to them, until they see the painful punishment.

10:98.text: Why was there no town which believed, and its belief benefited it, except the people of Jonah? When they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in this present life and gave them enjoyment (of life) for a time.

10:99.text: If your Lord had (so) pleased, whoever was on the earth would indeed have believed – all of them together. Will you compel the people until they become believers?

10:100.text: It is not for any person to believe, except by the permission of God. He places abomination on those who do not understand.

10:101.text: Say: ‘See what is in the heavens and the earth!’ But signs and warnings are of no use to a people who do not believe.

10:102.text: Do they expect (anything) but the same as the days of those who passed away before them? Say: ‘(Just) wait! Surely I shall be one of those waiting with you.’

10:103.text: Then We rescue Our messengers and those who believe. In this way – (it is) an obligation on Us – We shall rescue the believers.

10:104.text: Say: ‘People! If you are in doubt about my religion, (know that) I do not serve those whom you serve instead of God, but I serve God, the One who takes you. I have been commanded to be one of the believers.’

10:105.text: And: ‘Set your face to the religion (as) a Ḥanīf, and do not be one of the idolaters.

10:106.text: Do not call on what can neither benefit nor harm you, instead of God (alone). If you do, surely then you will be one of the evildoers.

10:107.text: If God touches you with any harm, (there is) no one to remove it but Him, and if He intends for you any good, (there is) no one to turn back His favor. He smites with it whomever He pleases of His servants. He is the Forgiving, the Compassionate.’

10:108.text: Say: ‘People! The truth has come to you from your Lord. Whoever is (rightly) guided, is guided only for himself, and whoever goes astray, goes astray only against himself. I am not a guardian over you.’

10:109.text: Follow what you are inspired (with), and be patient until God judges, (for) He is the best of judges.

41:2.text: A sending down from the Merciful, the Compassionate.

41:3.text: A Book – its verses made distinct – an Arabic Qur’ān for a people who know.

41:4.text: (He is) a bringer of good news and a warner. But most of them have turned away, and they do not hear.

41:5.text: They say, ‘Our hearts are covered from what you call us to, and (there is) a heaviness in our ears, and between us and you (there is) a veil. So do (as you are able). Surely we are going to do (what we can).’

41:6.text: Say: ‘I am only a human being like you. I am inspired that your God is one God. So go straight with Him, and ask forgiveness from Him. But woe to the idolaters,

41:7.text: who do not give the alms, and (who) are disbelievers in the Hereafter!

41:8.text: Surely those who believe and do righteous deeds – for them (there is) a reward without end.’

41:9.text: Say: ‘Do you indeed disbelieve in the One who created the earth in two days, and do you set up rivals to Him? That is the Lord of the worlds.

41:10.text: He placed on it firm mountains (towering) above it, and blessed it, and decreed for it its (various) foods in four days, equal to the ones who ask.

41:11.text: Then He mounted (upward) to the sky, while it was (still) smoke, and said to it and to the earth, “Come, both of you, willingly or unwillingly!” They both said, “We come willingly.”

41:12.text: He finished them (as) seven heavens in two days, and inspired each heaven (with) its affair. And We adorned the sky of this world with lamps, and (made them) a protection. That is the decree of the Mighty, the Knowing.’

41:13.text: If they turn away, say: ‘I warn you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt of ‘Ād and Thamūd.’

41:14.text: When the messengers came to them from before them and from behind them (saying): ‘Do not serve (anyone) but God,’ they said, ‘If our Lord had (so) pleased, He would indeed have sent down angels. Surely we are disbelievers in what you are sent with.’

41:15.text: As for ‘Ād, they became arrogant on the earth without any right, and said, ‘Who is stronger than us in power?’ Did they not see that God, who created them, was stronger than them in power? They denied Our signs.

41:16.text: So We sent a furious wind against them in the days of calamity, so that We might make them taste the punishment of disgrace in this present life. But the punishment of the Hereafter is indeed more disgraceful, and they will not be helped.

41:17.text: As for Thamūd, We guided them, but they preferred blindness over the guidance. So the thunderbolt of the punishment of humiliation took them for what they had earned.

41:18.text: But We rescued those who believed and guarded (themselves).

41:19.text: On the Day when the enemies of God are gathered to the Fire, and they are arranged (in rows)

41:20.text: – until, when they have come to it, their hearing and their sight and their skins will bear witness against them about what they have done,

41:21.text: and they will say to their skins, ‘Why did you bear witness against us?’ They will say, ‘God, who gave speech to everything, has given us speech. He created you the first time, and to Him you are (now) returned.

41:22.text: You did not protect yourselves against your hearing and your sight and your skins bearing witness against you. You thought that God would not know much of what you had done.

41:23.text: And that – the thought you thought about your Lord – has brought you to ruin. (Now) you are among the losers.’

41:24.text: If they persist, the Fire will be a dwelling place for them, and if they ask to make amends, they will not be among the ones allowed to make amends.

41:25.text: We have allotted to them comrades, and they have made what is before them and behind them appear enticing to them. The word about the communities of jinn and humankind (which) have passed away before them has proved true against them (as well). Surely they were losers.

41:26.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘Do not listen to this Qur’ān, but talk frivolously about it, so that you may overcome (them).’

41:27.text: We shall indeed make those who disbelieve taste a harsh punishment, and We shall indeed repay them for the worst of what they have done.

41:28.text: That is the payment of the enemies of God – the Fire – where they will have the Home of Eternity as payment for their having denied Our signs.

41:29.text: Those who disbelieve (will) say, ‘Our Lord, show us those of the jinn and humankind who led us astray. We shall place them beneath our feet, so that they may be among the lowest.’

41:30.text: Surely those who have said, ‘Our Lord is God,’ (and) then have gone straight – the angels will come down on them (saying): ‘Do not fear, and do not sorrow, but welcome the good news of the Garden which you were promised.

41:31.text: We are your allies in this present life and in the Hereafter, where you will have whatever you desire, whatever you call for

41:32.text: – a reception from One forgiving, compassionate.’

41:33.text: Who is better in speech than the one who calls (people) to God, and does righteousness, and says, ‘Surely I am one of those who submit’?

41:34.text: The good deed and the evil deed are not equal. Repel (evil) with that which is better, and suddenly the one with whom (there was) enmity between you and him (will behave) as if he were an ally.

41:35.text: Yet no one will receive it except those who are patient, and no one will receive it except one who possesses great good luck.

41:36.text: If any provocation from Satan provokes you, take refuge with God. Surely He – He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

41:37.text: Among His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate yourselves before the sun or before the moon, but prostrate yourselves before God, who created them, if you serve Him.

41:38.text: If they are (too) proud, those who are in the presence of your Lord glorify Him by night and day, and do not become tired.

41:39.text: (Another) of His signs is that you see the earth barren, (and) then, when We send down water on it, it stirs and swells. Surely the One who gives it life is indeed the giver of life to the dead. Surely He is powerful over everything.

41:40.text: Surely those who pervert Our signs are not hidden from Us. Is the one who is cast into the Fire better, or the one who comes (out) secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do whatever you please. Surely He sees what you do.

41:41.text: Surely those who disbelieve in the Reminder when it comes to them – surely it is a mighty Book indeed!

41:42.text: Falsehood does not come to it, (either) from before it or from behind it. (It is) a sending down from One wise, praiseworthy.

41:43.text: Nothing is said to you except what has already been said to the messengers before you. Surely your Lord is indeed full of forgiveness, but (also) full of painful retribution.

41:44.text: If We had made it a foreign Qur’ān, they would indeed have said, ‘Why are its signs not made distinct? Foreign and Arabic?’ Say: ‘It is a guidance and healing for those who believe, but those who do not believe – (there is) a heaviness in their ears, and for them it is a blindness. Those – (it is as if) they are being called from a place far away.’

41:45.text: Certainly We gave Moses the Book, and then differences arose about it. Were it not for a preceding word from your Lord, it would indeed have been decided between them. Surely they are in grave doubt indeed about it.

41:46.text: Whoever does righteousness, it is for himself, and whoever does evil, it is (likewise) against himself. Your Lord is not an evildoer to (His) servants.

41:47.text: Knowledge of the Hour is reserved for Him. No fruit comes forth from its sheath, and no female conceives or delivers, except with His knowledge. On the Day when He will call to them, ‘Where are My associates?,’ they will say, ‘We proclaim to You: (there is) no witness among us.’

41:48.text: What they called on before will abandon them, and they will know (that there is) no place of escape for them.

41:49.text: The human does not tire of calling for good, but if evil touches him, he is in despair (and) downcast.

41:50.text: If indeed We give him a taste of mercy from Us, after hardship has touched him, he will indeed say, ‘This is mine! I do not think the Hour is coming. If indeed I am returned to my Lord, surely I shall have the best (reward) indeed with Him.’ We shall indeed inform those who disbelieve about what they have done, and indeed make them taste a stern punishment.

41:51.text: When We bless a person, he turns away and distances himself, but when evil touches them, he is full of long prayers.

41:52.text: Say: ‘Do you see? If it is from God, and you disbelieve in it – who is farther astray than the one who is in extreme defiance?’

41:53.text: We shall show them Our signs in the skies and in themselves, until it becomes clear to them that it is the truth. Is it not sufficient in (regard to) your Lord that He is a witness over everything?

41:54.text: Is it not a fact that they are in doubt about the meeting with their Lord? Is it not a fact that He encompasses everything?

101:3.text: And what will make you know what the striking is?

101:4.text: The Day when the people will be like scattered moths,

101:5.text: and the mountains will be like (tufts of) wool.

101:6.text: As for the one whose scales are heavy,

101:7.text: he will be in a pleasing life,

101:8.text: but as for the one whose scales are light,

101:10.text: And what will make you know what she is?

114:1.text: Say: ‘I take refuge with the Lord of the people,

114:4.text: from the evil of the whispering one, the slinking one,

114:5.text: who whispers in the hearts of the people,

114:6.text: of the jinn and the people.’

102:3.text: By no means! Soon you will know!

102:4.text: Once again, by no means! Soon you will know!

102:5.text: By no means! If (only) you knew (now) with the knowledge of certainty:

102:6.text: you will indeed see the Furnace.

102:7.text: Once again, you will indeed see it with the eye of certainty.

102:8.text: Then, on that Day, you will indeed be asked about (what) bliss (is).

43:3.text: Surely We have made it an Arabic Qur’ān, so that you may understand.

43:4.text: And surely it is in the mother of the Book, with Us, most high indeed, wise.

43:5.text: Shall We strike the Reminder away from you, on the excuse that you have been a wanton people?

43:6.text: How many prophets have We sent among those of old!

43:7.text: Yet not one prophet came to them whom they did not ridicule.

43:8.text: So We destroyed (those peoples who were) stronger than them in power, and the example of those of old has passed away.

43:9.text: If indeed you ask them, ‘Who created the heavens and the earth?,’ they will indeed say, ‘The Mighty, the Knowing created them.’

43:10.text: (It is He) who made the earth as a cradle for you, and made (path)ways in it for you, so that you might be guided,

43:11.text: and (it is He) who sends down water from the sky in measure – and by means of it We give some barren land life, and in this way you (too) will be brought forth –

43:12.text: and (it is He) who created the pairs, all of them, and made for you what you ride on from the ship(s) and the livestock,

43:13.text: so that you may mount their backs, (and) then remember the blessing of your Lord when you are mounted upon them, and say, ‘Glory to the One who has subjected this to us, when we (ourselves) were not fit for it.

43:14.text: Surely we are indeed going to return to our Lord.’

43:15.text: Yet they assign to Him a part of His (own) servants. Surely the human is clearly ungrateful indeed.

43:16.text: Or has He taken (for Himself) daughters from what He creates, and singled you out with sons?

43:17.text: When one of them is given news of what he has struck as a parable for the Merciful, his face turns dark and he chokes back his disappointment.

43:18.text: ‘One who is brought up in luxury, and he is not clear in the (time of) dispute?’

43:19.text: Yet they have made the angels – those who are themselves servants of the Merciful – females. Did they witness their creation? Their testimony will be written down, and they will be questioned.

43:20.text: They say, ‘If the Merciful had (so) pleased, we would not have served them.’ They have no knowledge about that; they are only guessing.

43:21.text: Or have We given them a Book before it, and do they hold fast to it?

43:22.text: No! They say, ‘Surely we found our fathers (set) on a community, and surely we are guided in their footsteps.’

43:23.text: In this way We have not sent any warner before you to a town, except that its affluent ones said, ‘Surely we found our fathers (set) on a community, and surely we are following in their footsteps.’

43:24.text: He said, ‘Even if I bring you better guidance than what you found your fathers (set) on?’ They said, ‘Surely we are disbelievers in what you are sent with.’

43:25.text: So We took vengeance on them. See how the end was for the ones who called (it) a lie!

43:26.text: (Remember) when Abraham said to his father and his people, ‘Surely I am free of what you serve,

43:27.text: except for the One who created me. Surely He will guide me.’

43:28.text: And he made it a lasting word among his descendants, so that they might return.

43:29.text: No! I gave these (people) and their fathers enjoyment (of life), until the truth came to them, and (also) a clear messenger.

43:30.text: But when the truth came to them, they said, ‘This is magic. Surely we are disbelievers in it.’

43:31.text: They said, ‘If only this Qur’ān had been sent down on some great man of the two towns.’

43:32.text: Do they distribute the mercy of your Lord? We have distributed their livelihood among them in this present life, and raised some of them above others in rank, so that some of them may take others in slavery. But the mercy of your Lord is better than what they accumulate.

43:33.text: If it were not that humankind would be one community, We would indeed have made for those who disbelieve in the Merciful roofs of silver for their houses, and stairways on which to ascend,

43:34.text: and doors for their houses, and couches on which to recline,

43:35.text: and (all manner of) decoration. Yet all that is but the enjoyment of this present life – the Hereafter with your Lord is for the ones who guard (themselves).

43:36.text: Whoever turns away from the Reminder of the Merciful – We allot to him a satan, and he becomes his comrade.

43:37.text: Surely they indeed keep them from the way, even though they think that they are (rightly) guided,

43:38.text: until, when he comes to Us, he says, ‘Would that (there were) between me and you the distance of the two Easts!’ And: ‘Evil is the comrade!

43:39.text: It will not benefit you today – since you have done evil – that you are partners in the punishment.’

43:40.text: Can you make the deaf to hear, or can you guide the blind and the one who is clearly astray?

43:41.text: Whether We take you away – surely We are going to take vengeance on them –

43:42.text: or show you what We have promised them – surely We are powerful over them.

43:43.text: So hold fast to what you are inspired (with). Surely you are on a straight path.

43:44.text: Surely it is a reminder indeed to you and to your people. Soon you will (all) be questioned.

43:45.text: Ask those of Our messengers whom We sent before you: Did We appoint any other gods than the Merciful to be served?

43:46.text: Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his assembly. He said, ‘Surely I am a messenger of the Lord of the worlds.’

43:47.text: But when he brought them Our signs, suddenly they began to laugh at them,

43:48.text: even though every sign We showed them was greater than the one before it. We seized them with the punishment, so that they might return.

43:49.text: They said, ‘Magician! Call on your Lord for us by whatever covenant He has made with you, (and) surely we shall indeed be (rightly) guided.’

43:50.text: But when We removed the punishment from them, immediately they broke (their promise).

43:51.text: Pharaoh called out among his people: ‘My people! Is the kingdom of Egypt not mine, and these rivers (which) flow beneath me? Do you not see?

43:52.text: Am I not better than this (man), who is despicable and scarcely makes (things) clear?

43:53.text: If only bracelets of gold were cast (down) on him or the accompanying angels came with him.’

43:54.text: So he unsettled his people, and they obeyed him. Surely they were a wicked people.

43:55.text: When they had angered Us, We took vengeance on them and drowned them – all (of them)!

43:56.text: We made them a thing of the past, and an example for the later (generations).

43:57.text: When the son of Mary is cited as an example, suddenly your people keep (others) from it,

43:58.text: and they say, ‘Are our gods better, or is he?’ They only cite him to you as a (matter of) dispute. Yes! They are a contentious people.

43:59.text: He was only a servant whom We blessed, and We made him an example for the Sons of Israel.

43:60.text: If We (so) pleased, We could indeed make angels out of you to be successors on the earth.

43:61.text: Surely it is indeed knowledge for the Hour, so do not be in doubt about it, but follow me. This is a straight path.

43:62.text: Do not let Satan keep you from (it). Surely he is a clear enemy to you.

43:63.text: When Jesus brought the clear signs, he said, ‘I have brought you the wisdom, and (I have done so) to make clear to you some of your differences. Guard (yourselves) against God and obey me.

43:64.text: Surely God – He is my Lord and your Lord, so serve Him! This is a straight path.’

43:65.text: But the factions differed among themselves. Woe to those who have done evil because of the punishment of a painful Day!

43:66.text: Are they looking for anything but the Hour – that it should come upon them unexpectedly, when they do not realize (it)?

43:67.text: Friends on that Day – some of them will be enemies to others, except for the ones who guard (themselves).

43:68.text: ‘My servants! (There is) no fear on you today, nor will you sorrow

43:69.text: – those (of you) who believed in Our signs and submitted.

43:70.text: Enter the Garden, you and your wives, you will be made happy!’

43:71.text: Plates and cups of gold will be passed around among them, and there (they will have) whatever they desire and their eyes delight in. And: ‘There you will remain.’

43:72.text: And: ‘That is the Garden which you have been given as an inheritance for what you have done.

43:73.text: There you have many fruits from which you will eat.’

43:74.text: Surely the evildoers will remain in the punishment of Gehenna.

43:75.text: It will not subside for them, and there they will be in despair.

43:76.text: We did not do them evil, but they themselves were the evildoers.

43:77.text: They will call out, ‘Master! Let your Lord finish us off!’ He will say, ‘Surely you will remain.

43:78.text: Certainly we brought you the truth, but most of you were averse to the truth.’

43:79.text: Or have they woven some plot? We (too) are weaving (a plot).

43:80.text: Or do they think that We do not hear their secret and their secret talk? Yes indeed! Our messengers are present with them writing (it) down.

43:81.text: Say: ‘If the Merciful had a son, I (would be) the first of the ones who served (him).

43:82.text: Glory to the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Lord of the throne, above what they allege!’

43:83.text: So leave them! Let them banter and jest, until they meet their Day which they are promised.

43:84.text: He (it is) who is God in the sky and God on the earth. He is the Wise, the Knowing.

43:85.text: Blessed (be) the One who – to Him (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them. With Him is the knowledge of the Hour, and to Him you will be returned.

43:86.text: Those whom they call on instead of Him have no power of intercession, except for the one who has borne witness to the truth – and they know (this).

43:87.text: If indeed you ask them, ‘Who created them?,’ they will indeed say, ‘God.’ How are they (so) deluded?

43:88.text: And his saying: ‘My Lord! Surely these are a people who do not believe.’

43:89.text: So excuse them, and say: ‘Peace!’ Soon they will know!

49:1.text: You who believe! Do not be forward before God and His messenger, but guard (yourselves) against God. Surely God is hearing, knowing.

49:2.text: You who believe! Do not raise your voices above the voice of the prophet, and do not be loud in (your) speech to him, like the loudness of some of you to others, or your deeds will come to nothing without your realizing (it).

49:3.text: Surely those who lower their voices in the presence of the messenger of God, those are the ones whose hearts God has tested for the guarding (of themselves). For them (there is) forgiveness and a great reward.

49:4.text: Surely those who call out to you from behind the private rooms – most of them do not understand.

49:5.text: If they were patient, until you come out to them, it would indeed be better for them. Yet God is forgiving, compassionate.

49:6.text: You who believe! If a wicked person brings you some (piece of) news, be discerning, or you will smite a people in ignorance, and then become regretful over what you have done.

49:7.text: Know that the messenger of God is among you. If he obeyed you in much of the affair, you would indeed be in distress. But God has made belief dear to you, and made it appear enticing in your hearts, and made disbelief and wickedness and disobedience hateful to you. Those – they are the right-minded.

49:8.text: Favor from God and a blessing! God is knowing, wise.

49:9.text: If two contingents of the believers fight, set (things) right between them, and if one of them oppresses the other, fight the one which oppresses until it returns to the command of God. If it returns, set (things) right between them with justice, and act fairly. Surely God loves the ones who act fairly.

49:10.text: Only the believers are brothers, so set (things) right between your two brothers, and guard (yourselves) against God, so that you may receive mercy.

49:11.text: You who believe! Do not let one people ridicule (another) people who may be better than them, or women (ridicule other) women who may be better than them. Do not find fault with each other, or insult each other with nicknames. A bad name is wickedness after belief. Whoever does not turn (in repentance), those – they are the evildoers.

49:12.text: You who believe! Avoid too much conjecture, (for) surely some conjecture is a sin. Do not pry or go behind each other’s back. Would any of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother? You would hate it! Guard (yourselves) against God. Surely God turns (in forgiveness), compassionate.

49:13.text: People! Surely We have created you from a male and a female, and made you different peoples and tribes, so that you may recognize one another. Surely the most honorable among you in the sight of God is the one among you who guards (himself) most. Surely God is knowing, aware.

49:14.text: The Arabs say, ‘We believe.’ Say: ‘You do not believe. Rather say, “We submit,” (for) belief has not yet entered your hearts. But if you obey God and His messenger, He will not deprive you of your deeds at all. Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

49:15.text: The believers are only those who believe in God and His messenger, (and) then have not doubted but struggled with their wealth and their lives in the way of God. Those – they are the truthful.’

49:16.text: Say: ‘Will you teach God about your religion, when God knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? God has knowledge of everything.’

49:17.text: (They think) they bestow a favor on you in that they have submitted! Say: ‘Do not bestow your submission on me as a favor! No! God bestows a favor on you, in that He has guided you to belief, if you are truthful.

49:18.text: Surely God knows the unseen (things) of the heavens and the earth. God sees what you do.

20:2.text: We have not sent down the Qur’ān on you for you to be miserable,

20:3.text: but as a reminder to the one who fears

20:4.text: – as a sending down from the One who created the earth and the high heavens.

20:5.text: The Merciful is mounted upon the throne.

20:6.text: To Him (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and whatever is between them, and whatever is beneath the ground.

20:7.text: If you speak the word publicly, surely He knows the secret and (what is even) more hidden.

20:8.text: God – (there is) no god but Him. To Him (belong) the best names.

20:9.text: Has the story of Moses come to you?

20:10.text: When he saw a fire, he said to his family, ‘Stay (here). Surely I perceive a fire. Perhaps I shall bring you a flaming torch from it, or I shall find at the fire guidance.’

20:11.text: But when he came to it, he was called: ‘Moses!

20:12.text: Surely I am your Lord, so take off your shoes. Surely you are in the holy wādī of Ṭuwā.

20:13.text: I have chosen you, so listen to what is inspired.

20:14.text: Surely I am God – (there is) no god but Me. So serve Me, and observe the prayer for My remembrance!

20:15.text: Surely the Hour is coming – I almost hide it, so that every person may be repaid for what he strives after.

20:16.text: So do not let anyone who does not believe in it, and who (only) follows his (own) desire, keep you from it, or you will be brought to ruin.’

20:17.text: ‘What is that in your right (hand), Moses?’

20:18.text: He said, ‘It is my staff. I lean on it, and I bring down leaves with it to (feed) my sheep, and I have other uses for it.’

20:20.text: So he cast it, and suddenly it became a slithering snake.

20:21.text: He said, ‘Take hold of it, and do not fear. We shall restore it to its former state.

20:22.text: Now draw your hand to your side. It will come out white, unharmed – another sign.

20:23.text: (We have done this) to show you one of Our greatest signs.’

20:24.text: ‘Go to Pharaoh! Surely he has transgressed insolently.’

20:25.text: He said, ‘My Lord, expand my heart for me,

20:26.text: and make my task easy for me.

20:27.text: Untie the knot from my tongue,

20:28.text: so that they may understand my words.

20:29.text: And appoint an assistant for me from my family:

20:32.text: and associate him in my task,

20:33.text: so that we may glorify You often,

20:36.text: He said, ‘You are granted your request, Moses.’

20:37.text: ‘Certainly We bestowed favor on you another time,

20:38.text: when We inspired your mother (with) what was inspired:

20:39.text: “Cast him into the ark, and cast it into the sea, and let the sea throw it up on the shore, and an enemy to Me and an enemy to him will take him.” But I cast love on you from Me, and (I did this) so that you might be brought up under My eye.

20:40.text: When your sister went out, she said, “Shall I direct you to (someone) who will take charge of him?” And We returned you to your mother, so that she might be comforted and not sorrow. And (then) you killed a man, and We rescued you from that distress, and We tested you thoroughly. So you remained for (some) years with the people of Midian, (and) then you came (here), according to a decree, Moses.’

20:41.text: ‘I have brought you up for Myself.

20:42.text: Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and do not be lax in My remembrance.

20:43.text: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh. Surely he has transgressed insolently.

20:44.text: But speak to him a soft word. Perhaps he may take heed or fear.’

20:45.text: They said, ‘Our Lord, surely we are afraid that he may act rashly against us, or that he may transgress insolently.’

20:46.text: He said, ‘Do not be afraid! Surely I am with both of you. I hear and I see.

20:47.text: So go to him, both of you, and say: “We are two messengers of your Lord, so send forth the Sons of Israel with us, and do not punish them. We have brought you a sign from your Lord. Peace (be) upon anyone who follows the guidance!

20:48.text: Surely we have been inspired that the punishment (will fall) on anyone who calls (it) a lie and turns away.”’

20:49.text: He said, ‘And who is your Lord, Moses?’

20:50.text: He said, ‘Our Lord is the One who gave everything its creation, (and) then guided (it).’

20:51.text: He said, ‘What (about the) case of the former generations?’

20:52.text: He said, ‘The knowledge of it is with my Lord in a Book. My Lord does not go astray, nor does He forget.

20:53.text: (It is He) who made the earth as a cradle for you, and put (path)ways in it for you, and sent down water from the sky. And by means of it We brought forth pairs of various (kinds of) vegetation.

20:54.text: Eat (of it) and pasture your livestock (on it). Surely in that are signs indeed for those with reason.

20:55.text: We created you from it, and into it We shall return you, and from it We shall bring you forth another time.’

20:56.text: Certainly We showed him Our signs, all of them, but he called (them) a lie and refused.

20:57.text: He said, ‘Have you come to bring us forth from our land by your magic, Moses?

20:58.text: We shall indeed bring you magic like it. So set an appointment between us and you – We shall not break it, nor will you – at a fair place.’

20:59.text: He said, ‘Your appointment is on the Day of Splendor. Let the people be gathered at morning light.’

20:60.text: So Pharaoh turned away, and put together his plot. Then he came (again).

20:61.text: Moses said to them, ‘Woe to you! Do not forge a lie against God, or He will destroy you with a punishment. Whoever forges (a lie) has failed.’

20:62.text: So they disputed their situation among themselves, but they kept their talk secret.

20:63.text: They said, ‘Surely these two magicians want to expel you from your land by their magic, and to do away with your exemplary way (of life).

20:64.text: So put together your plot, (and) then line up. He who has the upper hand today will indeed prosper.’

20:65.text: They said, ‘Moses! Are you going to cast or shall we be first to cast?’

20:66.text: He said, ‘No! You cast (first)!’ And suddenly their ropes and their staffs seemed to him to be moving as a result of their magic.

20:67.text: So Moses felt fear within himself.

20:68.text: We said to him, ‘Do not be afraid! Surely you will be the superior one.

20:69.text: Cast (down) what is in your right (hand), and it will swallow up what they have made. What they have done is only a magician’s trick, and the magician does not prosper, (no matter) where he comes.’

20:70.text: And the magicians were cast (down) in prostration. They said, ‘We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.’

20:71.text: He said, ‘Have you believed in him before I gave you permission? Surely he indeed is your master, the (very) one who taught you magic? I shall indeed cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I shall indeed crucify you on the trunks of date palms, and you will indeed know which of us is harsher in punishment, and more lasting.’

20:72.text: They said, ‘We shall not prefer you over the clear signs which have come to us, nor (over) Him who created us. So decree whatever you are going to decree. You can only decree for this present life.

20:73.text: Surely we have believed in our Lord, so that He may forgive us our sins and the magic you forced us to (practice). God is better and more lasting.’

20:74.text: Surely the one who comes to his Lord as a sinner, surely for him (there is) Gehenna, where he will neither die nor live.

20:75.text: But whoever comes to Him as a believer, (and) he has done righteous deeds, those – for them (there are) the highest ranks:

20:76.text: Gardens of Eden, through which rivers flow, there to remain. That is the payment for the one who purifies himself.

20:77.text: Certainly We inspired Moses: ‘Journey with My servants, and strike for them a dry passage in the sea, without fear of being overtaken or being afraid.’

20:78.text: So Pharaoh followed them with his forces, but (there) covered them of the sea what covered them.

20:79.text: Pharaoh led his people astray and did not guide (them).

20:80.text: Sons of Israel! We have rescued you from your enemy, and made a covenant with you at the right side of the mountain, and sent down on you the manna and the quails:

20:81.text: ‘Eat from the good things which We have provided you, but do not transgress insolently in that, or My anger will descend on you. Whoever My anger falls on has perished.

20:82.text: Yet surely I am indeed forgiving to whoever turns (in repentance) and believes, and does righteousness, (and) then is (rightly) guided.’

20:83.text: ‘What has made you hurry (ahead) of your people, Moses?’

20:84.text: He said, ‘They are close on my footsteps, but I have hurried (ahead) to you, my Lord, in order that You might be pleased.’

20:85.text: He said, ‘Surely We have tempted your people after you (left them), and al-Sāmirī has led them astray.’

20:86.text: So Moses returned to his people, angry (and) sorrowful. He said, ‘My people! Did your Lord not promise you a good promise? Did (the time of) the covenant last too long for you, or did you wish that the anger of your Lord would descend on you, and so you broke (your) appointment with me?’

20:87.text: They said, ‘We did not break (our) appointment with you by our (own) will, but we were loaded with burdens of the ornaments of the people, and we cast them (down), and so did al-Sāmirī.’

20:88.text: Then he brought forth for them a calf, a (mere) image of it (having) a mooing sound, and they said, ‘This is your god, and the god of Moses, though he has forgotten.’

20:89.text: Did they not see that it did not return a word to them, and had no power to (cause) them harm or benefit?

20:90.text: Certainly Aaron had said to them before, ‘My people! You are only being tempted by it. Surely your Lord is the Merciful, so follow me and obey my command!’

20:91.text: They said, ‘We shall continue (to be) devoted to it until Moses returns to us.’

20:92.text: He said, ‘Aaron! What prevented you, when you saw them going astray,

20:93.text: from following me? Did you disobey my command?’

20:94.text: He said, ‘Son of my mother! Do not seize (me) by my beard or by my head! Surely I was afraid that you would say, “You have caused a division among the Sons of Israel, and you have not respected my word.”’

20:95.text: He said, ‘What was this business of yours, al-Sāmirī?’

20:96.text: He said, ‘I saw what they did not see, and I took a handful (of dust) from the footprint of the messenger, and I tossed it. In this way my mind contrived (it) for me.’

20:97.text: He said, ‘Go! Surely it is yours in this life to say, “Do not touch (me)!” And surely for you (there is) an appointment – you will not break it. Look at your god which you remained devoted to! We shall indeed burn it (and) then scatter it as dust in the sea.

20:98.text: Your only god is God – (there is) no god but Him – who comprehends everything in knowledge.’

20:99.text: In this way We recount to you some of the stories of what has already gone before, and We have given you a reminder from Us.

20:100.text: Whoever turns away from it, surely he will bear a burden on the Day of Resurrection.

20:101.text: [. . .] there to remain, and evil (will be the) load for them on the Day of Resurrection.

20:102.text: On the Day when there is a blast on the trumpet – and We shall gather the sinners on that Day blue –

20:103.text: they will murmur among themselves: ‘You have remained (in the grave) only for ten (days).’

20:104.text: We know what they will say, when the best of them in way (of life) will say, ‘You have remained only for a day.’

20:105.text: They ask you about the mountains. Say: ‘My Lord will scatter them as dust,

20:106.text: and He will leave it a barren plain

20:107.text: in which you will not see any crookedness or curve.’

20:108.text: On that Day they will follow the Caller, in whom (there is) no crookedness, and voices will be hushed before the Merciful, so that you will hear nothing but a faint murmur.

20:109.text: On that Day intercession will not be of any benefit, except for the one to whom the Merciful gives permission, and whose word He approves.

20:110.text: He knows what is before them and what is behind them, but they do not encompass Him in knowledge.

20:111.text: Faces will be humbled before the Living, the Everlasting. Whoever carries (a load of) evildoing will have failed,
