20:112.text: but whoever does any righteous deeds – and he is a believer – he will not fear (any) evil or dispossession.

20:113.text: In this way We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur’ān, and We have varied some of the promise(s) in it, so that they may guard (themselves), or so that it may arouse in them a reminder.

20:114.text: Exalted is God, the true King! Do not be in a hurry with the Qur’ān, before its inspiration is completed to you, but say: ‘My Lord, increase me in knowledge.’

20:115.text: Certainly We made a covenant with Adam before, but he forgot, and We found in him no determination.

20:116.text: (Remember) when We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate yourselves before Adam,’ and they prostrated themselves, except Iblīs. He refused.

20:117.text: And We said, ‘Adam! Surely this is an enemy to you and to your wife. Do not let him expel you both from the Garden, and you become miserable.

20:118.text: Surely it is yours not to hunger there or go naked,

20:119.text: nor to thirst there or be exposed to the sun.’

20:120.text: But Satan whispered to him. He said, ‘Adam! Shall I direct you to the Tree of Immortality, and (to) a kingdom that does not decay?’

20:121.text: So they both ate from it, and their shameful parts became apparent to them, and they both began fastening on themselves some leaves of the Garden, and Adam disobeyed his Lord and erred.

20:122.text: Then his Lord chose him, and turned to him (in forgiveness), and guided (him).

20:123.text: He said, ‘Go down from it, both of you together, some of you an enemy to others. If any guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows My guidance will not go astray, nor become miserable.

20:124.text: But whoever turns away from My reminder, surely for him (there will be) a life of deprivation, and We shall gather him blind on the Day of Resurrection.’

20:125.text: He will say, ‘My Lord, why have you gathered me blind, when I had sight before?’

20:126.text: He will say, ‘So (it is). Our signs came to you, but you forgot them, so today you are forgotten.’

20:127.text: In this way We repay anyone who acts wantonly and does not believe in the signs of his Lord. Yet the punishment of the Hereafter is indeed harsher and more lasting.

20:128.text: Is it not a guide for them how many generations We destroyed before them, (seeing that) they walk in (the midst of) their dwelling places? Surely in that are signs indeed for those with reason.

20:129.text: Were it not for a preceding word from your Lord, it would indeed be close at hand – but (there is) a time appointed (for punishment).

20:130.text: So be patient with what they say, and glorify your Lord with praise before the rising of the sun, and before its setting, and during the hours of the night, and glorify (Him) at the ends of the day, so that you may find satisfaction.

20:131.text: Do not yearn after what We have given classes of them to enjoy – the flower of this present life – that We may test them by means of it. The provision of your Lord is better and more lasting.

20:132.text: Command your family (to observe) the prayer, and be patient in it. We do not ask you for provision. We provide for you, and the outcome (is) for the guarding (of yourself).

20:133.text: They say, ‘If only he would bring us a sign from his Lord.’ Has there not come to them a clear sign (of) what was in the former pages?

20:134.text: If We had destroyed them with a punishment before him, they would indeed have said, ‘Our Lord, if only you had sent us a messenger, so that we might have followed your signs before we were humiliated and disgraced?’

20:135.text: Say: ‘Each one is waiting, so you wait (too), and then you will know who are the followers of the even path, and who is (rightly) guided.’

78:1.text: What are they asking each other questions about?

78:4.text: By no means! Soon they will know!

78:5.text: Once again, by no means! Soon they will know!

78:6.text: Have We not made the earth as a bed,

78:9.text: and made your sleep as a rest,

78:10.text: and made the night as a covering,

78:11.text: and made the day for (your) livelihood.

78:12.text: We have built above you seven firm (heavens),

78:14.text: We have sent down water from the rainclouds, pouring forth,

78:15.text: so that by means of it We may bring forth grain and vegetation,

78:17.text: Surely the Day of Decision is an appointed time:

78:18.text: the Day when there will be a blast on the trumpet, and you will come in crowds,

78:19.text: and the sky will be opened and become gates,

78:20.text: and the mountains will be moved and become a mirage.

78:22.text: as a (place of) return for the insolent transgressors,

78:24.text: They will not taste there any coolness or drink,

78:27.text: Surely they were not expecting a reckoning

78:28.text: when they called Our signs an utter lie.

78:29.text: But We have counted up everything in a Book.

78:30.text: So: ‘Taste (it)! We shall only increase you in punishment.’

78:31.text: Surely for the ones who guard (themselves) (there is) a (place of) safety:

78:33.text: and full-breasted (maidens), (all) of the same age,

78:35.text: – in which they will not hear any frivolous talk, nor any lying

78:36.text: – a payment from your Lord, a gift, a reckoning!

78:37.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, the Merciful, of whom they have no power to speak.

78:38.text: On the Day when the spirit and the angels stand in lines, they will not speak, except the one to whom the Merciful has given permission, and he will say what is correct.

78:39.text: That is the true Day. Whoever pleases takes a (way of) return to his Lord.

78:40.text: Surely We have warned you of a punishment near (at hand), on the Day when a person will see what his hands have sent forward, and the disbeliever will say, ‘Would that I were dust!’

42:37.text: – and (also for) those who avoid great sins and immoral deeds, and when they are angry, they forgive,

42:38.text: and those who respond to their Lord and observe the prayer, and their affair (is a matter of) consultation among themselves, and they contribute from what We have provided them,

42:39.text: and those who, when envy smites them, defend themselves (against it).

42:40.text: (The) payment for an evil deed is an evil like it, but whoever pardons and sets (things) right – his reward (depends) on God. Surely He does not love the evildoers.

42:41.text: Whoever indeed defends himself after he has suffered evil, those – against them (there is) no way.

42:42.text: The way is only open against those who do the people evil, and act oppressively on the earth without any right. Those – for them (there is) a painful punishment.

42:43.text: But whoever indeed is patient and forgives – surely that indeed is one of the determining factors in (all) affairs.

59:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies God. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

59:2.text: He (it is) who expelled those of the People of the Book who disbelieved from their homes for the first gathering. You did not think that they would go forth, and they thought that their strongholds would defend them against God. But God came upon them from where they were not expecting, and cast dread into their hearts. They destroyed their houses with their (own) hands and the hands of the believers. Learn a lesson, you who have sight!

59:3.text: If God had not prescribed exile for them, He would indeed have punished them in this world – and for them (there is) the punishment of the Fire in the Hereafter.

59:4.text: That is because they opposed God and His messenger. Whoever opposes God – surely God is harsh in retribution.

59:5.text: Whatever palm trees you cut down, or left standing on their roots – (it was) was by the permission of God, and (it was) so that He might disgrace the wicked.

59:6.text: What God has given to His messenger (as spoils) from them – you did not spur on any horse or camel for it, but God gives authority to His messengers over whomever He pleases. God is powerful over everything.

59:7.text: What God has given to His messenger (as spoils) from the people of the towns (belongs) to God and to the messenger, and to family, and the orphans, and the poor, and the traveler, so that it does not (just) circulate among the wealthy of you. Whatever (spoils) the messenger gives you, take it, and whatever he forbids you, stop (asking for it). Guard (yourselves) against God! Surely God is harsh in retribution.

59:8.text: (Spoils belong) to the poor emigrants, who were expelled from their homes and their wealth, seeking favor from God and approval, and helping God and His messenger. Those – they are the truthful.

59:9.text: And those who settled in ‘the home’ and in belief before them, they love whoever emigrates to them, and do not find in their hearts any need for what they have been given, but prefer (emigrants) above themselves, even though there is poverty among them. Whoever is guarded against his own greed, those – they are the ones who prosper.

59:10.text: Those who came after them say, ‘Our Lord, forgive us and our brothers, who preceded us in belief, and do not place any rancor in our hearts toward those who believe. Our Lord, surely You are kind (and) compassionate.’

59:11.text: Do you not see those who have played the hypocrite? They say to their brothers who disbelieve among the People of the Book, ‘If indeed you are expelled, we shall indeed go forth with you, and we shall never obey anyone concerning you. And if you are fought against, we shall indeed help you.’ God bears witness: ‘Surely they are liars indeed!’

59:12.text: If indeed they are expelled, they will not go forth with them, and if indeed they are fought against, they will not help them. And if indeed they do help them, they will indeed turn their backs. Then they will not be helped.

59:13.text: Indeed you (strike) greater fear in their hearts than God. That is because they are a people who do not understand.

59:14.text: They will not fight against you all together, except in fortified towns or from behind walls. Their violence among themselves is (so) harsh, you (might) think them all (united) together, but their hearts are divided. That is because they are a people who have no sense.

59:15.text: (They are) like those who shortly before them tasted the consequence of their action – for them (there is) a painful punishment.

59:16.text: (They are) like Satan, when he said to the human, ‘Disbelieve!,’ and when he disbelieved, he said, ‘Surely I am free of you. Surely I fear God, Lord of the worlds.’

59:17.text: So the end of both of them is: they will both be in the Fire, (and) there they both will remain. That is the payment of the evildoers.

59:18.text: You who believe! Guard (yourselves) against God, and let each person look to what he sends forward for tomorrow. Guard (yourselves) against God! Surely God is aware of what you do.

59:19.text: Do not be like those who forgot God, and He caused them to forget their own selves. Those – they are the wicked.

59:20.text: The companions of the Fire and the companions of the Garden are not equal. The companions of the Garden – they are the triumphant.

59:21.text: If We had sent down this Qur’ān on a mountain, you would indeed have seen it humbled (and) split apart out of the fear of God. These parables – We strike them for the people so that they will reflect.

59:22.text: He is God, the One who – (there is) no god but Him – is the Knower of the unseen and the seen. He is the Merciful, the Compassionate.

59:23.text: He is God, the One who – (there is) no god but Him – is the King, the Holy One, the Peace, the Faithful, the Preserver, the Mighty, the Sole Ruler, the Magnificent. Glory to God above what they associate!

59:24.text: He is God – the Creator, the Maker, the Fashioner. To Him (belong) the best names. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Him. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

3:52.text: When Jesus perceived disbelief from them, he said, ‘Who will be my helpers to God?’ The disciples said, ‘We will be the helpers of God. We believe in God. Bear witness that we submit.

3:53.text: Our Lord, we believe in what You have sent down, and we follow the messenger. So write us down among the witnesses.’

3:54.text: They schemed, but God schemed (too), and God is the best of schemers.

3:55.text: (Remember) when God said, ‘Jesus! Surely I am going to take you and raise you to Myself, and purify you from those who disbelieve. And I am going to place those who follow you above those who disbelieve until the Day of Resurrection. Then to Me is your return, and I shall judge between you concerning your differences.

3:56.text: As for those who disbelieve, I shall punish them (with) a harsh punishment in this world and the Hereafter. They will have no helpers.’

3:57.text: As for those who believe and do righteous deeds, He will pay them their rewards in full. God does not love the evildoers.

3:58.text: That – We recite it to you from the signs and the wise Reminder.

3:59.text: Surely the likeness of Jesus is, with God, as the likeness of Adam. He created him from dust, (and) then He said to him, ‘Be!’ and he was.

3:60.text: The truth (is) from your Lord, so do not be one of the doubters.

3:61.text: Whoever disputes with you about him, after what has come to you of the knowledge, say: ‘Come, let us call our sons and your sons, our wives and your wives, ourselves and yourselves. Then let us pray earnestly and place the curse of God upon the liars.’

3:62.text: Surely this – it indeed is the true account. (There is) nothing of (the nature of) a god but God. Surely God – He indeed is the Mighty, the Wise.

3:63.text: If they turn away – surely God knows the fomenters of corruption.

3:64.text: Say: ‘People of the Book! Come to a word (which is) common between us and you: “We do not serve (anyone) but God, and do not associate (anything) with Him, and do not take each other as Lords instead of God.”’ If they turn away, say: ‘Bear witness that we are Muslims.’

3:65.text: People of the Book! Why do you dispute about Abraham, when the Torah and the Gospel were not sent down until after him. Will you not understand?

3:66.text: There you are! Those who have disputed about what you know. Why do you dispute about what you do not know? God knows, but you do not know.

3:67.text: Abraham was not a Jew, nor a Christian, but he was a Ḥanīf, a Muslim. He was not one of the idolaters.

3:68.text: Surely the people nearest to Abraham are those indeed who followed him, and this prophet, and those who believe. God is the ally of the believers.

108:1.text: Surely We have given you the abundance.

108:2.text: So pray to your Lord and sacrifice.

108:3.text: Surely your hater – he is the one cut off!

112:3.text: He has not begotten and was not begotten,

112:4.text: and He has no equal. None!’

90:2.text: – and you are a lawful (resident) in this land –

90:3.text: by a begetter and what he begot!

90:4.text: Certainly We created the human in trouble.

90:5.text: Does he think that no one has power over him?

90:6.text: He says, ‘I have squandered vast wealth!’

90:7.text: Does he think that no one has seen him?

90:8.text: Have We not made two eyes for him,

90:9.text: and a tongue, and two lips?

90:10.text: And have We not guided him to the two ways?

90:11.text: Yet he has not attempted the (steep) ascent.

90:12.text: And what will make you know what the (steep) ascent is?

90:13.text: The setting free of a slave,

90:14.text: or feeding on a day of hunger

90:16.text: or a poor person (lying) in the dust.

90:17.text: Then he has become one of those who believe, and (who) exhort (each other) to patience, and (who) exhort (each other) to mercy.

90:18.text: Those are the companions on the right.

90:19.text: But those who disbelieve in Our signs, they are the companions on the left.

90:20.text: A fire (will be) closed over them.

62:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies God, the King, the Holy One, the Mighty, the Wise.

62:2.text: He (it is) who has raised up among the common people a messenger from among them, to recite His signs to them, and to purify them, and to teach them the Book and the wisdom, though before (this) they were indeed clearly astray

62:3.text: – and others of them who have not (yet) joined them. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

62:4.text: That is the favor of God. He gives (it) to whomever He pleases. God is full of great favor.

62:5.text: Those who have been loaded down with the Torah, (and) then have not carried it, are like a donkey carrying books. Evil is the parable of the people who have called the signs of God a lie. God does not guide the people who are evildoers.

62:6.text: Say: ‘You who are Jews! If you claim that you are the allies of God to the exclusion of the people, wish for death, if you are truthful.’

62:7.text: But they will never wish for it because of what their (own) hands have sent forward. God knows the evildoers.

62:8.text: Say: ‘Surely the death from which you flee – surely it will meet you. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the seen, and He will inform you about what you have done.’

62:9.text: You who believe! When the call to prayer is made on the day of assembly, hurry to the remembrance of God, and leave business aside. That is better for you, if (only) you knew.

62:10.text: Then, when the prayer is finished, disperse on the earth and seek some favor from God, and remember God often, so that you may prosper.

62:11.text: But when they see (the chance of) some (business) transaction or diversion, they rush off to it, and leave you standing. Say: ‘What is with God is better than any diversion or transaction. God is the best of providers.’

3:2.text: God – (there is) no god but Him, the Living, the Everlasting.

3:3.text: He has sent down on you the Book with the truth, confirming what was before it, and He sent down the Torah and the Gospel

3:4.text: before (this) as guidance for the people, and (now) He has sent down the Deliverance. Surely those who disbelieve in the signs of God – for them (there is) a harsh punishment. God is mighty, a taker of vengeance.

3:5.text: Surely God – nothing is hidden from Him on the earth or in the sky.

3:6.text: He (it is) who fashions you in the wombs as He pleases. (There is) no god but Him, the Mighty, the Wise.

3:7.text: He (it is) who has sent down on you the Book, of which some verses are clearly composed – they are the mother of the Book – but others are ambiguous. As for those in whose hearts (there is) a turning aside, they follow the ambiguous part of it, seeking (to cause) trouble and seeking its interpretation. No one knows its interpretation except God. And (as for) the ones firmly grounded in knowledge, they say, ‘We believe in it. All (of it) is from our Lord.’ Yet no one takes heed except those with understanding.

3:8.text: ‘Our Lord, do not cause our hearts to turn aside after You have guided us, and grant us mercy from Yourself. Surely You – You are the Giver.

3:9.text: Our Lord, surely You will gather the people for a Day – (there is) no doubt about it. Surely God will not break the appointment.’

3:10.text: Surely those who disbelieve – neither their wealth nor their children will be of any use against God. And those – they will be fuel for the Fire,

3:11.text: like the case of the house of Pharaoh, and those who were before them, who called Our signs a lie. God seized them because of their sins, and God is harsh in retribution.

3:12.text: Say to those who disbelieve: ‘You will be conquered and gathered into Gehenna – it is an evil bed!’

3:13.text: There was a sign for you in the two cohorts which met: one cohort fighting in the way of God, and another disbelieving. They saw them twice as many (as themselves) with (their own) eyesight. God supports with His help whomever He pleases. Surely in that is a lesson indeed for those have sight.

3:14.text: Enticing to the people is love of desires: women and sons, qinṭrs upon qinṭrs of gold and silver, and the finest horses, cattle, and fields. That is the provision of this present life. But God – with Him is the best place of return.

3:15.text: Say: ‘Shall I inform you of (something) better than that? For the ones who guard (themselves), (there are) Gardens with their Lord, through which rivers flow, there to remain, and (there are) pure spouses and approval from God.’ God sees (His) servants

3:16.text: who say, ‘Our Lord, surely we believe. Forgive us our sins and guard us against the punishment of the Fire.’

3:17.text: (They are) the patient, the truthful, the obedient, those who contribute, the askers of forgiveness in the mornings.

3:18.text: God has borne witness that (there is) no god but Him – and (so have) the angels, and the people of knowledge, (who) uphold justice. (There is) no god but Him, the Mighty, the Wise.

3:19.text: Surely the religion with God is Islam. Those who were given the Book did not differ until after the knowledge had come to them, (because of) envy among themselves. Whoever disbelieves in the signs of God – surely God is quick at the reckoning.

3:20.text: If they dispute with you, say: ‘I have submitted to God, and (so have) those who follow me.’ And say to those who have been given the Book, and to the common people: ‘Have you submitted?’ If they submit, they have been (rightly) guided, but if they turn away – only (dependent) on you is the delivery (of the message). God sees (His) servants.

3:21.text: Surely those who disbelieve in the signs of God, and kill the prophets without any right, and kill those of the people who command justice – give them news of a painful punishment.

3:22.text: Those are the ones whose deeds come to nothing in this world and the Hereafter. They will have no helpers.

7:2.text: A Book sent down to you – so let there be no heaviness in your heart because of it – in order that you may warn by means of it, and as a reminder to the believers.

7:3.text: Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not follow any allies other than Him. Little do you take heed!

7:4.text: How many a town have We destroyed! Our violence came upon it at night, or (while) they were relaxing at midday.

7:5.text: Their only cry, when Our violence came upon them, was that they said, ‘Surely We were evildoers!’

7:6.text: We shall indeed question those to whom (a messenger) was sent, and We shall indeed question the envoys.

7:7.text: We shall indeed recount to them with knowledge, (for) We were not absent.

7:8.text: The weighing on that Day (will be) the true (weighing). Whoever’s scales are heavy, those – they are the ones who prosper,

7:9.text: but whoever’s scales are light, those are the ones who have lost their (own) selves, because of the evil they have done to Our signs.

7:10.text: Certainly We have established you on the earth, and provided for you a means of living on it – little thanks you show!

7:11.text: Certainly We created you, (and) then fashioned you. Then We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate yourselves before Adam,’ and they prostrated themselves, except Iblīs. He was not one of those who prostrated themselves.

7:12.text: He said, ‘What kept you from prostrating yourself when I commanded you?’ He said, ‘I am better than him. You created me from fire, but You created him from clay.’

7:13.text: He said, ‘Go down from here! It is not for you to be arrogant here. Get out! Surely you are one of the disgraced.’

7:14.text: He said, ‘Spare me until the Day when they are raised up.’

7:15.text: He said, ‘Surely you are one of the spared.’

7:16.text: He said, ‘Because you have made me err, I shall indeed sit (in wait) for them (on) Your straight path.

7:17.text: Then I shall indeed come upon them, from before them and from behind them, and from their right and from their left, and You will not find most of them thankful.’

7:18.text: He said, ‘Get out of here, detested (and) rejected! Whoever of them follows you – I shall indeed fill Gehenna with you – all (of you)!’

7:19.text: ‘Adam! Inhabit the Garden, you and your wife, and eat freely of it wherever you please, but do not go near this tree, or you will both be among the evildoers.’

7:20.text: Then Satan whispered to them both, to reveal to them both what was hidden from them of their shameful parts. He said, ‘Your Lord has only forbidden you both from this tree to keep you both from becoming two angels, or from becoming two of the immortals.’

7:21.text: And he swore to them both, ‘Surely I am indeed one of your trusty advisers.’

7:22.text: So he caused them both to fall by means of deception. And when they both had tasted the tree, their shameful parts became apparent to them, and they both began fastening on themselves some leaves of the Garden. But their Lord called to them both, ‘Did I not forbid you both from that tree, and say to you both, “Surely Satan is a clear enemy to you”?’

7:23.text: They both said, ‘Our Lord, we have done ourselves evil. If You do not forgive us, and have compassion on us, we shall indeed be among the losers.’

7:24.text: He said, ‘Go down, some of you an enemy to others! The earth is a dwelling place for you, and enjoyment (of life) for a time.’

7:25.text: He said, ‘On it you will live and on it you will die, and from it you will be brought forth.’

7:26.text: Sons of Adam! We sent down on you clothing – it covers your shameful parts – and feathers. Yet the clothing of guarding (yourselves) – that is better. That is one of the signs of God, so that they may take heed.

7:27.text: Sons of Adam! Do not let Satan tempt you, as he drove your parents out of the Garden, stripping both of them of their clothing in order to show both of them their shameful parts. Surely he sees you – he and his ilk – from where you do not see them. Surely We have made the satans allies of those who do not believe.

7:28.text: When they commit immorality, they say, ‘We found our fathers doing it, and God has commanded us (to do) it.’ Say: ‘Surely God does not command immorality. Do you say about God what you do not know?’

7:29.text: Say: ‘My Lord has commanded justice. Set your faces in every mosque, and call on Him, devoting (your) religion to Him. As He brought you about, (so) will you return.

7:30.text: (One) group He has guided, and (another) group – their going astray was deserved. Surely they have taken the satans as allies instead of God, and they think that they are (rightly) guided.’

7:31.text: Sons of Adam! Take your adornment in every mosque, and eat and drink, but do not act wantonly. Surely He does not love the wanton.

7:32.text: Say: ‘Who has forbidden the adornment of God which He has brought forth for His servants, and the good things of (His) provision?’ Say: ‘It is exclusively for those who have believed in this present life on the Day of Resurrection.’ In this way We make the signs distinct for a people who know.

7:33.text: Say: ‘My Lord has only forbidden immoral deeds – (both) what is obvious of them and what is hidden – and all sin and envy – without any right – and that you associate with God what He has not sent down any authority for, and that you say about God what you do not know.’

7:34.text: For every community (there is) a time. When their time comes, they will not delay (it) by an hour, nor will they advance (it by an hour).

7:35.text: Sons of Adam! If messengers from among you should come to you, recounting to you My signs, whoever guards (himself) and sets (things) right – (there will be) no fear on them, nor will they sorrow.

7:36.text: But those who call Our signs a lie, and become arrogant about it – those are the companions of the Fire. There they will remain.

7:37.text: Who is more evil than the one who forges a lie against God, or calls His signs a lie? Those – their portion of the Book will reach them, until, when Our messengers come to them, to take them, they say, ‘Where is what you used to call on (as gods) instead of God?’ They will say, ‘They have abandoned us,’ and they will bear witness against themselves that they were disbelievers.

7:38.text: He will say, ‘Enter into the Fire, among the communities of jinn and humans who have passed away before you.’ Whenever a (new) community enters, it curses its sister (community), until, when they have all followed each other into it, the last of them will say to the first of them, ‘Our Lord, these led us astray, so give them a double punishment of the Fire.’ He will say, ‘To each a double, but you do not know.’

7:39.text: And the first of them will say to the last of them, ‘You have no advantage over us, so taste the punishment for what you have earned.’

7:40.text: Surely those who call Our signs a lie, and are arrogant about it – the gates of the sky will not be opened for them, nor will they enter the Garden, until the camel passes through the eye of the needle. In this way We repay the sinners.

7:41.text: They have a bed in Gehenna, and coverings above them. In this way We repay the evildoers.

7:42.text: But those who believe and do righteous deeds – We do not burden anyone beyond their capacity – those are the companions of the Garden. There they will remain.

7:43.text: We shall strip away whatever rancor is in their hearts. Beneath them rivers will flow, and they will say, ‘Praise (be) to God, who has guided us to this! We would not have been guided if God had not guided us. Certainly the messengers of our Lord have brought the truth.’ And they will be called out to: ‘That is the Garden! You have inherited it for what you have done.’

7:44.text: The companions of the Garden will call out to the companions of the Fire: ‘We have found what our Lord promised us (to be) true. So have you found what your Lord promised (to be) true?’ They will say, ‘Yes!’ And then a caller will call out among them: ‘The curse of God is on the evildoers,

7:45.text: who keep (people) from the way of God and desire (to make) it crooked, and they are disbelievers in the Hereafter.’

7:46.text: Between both (groups) of them (there is) a partition, and on the heights (there are) men who recognize each (of them) by their marks, and they call out to the companions of the Garden: ‘Peace (be) upon you! They have not entered it, as much as they were eager (to do so).’

7:47.text: And when their sight is turned toward the companions of the Fire, they say, ‘Our Lord, do not place us among the people who are evildoers.’

7:48.text: The men of the heights will call out to men whom they recognize by their marks, (and) say, ‘Your hoarding is of no use to you, nor what you were arrogant (about).

7:49.text: Are these the ones whom you swore God would not reach with (His) mercy? Enter the Garden! (There will be) no fear on you, nor will you sorrow.’

7:50.text: And the companions of the Fire will call out to the companions of the Garden: ‘Pour some water on us, or some of what God has provided you!’ They will say, ‘Surely God has forbidden both to the disbelievers,

7:51.text: who have taken their religion as diversion and jest. This present life has deceived them.’ So today We forget them as they forgot the meeting of this Day of theirs, and because they have denied Our signs.

7:52.text: Certainly We have brought them a Book – We have made it distinct on (the basis of) knowledge – as a guidance and mercy for a people who believe.

7:53.text: Do they expect anything but its interpretation? On the Day when its interpretation comes, those who forgot it before will say, ‘The messengers of our Lord have brought the truth. Have we any intercessors to intercede for us? Or (may) we return so that we might do other than what we have done?’ They have lost their (own) selves, and what they forged has abandoned them.

7:54.text: Surely your Lord is God, who created the heavens and the earth in six days. Then He mounted the throne. The night covers the day, which it pursues urgently, and the sun, and the moon, and the stars are subjected, (all) by His command. Is it not (a fact) that to Him (belong) the creation and the command? Blessed (be) God, Lord of the worlds!

7:55.text: Call on your Lord in humility and in secret. Surely He does not love the transgressors.

7:56.text: Do not foment corruption on the earth after it has been set right, and call on Him in fear and in eagerness. Surely the mercy of God is near to the doers of good.

7:57.text: He (it is) who sends the winds as good news before His mercy, until, when it brings a cloud heavy (with rain), We drive it to some barren land, and send down water by means of it, and bring forth by means of it every (kind of) fruit. In this way We bring forth the dead, so that you may take heed.

7:58.text: (As for) the good land, its vegetation comes forth by the permission of its Lord, but (as for) the bad, (its vegetation) comes forth only poorly. In this way We vary the signs for a people who are thankful.

7:59.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he said, ‘My people! Serve God! You have no god other than Him. Surely I fear for you the punishment of a great Day.’

7:60.text: The assembly of his people said, ‘Surely we see you are indeed clearly astray.’

7:61.text: He said, ‘My people! There is nothing astray in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds.

7:62.text: I deliver to you the messages of my Lord and I offer advice to you. I know from God what you do not know.

7:63.text: Are you amazed that a reminder has come to you from your Lord by means of a (mere) man from among you, so that he may warn you, and that you may guard (yourselves), and that you may receive compassion?’

7:64.text: But they called him a liar, so We rescued him and those with him in the ship, and We drowned those who called Our signs a lie. Surely they were a blind people.

7:65.text: And to ‘Ād (We sent) their brother Hūd. He said, ‘My people! Serve God! You have no god other than Him. Will you not guard (yourselves)?’

7:66.text: The assembly of those who disbelieved among his people said, ‘Surely we see you are indeed in foolishness, and surely we think you are indeed one of the liars.’

7:67.text: He said, ‘My people! There is no foolishness in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds.

7:68.text: I deliver to you the messages of my Lord and I am a trustworthy adviser for you.

7:69.text: Are you amazed that a reminder has come to you from your Lord by means of a (mere) man from among you, so that he may warn you? (Remember) when He made you successors after the people of Noah, and increased you in size abundantly. Remember the blessings of God, so that you may prosper.’

7:70.text: They said, ‘Have you come to us (with the message) that we should serve God alone, and forsake what our fathers have served? Bring us what you promise us, if you are one of the truthful.’

7:71.text: He said, ‘Abomination and anger from your Lord have fallen upon you. Will you dispute with me about names which you have named, you and your fathers? God has not sent down any authority for it. (Just) wait! Surely I shall be one of those waiting with you.’

7:72.text: So We rescued him and those with him by a mercy from Us, and We cut off the last remnant of those who called Our signs a lie and were not believers.

7:73.text: And to Thamūd (We sent) their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, ‘My people! Serve God! You have no god other than Him. A clear sign has come to you from your Lord: this is the she-camel of God, a sign for you. Let her graze on God’s earth, and do not touch her with evil, or a painful punishment will seize you.

7:74.text: Remember when He made you successors after ‘Ād and settled you on the earth: you took palaces from its plains, and carved houses out of the mountains. Remember the blessings of God, and do not act wickedly on the earth, fomenting corruption.’

7:75.text: The assembly of those who were arrogant among his people said to those who were weak, to those of them who believed, ‘Do you (really) know that Ṣāliḥ is an envoy from his Lord?’ They said, ‘Surely We are believers in what he has been sent with.’

7:76.text: Those who were arrogant said, ‘Surely we are disbelievers in what you have believed.’

7:77.text: So they wounded the she-camel, and disdained the command of their Lord, and said, ‘Ṣāliḥ! Bring us what you promise us, if you are one of the envoys.’

7:78.text: And then the earthquake seized them, and morning found them leveled in their home(s).

7:79.text: So he turned away from them, and said, ‘My people! Certainly I have delivered to you the message of my Lord and I offered advice to you, but you do not like advisers.’

7:80.text: And Lot, when he said to his people, ‘Do you commit (such) immorality (as) no one in all the worlds has committed before you?

7:81.text: Surely you approach men with lust instead of women. Yes! You are a wanton people.’

7:82.text: But the only response of his people was that they said, ‘Expel them from your town, (for) surely they are men who keep themselves clean.’

7:83.text: So We rescued him and his family, except his wife – she was one of those who stayed behind.

7:84.text: And We rained down on them a rain. See how the end was for the sinners!

7:85.text: And to Midian (We sent) their brother Shu‘ayb. He said, ‘My people! Serve God! You have no god other than Him. A clear sign has come to you from your Lord. Fill up the measure and the scale, and do not shortchange the people of their wealth, and do not foment corruption on the earth after it has been set right. That is better for you, if you are believers.

7:86.text: And do not sit in every path making threats, and keeping from the way of God those who believe in Him, and desiring (to make) it crooked. Remember when you were few (in number) and He multiplied you. And see how the end was for the fomenters of corruption!

7:87.text: If (there is) a contingent of you who believe in that with which I have been sent, and a contingent who do not believe, be patient until God judges between us, (for) He is the best of judges.’

7:88.text: The assembly of those who were arrogant among his people said, ‘We shall indeed expel you, Shu‘ayb, and those who believe with you, from our town, or else you will indeed return to our creed.’ He said, ‘Even if we are unwilling?

7:89.text: We would have forged a lie against God, if we returned to your creed after God rescued us from it. It is not for us to return to it, unless God our Lord (so) pleases. Our Lord comprehends everything in knowledge. In God we have put our trust. Our Lord, disclose the truth between us and our people, (for) You are the best of disclosers.’

7:90.text: The assembly of those who disbelieved among his people said, ‘If indeed you follow Shu‘ayb, surely then you will be losers indeed.’

7:91.text: And then the earthquake seized them, and morning found them leveled in their home(s).

7:92.text: Those who called Shu‘ayb a liar – (it was) as if they had not lived in prosperity there. Those who called Shu‘ayb a liar – they were the losers.

7:93.text: So he turned away from them, and said, ‘My people! Certainly I have delivered to you the messages of my Lord and I offered advice to you. How shall I grieve over a disbelieving people?’

7:94.text: We have not sent any prophet to a town, except that We seized its people with violence and hardship, so that they might humble themselves.

7:95.text: Then We exchanged good for evil, until they forgot (about it), and said, ‘Hardship and prosperity have touched our fathers.’ So we seized them unexpectedly, when they did not realize (it).

7:96.text: Yet if the people of the towns had believed and guarded (themselves), We would indeed have opened on them blessings from the sky and the earth. But they called (it) a lie, so We seized them for what they had earned.

7:97.text: Do the people of the towns feel secure that Our violence will not come upon them at night, while they are sleeping?

7:98.text: Or do the people of the towns feel secure that Our violence will not come upon them in the daylight, while they jest?

7:99.text: Do they feel secure against the scheme of God? No one feels secure against the scheme of God except the people who are losers.

7:100.text: Or is it not a guide for those who inherit the earth after its (former) people that, if We (so) please, We could smite them because of their sins, and We could set a seal on their hearts so that they do not hear?

7:101.text: Those were the towns – We recount to you some of their stories. Certainly their messengers brought them the clear signs, but they were not (able) to believe what they had called a lie before. In this way God sets a seal on the hearts of the disbelievers.

7:102.text: We did not find any covenant with most of them, but We found most of them wicked.

7:103.text: Then, after them, We raised up Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his assembly, but they did evil to them. See how the end was for the fomenters of corruption!

7:104.text: Moses said, ‘Pharaoh! Surely I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds.

7:105.text: (There is) an obligation on (me) that I do not say about God (anything) but the truth. I have brought you a clear sign from your Lord, so send forth the Sons of Israel with me.’

7:106.text: He said, ‘If you have come with a sign, bring it, if you are one of the truthful.’

7:107.text: So he cast (down) his staff, and suddenly it became a real snake.

7:108.text: And he drew forth his hand, and suddenly it became white to the onlookers.

7:109.text: The assembly of the people of Pharaoh said, ‘Surely this man is a skilled magician indeed.

7:110.text: He wants to expel you from your land. So what do you command?’

7:111.text: They said, ‘Put him and his brother off (for a while), and send searchers into the cities

7:112.text: to bring you every skilled magician.’

7:113.text: And the magicians came to Pharaoh, (and) said, ‘Surely for us (there will be) a reward indeed, if we are the victors.’

7:114.text: He said, ‘Yes, and surely you will indeed be among the ones brought near.’

7:115.text: They said, ‘Moses! Are you going to cast (first), or are we to be the ones who cast?’

7:116.text: He said, ‘Cast!’ So when they cast, they bewitched the eyes of the people, and terrified them, and produced a great (feat of) magic.

7:117.text: And We inspired Moses: ‘Cast (down) your staff!,’ and suddenly it swallowed up what they were falsely contriving.

7:118.text: So the truth came to pass, and what they were doing was invalidated.

7:119.text: They were overcome there, and turned back disgraced.

7:120.text: And the magicians were cast (down) in prostration.

7:121.text: They said, ‘We believe in the Lord of the worlds,

7:122.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.’

7:123.text: Pharaoh said, ‘You have believed in Him before I gave you permission. Surely this is indeed a scheme which you have schemed in the city to expel its people from it. But soon you will know!

7:124.text: I shall indeed cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, (and) then I shall indeed crucify you – all (of you)!’

7:125.text: They said, ‘Surely we are going to return to our Lord.

7:126.text: You are not taking vengeance on us (for any other reason) than that we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord, pour out on us patience, and take us as ones who have submitted.’

7:127.text: The assembly of the people of Pharaoh said, ‘Will you leave Moses and his people to foment corruption on the earth and to forsake you and your gods?’ He said, ‘We shall kill their sons and keep their women alive. Surely we shall be supreme over them!’

7:128.text: Moses said to his people, ‘Seek help from God and be patient. Surely the earth (belongs) to God. He causes whomever He pleases of His servants to inherit it. The outcome (belongs) to the ones who guard (themselves).’

7:129.text: They said, ‘We have suffered harm before you came to us and after you came to us.’ He said, ‘It may be that your Lord will destroy your enemy and make you successors on the earth, and then see how you will act.’

7:130.text: Certainly We seized the house of Pharaoh with years (of famine), and scarcity of fruits, so that they might take heed.

7:131.text: But when good came to them, they said, ‘This (belongs) to us,’ but if evil smote them, they attributed it to the evil omen of Moses and those with him. Is it not a fact that their evil omen was with God? But most of them did not know (it).

7:132.text: And they said, ‘Whatever kind of sign you bring us, to bewitch us by means of it, we are not going to believe in you.’

7:133.text: So We sent on them the flood, and the locusts, and the lice, and the frogs, and the blood, as distinct signs. But they became arrogant and were a sinful people.

7:134.text: When the wrath fell upon them, they said, ‘Moses! Call on your Lord for us by whatever covenant He has made with you. If indeed you remove this wrath from us, we shall indeed believe in you, and send forth the Sons of Israel with you.’

7:135.text: But when We removed the wrath from them, until a time they reached (later), suddenly they broke (their promise).

7:136.text: So We took vengeance on them and drowned them in the sea, because they called Our signs a lie and were oblivious of them.

7:137.text: And We caused the people who were weak to inherit the land We had blessed – the east (parts) and the west (parts) of it – and the best word of your Lord was fulfilled for the Sons of Israel, because they were patient. And We destroyed what Pharaoh and his people had been making and what they had been building.

7:138.text: We crossed the sea with the Sons of Israel, and they came upon a people devoted to their idols. They said, ‘Moses! Make for us a god like the gods they have.’ He said, ‘Surely you are an ignorant people.

7:139.text: Surely these – what they (are engaged) in (will be) destroyed, and what they are doing is worthless.’

7:140.text: He said, ‘Shall I seek a god for you other than God, when He has favored you over the worlds?’

7:141.text: (Remember) when We rescued you from the house of Pharaoh. They were inflicting on you the evil punishment, killing your sons and sparing your women. In that was a great test from your Lord.

7:142.text: And We appointed for Moses (a period of) thirty night(s), and We completed them with ten (more), so the meeting with his Lord was completed in forty night(s). And Moses said to his brother Aaron, ‘Be my successor among my people, and set (things) right, and do not follow the way of the fomenters of corruption.’

7:143.text: And when Moses came to Our meeting, and his Lord spoke to him, he said, ‘My Lord, show me (Yourself), so that I may look at You.’ He said, ‘You will not see Me, but look at the mountain. If it remains in its place, you will see Me.’ But when his Lord revealed His splendor to the mountain, He shattered it, and Moses fell down thunderstruck. And when he recovered, he said, ‘Glory to You! I turn to You (in repentance), and I am the first of the believers.’

7:144.text: He said, ‘Moses! I have chosen you over the people for My messages and for My word. So take what I have given you, and be one of the thankful.’

7:145.text: And We wrote for him on the Tablets an admonition of everything, and a distinct setting forth of everything: ‘So hold it fast, and command your people to take the best of it. I shall show you the home of the wicked.

7:146.text: I shall turn away from My signs those who are arrogant on the earth without any right. Even if they see every sign, they will not believe in it. And if they see the right way, they will not take it as a way, but if they see the way of error, they will take it as a way. That (is) because they called Our signs a lie and were oblivious of them.

7:147.text: Those who have called Our signs a lie, and (also) the meeting of the Hereafter – their deeds come to nothing. Will they be repaid (for anything) except for what they have done?’

7:148.text: And the people of Moses, after he (was gone), made a calf out of their ornaments – a (mere) image of it (having) a mooing sound. Did they not see that it could not speak to them or guide them to a way? (Yet) they made it and became evildoers.

7:149.text: But when they stumbled and saw that they had gone astray, they said, ‘If indeed our Lord does not have compassion on us, and does not forgive us, we shall indeed be among the losers.’

7:150.text: When Moses returned to his people, in anger (and) grief, he said, ‘Evil is what you have done as my successors, after I (left you). Have you sought to hurry the command of your Lord?’ And he cast (down) the Tablets, and seized his brother’s head, dragging him toward himself. He said, ‘Son of my mother! Surely the people thought me weak, and nearly killed me. So do not let (my) enemies gloat over me, and do not place me among the people who are evildoers.’

7:151.text: He said, ‘My Lord, forgive me and my brother, and cause us to enter into Your mercy, (for) You are the most compassionate of the compassionate.’

7:152.text: Surely those who made the calf – anger from their Lord will reach them, and humiliation in this present life. In this way We repay the forgers (of lies).

7:153.text: But those who do evil deeds, (and) then turn (in repentance) after that, and believe – surely after that your Lord is indeed forgiving, compassionate.

7:154.text: When the anger of Moses abated, he took (up) the Tablets, and in their inscription (there was) a guidance and mercy for those who fear their Lord.

7:155.text: And Moses chose his people – seventy men – for Our meeting. So when the earthquake seized them, he said, ‘My Lord, if You had pleased, You could have destroyed them before, and me (as well). Will You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done? It is only Your test by which You lead astray whomever You please and guide whomever You please. You are our ally, so forgive us and have compassion on us, (for) You are the best of forgivers.

7:156.text: And prescribe for us good in this world and in the Hereafter. Surely we have turned to You.’ He said, ‘My punishment – I smite with it whomever I please, but My mercy comprehends everything. I shall prescribe it for the ones who guard (themselves), and give the alms, and those who – they believe in Our signs –

7:157.text: those who follow the messenger, the prophet of the common people, whom they find written in their Torah and Gospel. He will command them what is right and forbid them what is wrong, and he will permit them good things and forbid them bad things, and he will deliver them of their burden and the chains that were on them. Those who believe in him, and support him and help him, and follow the light which has been sent down with him, those – they are the ones who will prosper.’

7:158.text: Say: ‘People! Surely I am the messenger of God to you – all (of you) – (the messenger of) the One to whom (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. (There is) no god but Him. He gives life and causes death. So believe in God and His messenger, the prophet of the common people, who believes in God and His words, (and) follow him, so that you may be (rightly) guided.’

7:159.text: Among the people of Moses (there was) a community which guided by the truth, and by means of it acted fairly.

7:160.text: We divided them (into) twelve tribes as communities, and We inspired Moses, when his people asked him for water: ‘Strike the rock with your staff,’ and (there) gushed forth from it twelve springs – each tribe knew its drinking place – and We overshadowed them (with) the cloud, and We sent down on them the manna and the quails: ‘Eat from the good things which We have provided you.’ They did not do Us evil, but they did themselves evil.

7:161.text: (Remember) when it was said to them, ‘Inhabit this town and eat of it wherever you please, and say: “Ḥiṭṭa,” and enter the gate in prostration. We shall forgive you your sins and increase the doers of good.’

7:162.text: But those of them who did evil exchanged a word other than that which had been spoken to them. So We sent down on them wrath from the sky, because of the evil they were doing.

7:163.text: Ask them them about the town which was near the sea, when they transgressed in (the matter of) the sabbath, when their fish came to them on the day of their sabbath, (swimming) right to the shore. But on the day when they did not observe the sabbath, they did not come to them. In this way We were testing them because they were acting wickedly.

7:164.text: (Remember) when a (certain) community of them said, ‘Why do you admonish a people whom God is going to destroy or punish (with) a harsh punishment?’ They said, ‘(As) an excuse to your Lord, and so that they might guard (themselves).’

7:165.text: So when they forgot what they were reminded of, We rescued those who had been forbidding evil, and We seized the evildoers with a violent punishment because they were acting wickedly.

7:166.text: So when they disdained what they had been forbidden from, We said to them, ‘Become apes, skulking away!’

7:167.text: (Remember) when your Lord proclaimed (that) He would indeed raise up against them – until the Day of Resurrection – those who would inflict them (with) evil punishment. Surely your Lord is indeed quick in retribution, yet surely He is indeed forgiving, compassionate.

7:168.text: We divided them (into) communities on the earth, some of them righteous and some of them other than that, and We tested them with good things and bad, so that they might return.

7:169.text: And after them came successors (who) inherited the Book, taking (the fleeting) goods of this lower (world) and saying, ‘It will be forgiven us.’ And if there comes to them goods like that (again), they will take them. Has the covenant of the Book not been taken upon them, (namely) that they should not say about God (anything) but the truth? And have they (not) studied what is in it? The Home of the Hereafter is better for the ones who guard (themselves). Will you not understand?

7:170.text: Those who hold fast the Book and observe the prayer – surely We do not let the reward of those who set (things) right go to waste.

7:171.text: (Remember) when We shook the mountain above them, as if it were a canopy, and they thought it was going to fall on them: ‘Hold fast what We have given you, and remember what is in it, so that you may guard (yourselves).’

7:172.text: (Remember) when your Lord took from the sons of Adam – from their loins – their descendants, and made them bear witness about themselves: ‘Am I not your Lord?’ They said, ‘Yes indeed! We bear witness.’ (We did that) so that you would not say on the Day of Resurrection, ‘Surely we were oblivious of this,’

7:173.text: or say, ‘Our fathers were idolaters before (us), and we are descendants after them. Will You destroy us for what the perpetrators of falsehood did?’

7:174.text: In this way We make the signs distinct, so that they will return.

7:175.text: Recite to them the story of the one to whom We gave Our signs, but he passed them by, and Satan followed him, and he became one of those who are in error.

7:176.text: If We had (so) pleased, We would indeed have raised him by it, but he clung to the earth and followed his (vain) desire. So his parable is like the parable of the dog: If you attack it, it lolls its tongue out, or if you leave it alone, it (still) lolls its tongue out. That is the parable of the people who called Our signs a lie. So recount the account, that they may reflect.

7:177.text: Evil is the parable of the people who called Our signs a lie, but (who only) did themselves evil.

7:178.text: Whoever God guides is the (rightly) guided one, and whoever He leads astray, those – they are the losers.

7:179.text: Certainly We have created for Gehenna many of the jinn and humans: they have hearts, but they do not understand with them; they have eyes, but they do not see with them; they have ears, but they do not hear with them. Those (people) are like cattle – No! They are (even) farther astray! Those – they are the oblivious.

7:180.text: To God (belong) the best names. So call on Him with them, and leave those who pervert His names. They will be repaid for what they have done.

7:181.text: Among those whom We have created is a community which guides by the truth and by means of it acts fairly.

7:182.text: But those who call Our signs a lie – We shall lead them on step by step without their realizing it,

7:183.text: and I shall spare them – surely My plan is strong.

7:184.text: Do they not reflect? Their companion is not possessed. He is only a clear warner.

7:185.text: Do they not look into the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever things God has created, and that it may be that their time has already drawn near? So in what (kind of) proclamation will they believe after this?

7:186.text: Whoever God leads astray has no guide. He leaves them in their insolent transgression, wandering blindly.

7:187.text: They ask you about the Hour: ‘When is its arrival?’ Say: ‘Knowledge of it is only with my Lord. No (one) will reveal it at its (appointed) time but He. It is heavy in the heavens and the earth, (but) it will only come upon you unexpectedly.’ They ask you as if you are well informed about it. Say: ‘Knowledge of it is only with God, but most of the people do not know (it).’

7:188.text: Say: ‘I have no power to (cause) myself benefit or harm, except for whatever God pleases. If I had knowledge of the unseen, I would indeed have acquired much good, and evil would not have touched me. I am only a warner and bringer of good news to a people who believe.’

7:189.text: He (it is) who created you from one person, and made from him his spouse, so that he might dwell with her. And when he covered her, she bore a light burden and passed on with it (unnoticed). But when she became heavy, they both called on God their Lord, ‘If indeed You give us a righteous (son), we shall indeed be among the thankful.’

7:190.text: But when He gave them a righteous (son), they set up associates for Him in (return for) what He had given them. Yet God is exalted above what they associate.

7:191.text: Do they associate (with Him) what does not create anything, since they are (themselves) created?

7:192.text: They cannot (give) them any help, nor can they (even) help themselves.

7:193.text: If you call them to the guidance, they will not follow you. (It is) the same for you whether you call them or you remain silent.

7:194.text: Surely those you call on instead of God are servants like you. So call on them and let them respond to you, if you are truthful.

7:195.text: Do they have feet with which they walk, or do they have hands with which they grasp, or do they have eyes with which they see, or do they have ears with which they hear? Say: ‘Call on your associates, (and) then plot against me and do not spare me!

7:196.text: Surely my ally is God, who has sent down the Book. He takes the righteous as allies.

7:197.text: Those you call on instead of Him cannot help you, nor can they (even) help themselves.’

7:198.text: If you call them to the guidance, they do not hear. You see them looking at you, but they do not see.

7:199.text: Take the excess, and command what is right, and turn away from the ignorant.

7:200.text: If any provocation from Satan provokes you, take refuge with God. Surely He is hearing, knowing.

7:201.text: Surely the ones who guard (themselves), when a circler from Satan touches them, remember, and suddenly they see (clearly).

7:202.text: But their brothers increase them in error, (and) then they do not stop.

7:203.text: When you do not bring them a sign, they say, ‘If only you would choose (to do) it.’ Say: ‘I only follow what I am inspired (with) from my Lord. This is evidence from your Lord, and a guidance and mercy for a people who believe.’

7:204.text: When the Qur’ān is recited, listen to it and remain silent, so that you may receive compassion.

7:205.text: Remember your Lord within yourself, in humility and in fear, and without loud words, in the mornings and the evenings. Do not be one of the oblivious.

7:206.text: Surely those who are with your Lord are not too proud to serve Him. They glorify Him and prostrate themselves before Him.

21:1.text: Their reckoning has drawn near to the people, while they are turning away oblivious.

21:2.text: No new reminder comes to them from their Lord, without their listening to it while they jest,

21:3.text: their hearts diverted. Those who do evil keep their talk secret: ‘Is this (anything) but a human being like you? Will you surrender to magic when you see (it)?’

21:4.text: Say: ‘My Lord knows the words (spoken) in the sky and the earth. He is the Hearing, the Knowing.’

21:5.text: ‘No!’ they say, ‘(It is) a jumble of dreams! No! He has forged it! No! He is a poet! Let him bring us a sign, as the ones of old were sent (with signs).’

21:6.text: Not one town which We destroyed before them believed. Will they believe?

21:7.text: We have not sent (anyone) before you except men whom We inspired – just ask the People of the Reminder, if you do not know (it) –

21:8.text: nor did We give them a body not eating food, nor were they immortal.

21:9.text: But We were true to them in the promise, so We rescued them and whomever We pleased, and We destroyed the wanton.

21:10.text: Certainly We have sent down to you a Book in which (there is) your reminder. Will you not understand?

21:11.text: How many a town (which) was doing evil have We smashed, and produced another people after it!

21:12.text: And when they sensed Our violence, suddenly they began fleeing from it.

21:13.text: ‘Do not flee, but return to what luxury you were given to delight in, and (to) your dwellings, so that you may be questioned.’

21:14.text: They said, ‘Woe to us! Surely we have been evildoers.’

21:15.text: This cry of theirs did not stop until We cut them down (and) snuffed (them) out.

21:16.text: We did not create the sky and the earth, and whatever is between them, in jest.

21:17.text: If We wanted to choose a diversion, We would indeed have chosen it from Ourselves, if We were going to do (anything).

21:18.text: No! We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it breaks its head, and suddenly it passes away. Woe to you for what you allege!

21:19.text: To Him (belongs) whoever is in the heavens and the earth, and those who are in His presence are not (too) proud for His service, nor do they grow weary (of it).

21:20.text: They glorify (Him) night and day – they do not cease.

21:21.text: Or have they taken gods from the earth? Do they raise (the dead)?

21:22.text: If there were any gods in the two of them other than God, the two would indeed go to ruin. So glory to God, Lord of the throne, above what they allege!

21:23.text: He will not be questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.

21:24.text: Or have they taken gods other than Him? Say: ‘Bring your proof! This is a Reminder (for) those who are with me, and a Reminder (for) those who were before me.’ But most of them do not know the truth, so they turn away.

21:25.text: We have not sent any messenger before you except that We inspired him: ‘(There is) no god but Me, so serve Me!’

21:26.text: They say, ‘The Merciful has taken a son.’ Glory to Him! No! (They are) honored servants.

21:27.text: They do not precede Him in speech, but they act on His command.

21:28.text: He knows what is before them and what is behind them, and they do not intercede, except for the one whom He approves, and they are apprehensive because of fear of Him.

21:29.text: Whoever of them says, ‘Surely I am a god instead of Him,’ We repay that one with Gehenna. In this way We repay the evildoers.

21:30.text: Do those who disbelieve not see that the heavens and the earth were (once) a solid mass, and We split the two of them apart, and We made every living thing from water? Will they not believe?

21:31.text: We have placed on the earth firm mountains, so that it does not sway with them (on it), and placed in it passes (to serve) as (path)ways, so that they might be guided.

21:32.text: And We have made the sky as a guarded roof. Yet they (still) turn away from its signs.

21:33.text: He (it is) who created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon, each floating in (its own) orbit.

21:34.text: We have not granted immortality to any human being before you. If you die, will they live forever?

21:35.text: Every person will taste death. We try you with evil and good as a test, and to Us you will be returned.

21:36.text: When those who disbelieve see you, they take you only in mockery: ‘Is this the one who makes mention of your gods?’ Yet they (become) disbelievers at any mention of the Merciful.

21:37.text: The human was created out of haste. I shall show you My signs, so do not ask Me to hasten (them).

21:38.text: But they say, ‘When (will) this promise (come to pass), if you are truthful?’

21:39.text: If (only) those who disbelieved knew the time when they will not be able to hold off the Fire from their faces and from their backs, nor will they be helped!

21:40.text: No! It will come upon them unexpectedly and confound them, and they will not be able to turn it back, nor will they be spared.

21:41.text: Certainly messengers have been mocked before you, but those of them who ridiculed (were) overwhelmed (by) what they were mocking.

21:42.text: Say: ‘Who will guard you in the night and the day from the Merciful?’ No! They (still) turn away from (any) reminder of their Lord.

21:43.text: Or do they have gods other than Us to protect them? They are not able to help themselves, nor will they have any companions (to shield them) from Us.

21:44.text: No! We gave these (people) and their fathers enjoyment (of life), until life had lasted a long time for them. Do they not see that We come to the land, pushing back its borders? Are they the victors?

21:45.text: Say: ‘I warn you only by means of inspiration.’ But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.

21:46.text: If indeed a whiff of the punishment of your Lord should touch them, they would indeed say, ‘Woe to us! Surely we – we have been evildoers.’

21:47.text: We shall lay down the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, and no one will be done any evil. (Even) if there is (only) the weight of a mustard seed, We shall produce it, and We are sufficient as reckoners.

21:48.text: Certainly We gave Moses and Aaron the Deliverance, and a light, and a reminder to the ones who guard (themselves),

21:49.text: who fear their Lord in the unseen, and they are apprehensive of the Hour.

21:50.text: And this is a blessed Reminder. We have sent it down. Will you reject it?

21:51.text: Certainly We gave Abraham his right (path) before (them), (for) We knew him.

21:52.text: (Remember) when he said to his father and his people: ‘What are these images which you are devoted to?’

21:53.text: They said, ‘We found our fathers serving them.’

21:54.text: He said, ‘Certainly you and your fathers were clearly astray.’

21:55.text: They said, ‘Have you brought us the truth, or are you one of those who jest?’

21:56.text: He said, ‘No! Your Lord – Lord of the heavens and the earth – is the One who created them, and I am one of the witnesses to this.

21:57.text: By God! I shall indeed plot (to destroy) your idols after you have turned away, withdrawing.’

21:58.text: So he broke them into pieces, (all) except a big one they had, so that they would return to it.

21:59.text: They said, ‘Who has done this with our gods? Surely he is indeed one of the evildoers.’

21:60.text: They said, ‘We heard a young man mentioning them – he is called Abraham.’

21:61.text: They said, ‘Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may bear witness.’

21:62.text: They said, ‘Have you done this with our gods, Abraham?’

21:63.text: He said, ‘No! This big one of them did it. Just ask them, if they are able to speak.’

21:64.text: And they turned to each other, and said, ‘Surely you – you are the evildoers.’

21:65.text: Then they became utterly confused: ‘Certainly you know (that) these do not speak.’

21:66.text: He said, ‘Do you serve what does not benefit you at all, or harm you, instead of God (alone)?

21:67.text: Uff to you and to what you serve instead of God! Will you not understand?’

21:68.text: They said, ‘Burn him, and help your gods, if you are going to do (anything).’

21:69.text: We said, ‘Fire! Be coolness and peace for Abraham!’

21:70.text: They intended a plot against him, but We made them the worst losers,

21:71.text: and We rescued him, and Lot, (and brought them) to the land which We have blessed for the worlds.

21:72.text: And We granted him Isaac, and Jacob as a gift, and each (of them) We made righteous.

21:73.text: And We made them leaders (who) guide (others) by Our command, and We inspired them (with) the doing of good deeds, and the observance of the prayer and the giving of the alms, and they served Us.

21:74.text: And Lot – We gave him judgment and knowledge, and we rescued him from the town which was doing bad things. Surely they were an evil people (and) wicked.

21:75.text: And We caused him to enter Our mercy. Surely He was one of the righteous.

21:76.text: And Noah – when he called out before (that), and We responded to him, and rescued him and his family from great distress.

21:77.text: We helped him against the people who called Our signs a lie. Surely they were an evil people, and so We drowned them – all (of them)!

21:78.text: And David and Solomon – when they rendered judgment concerning the field, when the people’s sheep had grazed in it – We were witnesses to their judgment.

21:79.text: We caused Solomon to understand it, and to each We gave judgment and knowledge. (Along) with David We subjected the mountains and the birds to glorify (Us) – We were the doers (of it).

21:80.text: We taught him the making of clothing to protect you from your (own) violence. Are you thankful?

21:81.text: And to Solomon (We subjected) the wind, blowing strongly at his command to the land which We have blessed – We have knowledge of everything.

21:82.text: And among the satans, (there were) those who dived for him and did other work besides – We were watching over them.

21:83.text: And Job – when he called on his Lord, ‘Surely hardship has touched me, and You are the most compassionate of the compassionate.’

21:84.text: We responded to him, and removed what hardship was upon him, and We gave him his family, and as much again, as a mercy from Us, and a reminder to the ones who serve.

21:85.text: And Ishmael, and Idrīs, and Dhū-l-Kifl – each one was among the patient.

21:86.text: And We caused them to enter Our mercy. Surely they were among the righteous.

21:87.text: And Dhū-l-Nūn – when he went away angry, and thought that We had no power over him, but he called out in the darkness: ‘(There is) no god but You. Glory to You! Surely I – I have been one of the evildoers.’

21:88.text: We responded to him and rescued him from (his) distress. In this way We rescue the believers.

21:89.text: And Zachariah – when he called out to his Lord: ‘My Lord, do not leave me alone, when You are the best of inheritors.’

21:90.text: We responded to him, and granted him John, and set his wife right for him. Surely they were quick in the (doing of) good deeds, and they used to call on Us in hope and fear, and humble (themselves) before Us.

21:91.text: And she who guarded her private part – We breathed into her some of Our spirit, and made her and her son a sign to the worlds:

21:92.text: ‘Surely this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord. So serve Me!’

21:93.text: But they cut up their affair among them. (Yet) all (of them) will return to Us.

21:94.text: Whoever does any righteous deeds – and he is a believer – (there will be) no ingratitude for his striving. Surely We are writing (them) down for him.

21:95.text: (There is) a ban on any town which We have destroyed: they shall not return

21:96.text: until, when Yajūj and Majūj are opened, and they come swooping down from every height,

21:97.text: and the true promise draws near, and suddenly (there) it (is)! The eyes of those who disbelieved (will be) staring (and they will say): ‘Woe to us! We were oblivious of this. No! We were evildoers.’

21:98.text: ‘Surely you, and what you were serving instead of God, are stones for Gehenna – you will go down to it.’

21:99.text: If these had been gods, they would not have gone down to it, but everyone (of them) will remain in it.

21:100.text: In it (there is) a moaning for them, and in it they do not hear (anything else).

21:101.text: Surely those for whom the best (reward) has gone forth from Us – those will be (kept) far from it.

21:102.text: They will not hear (even) a slight sound of it, and they will remain in what they themselves desired.

21:103.text: The great terror will not cause them sorrow, and the angels will meet them: ‘This is your Day which you were promised.’

21:104.text: On the Day when We shall roll up the sky like the rolling up of a scroll for the writings: as We brought about the first creation, (so) We shall restore it – (it is) a promise (binding) on Us. Surely We shall do (it)!

21:105.text: Certainly We have written in the Psalms, after the Reminder: ‘The earth – My righteous servants will inherit it.’

21:106.text: Surely in this (there is) a delivery indeed for a people who serve.

21:107.text: We have sent you only as a mercy to the worlds.

21:108.text: Say: ‘I am only inspired that your God is one God. Are you going to submit?’

21:109.text: If they turn away, say: ‘I have proclaimed to all of you equally, but I do not know whether what you are promised is near or far.

21:110.text: Surely He knows what is spoken publicly and He knows what you conceal.

21:111.text: I do not know. Perhaps it is a test for you, and enjoyment (of life) for a time.’

21:112.text: Say: ‘My Lord, judge in truth! Our Lord is the Merciful – the One to be sought for help against what you allege.’

40:2.text: The sending down of the Book is from God, the Mighty, the Knowing,

40:3.text: Forgiver of sin and Accepter of repentance, harsh in retribution, full of forbearance. (There is) no god but Him. To Him is the (final) destination.

40:4.text: No one disputes about the signs of God, except those who disbelieve. Do not let their comings and goings in the lands deceive you.

40:5.text: The people of Noah before them (also) called (it) a lie, and the factions after them, and each community was determined to seize its messenger, and disputed by means of falsehood to refute the truth. So I seized them – and how was My retribution?

40:6.text: In this way the word of your Lord has proved true against those who disbelieve: ‘They are the companions of the Fire.’

40:7.text: Those who bear the throne, and those around it, glorify their Lord with praise, and believe in Him, and they ask forgiveness for those who believe: ‘Our Lord, You comprehend everything in mercy and knowledge, so forgive those who turn (in repentance) and follow Your way. Guard them against the punishment of the Furnace,

40:8.text: Our Lord, and cause them to enter the Gardens of Eden, which You have promised them – and anyone who was righteous among their fathers, and their wives, and their descendants. Surely You – You are the Mighty, the Wise.

40:9.text: And guard them against evil deeds. Whoever You guard against evil deeds on that Day, You have had compassion on him. That is the great triumph!’

40:10.text: Surely those who disbelieved will be called to: ‘God’s hatred is indeed greater than your hatred of one another, when you were called to belief and you disbelieved.’

40:11.text: They will say, ‘Our Lord, You have caused us to die twice, and You have given us life twice. (Now) we confess our sins. (Is there) any way to get out?’

40:12.text: That is because, when God was called on alone, you disbelieved, but if (another) was associated with Him, you believed. Judgment (belongs) to God, the Most High, the Great.

40:13.text: He (it is) who shows you His signs, and sends down provision for you from the sky, but no one takes heed except the one who turns (in repentance).

40:14.text: So call on God, devoting (your) religion to Him, (even) though the disbelievers dislike (it).

40:15.text: Exalter of ranks, Holder of the throne, He casts the spirit of His command on whomever He pleases of His servants, to warn of the Day of Meeting.

40:16.text: On the Day when they go forth, nothing of theirs will be hidden from God. ‘To whom (belongs) the kingdom today?’ ‘To God, the One, the Supreme!

40:17.text: Today each person will be repaid for what he has earned. (There will be) no evil (done) today. Surely God is quick at the reckoning.’

40:18.text: Warn them of the Day of the Impending, when (their) hearts will be in (their) throats, choking (them). The evildoers will not have any loyal friend or intercessor (who) will be obeyed.

40:19.text: He knows the treachery of the eyes and what the hearts hide.

40:20.text: God will decide in truth, while those they call on instead of Him will not decide at all. Surely God – He is the Hearing, the Seeing.

40:21.text: Have they not traveled on the earth and seen how the end was for those who were before them? They were stronger than them in power, and in the traces (they left behind) on the earth. Yet God seized them in their sins, and they had no defender against God.

40:22.text: That was because they – (when) their messengers came to them with the clear signs – they disbelieved. So God seized them. Surely He is strong, harsh in retribution.

40:23.text: Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs and clear authority

40:24.text: to Pharaoh, and Haman, and Qārūn, but they said, ‘A magician, a liar!’

40:25.text: When he brought them the truth from Us, they said, ‘Kill the sons of those who believe with him, and keep their women alive.’ Yet the plot of the disbelievers always goes astray.

40:26.text: Pharaoh said, ‘Let me kill Moses, and let him call on his Lord. Surely I fear that he will change your religion, or that he will cause corruption to appear on the earth.’

40:27.text: Moses said, ‘I take refuge with my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant one (who) does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.’

40:28.text: A (certain) man, a believer from the house of Pharaoh, (who) concealed his belief, said, ‘Will you kill a man because he says, “My Lord is God,” when he has brought you the clear signs from your Lord? If he is a liar, his lie is on him, but if he is truthful, some of what he promises you will smite you. Surely God does not guide anyone who is wanton (and) a liar.

40:29.text: My people! Today the kingdom (belongs) to you (who) prevail on the earth, but who will help us against the violence of God, if it comes upon us?’ Pharaoh said, ‘I only show you what I see, and I only guide you to the right way.’

40:30.text: But the one who believed said, ‘My people! Surely I fear for you something like the Day of the Factions,

40:31.text: like the case of the people of Noah, and ‘Ād, and Thamūd, and those who (came) after them. Yet God does not intend any evil to (His) servants.

40:32.text: My people! Surely I fear for you the Day of Calling,

40:33.text: the Day when you will turn back, retreating, having no protector from God. Whoever God leads astray has no guide.
