40:34.text: Certainly Joseph brought you the clear signs before, but you did not stop doubting about what he brought you, until, when he perished, you said, “God will never raise up a messenger after him.” In this way God leads astray anyone who is wanton (and) a doubter.’

40:35.text: Those who dispute about the signs of God, without any authority having come to them – (that) is a very hateful thing in the sight of God and those who believe. In this way God sets a seal on the heart of every arrogant tyrant.

40:36.text: Pharaoh said, ‘Haman! Build a tower for me, so that I may reach the ropes,

40:37.text: the ropes of the heavens, and look upon the god of Moses. Surely I think he is a liar indeed.’ In this way the evil of his deed was made to appear enticing to Pharaoh, and he was kept from the way. But the plot of Pharaoh only (came) to ruin.

40:38.text: Certainly the one who believed said, ‘My people! Follow me, and I shall guide you to the right way.

40:39.text: My people! Surely this present life is enjoyment, but surely the Hereafter – it is the permanent Home.

40:40.text: Whoever does an evil deed will only be repaid the equal of it, but whoever does a righteous deed, whether male or female – and is a believer – those will enter the Garden, where they will be provided for without reckoning.

40:41.text: My people! Why is it that I call you to salvation, but you call me to the Fire.

40:42.text: You call me to disbelieve in God, and to associate with Him what I have no knowledge of, but I call you to the Mighty, the Forgiving.

40:43.text: (There is) no doubt that what you call me to has no calling to in this world or in the Hereafter, and that our return is to God, and that the wanton – they will be the companions of the Fire.

40:44.text: You will remember what I say to you, and (now) I commit my affair to God. Surely God sees His servants.’

40:45.text: So God guarded him against the evils of what they devised, and the evil punishment overwhelmed the house of Pharaoh.

40:46.text: The Fire – they will be presented to it morning and evening. On the Day when the Hour strikes: ‘Cause the house of Pharaoh to enter the harshest punishment!’

40:47.text: When they argue with each other in the Fire, and the weak say to those who were arrogant: ‘Surely we were your followers, so are you going relieve us (now) of any portion of the Fire?’

40:48.text: Those who were arrogant will say, ‘Surely we are all in it. Surely God has already rendered judgment among (His) servants.’

40:49.text: Those who are in the Fire will say to the keepers of Gehenna, ‘Call on your Lord to lighten for us one day of the punishment!’

40:50.text: They will say, ‘Did your messengers not bring you the clear signs?’ They will say, ‘Yes indeed!’ They will say, ‘Then call!’ But the call of the disbelievers only goes astray.

40:51.text: Surely We do indeed help Our messengers and those who believe, (both) in this present life and on the Day when the witnesses arise

40:52.text: – the Day when their excuse will not benefit the evildoers. For them (there will be) the curse, and for them (there will be) the evil home.

40:53.text: Certainly We gave Moses the guidance, and caused the Sons of Israel to inherit the Book,

40:54.text: as a guidance and reminder to those with understanding.

40:55.text: So be patient! Surely the promise of God is true. Ask forgiveness for your sin, and glorify your Lord with praise in the evening and the morning.

40:56.text: Surely those who dispute about the signs of God, without any authority having come to them – they have their minds set only on greatness, but they will not reach it. So take refuge in God! Surely He – He is the Hearing, the Seeing.

40:57.text: Indeed the creation of the heavens and earth is greater than the creation of the people, but most of the people do not know (it).

40:58.text: The blind and the sighted are not equal, nor are those who believe and do deeds of righteousness and the evildoer. Little do you take heed!

40:59.text: Surely the Hour is coming indeed – (there is) no doubt about it – but most of the people do not believe.

40:60.text: Your Lord has said, ‘Call on Me! I shall respond to you. Surely those who are too proud to serve Me will enter Gehenna humbled.’

40:61.text: (It is) God who made the night for you to rest in, and the day to see. Surely God is indeed full of favor to the people, but most of the people are not thankful (for it).

40:62.text: That is God, your Lord, Creator of everything. (There is) no god but Him. How are you (so) deluded?

40:63.text: In this way those who denied the signs of God were (also) deluded.

40:64.text: (It is) God who made the earth a dwelling place for you, and the sky a dome. He fashioned you, and made your forms well, and provided you with good things. That is God, your Lord. Blessed (be) God, Lord of the worlds!

40:65.text: He is the Living One. (There is) no god but Him. Call on Him, devoting (your) religion to Him. Praise (be) to God, Lord of the worlds!

40:66.text: Say: ‘I am forbidden to serve those whom you call on, instead of God, when the clear signs have come to me from my Lord, and I am commanded to submit to the Lord of the worlds.’

40:67.text: He (it is) who created you from dust, then from a drop, then from a clot, then He brings you forth as children, then (He provides for you) so that you may reach your maturity, then that you may become old men – though among you (there is) one who is taken before (this) – and that you may reach an appointed time, and that you may understand.

40:68.text: He (it is) who gives life and causes death, and when He decrees something, He simply says to it, ‘Be!’ and it is.

40:69.text: Do you not see those who dispute about the signs of God? How they are turned away?

40:70.text: – Those who call the Book a lie and what We sent Our messengers with? Soon they will know!

40:71.text: – When (there are) chains on their necks, and they are dragged (by) chains

40:72.text: into the boiling (water), (and) then they are poured into the Fire.

40:73.text: Then it will be said to them, ‘Where is what you used to associate,

40:74.text: instead of God?’ They will say, ‘They have abandoned us. No! We were not calling on anything before!’ In this way God leads the disbelievers astray.

40:75.text: ‘That is because you gloated on the earth without any right, and because you were jubilant.

40:76.text: Enter the gates of Gehenna, there to remain.’ Evil is the dwelling place of the arrogant!

40:77.text: So be patient! Surely the promise of God is true. Whether We show you some of that which We promise them, or take you, to Us they will be returned.

40:78.text: Certainly We sent messengers before you: some of whom We have recounted to you, and some of whom We have not recounted to you. But it was not for any messenger to bring a sign, except by the permission of God. When the command of God comes, it will be decided in truth, and the perpetrators of falsehood will lose.

40:79.text: (It is) God who made the livestock for you, for you to ride some of them and some of them to eat

40:80.text: – and (there are other) benefits for you in them – and for you to reach any place you set your mind on, and on them, and on the ship (as well), you are carried.

40:81.text: He shows you His signs – so which of the signs of God do you reject?

40:82.text: Have they not traveled on the earth and seen how the end was for those who were before them? They were more numerous than them, and stronger in power, and in the traces (they left behind) on the earth. Yet what they earned was of no use to them.

40:83.text: When their messengers brought them the clear signs, they gloated over what knowledge they (already) had, and what they were mocking overwhelmed them.

40:84.text: Then, when they saw Our violence, they said, ‘We believe in God alone, and we disbelieve in what we were associating (with Him).’

40:85.text: But their belief did not benefit them when they saw Our violence – the customary way of God, which has already occurred in the past concerning His servants – and then the disbelievers were lost.

52:7.text: Surely the punishment of your Lord is indeed going to fall!

52:8.text: (There is) no one to repel it.

52:9.text: On the Day when the sky will shake,

52:11.text: woe that Day to the ones who called (it) a lie,

52:12.text: who – they were playing around in (their) banter.

52:13.text: On the Day when they will be shoved forcefully into the fire of Gehenna:

52:14.text: ‘This is the Fire which you called a lie!

52:15.text: Is this magic or do you not see?

52:16.text: Burn in it! Bear it patiently or do not bear it patiently – (it is) the same for you. You are only being repaid for what you have done.’

52:17.text: Surely the ones who guard (themselves) will be in Gardens and bliss,

52:18.text: rejoicing in what their Lord has given them, and (because) their Lord has guarded them against the punishment of the Furnace.

52:19.text: ‘Eat and drink with satisfaction, (in return) for what you have done.’

52:20.text: (There they will be) reclining on couches lined up, and We shall marry them to (maidens) with dark, wide eyes.

52:21.text: (For) those who believe, and whose descendants followed them in belief, We shall join their descendants with them, and We shall not deprive them of any of their deeds. Each person (is held) in pledge for what he has earned.

52:22.text: We shall increase them with fruits and meat of whatever (kind) they desire.

52:23.text: There they will pass (around) a cup to each other in which there is no frivolous or sinful talk,

52:24.text: and among them will circulate boys of their own, as if they were hidden pearls.

52:25.text: Some of them will approach others asking each other questions.

52:26.text: They will say, ‘Surely we were fearful among our family before,

52:27.text: but God has bestowed favor on us, and guarded us against the punishment of the scorching (Fire).

52:28.text: Surely we used to call on Him before. Surely He – He is the Beneficent, the Compassionate.’

52:29.text: So remind (them)! By the blessing of your Lord, you are neither an oracle-giver nor possessed.

52:30.text: Or do they say, ‘A poet, for whom we await the uncertainty of Fate’?

52:31.text: Say: ‘(Just) wait! Surely I shall be one of those waiting with you.’

52:32.text: Or do their minds command them (to do) this, or are they a people who transgress insolently?

52:33.text: Or do they say, ‘He has invented it?’ No! They do not believe.

52:34.text: Let them bring a proclamation like it, if they are truthful.

52:35.text: Or were they created out of nothing? Or were they the creators?

52:36.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? No! They are not certain.

52:37.text: Or are the storehouses of your Lord with them, or are they the record-keepers?

52:38.text: Or do they have a ladder on which they (can) listen? Then let their listener bring clear authority.

52:39.text: Or does He have daughters while you have sons?

52:40.text: Or do you ask them for a reward, so that they are burdened with debt?

52:41.text: Or is the unseen in their keeping, so that they are writing it down?

52:42.text: Or do they intend a plot? Then those who disbelieve will be the ones plotted against.

52:43.text: Or do they have a god other than God? Glory to God above what they associate!

52:44.text: Even if they see fragments of the sky falling, they will say, ‘A heap of clouds!’

52:45.text: So leave them, until they meet their Day on which they will be thunderstruck

52:46.text: – the Day when their plot will be of no use to them at all, and they will not be helped.

52:47.text: Surely for those who do evil (there is) a punishment before that, but most of them do not know (it).

52:48.text: Be patient for the judgment of your Lord. Surely you are in Our sight. Glorify your Lord with praise when you arise,

52:49.text: and glorify Him during part of the night, and (at) the setting of the stars.

35:1.text: Praise (be) to God, Creator of the heavens and the earth, (who) makes the angels messengers having two, and three, and four wings. He adds to the creation whatever He pleases. Surely God is powerful over everything.

35:2.text: Whatever mercy God opens to the people, (there is) no withholder of it, and whatever (mercy) He withholds, (there is) no sender of it after that. He is the Mighty, the Wise.

35:3.text: People! Remember the blessing of God on you. (Is there) any creator other than God, (who) provides for you from the sky and the earth? (There is) no god but Him. How are you (so) deluded?

35:4.text: If they call you a liar, (know that) messengers have been called liars before you. To God all affairs are returned.

35:5.text: People! Surely the promise of God is true, so (do) not let this present life deceive you, and (do) not let the Deceiver deceive you about God.

35:6.text: Surely Satan is an enemy to you, so take him as an enemy. He only calls his faction so that they may be among the companions of the blazing (Fire).

35:7.text: Those who disbelieve – for them (there is) a harsh punishment, but those who believe and do righteous deeds – for them (there is) forgiveness and a great reward.

35:8.text: Is the one whose evil deed is made to appear enticing to him, and he perceives it as good, (like the one who is rightly guided)? Surely God leads astray whomever He pleases and guides whomever He pleases. So do not exhaust yourself in regrets over them. Surely God is aware of what they do.

35:9.text: (It is) God who sends the winds, and it stirs up a cloud, and We drive it to some barren land, and by means of it give the earth life after its death. So (too) is the raising up.

35:10.text: Whoever desires honor – honor (belongs) to God altogether. To Him good words ascend, and the righteous deed – He raises it. But those who scheme evil deeds – for them (there is) a harsh punishment, and their scheming – it will be in vain.

35:11.text: God created you from dust, then from a drop, (and) then He made you pairs. No female conceives or delivers, except with His knowledge, and no one grows old who grows old, or is diminished in his life, except (it) is in a Book. Surely that is easy for God.

35:12.text: The two seas are not alike: this one is sweet, fresh, good to drink, and this (other) one is salty (and) bitter. Yet from each you eat fresh fish, and bring out of it an ornament which you wear, and you see the ship cutting through it, so that you may seek some of His favor, and that you may be thankful.

35:13.text: He causes the night to pass into the day, and causes the day to pass into the night, and He has subjected the sun and the moon, each one running (its course) for an appointed time. That is God, your Lord – to Him (belongs) the kingdom, and those you call on, instead of Him, do not possess even the skin of a date seed.

35:14.text: If you call on them, they do not hear your calling, and (even) if they heard, they would not respond to you. On the Day of Resurrection they will deny your association. No one (can) inform you like One who is aware.

35:15.text: People! You are the ones in need of God, and God – He is the wealthy One, the Praiseworthy.

35:16.text: If He (so) pleases, He will do away with you and bring a new creation.

35:17.text: That is no great matter for God.

35:18.text: No one bearing a burden bears the burden of another. If one heavy-burdened calls for his load (to be carried), nothing of it will be carried, even though he be a family member. You warn only those who fear their Lord in the unseen, and (who) observe the prayer. Whoever purifies himself, only purifies (himself) for (the sake of) his own self. To God is the (final) destination.

35:19.text: The blind and the sighted are not equal,

35:20.text: nor are the darkness and the light,

35:21.text: nor the shade and the heat.

35:22.text: The living and the dead are not equal. Surely God causes whomever He pleases to hear. You will not cause those who are in the graves to hear

35:24.text: Surely We have sent you with the truth, as a bringer of good news and a warner. (There has) not (been) any community except that a warner has passed away in it.

35:25.text: If they call you a liar, (know that) those who were before them called (their messengers) liars. Their messengers brought them the clear signs, and the scriptures, and the illuminating Book.

35:26.text: Then I seized those who disbelieved, and how was My loathing (of them)!

35:27.text: Do you not see that God sends down water from the sky, and by means of it We bring forth fruits of various colors? And in the mountains (there are) streaks (of) white and red – their colors are diverse – and (of) deep black.

35:28.text: And among people and (wild) animals and livestock – their colors are diverse as well. Only those of His servants who have knowledge fear God. Surely God is mighty, forgiving.

35:29.text: Surely those who recite the Book of God, and observe the prayer, and contribute from what We have provided them, in secret and in open, hope for a transaction – it will not be in vain –

35:30.text: so that He may pay them their rewards in full, and increase them from His favor. Surely He is forgiving, thankful.

35:31.text: What We have inspired you (with) of the Book – it is the truth, confirming what was before it. Surely God is indeed aware of His servants (and) sees (them).

35:32.text: Then We caused those of Our servants whom We chose to inherit the Book. Some of them do themselves evil, and some of them are moderate, and some of them are foremost in good deeds, by the permission of God. That is the great favor!

35:33.text: Gardens of Eden – they will enter them. There they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and (with) pearls, and there their clothes (will be of) silk.

35:34.text: And they will say, ‘Praise (be) to God, who has taken away all sorrow from us! Surely our Lord is indeed forgiving, thankful,

35:35.text: who out of His favor has settled us in a lasting Home. No fatigue will touch us here, and no weariness will touch us here.’

35:36.text: But those who disbelieve – for them (there is) the fire of Gehenna. (Death) is not decreed for them, and so they do not die, nor will any of its punishment be lightened for them. In this way We repay every ungrateful one.

35:37.text: There they will cry out, ‘Our Lord, bring us out! We will do righteousness instead of what we used to do.’ ‘Did We not give you a long life, enough of it for the one who would take heed to take heed? The warner came to you, so taste (the punishment)! The evildoers will have no helper.’

35:38.text: Surely God knows the unseen (things) of the heavens and the earth. Surely He knows what is in the hearts.

35:39.text: He (it is) who made you successors on the earth. Whoever disbelieves, his disbelief (will be) on him. Their disbelief only increases the disbelievers in hatred in the sight of their Lord. Their disbelief only increases the disbelievers in loss.

35:40.text: Say: ‘Have you seen your associates whom you call on instead of God? Show me what part of the earth they have created. Or do they have any partnership in (the creation of) the heavens?’ Or have We given them a Book, so that they (stand) on a clear sign from it? No! The evildoers promise each other nothing but deception.

35:41.text: Surely God holds the heavens and the earth, or they would move. If indeed they moved, no one would hold them after Him. Surely He is forbearing, forgiving.

35:42.text: They have sworn by God the most solemn of their oaths: if a warner comes to them, they will be more (rightly) guided than one of the (other) communities. But when a warner came to them, it only increased them in aversion (to it),

35:43.text: (and) in arrogance on the earth, and in scheming evil. Yet evil scheming only overwhelms its own people. Do they expect anything but the customary way of those of old? You will find no change in the customary way of God. You will find no change in the customary way of God.

35:44.text: Have they not traveled on the earth and seen how the end was for those who were before them, though they were stronger than them in power? But God is not one that anything should escape Him in the heavens or on the earth. Surely He is knowing, powerful.

35:45.text: If God were to take the people to task for what they have earned, He would not leave on it any living creature. But He is sparing them until an appointed time. When their time comes – surely God sees His servants.

54:1.text: The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has been split open!

54:2.text: Yet if they see a sign, they turn away, and say, ‘Non-stop magic!’

54:3.text: They call (it) a lie, and follow their (own vain) desires, yet everything is set.

54:4.text: Certainly enough of the story has come to them to act as a deterrent –

54:5.text: far-reaching wisdom (it is) – but warnings are of no use.

54:6.text: So turn away from them. On the Day when the Caller will call to a terrible thing:

54:7.text: with sight downcast, they will come forth from the graves as if they were locusts spreading,

54:8.text: rushing to the Caller. The disbelievers will say, ‘This is a hard day!’

54:9.text: The people of Noah called (it) a lie before them, and they called Our servant a liar, and said, ‘A man possessed!’ He was deterred

54:10.text: and called on his Lord: ‘I am overcome. Help (me)!’

54:11.text: So We opened the gates of the sky with water pouring (down),

54:12.text: and made the earth gush forth with springs, and the water met for a purpose already decreed.

54:13.text: We carried him on a vessel of planks and nails,

54:14.text: running before Our eyes – a payment for the one who was disbelieved.

54:15.text: Certainly We left it as a sign, yet (is there) anyone who takes heed?

54:16.text: How were My punishment and My warnings?

54:17.text: Certainly We have made the Qur’ān easy for remembrance, yet (is there) anyone who takes heed?

54:18.text: ‘Ād called (it) a lie. How were My punishment and My warnings?

54:19.text: Surely We sent a furious wind against them on a day of the non-stop calamity.

54:20.text: It snatched the people away as if they were trunks of uprooted date palms.

54:21.text: How were My punishment and My warnings?

54:22.text: Certainly We have made the Qur’ān easy for remembrance, yet (is there) anyone who takes heed?

54:23.text: Thamūd called the warnings a lie,

54:24.text: and said, ‘Shall we follow a single human being from among us? Surely then we would indeed be astray and raving mad.

54:25.text: Has the Reminder been cast (down) on him (alone) among us? No! He is an impudent liar.’

54:26.text: ‘Tomorrow they will know who the impudent liar is!

54:27.text: We are sending the she-camel as a test for them, so watch them and be patient.

54:28.text: And inform them that the water is to be divided between them, each drink is to be brought (in turn).’

54:29.text: But they called their companion, and he took (a sword) and wounded (her).

54:30.text: How were My punishment and My warnings?

54:31.text: Surely We sent against them a single cry, and they were like the rubble (used by) the fence maker.

54:32.text: Certainly We have made the Qur’ān easy for remembrance, yet (is there) anyone who takes heed?

54:33.text: The people of Lot called the warnings a lie.

54:34.text: Surely We sent a sandstorm against them, except for the house(hold) of Lot. We rescued them at dawn

54:35.text: – a blessing from Us. In this way We repay the one who is thankful.

54:36.text: Certainly he had warned them of Our attack, and they disputed the warnings.

54:37.text: Certainly they solicited him for his guest(s), but We obliterated their eyes: ‘Taste My punishment and My warnings!’

54:38.text: Certainly in the morning a set punishment came upon them:

54:40.text: Certainly We have made the Qur’ān easy for remembrance, yet (is there) anyone who takes heed?

54:41.text: Certainly the warnings came to the house of Pharaoh.

54:42.text: They called Our signs a lie – all of it – so We seized them with the seizing of a mighty, powerful (One).

54:43.text: Are your disbelievers better than those? Or do you have an exemption in the scriptures?

54:44.text: Or do they say, ‘We shall all be victorious’?

54:45.text: They will be routed and turn their back!

54:46.text: Yes! The Hour is their appointed time, and the Hour is grievous and bitter.

54:47.text: Surely the sinners are astray and raving mad!

54:48.text: On the Day when they are dragged on their faces into the Fire: ‘Taste the effect of Saqar!’

54:49.text: Surely We have created everything in measure,

54:50.text: and Our command is but a single (act), like a blink of the eye.

54:51.text: Certainly We have destroyed your parties (before), yet (is there) anyone who takes heed?

54:52.text: Everything they have done is in the scriptures,

54:53.text: and every small and great (deed) is inscribed.

54:54.text: Surely the ones who guard (themselves) will be in (the midst of) gardens and a river,

54:55.text: in a sure seat in the presence of a powerful King.

25:1.text: Blessed (be) the One who sent down the Deliverance on His servant, so that he may be a warner to the worlds

25:2.text: – the One who – to Him (belongs) the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He has not taken a son, nor has He any associate in the kingdom. He created everything and decreed it exactly.

25:3.text: Yet they have taken gods other than Him. They do not create anything, since they are (themselves) created, and do not have power to (cause) themselves harm or benefit, and do not have power over death or life or raising up.

25:4.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘This is nothing but a lie! He has forged it, and other people have helped him with it.’ So they have come to evil and falsehood.

25:5.text: And they say, ‘Old tales! He has written it down, and it is dictated to him morning and evening.’

25:6.text: Say: ‘He has sent it down – He who knows the secret in the heavens and earth. Surely He is forgiving, compassionate.’

25:7.text: They say, ‘What is wrong with this messenger? He eats food and walks about in the markets. If only an angel were sent down to him to be a warner with him,

25:8.text: or a treasure were cast (down) to him, or he had a garden from which to eat.’ The evildoers say, ‘You are only following a man (who is) bewitched.’

25:9.text: See how they strike parables for you! But they have gone astray and cannot (find) a way.

25:10.text: Blessed is He who, if He pleases, will give you (what is) better than that – Gardens through which rivers flow – and He will give you palaces.

25:11.text: No! They have called the Hour a lie – and We have prepared a blazing (Fire) for whoever calls the Hour a lie.

25:12.text: When it sees them from a place far off, they will hear its raging and moaning.

25:13.text: When they are cast into a narrow part of it, bound in chains, they will call out there (for) destruction.

25:14.text: ‘Do not call out today (for) one destruction, but call out (for) many destruction(s)!’

25:15.text: Say: ‘Is that better, or the Garden of Eternity which is promised to the ones who guard (themselves)? It is their payment and (final) destination.’

25:16.text: Remaining there, they will have whatever they please. It is a promise (binding) on your Lord, (something) to be asked for.

25:17.text: On the Day when He will gather them and what they serve instead of God, He will say, ‘Did you lead astray these servants of Mine, or did they (themselves) go astray from the way?’

25:18.text: They will say, ‘Glory to You! It was not fitting for us to take any allies other than You, but You gave them and their fathers enjoyment (of life), until they forgot the Reminder and became a ruined people.’

25:19.text: ‘So they have called you a liar in what you say, and you are incapable of turning (it) aside or (finding any) help. Whoever among you does evil – We shall make him taste a great punishment.’

25:20.text: We have not sent any of the envoys before you, except that they indeed ate food and walked about in the markets. We have made some of you a test for others: ‘Will you be patient?’ Your Lord is seeing.

25:21.text: Those who do not expect to meet Us say, ‘If only the angels were sent down on us, or we saw our Lord.’ Certainly they have become arrogant within themselves and behaved with great disdain.

25:22.text: On the Day when they see the angels, (there will be) no good news that Day for the sinners, and they will say, ‘An absolute ban!’

25:23.text: We shall press forward to whatever deeds they have done, and make them scattered dust.

25:24.text: The companions of the Garden on that Day (will be in) a better dwelling place and a finer resting place.

25:25.text: On the Day when the sky is split open, (along) with the clouds, and the angels are sent down all at once,

25:26.text: the true kingdom that Day (will belong) to the Merciful, and it will be a hard Day for the disbelievers.

25:27.text: On the Day when the evildoer bites down on both his hands, he will say, ‘Would that I had taken a way with the messenger!

25:28.text: Woe to me! Would that I had not taken So-and-so as a friend!

25:29.text: Certainly he led me astray from the Reminder, after it came to me. Satan is the betrayer of humankind.’

25:30.text: The messenger said, ‘My Lord! Surely my people have taken this Qur’ān (as a thing to be) shunned.’

25:31.text: In this way We have assigned to every prophet an enemy from the sinners. Yet your Lord is sufficient as a guide and helper.

25:32.text: Those who disbelieve say, ‘If only the Qur’ān were sent down on him all of one piece.’ (It has been sent down) in this way, so that We may make firm your heart by means of it. We have arranged it very carefully.

25:33.text: They do not bring you any parable, except (that) We have (already) brought you the truth, and (something) better in exposition.

25:34.text: Those who are going to be gathered to Gehenna on their faces – those will be worse in position and farther astray (from the) way.

25:35.text: Certainly We gave Moses the Book, and appointed his brother Aaron as an assistant with him.

25:36.text: We said, ‘Both of you go to the people who have called Our signs a lie.’ We destroyed them completely.

25:37.text: And the people of Noah – when they called the messengers liars – We drowned them, and made them a sign for the people. We have prepared for the evildoers a painful punishment.

25:38.text: And ‘Ād, and Thamūd, and the companions of al-Rass, and many generations between that.

25:39.text: Each – We struck parables for it, and each We destroyed completely.

25:40.text: Certainly they have come upon the town which was rained on by an evil rain. Have they not seen it? No! They do not expect any raising up.

25:41.text: When they see you, they take you only in mockery: ‘Is this the one whom God has raised up as a messenger?

25:42.text: He would indeed have led us astray from our gods, had we not been patient toward them.’ Soon they will know, when they see the punishment, who is farther astray (from the) way.

25:43.text: Do you see the one who takes his own desire as his god? Will you be a guardian over him?

25:44.text: Or do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are just like cattle – No! They are (even) farther astray (from the) way!

25:45.text: Have you not regarded your Lord, how He has stretched out the shadow? If He had (so) pleased, He would indeed have made it stand still. Then We made the sun a guide for it,

25:46.text: (and) then We drew it to Us gradually.

25:47.text: He (it is) who has made the night as a covering for you, and made sleep as a rest, and He has made the day as a raising up.

25:48.text: He (it is) who has sent the winds as good news before His mercy, and We have sent down pure water from the sky,

25:49.text: so that We might give some barren land life by means of it, and give it as a drink to some of what We have created – livestock and many people.

25:50.text: Certainly We have varied it among them so that they might take heed, yet most of the people refuse (everything) but disbelief.

25:51.text: If We had (so) pleased, We would indeed have raised up a warner in every town.

25:52.text: So do not obey the disbelievers, but struggle mightily against them by means of it.

25:53.text: He (it is) who has let loose the two seas, this one sweet and fresh, and this (other) one salty (and) bitter, and placed between them a barrier, and an absolute ban.

25:54.text: He (it is) who created a human being from water, and made him related by blood and by marriage, (for) Your Lord is powerful.

25:55.text: Yet they serve what neither benefits them nor harms them, instead of God (alone). The disbeliever (always) allies himself against his Lord.

25:56.text: We have sent you only as a bringer of good news and a warner.

25:57.text: Say: ‘I do not ask you for any reward for it, except for whoever pleases to take a way to his Lord.’

25:58.text: Put your trust in the Living One who does not die, and glorify (Him) with His praise. He is sufficient (as One who) is aware of the sins of His servants,

25:59.text: who created the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, in six days. Then He mounted the throne (as) the Merciful. Ask anyone (who is) aware about Him!

25:60.text: But when it is said to them, ‘Prostrate yourselves before the Merciful,’ they say, ‘What is the Merciful? Shall we prostrate ourselves before what you command us?’ And it (only) increases them in aversion (to Him).

25:61.text: Blessed is He who has made constellations in the sky, and has made a lamp in it, and an illuminating moon.

25:62.text: He (it is) who has made the night and the day a succession – for anyone who wishes to take heed or wishes to be thankful.

25:63.text: The servants of the Merciful are those who walk humbly on the earth, and when the ignorant address them, say: ‘Peace!’

25:64.text: (They are) those who spend the night prostrating themselves and standing before their Lord,

25:65.text: and those who say, ‘Our Lord, turn away from us the punishment of Gehenna. Surely its punishment is torment!

25:66.text: Surely it is an evil dwelling and resting place!’

25:67.text: And (they are) those who, when they contribute, are neither wanton nor stingy, but right between that,

25:68.text: and those who do not call on another god with God, and do not kill the person whom God has forbidden (to be killed), except by right, and do not commit adultery – whoever does that will meet (his) penalty.

25:69.text: The punishment will be doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will remain in it humiliated,

25:70.text: except for the one who turns (in repentance), and believes, and does a righteous deed – and those, God will change their evil deeds (into) good ones, (for) God is forgiving, compassionate.

25:71.text: Whoever turns (in repentance) and does righteousness, surely he turns to God in complete repentance.

25:72.text: (They are) those who do not bear false witness, and, when they pass by any frivolous talk, they pass by with dignity,

25:73.text: and those who, when they are reminded by the signs of their Lord, do not fall down over it, deaf and blind,

25:74.text: and those who say, ‘Our Lord, grant us comfort (to our) eyes from our wives and descendants, and make us a model for the ones who guard (themselves).’

25:75.text: Those will be repaid with the exalted room because they were patient, and there they will meet a greeting and ‘Peace!’

25:76.text: There they will dwell – it is good as a dwelling and resting place.

25:77.text: Say: ‘My Lord would not care about you, if it were not for your prayer. But you have called (it) a lie, so it will be close at hand.’

22:1.text: People! Guard (yourselves) against your Lord! Surely the earthquake of the Hour is a great thing.

22:2.text: On the Day when you see it, every nursing woman will forget what she has nursed, and every pregnant female will deliver her burden, and you will see the people drunk when they are not drunk – but the punishment of God is harsh!

22:3.text: Yet among the people (there is) one who disputes about God without any knowledge, and follows every rebellious satan.

22:4.text: It is written about him: ‘He who takes him as an ally – he will lead him astray and guide him to the punishment of the blazing (Fire).’

22:5.text: People! If you are in doubt about the raising up – surely We created you from dust, then from a drop, then from a clot, (and) then from a lump, formed and unformed, so that We may make (it) clear to you. We establish in the wombs what We please for an appointed time, then We bring you forth as a child, (and) then (We provide for you) so that you may reach your maturity. Among you (there is) one who is taken, and among you (there is) one who is reduced to the worst (stage) of life, so that he knows nothing after (having had) knowledge. And you see the earth withered, but when We send down water on it, it stirs and swells, and grows (plants) of every beautiful kind.

22:6.text: That is because God – He is the Truth, and because He gives the dead life, and because He is powerful over everything,

22:7.text: and because the Hour is coming – (there is) no doubt about it – and because God will raise up those who are in the graves.

22:8.text: Yet among the people (there is) one who disputes about God without any knowledge or guidance or illuminating Book,

22:9.text: (who) turns away in scorn to lead (people) astray from the way of God. For him (there is) disgrace in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection We shall make him taste the punishment of the burning (Fire):

22:10.text: ‘That is for what your (own) hands have sent forward, and (know) that God is not an evildoer to (His) servants.’

22:11.text: Among the people (there is also) one who serves God sitting on the fence. If some good smites him, he is satisfied with it, but if some trouble smites him, he is overturned (by it). He loses this world and the Hereafter. That – it is the clearest loss!

22:12.text: Instead of God, he calls on what does not harm him, and what does not benefit him. That – it is straying the farthest!

22:13.text: He calls indeed on one whose harm is nearer than his benefit. Evil indeed is the protector, and evil indeed the friend!

22:14.text: Surely God will cause those who believe and do righteous deeds to enter Gardens through which rivers flow. Surely God does whatever He wills.

22:15.text: Whoever thinks that God will not help him in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the sky. Then let him cut (it), and see whether his scheme will take away what enrages (him).

22:16.text: In this way We have sent it down as signs – clear signs – and because God guides whomever He wills.

22:17.text: Surely those who believe, and those who are Jews, and the Sabians, and the Christians, and the Magians, and the idolaters – surely God will distinguish between them on the Day of Resurrection. Surely God is a witness over everything.

22:18.text: Do you not see that God – whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth prostrates before Him, and (so do) the sun, and the moon, and the stars, and the mountains, and the trees, and the animals, and many of the people? But (there are) many for whom the punishment is justified, and whomever God humiliates, (there is) no one to honor him. Surely God does whatever He pleases.

22:19.text: These two disputants dispute about their Lord. But those who disbelieve – clothes of fire have been cut for them, and boiling (water) will be poured (on them) from above their heads,

22:20.text: by which what is in their bellies and (their) skins will be melted.

22:21.text: And for them (there are) hooked rods of iron.

22:22.text: Whenever they want to come out of it, because of (their) agony, they will be sent back into it, and: ‘Taste the punishment of the burning (Fire)!’

22:23.text: Surely God will cause those who believe and do righteous deeds to enter Gardens through which rivers flow. There they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and (with) pearls, and there their clothes (will be of) silk.

22:24.text: They have been guided to good speech, and they have been guided to the path of the Praiseworthy.

22:25.text: Surely those who disbelieve and keep (people) from the way of God and the Sacred Mosque, which We have made for the people equally – the resident there and the visitor – and whoever intends to pervert it in an evil manner – We shall make him taste a painful punishment.

22:26.text: (Remember) when We settled the place of the House for Abraham: ‘Do not associate anything with Me, but purify My House for the ones who go around (it), and the ones who stand, and the ones who bow, (and) the ones who prostrate themselves.

22:27.text: And proclaim the pilgrimage among the people. Let them come to you on foot and on every lean animal. They will come from every remote mountain pass,

22:28.text: so that they may witness things of benefit to them, and mention the name of God, on (certain) specified days, over whatever animal of the livestock He has provided them: “Eat from them, and feed the wretched poor.”

22:29.text: Then let them bring an end to their ritual state, and fulfill their vows, and go around the ancient House.’

22:30.text: That (is the rule). Whoever respects the sacred things of God – it will be better for him with his Lord. Permitted to you (to eat) are the livestock, except for what is recited to you. Avoid the abomination of the idols, and avoid the speaking of falsehood,

22:31.text: (being) Ḥanīfs before God, not associating (anything) with Him. Whoever associates (anything) with God – (it is) as if he has fallen from the sky, and the birds snatched him away, or (as if) the wind has swept him away to some far off place.

22:32.text: That (is the rule). Whoever respects the symbols of God – surely that (comes) from the guarding of (your) hearts.

22:33.text: (There are) benefits for you in this up to an appointed time. Then their lawful place is to the ancient House.

22:34.text: For every community We have appointed a ritual: that they should mention the name of God over whatever animal of the livestock He has provided them. Your God is one God, so submit to Him. And give good news to the humble,

22:35.text: those who, when God is mentioned, their hearts become afraid, and the ones who are patient with whatever smites them, and the ones who observe the prayer, and contribute from what We have provided them.

22:36.text: The (sacrificial) animals – We have appointed them for you among the symbols of God: there is good for you in them. So mention the name of God over them, (as they stand) in lines. Then when their sides fall (to the ground), eat from them, and feed the needy and the beggar. In this way We have subjected them to you, so that you may be thankful.

22:37.text: Its flesh will not reach God, nor its blood, but the guarding (of yourselves) will reach Him from you. In this way He has subjected them to you, so that you may magnify God because He has guided you. Give good news to the doers of good.

22:38.text: Surely God will repel (evil) from those who believe. Surely God does not love any traitor (or) ungrateful one.

22:39.text: Permission is given to those who fight because they have been done evil – and surely God is indeed able to help them –

22:40.text: those who have been expelled from their homes without any right, only because they said, ‘Our Lord is God.’ But if God had not repelled some of the people by the means of others, many monasteries, and churches, and synagogues, and mosques, in which the name of God is mentioned often, would indeed have been destroyed. God will indeed help the one who helps Him – surely God is indeed strong, mighty –

22:41.text: those who, if We establish them on the earth, observe the prayer and give the alms, and command right and forbid wrong. To God (belongs) the outcome of all affairs.

22:42.text: If they call you a liar, the people of Noah called (him) a liar before you, and (so did) ‘Ād, and Thamūd,

22:43.text: and the people of Abraham, and the people of Lot,

22:44.text: and the companions of Midian, and Moses was called a liar (too). I spared the disbelievers, then I seized them, and how was My loathing (of them)!

22:45.text: How many a town have We destroyed while it was doing evil, so it is (now) collapsed on its supports! (How many) an abandoned well and well-built palace!

22:46.text: Have they not traveled on the earth? Do they have hearts to understand with or ears to hear with? Surely it is not the sight (which) is blind, but the hearts which are within the chests are blind.

22:47.text: They seek to hurry you with the punishment. God will not break His promise. Surely a day with your Lord is like a thousand years of what you count.

22:48.text: How many a town have I have spared while it was doing evil! Then I seized it. To Me is the (final) destination.

22:49.text: Say: ‘People! I am only a clear warner for you.’

22:50.text: Those who believe and do righteous deeds – for them (there is) forgiveness and generous provision.

22:51.text: But those who strive against Our signs to obstruct (them) – those are the companions of the Furnace.

22:52.text: We have not sent any messenger or any prophet before you, except that, when he began to wish, Satan cast (something) into his wishful thinking. But God cancels what Satan casts, (and) then God clearly composes His verses – surely God is knowing, wise –

22:53.text: so that He may make what Satan casts a test for those in whose hearts is a sickness, and whose hearts are hardened – and surely the evildoers are indeed in extreme defiance –

22:54.text: and so that those who have been given the knowledge may know that it is the truth from your Lord, and may believe in it, and so that their hearts may be humble before Him. Surely God is indeed guiding those who believe to a straight path.

22:55.text: Those who disbelieve will continue (to be) in doubt about it, until the Hour comes upon them unexpectedly, or the punishment of a barren Day comes upon them.

22:56.text: The kingdom on that Day (will belong) to God. He will judge between them. Those who believe and do righteous deeds (will be) in Gardens of Bliss,

22:57.text: but those who disbelieve and call Our signs a lie – for them (there will be) a humiliating punishment.

22:58.text: Those who have emigrated in the way of God, (and) then were killed or died, God will indeed provide them (with) a good provision. Surely God – He indeed is the best of providers.

22:59.text: He will indeed cause them to enter by an entrance with which they will be pleased. Surely God is indeed knowing, forbearing.

22:60.text: That (will be so). And whoever takes retribution with the same retribution he suffered, (and) then is sought out – God will indeed help him. Surely God is indeed pardoning, forgiving.

22:61.text: That is because God causes the night to pass into the day, and causes the day to pass into the night, and because God is hearing, seeing.

22:62.text: That is because God – He is the Truth, and because what they call on instead of Him, that is the falsehood, and because God is the Most High, the Great.

22:63.text: Do you not see that God sends down water from the sky, (and) then the earth becomes green? God is astute, aware.

22:64.text: To Him (belongs) whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Surely God – He indeed is the wealthy One, the Praiseworthy.

22:65.text: Do you not see that God has subjected to you whatever is on the earth, and the ship that runs on the sea by His command, and He (that) holds up the sky so that it does not fall upon the earth, except by His permission? Surely God is indeed kind (and) compassionate with the people.

22:66.text: He (it is) who gave you life, then He causes you to die, (and) then He will give you life (again). Surely the human is ungrateful indeed.

22:67.text: For every community we have appointed a ritual which they practice. So let them not argue with you about the matter, but call (them) to your Lord. Surely you are indeed on a straight guidance.

22:68.text: If they dispute with you, say: ‘God knows what you do.

22:69.text: God will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning your differences.’

22:70.text: Do you not know that God knows whatever is in the sky and the earth? Surely that is in a Book. Surely that is easy for God.

22:71.text: Instead of God, they serve what He has not sent down any authority for, and what they have no knowledge of. The evildoers will have no helper.

22:72.text: When Our signs are recited to them as clear signs, you recognize defiance in the faces of those who disbelieve – they all but attack those who recite Our signs to them. Say: ‘Shall I inform you about (something) worse than that? The Fire! God has promised it to those who disbelieve – and it is an evil destination!’

22:73.text: People! A parable is struck, so listen to it. Surely those you call on instead of God will not create a fly, even if they joined together for it. And if a fly were to snatch anything away from them, they would not (be able to) rescue it from it. Weak is the seeker and the sought (alike)!

22:74.text: They have not measured God (with) the measure due Him. Surely God is indeed strong, mighty.

22:75.text: God chooses messengers from the angels and from the people. Surely God is hearing, seeing.

22:76.text: He knows what is before them and what is behind them. To God all affairs are returned.

22:77.text: You who believe! Bow and prostrate yourselves, and serve your Lord, and do good, so that you may prosper.

22:78.text: And struggle for God with the struggling due Him. He has chosen you, and has not placed any difficulty on you in the (matter of) religion: the creed of your father Abraham. He named you Muslims, (both) before and in this, so that the messenger might be a witness against you, and that you might be witnesses against the people. So observe the prayer and give the alms, and hold fast to God. He is your Protector. Excellent is the Protector, and excellent is the Helper!

55:5.text: The sun and the moon (move) in predictable paths,

55:6.text: and the star and the tree prostrate themselves.

55:7.text: The sky – He raised it, and He laid down the scale

55:8.text: – do not transgress insolently concerning the scale,

55:9.text: but establish the weight in justice, and do not cheat concerning the scale.

55:10.text: And the earth – He laid it down for all living creatures.

55:11.text: On it (there are) fruit, and date palms with sheaths,

55:12.text: and grain with its husk, and fragrant herbs.

55:13.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:14.text: He created the human from clay like pottery,

55:15.text: and He created the jinn from a mixture of fire.

55:16.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:17.text: Lord of the two Easts, Lord of the two Wests.

55:18.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:19.text: He let loose the two seas (which) meet.

55:20.text: Between them (there is) a barrier (which) they do not seek (to cross).

55:21.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:22.text: Pearl and coral come forth from both of them.

55:23.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:24.text: His are the (ships) running, raised up on the sea like landmarks.

55:25.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:26.text: All who are on it pass away,

55:27.text: but the face of your Lord remains, full of splendor and honor.

55:28.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:29.text: (All) who are in the heavens and the earth make requests of Him. Every day He (is engaged) in some task.

55:30.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:31.text: Soon We shall be free (to attend) to you, you two burdens!

55:32.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:33.text: Assembly of jinn and humankind! If you are able to pass beyond the confines of the heavens and the earth, pass! You will not pass beyond (them) except by authority.

55:34.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:35.text: A flame of fire and a furious wind will be sent against you, and you will not (be able to) defend yourselves.

55:36.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:37.text: When the sky is split open and turns red like oil –

55:38.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:39.text: – on that Day neither human nor jinn will be questioned about his sin.

55:40.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:41.text: The sinners will be known by their mark, and they will be seized by the hair and the feet.

55:42.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:43.text: This is Gehenna which the sinners called a lie.

55:44.text: They will go around between it and hot, boiling (water).

55:45.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:46.text: But for the one who fears the position of his Lord, (there are) two gardens –

55:47.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:49.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:50.text: – in both (there are) two flowing springs –

55:51.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:52.text: – in both (there are) two of every (kind of) fruit –

55:53.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:54.text: – (they are) reclining on couches lined with brocade, and fresh fruit of both gardens is near (at hand) –

55:55.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:56.text: – in them (there are maidens) restraining (their) glances – no man or jinn has had sex with them before them –

55:57.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:58.text: – as if they were rubies and coral –

55:59.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:60.text: Is the payment for good anything but the good?

55:61.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:62.text: And besides these two (there are another) two gardens –

55:63.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:65.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:66.text: – in both (there are) two springs gushing forth –

55:67.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:68.text: – in both (there are) fruit, and date palms, and pomegranates –

55:69.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:70.text: – in them (there are) good and beautiful (maidens) –

55:71.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:73.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:74.text: – no man or jinn has had sex with them before them –

55:75.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:76.text: – (they are) reclining on green cushions and beautiful carpets –

55:77.text: Which of the blessings of your Lord will you two call a lie?

55:78.text: Blessed (be) the name of your Lord, full of splendor and honor.

82:2.text: and when the stars are scattered,

82:3.text: and when the seas are made to gush forth,

82:4.text: and when the graves are ransacked,

82:5.text: (then each) person will know what he has sent forward and kept back.

82:6.text: Human! What has deceived you about your generous Lord,

82:7.text: who created you and fashioned you and balanced you?

82:8.text: He constructed you in whatever form He pleased.

82:9.text: By no means! No! You (still) call the Judgment a lie.

82:10.text: Surely (there are) indeed watchers over you,

82:13.text: Surely the pious will indeed be in (a place of) bliss,

82:14.text: and surely the depraved will indeed be in a Furnace.

82:15.text: They will burn in it on the Day of Judgment,

82:16.text: and from it they will not be absent.

82:17.text: What will make you know what the Day of Judgment is?

82:18.text: Once again, what will make you know what the Day of Judgment is?

82:19.text: The Day when no one will have any power to (help) another. The command on that Day (will belong) to God.

34:39.text: Say: ‘Surely my Lord extends (His) provision to whomever He pleases of His servants, and restricts (it) from him (whom He pleases). Whatever thing you contribute, He will replace it, (for) He is the best of providers.’

93:2.text: By the night when it darkens!

93:3.text: Your Lord has not forsaken you, nor does He despise you.

93:4.text: The last will indeed be better for you than the first.

93:5.text: Soon indeed your Lord will give to you, and you will be pleased.

93:6.text: Did He not find you an orphan and give (you) refuge?

93:7.text: Did He not find you astray and guide (you)?

93:8.text: Did He not find you poor and enrich (you)?

93:9.text: As for the orphan, do not oppress (him),

93:10.text: and as for the beggar, do not repulse (him),

93:11.text: and as for the blessing of your Lord, proclaim (it).

81:2.text: and when the stars become dim,

81:3.text: and when the mountains are moved,

81:4.text: and when the pregnant camels are abandoned,

81:5.text: and when the wild beasts are herded together,

81:6.text: and when the seas are made to surge,

81:8.text: and when the buried baby girl is asked

81:9.text: for what sin she was killed,

81:10.text: and when the pages are spread open,

81:11.text: and when the sky is stripped off,

81:12.text: and when the Furnace is set ablaze,

81:13.text: and when the Garden is brought near,

81:14.text: (then each) person will know what he has presented.

81:17.text: by the night when it departs,

81:18.text: by the dawn when it breathes!

81:19.text: Surely it is indeed the word of an honorable messenger

81:20.text: – one full of power, secure with the Holder of the throne,

81:23.text: Certainly he did see Him on the clear horizon.

81:24.text: He is not grudging of the unseen.

81:25.text: It is not the word of an accursed satan.

81:27.text: It is nothing but a reminder to the worlds

81:28.text: – to whoever of you pleases to go straight.

81:29.text: But you will not (so) please unless God pleases, the Lord of the worlds.

89:3.text: By the even and the odd!

89:4.text: By the night when it journeys on!

89:5.text: (Is there) in that an oath for a person of understanding?

89:6.text: Do you not see how your Lord dealt with ‘Ād,

89:8.text: the like of which was never created in (all) the lands,

89:9.text: and Thamūd, who carved out the rock in the wādī,

89:10.text: and Pharaoh, he of the stakes,

89:13.text: So your Lord poured on them a scourge of punishment.

89:14.text: Surely your Lord indeed lies in wait.

89:15.text: As for the human, whenever his Lord tests him, and honors him and blesses him, he says, ‘My Lord has honored me.’

89:16.text: But whenever he tests him, and restricts his provision for him, he says, ‘My Lord has humiliated me.’

89:17.text: By no means! No! You do not honor the orphan,

89:18.text: nor do you urge the feeding of the poor,

89:19.text: yet you devour the inheritance greedily,

89:21.text: By no means! When the earth is shattered with a double shattering,

89:22.text: and your Lord comes, and the angels, line after line,

89:23.text: and Gehenna is brought (forth) on that Day – on that Day the human will (finally) take heed, but how will the reminder be for him?

89:24.text: He will say, ‘Would that I had sent forward (righteous deeds) for my life!’

89:25.text: On that Day no one will punish as He punishes,

89:26.text: and no one will bind as He binds.

89:28.text: Return to your Lord, approving (and) approved!

99:1.text: When the earth is shaken with her shaking,

99:2.text: and the earth brings forth her burdens,

99:3.text: and a person says, ‘What is (the matter) with her?’

99:4.text: On that Day she will proclaim her news,

99:5.text: because your Lord has inspired her (with it).

99:6.text: On that Day the people will come forth separately to be shown their deeds.

99:7.text: Whoever has done a speck’s weight of good will see it,

99:8.text: and whoever has done a speck’s weight of evil will see it.

38:1.text: Ṣād. By the Qur’ān, containing the Reminder!

38:2.text: – No! Those who disbelieve are in false pride and defiance.

38:3.text: How many a generation We have destroyed before them! They (all) called out, but there was no time for escape.

38:4.text: Yet they are amazed that a warner has come to them from among them. The disbelievers say, ‘This (man) is a magician, a liar!

38:5.text: Has he made the gods (into) one god? Surely this is an amazing thing indeed.’

38:6.text: The assembly of them set out (saying): ‘Walk (away), and remain steadfast to your gods. Surely this is a thing to be desired indeed.

38:7.text: We have not heard of this in the last creed. This is only a fabrication.

38:8.text: Has the Reminder been sent down on him (alone) among us?’ No! They are in doubt about My Reminder! No! They have not yet tasted My punishment!

38:9.text: Or do they have the storehouses of the mercy of your Lord, the Mighty, the Giver?

38:10.text: Or do they have the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them? Let them ascend on the ropes.

38:11.text: An army of the factions will be routed there.

38:12.text: Before them the people of Noah called (it) a lie, and ‘Ād, and Pharaoh, he of the stakes,

38:13.text: and Thamūd, and the people of Lot, and the people of the Grove – those were the factions.

38:14.text: Each of them called the messengers liars, and My retribution was justified.

38:15.text: What do these (people) expect but a single cry, for which (there will be) no delay.

38:16.text: They say, ‘Our Lord, hurry our share to us before the Day of Reckoning!’

38:17.text: Bear with what they say, and remember Our servant David, (who was) endowed with strength. Surely he turned regularly (in repentance).

38:18.text: Surely We subjected the mountains (along) with him to glorify (Us) in the evening and at sunrise,

38:19.text: and the birds (too), gathered together, all regularly turning to Him (in praise).

38:20.text: We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom and a decisive word.

38:21.text: Has the story of the dispute come to you? When they climbed over the wall of the place of prayer,

38:22.text: when they entered upon David, and he was terrified of them, but they said, ‘Do not fear! (We are) two disputants: one of us has acted oppressively toward the other. So judge between us in truth, and do not be unjust, and guide us to the right path.

38:23.text: Surely this (man) is my brother. He has ninety-nine ewes, and I have (only) one ewe. He said, “Give her into my charge,” and he overcame me in the argument.’

38:24.text: He said, ‘Certainly he has done you evil in asking for your ewe (in addition) to his ewes. Surely many (business) partners indeed act oppressively toward one another, except those who believe and do righteous deeds – but few they are.’ And David guessed that We had (somehow) tested him, so he asked his Lord for forgiveness, and fell down, bowing, and turned (in repentance).

38:25.text: So We forgave him that. Surely he has intimacy indeed with Us and a good (place of) return.

38:26.text: ‘David! Surely We have made you a ruler on the earth, so judge among the people in truth, and do not follow (vain) desire, or it will lead you astray from the way of God. Surely those who go astray from the way of God – for them (there is) a harsh punishment, because they have forgotten the Day of Reckoning.’

38:27.text: We did not create the sky and the earth, and whatever is between them, without purpose. That is the conjecture of those who disbelieve. So woe to those who disbelieve on account of the Fire!

38:28.text: Or shall We treat those who believe and do righteous deeds the same as the ones who foment corruption on the earth? Or shall We treat the ones who guard (themselves) the same as the depraved?

38:29.text: A blessed Book – We have sent it down to you, so that those with understanding may contemplate its verses and take heed.

38:30.text: To David We granted Solomon – an excellent servant he was! Surely he turned regularly (in repentance).

38:31.text: When the standing horses were presented before him in the evening,

38:32.text: he said, ‘Surely I have loved the love of good (things) more than the remembrance of my Lord, until the sun has (now) been hidden by the veil.

38:33.text: Return them to me!’ Then he began to stroke their legs and necks.

38:34.text: Certainly We tested Solomon, and placed on his throne a (mere) image. Then he turned (in repentance).

38:35.text: He said, ‘My Lord, forgive me, and grant me a kingdom (such as) will not be fitting for anyone after me (to have). Surely You – You are the Giver.’

38:36.text: So We subjected the wind to him, to blow gently at his command wherever he decided,

38:37.text: and (also) the satans, every builder and diver,

38:38.text: and others (as well) bound in chains:

38:39.text: ‘This is Our gift, so bestow or withhold without reckoning.’

38:40.text: Surely he had intimacy indeed with Us, and a good (place of) return.

38:41.text: And remember Our servant Job: When he called out to his Lord, ‘Surely I – Satan has touched me with weariness and punishment.’

38:42.text: ‘Stamp with your foot! This (will become) a cool (place for) washing and a drink.’

38:43.text: And We granted him his household, and their equivalent with them, as a mercy from Us, and a reminder to those with understanding.

38:44.text: ‘And take in your hand a bunch, and strike with it, and do not break your oath.’ Surely We found him patient – an excellent servant he was! Surely he turned regularly (in repentance).

38:45.text: And remember Our servants Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob: endowed with strength and vision.

38:46.text: Surely We purified them with a pure (thought): remembrance of the Home.

38:47.text: Surely with Us they are indeed among the chosen, the good.

38:48.text: And remember Our servants Ishmael, Elisha, and Dhū-l-Kifl: each (of them) was one of the good.

38:49.text: This is a Reminder. Surely for the ones who guard (themselves) (there is) indeed a good (place of) return:

38:50.text: Gardens of Eden, (where) the gates are open for them,

38:51.text: where they recline, (and) where they call for abundant fruit and drink.

38:52.text: With them (are maidens) restraining (their) glances, (all) of the same age.

38:53.text: ‘This is what you were promised for the Day of Reckoning.

38:54.text: Surely this is indeed Our provision – (there is) no end to it.

38:55.text: (All) this!’ But surely for the insolent transgressors (there is) indeed an evil (place of) return:

38:56.text: Gehenna, (where) they will burn – it is an evil bed!

38:57.text: (All) this! So make them taste it – boiling (water) and rotten (food),

38:58.text: and other (torments) of (this) kind in pairs.

38:59.text: ‘This is a crowd rushing in with you – for them (there is) no welcoming. Surely they will burn in the Fire.’

38:60.text: They say, ‘No! It is you for whom (there is) no welcoming. You sent it forward for us, and it is an evil resting place!’

38:61.text: They say, ‘Our Lord, whoever sent this forward for us, give him a double punishment in the Fire!’

38:62.text: And they say, ‘What is (the matter) with us (that) we do not see men (here) whom we used to count among the evil?

38:63.text: Did we take them in ridicule? Or has (our) sight turned aside from them?’

38:64.text: Surely that is true indeed – the disputing of the companions of the Fire.

38:65.text: Say: ‘I am only a warner. (There is) no god but God, the One, the Supreme,

38:66.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, the Mighty, the Forgiver.’

38:69.text: I had no knowledge of the exalted Assembly when they disputed.

38:70.text: I am only inspired that I am a clear warner.’

38:71.text: (Remember) when your Lord said to the angels: ‘Surely I am going to create a human being from clay.

38:72.text: When I have fashioned him, and breathed some of My spirit into him, fall down before him in prostration.’

38:73.text: So the angels prostrated themselves – all of them together

38:74.text: – except Iblīs. He became arrogant, and was one of the disbelievers.

38:75.text: He said, ‘Iblīs! What prevented you from prostrating yourself before what I created with My two hands? Have you become arrogant, or are you one of the exalted?’

38:76.text: He said, ‘I am better than him. You created me from fire, but You created him from clay.’

38:77.text: He said, ‘Get out of here! Surely you are accursed!

38:78.text: surely My curse (is going to remain) on you until the Day of Judgment.’

38:79.text: He said, ‘My Lord, spare me until the Day when they are raised up.’

38:80.text: He said, ‘Surely you are one of the spared

38:81.text: – until the Day of the known time.’

38:82.text: He said, ‘Then, by Your honor, I shall indeed make them err – all (of them)

38:83.text: – except for Your devoted servants among them.’

38:84.text: He said, ‘(This is) the truth, and the truth I say:

38:85.text: I shall indeed fill Gehenna with you and those of them who follow you – all (of you)!’

38:86.text: Say: ‘I do not ask you for any reward for it, nor am I one of the pretenders.

38:87.text: It is nothing but a reminder to the worlds.

38:88.text: You will indeed know its story after a time.’

83:2.text: who take full measure when they measure against the people,

83:3.text: but give less when they measure for themselves or weigh for themselves.

83:4.text: Do those (people) not think that they will be raised up

83:6.text: a Day when the people will stand before the Lord of the worlds?

83:7.text: By no means! Surely the book of the depraved is indeed in Sijjīn.

83:8.text: And what will make you know what Sijjīn is?

83:10.text: Woe that Day to the ones who call (it) a lie,

83:11.text: who call the Day of Judgment a lie!

83:12.text: No one calls it a lie except every transgressor (and) sinner.

83:13.text: When Our signs are recited to him, he says, ‘Old tales!’

83:14.text: By no means! No! What they have earned has rusted on their hearts.

83:15.text: By no means! Surely on that Day they will indeed be veiled from their Lord.

83:16.text: Then surely they will indeed burn in the Furnace.

83:17.text: Then it will be said to them, ‘This is what you called a lie.’

83:18.text: By no means! Surely the book of the pious is indeed in ‘Illiyyīn.

83:19.text: And what will make you know what ‘Illiyyīn is?

83:21.text: The ones brought near bear witness to it.

83:22.text: Surely the pious will indeed be in (a place of) bliss,

83:24.text: You will recognize in their faces the radiance of bliss.

83:25.text: They are given a pure, sealed wine to drink,

83:26.text: its seal is musk – for that let the seekers seek! –

83:28.text: (from) a spring at which the ones brought near drink.

83:29.text: Surely those who sinned used to laugh on account of those who believed,

83:30.text: and when they passed them by used to wink at each other.

83:31.text: And when they turned back to their people, they turned back amused,

83:32.text: and when they saw them, they said, ‘Surely these (people) have gone astray indeed!’

83:33.text: Yet they had not been sent as watchers over them.

83:34.text: So today those who believed are laughing on account of the disbelievers,

83:36.text: Have the disbelievers been rewarded for what they have done?

104:1.text: Woe to every slanderer, fault finder,

104:2.text: who accumulates wealth and counts it over and over!

104:3.text: He thinks that his wealth will make him last.

104:4.text: By no means! Indeed He will be tossed into al-Ḥuṭama.

104:5.text: And what will make you know what al-Ḥuṭama is?

104:7.text: which rises up to the hearts.

104:8.text: Surely it (will be) closed over them

113:1.text: Say: ‘I take refuge with the Lord of the daybreak,

113:2.text: from the evil of what He has created,

113:3.text: and from the evil of darkness when it looms,

113:4.text: and from the evil of the women who blow on knots,

113:5.text: and from the evil of an envier when he envies.’

4:1.text: People! Guard (yourselves) against your Lord, who created you from one person, and from him created his wife, and scattered from the two of them many men and women. And guard (yourselves) against God, whom you ask each other questions about, and (guard yourselves against) the wombs. Surely God is watching over you.

4:2.text: Give the orphans their property, and do not exchange the bad for the good, and do not consume their property along with your own. Surely it is a great crime.

4:3.text: If you fear that you will not act fairly toward the orphan girls, marry what seems good to you of the women: two, or three, or four. But if you fear that you will not be fair, (marry only) one, or what your right (hands) own. That (will make it) more likely that you will not be biased.

4:4.text: Give the women their dowries as a gift. If they remit to you any part of it on their own, consume it with satisfaction (and) pleasure.

4:5.text: Do not give the foolish your property which God has assigned to you to maintain, but provide for them by means of it and clothe them, and speak to them rightful words.

4:6.text: Test the orphan girls until they reach (the age of) marriage. If you perceive right judgment in them, hand over their property to them. Do not consume it wantonly or hastily before they are grown up. Whoever is wealthy should refrain (from using it), and whoever is poor should use (it) rightfully. And when you do hand over their property to them, take witnesses over them. God is sufficient as a reckoner.

4:7.text: To the men (belongs) a portion of what parents and family leave, and to the women (belongs) a portion of what parents and family leave, (whether there is) a little of it or a lot, an obligatory portion.

4:8.text: When the family, the orphans, and the poor are present at the distribution (of the estate), provide for them from it, and speak to them rightful words.

4:9.text: Let those fear who, if they left behind them weak descendants, would fear for them. Let them guard (themselves) against God, and speak a direct word.

4:10.text: Surely those who consume the property of the orphans in an evil manner, they only consume fire in their bellies, and they will burn in a blazing (Fire).

4:11.text: God charges you concerning your children: to the male, a share equal to two females. But if they be (only) women, more than two, then to them two-thirds of what he leaves. But if there be (only) one, then to her a half. And to his parents, to each of them, a sixth of what he leaves, if he has children. But if he has no children, and his heirs are his parents, then to his mother a third. And if he has brothers, then to his mother the sixth, after any bequest he may have made or any debt (has been paid). Whether your fathers or your sons are of most benefit to you, you do not know. (This is) an obligation from God. Surely God is knowing, wise.

4:12.text: And to you a half of what your wives leave, if they have no children. But if they have children, then to you the fourth of what they leave, after any bequest they may have made or any debt (has been paid). And to them the fourth of what you leave, if you have no children. But if you have children, then to them the eighth of what you leave, after any bequest you may have made or any debt (has been paid). If a man or a woman has no direct heir, but has a brother or a sister, then to each of them the sixth. But if they are more (numerous) than that, then they share in the third, after any bequest he may have made or any debt (has been paid), without prejudice (to anyone). (This is) a directive from God. God is knowing, forbearing.

4:13.text: Those are the limits (set by) God. Whoever obeys God and His messenger – He will cause him to enter Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain. That is the great triumph!

4:14.text: But whoever disobeys God and His messenger, and transgresses His limits – He will cause him to enter the Fire, there to remain. For him (there is) a humiliating punishment.

4:15.text: (As for) those of your women who commit immorality, call witnesses against them, four of you. If they bear witness (to the truth of the allegation), confine them in their houses until death takes them, or God makes a way for (dealing with) them.

4:16.text: And (if) two of you commit it, harm both of them. But if they turn (in repentance) and set (things) right, let them be. Surely God turns (in forgiveness), compassionate.

4:17.text: But God only turns (in forgiveness) to those who do evil in ignorance, (and) then turn (in repentance) soon after. Then God will turn to them (in forgiveness). God is knowing, wise.

4:18.text: But (His) turning (in forgiveness) is not for those who continue to do evil deeds, and only when death approaches say, ‘Surely I turn (in repentance) now.’ Nor (does He turn in forgiveness) to those who die while they are still disbelievers. Those – for them We have prepared a painful punishment.

4:19.text: You who believe! It is not permitted to you to inherit women against their will. And do not prevent them, so that you may take part of what you have given them, unless they commit clear immorality. Associate with them rightfully. If you dislike them, it may be that you dislike something in which God has placed much good.

4:20.text: And if you wish to exchange a wife for (another) wife, and you have given one of them a qinṭr, take (back) none of it. Would you take it (back by) slander and clear sin?

4:21.text: How can you take it (back), seeing that one of you has gone into the other, and they have taken a firm pledge from you?

4:22.text: Do not marry women whom your fathers have married, unless it is a thing of the past. Surely it is an immorality, an abhorrent thing, and an evil way.

4:23.text: Forbidden to you are: your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal aunts, your maternal aunts, (your) brothers’ daughters, (your) sisters’ daughters, the mothers who have nursed you, (those who are) your sisters by nursing, your wives’ mothers, and your stepdaughters who are in your care, (born) of wives you have gone into – but if you have not gone into them, (there is) no blame on you – and wives of your sons, those of your own loins, and that you should have two sisters at the same time, unless it is a thing of the past. Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

4:24.text: And (also forbidden to you are) married women, except what your right (hands) own. (This is) a written decree of God for you. (All women) beyond that are permitted to you to seek (to obtain) by means of your wealth, taking (them) in marriage, not in immorality. So (because of) what you enjoy from them in this way, give them their marriage gifts as an obligation. (There is) no blame on you in anything you may give them by mutual agreement beyond this obligation. Surely God is knowing, wise.

4:25.text: Whoever among you cannot wait to marry believing, free women, (let them take) believing young women from what your right (hands) own. God knows your belief, (for) you are all alike.
