6:159.text: Surely those who have divided up their religion and become (different) parties – you are no part of them. Their affair (belongs) only to God, and He will inform them about what they have done.

6:160.text: Whoever brings a good deed will have ten equal to it, but whoever brings an evil deed will only be paid the equal of it – and they will not be done evil.

6:161.text: Say: ‘Surely my Lord has guided me to a straight path, a right religion, the creed of Abraham the Ḥanīf. He was not one of the idolaters.’

6:162.text: Say: ‘Surely my prayer and my sacrifice, and my living and my dying, are for God, Lord of the worlds.

6:163.text: He has no associate. With that I have been commanded, and I am the first of those who submit.’

6:164.text: Say: ‘Shall I seek a Lord other than God, when He is the Lord of everything? No one earns (anything) except against himself, and no one bearing a burden bears the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, (and) then He will inform you about your differences.’

6:165.text: He (it is) who has made you successors on the earth, and raised some of you above others in rank, so that He might test you by what He has given you. Surely your Lord is quick in retribution, yet surely He is indeed forgiving, compassionate.

23:2.text: who are humble in their prayers,

23:3.text: and who turn away from frivolous talk,

23:5.text: and who guard their private parts,

23:6.text: except from their wives or what their right (hands) own – surely then they are not (to be) blamed,

23:7.text: but whoever seeks beyond that, those – they are the transgressors –

23:8.text: and those who keep their pledges and their promise(s),

23:11.text: who will inherit Paradise. There they will remain.

23:12.text: Certainly We created the human from an extract of clay.

23:13.text: Then We made him a drop in a secure dwelling place,

23:14.text: then We made a clot (from) the drop, then We made a lump (from) the clot, then We made bones (from) the lump, then We clothed the bones (with) flesh, (and) then We (re)produced him as another creature. So blessed (be) God, the best of creators!

23:15.text: Then, after that, you will indeed die,

23:16.text: (and) then surely on the Day of Resurrection you will be raised up.

23:17.text: Certainly We created above you seven orbits, and We were not oblivious of the creation.

23:18.text: We send down water from the sky in (due) measure, and cause it to settle in the earth – and surely We are able indeed to take it away.

23:19.text: By means of it We produce gardens of date palms and grapes for you, in which (there are) many fruits for you, and from which you eat,

23:20.text: and a tree (which) comes forth from Mount Sinai (which) bears oil and seasoning for eaters.

23:21.text: Surely in the cattle is a lesson indeed for you: We give you to drink from what is in their bellies, and in them (there are) many benefits for you, and from them you eat.

23:22.text: On them, and on the ship (as well), you are carried.

23:23.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he said, ‘My people! Serve God! You have no god other than Him. Will you not guard (yourselves)?’

23:24.text: But the assembly of those who disbelieved among his people said, ‘This is nothing but a human being like you. He wants to gain superiority over you. If God had (so) pleased, He would indeed have sent down angels. We have not heard of this among our fathers of old.

23:25.text: He is nothing but a man possessed. Wait on him for a time.’

23:26.text: He said, ‘My Lord, help me, because they are calling me a liar.’

23:27.text: So We inspired him: ‘Build the ship under Our eyes and Our inspiration, and when Our command comes and the oven boils, put into it two of every kind, a pair, and your family – except for him against whom the word has (already) gone forth. Do not address Me concerning those who have done evil. Surely they are going to be drowned!

23:28.text: When you have boarded the ship – you and (those) who are with you – say: “Praise (be) to God, who has rescued us from the people who were evildoers!”

23:29.text: And say: “My Lord, bring me to land (at) a blessed landing place, (for) You are the best of those who bring to land.”’

23:30.text: Surely in that are signs indeed. Surely We have been testing (people) indeed.

23:31.text: Then, after them, We produced another generation,

23:32.text: and We sent among them a messenger from among them: ‘Serve God! You have no god other than Him. Will you not guard (yourselves)?’

23:33.text: But the assembly of his people, those who disbelieved and called the meeting of the Hereafter a lie, even though We had given them luxury in this present life, said, ‘This is nothing but a human being like you. He eats from what you eat from, and drinks from what you drink.

23:34.text: If indeed you obey a human being like you, surely then you will be the losers indeed.

23:35.text: Does he promise you that when you are dead, and become dust and bones, that you will be brought forth?

23:36.text: Away! Away with what you are promised!

23:37.text: There is nothing but our present life. We die, and we live, and we are not going to be raised up.

23:38.text: He is nothing but a man who has forged a lie against God. We are not believers in him.’

23:39.text: He said, ‘My Lord, help me, because they are calling me a liar.’

23:40.text: He said, ‘In a little (while) they will indeed be full of regret.’

23:41.text: Then the cry seized them in truth, and We turned them into ruins. Away with the people who are evildoers!

23:42.text: Then, after them, We produced other generations.

23:43.text: No community precedes its time, nor do they delay (it).

23:44.text: Then We sent Our messengers in succession: whenever its messenger came to a community, they called him a liar. So We caused some of them to follow others, and We turned them (into) stories. Away with a people who do not believe!

23:45.text: Then We sent Moses and his brother Aaron, with Our signs and clear authority,

23:46.text: to Pharaoh and his assembly, but they became arrogant and were a haughty people.

23:47.text: They said, ‘Shall we believe in two human beings like us, when their people are serving us?’

23:48.text: So they called them both liars, and were among the destroyed.

23:49.text: Certainly We gave Moses the Book, so that they might be (rightly) guided.

23:50.text: And We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign, and We gave them both refuge on high ground, (where there was) a hollow (as) a dwelling place and a flowing spring:

23:51.text: ‘Messengers! Eat from the good things, and do righteousness! Surely I am aware of what you do.

23:52.text: Surely this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord. So guard (yourselves) against Me!’

23:53.text: But they cut their affair (in two) between them (over the) scriptures, each faction gloating over what was with them.

23:54.text: Leave them in their flood (of confusion) for a time.

23:55.text: Do they think that in increasing them with wealth and children

23:56.text: We are quick to do them good? No! But they do not realize (it).

23:57.text: Surely those who – they are apprehensive on account of fear of their Lord,

23:58.text: and those who – they believe in the signs of their Lord,

23:59.text: and those who – they do not associate (anything) with their Lord,

23:60.text: and those who give what they give, while their hearts are afraid because they are going to return to their Lord –

23:61.text: (it is) those who are quick in the (doing of) good deeds, and they are foremost in them.

23:62.text: We do not burden anyone beyond his capacity, and with Us is a Book (which) speaks in truth – and they will not be done evil.

23:63.text: No! But their hearts are in a flood (of confusion) about this, and they have deeds other than that, which they (will continue to) do

23:64.text: – until, when We seize their affluent ones with the punishment, suddenly they cry out.

23:65.text: ‘Do not cry out today! Surely you will receive no help from Us.

23:66.text: My signs were recited to you, but you turned on your heels,

23:67.text: being arrogant toward it, (and) forsaking (one who was) conversing by night.’

23:68.text: Have they not contemplated the word? Or did (there) come to them what did not come to their fathers of old?

23:69.text: Or did they not recognize their messenger, and so rejected him?

23:70.text: Or do they say, ‘He is possessed’? No! He brought them the truth, but most of them were averse to the truth.

23:71.text: If the truth had followed their desires, the heavens and the earth and whatever is in them would indeed have been corrupted. No! We brought them their Reminder, but they turned away from their Reminder.

23:72.text: Or do you ask them for payment? Yet the payment of your Lord is better, and He is the best of providers.

23:73.text: Surely you indeed call them to a straight path,

23:74.text: and surely those who do not believe in the Here-after are indeed deviating from the path.

23:75.text: Even if We had compassion on them, and removed whatever hardship they had, they would indeed persist in their insolent transgression, wandering blindly.

23:76.text: Certainly We have seized them with the punishment (already), but they did not submit themselves to their Lord, nor were they humble.

23:77.text: – Until, when We open a gate of harsh punishment on them, suddenly they are in despair about it.

23:78.text: He (it is) who has produced for you hearing and sight and hearts – little thanks you show!

23:79.text: He (it is) who has scattered you on the earth, and to Him you will be gathered.

23:80.text: He (it is) who gives life and causes death, and to Him (belongs) the alternation of the night and the day. Will you not understand?

23:81.text: No! They said just what those of old said.

23:82.text: They said, ‘When we are dead, and turned to dust and bones, shall we indeed be raised up?

23:83.text: We have been promised this before – we and our fathers. This is nothing but old tales.’

23:84.text: Say: ‘To whom (does) the earth and whatever is on it (belong), if you know?’

23:85.text: They will say, ‘To God.’ Say: ‘Will you not take heed?’

23:86.text: Say: ‘Who is Lord of the seven heavens, and Lord of the great throne?’

23:87.text: They will say, ‘To God.’ Say: ‘Will you not guard (yourselves)?’

23:88.text: Say: ‘Who is (it) in whose hand is the kingdom of everything, (who) protects and needs no protection, if you know?’

23:89.text: They will say, ‘To God.’ Say: ‘How are you (so) bewitched?’

23:90.text: No! We have brought them the truth. Surely they are liars indeed!

23:91.text: God has not taken a son, nor is there any (other) god with Him. Then each god would indeed have gone off with what he had created, and some of them would indeed have exalted (themselves) over others. Glory to God above what they allege!

23:92.text: (He is the) Knower of the unseen and the seen. He is exalted above what they associate.

23:93.text: Say: ‘My Lord, if You show me what they are promised,

23:94.text: my Lord, do not place me among the people who are evildoers.’

23:95.text: Surely We are able indeed to show you what We promise them.

23:96.text: Repel the evil with that which is better. We know what they allege.

23:97.text: And say: ‘My Lord, I take refuge with You from the incitements of the satans,

23:98.text: and I take refuge with You, my Lord, from their being present with me.’

23:99.text: – Until, when death comes to one of them, he says, ‘My Lord, send me back,

23:100.text: so that I may do righteousness concerning what I left (undone).’ By no means! Surely it is (only) a word which he says. Behind them is a barrier, until the Day when they will be raised up.

23:101.text: When there is a blast on the trumpet, (there will be) no (claims of kinship) among them on that Day, nor will they ask each other questions.

23:102.text: Whoever’s scales are heavy, those – they are the ones who prosper,

23:103.text: but whoever’s scales are light, those are the ones who have lost their (own) selves – remaining in Gehenna.

23:104.text: The Fire will scorch their faces, while they grimace in it.

23:105.text: ‘Were My signs not recited to you, and did you not call them a lie?’

23:106.text: They will say, ‘Our Lord, our miserableness overcame us, and we were a people in error.

23:107.text: Our Lord, bring us out of it! Then if we return (to evil), surely we shall be evildoers.’

23:108.text: He will say, ‘Skulk away into it, and do not speak to Me!

23:109.text: Surely there was a group of My servants (who) said, “Our Lord, we believe, so forgive us, and have compassion on us, (for) You are the best of the compassionate.”

23:110.text: But you took them (in) ridicule, until they made you forget My remembrance, and you were laughing at them.

23:111.text: Surely I have repaid them today for their patience. Surely they – they are the triumphant!’

23:112.text: He will say, ‘How long did you remain in the earth, (by) number of years?’

23:113.text: They will say, ‘We remained a day, or part of a day. Ask those who keep count.’

23:114.text: He will say, ‘You remained only a little (while) – if only you knew!

23:115.text: Did you think that We created you in vain, and that you would not be returned to Us?’

23:116.text: Exalted is God, the true King! (There is) no god but Him, Lord of the honorable throne.

23:117.text: Whoever calls on another god with God – for which he has no proof – his reckoning is with his Lord. Surely he will not prosper, (nor will) the disbelievers.

23:118.text: Say: ‘My Lord, forgive and have compassion, (for) You are the best of the compassionate.’

64:1.text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies God. To Him (belongs) the kingdom, and to Him (belongs) the praise. He is powerful over everything.

64:2.text: He (it is) who created you. One of you is a disbeliever, and one of you a believer. God sees what you do.

64:3.text: He created the heavens and the earth in truth. He fashioned you, and made your forms well. To Him is the (final) destination.

64:4.text: He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. He knows what you keep secret and what you speak aloud. God knows what is in the hearts.

64:5.text: Has the story not come to you of those who disbelieved before, and tasted the consequence of their action, and for whom (there was) a painful punishment?

64:6.text: That was because their messengers brought them the clear signs, and they said, ‘Will a human being guide us?’ So they disbelieved and turned away, but God had no need (of them). God is wealthy, praiseworthy.

64:7.text: Those who disbelieve claim that they will not be raised up. Say: ‘Yes indeed! By my Lord! You will indeed be raised up, (and) then you will indeed be informed about what you have done. That is easy for God.’

64:8.text: So believe in God and His messenger, and the light which We have sent down. God is aware of what you do.

64:9.text: On the Day when He will gather you for the Day of Gathering – that will be the Day of Mutual Defrauding. Whoever believes in God and does righteousness – He will absolve him of his evil deeds, and cause him to enter Gardens through which rivers flow, there to remain forever. That is the great triumph!

64:10.text: But those who disbelieved and called Our signs a lie – those are the companions of the Fire, there to remain – and it is an evil destination!

64:11.text: No smiting smites, except by the permission of God. Whoever believes in God – He will guide his heart. God has knowledge of everything.

64:12.text: Obey God and obey the messenger! If you turn away – only (dependent) on Our messenger is the clear delivery (of the message).

64:13.text: God – (there is) no god but Him. In God let the believers put their trust.

64:14.text: You who believe! Surely among your wives and your children (there is) an enemy to you. So beware of them. If you pardon and excuse and forgive – surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

64:15.text: Surely your wealth and your children are a trial, but God – with Him (there is) a great reward.

64:16.text: Guard (yourselves) against God as much as you are able, and hear and obey, and contribute! (That is) better for yourselves. Whoever is guarded against his own greed, those – they are the ones who prosper.

64:17.text: If you lend to God a good loan, He will double it for you, and will forgive you. God is thankful, forbearing,

64:18.text: Knower of the unseen and the seen, the Mighty, the Wise.

60:1.text: You who believe! Do not take My enemy and your enemy as allies. Do you offer them friendship when they have disbelieved in the truth which has come to you, expelling the messenger and you because you believe in God your Lord? If you have gone forth to struggle in My way and to seek My approval, do you keep secret (your) friendship for them? I know what you hide and what you speak aloud. Whoever of you does that has gone astray from the right way.

60:2.text: If they come upon you, they will be enemies to you, and will stretch out their hands and their tongues with evil against you, and want you to disbelieve.

60:3.text: Neither your family ties nor your children will benefit you on the Day of Resurrection. He will distinguish between you. God sees what you do.

60:4.text: There was a good example for you in Abraham, and those who were with him, when they said to their people, ‘Surely we are free of you and what you serve instead of God. We repudiate you, and between us and you enmity has shown itself, and hatred forever, until you believe in God alone’ – except for Abraham’s saying to his father: ‘I shall indeed ask forgiveness for you, but I have no power from God to (benefit) you at all’ – ’Our Lord, in You we put our trust, to You we turn (in repentance), and to You is the (final) destination.

60:5.text: Our Lord, do not make us an (object of) persecution for those who disbelieve, but forgive us, Our Lord. Surely You – You are the Mighty, the Wise.’

60:6.text: Certainly there was a good example for you in them – for whoever hopes in God and the Last Day. But whoever turns away – surely God – He is the wealthy One, the Praiseworthy.

60:7.text: It may be that God will (yet) establish friendship between you and those of them with whom you are on hostile terms. God is powerful, and God is forgiving, compassionate.

60:8.text: God does not forbid you from those who have not fought you in the (matter of) religion, and have not expelled you from your homes, that you should do good and act fairly toward them. Surely God loves the ones who act fairly.

60:9.text: God only forbids you from those who have fought you in the (matter of) religion, and have expelled you from your homes, and have supported your expulsion, that you should take them as allies. Whoever takes them as allies, those – they are the evildoers.

60:10.text: You who believe! When believing women come to you as emigrants, examine them – God knows their belief – and if you know them to be believers, do not return them to the disbelievers. They are not permitted to them, nor are they are permitted to them. Give them what they have spent. (There is) no blame on you if marry them, when you have given them their marriage gifts. Do not hold to ties with disbelieving women, but ask (back) what you have spent, and let them ask (back) what they have spent. That is the judgment of God. He judges between you, and God is knowing, wise.

60:11.text: If any of your wives escape from you to the disbelievers, and you take retribution, give those whose wives have gone off the equivalent of what they have spent. Guard (yourselves) against God, in whom you believe.

60:12.text: Prophet! When believing women come to you, swearing allegiance to you on (the condition) that they will not associate anything with God, and will not steal, and will not commit adultery, and will not kill their children, and will not bring a slander they have forged between their hands and their feet, and will not disobey you in anything right, accept their oath of allegiance, and ask forgiveness for them from God. Surely God is forgiving, compassionate.

60:13.text: You who believe! Do not take as allies a people with whom God is angry. They have despaired of the Hereafter, even as the disbelievers have despaired of the companions of the graves.

86:1.text: By the sky and the night visitor!

86:2.text: And what will make you know what the night visitor is?

86:4.text: Over every person (there is) a watcher.

86:5.text: So let the human consider: what was he created from?

86:6.text: He was created from spurting water.

86:7.text: It comes forth from (a place) between the spine and the ribs.

86:8.text: Surely He is able indeed to bring him back,

86:9.text: on the Day when (all) secrets will be examined,

86:10.text: and he will have no power (and) no helper.

86:11.text: By the sky full of returning (rain),

86:12.text: by the earth full of cracks!

86:13.text: Surely it is a decisive word indeed!

86:15.text: Surely they are hatching a plot,

86:16.text: but I (too) am hatching a plot.

86:17.text: So let the disbelievers be, let them be for a little (while).
